era.zh: 希腊化时期前夕（公元前323年）
era.en: Early Hellenistic Period (323 BCE)

summary.zh: 公元前323年，亚历山大大帝去世，其横跨巴尔干、埃及、近东与伊朗高原的帝国开始分裂；地中海与西亚进入希腊化时代的开端。与此同时，中国处于战国中后期，印度由难陀王朝主导北方，意大利有罗马与萨谟奈等势力并存，非洲、草原、美洲与东亚外围亦存在众多区域性政体与部族联盟。
summary.en: In 323 BCE, Alexander the Great died and his vast empire stretching across the Balkans, Egypt, the Near East, and the Iranian plateau began to fragment, marking the opening of the Hellenistic age. At the same time, China was in the middle Warring States era, northern India was dominated by the Nanda dynasty, Italy contained Rome alongside Samnite and other powers, and Africa, the steppe, the Americas, and the wider East Asian periphery were home to many regional kingdoms, tribal confederations, and emerging states.

ruler.zh: 阿吉斯四世与欧达米达斯三世

dynasty.zh: 阿吉阿德家族与欧里庞提德家族

governmentForm.zh: 双王制寡头城邦，由长老会、监察官与公民大会共同制衡
governmentForm.en: Dual kingship oligarchic polis balanced by the Gerousia, ephors, and citizen assembly

socialStructure.zh: 社会分为斯巴达人公民、边民与希洛人三大层次，公民人数已显著减少，土地与权力集中于少数家族
socialStructure.en: Society was divided into Spartiates, perioikoi, and helots, with the number of full citizens greatly reduced and land and power concentrated in a small number of families

rulingClass.zh: 少数拥有完整公民权与土地份额的斯巴达人贵族家族，以及监察官和长老会成员
rulingClass.en: A small body of Spartiate aristocratic families holding full citizenship and land allotments, together with the ephors and members of the Gerousia

succession.zh: 王位分别在阿吉阿德与欧里庞提德两王族内部按世袭继承，通常由合法男性继承人承袭
succession.en: The kingships were inherited hereditarily within the Agiad and Eurypontid royal houses, usually passing to legitimate male heirs

structure.zh: 斯巴达政体由两位国王、五名年度监察官、二十八名长老加两王组成的长老会，以及公民大会构成；实际行政影响力多掌握在监察官与少数显贵手中
structure.en: The Spartan constitution consisted of two kings, five annually elected ephors, the Gerousia of twenty-eight elders plus the two kings, and the citizen assembly; practical administrative influence often rested with the ephors and a narrow elite

function.zh: 监督国王、主持日常政务、外交与社会秩序，并对教育与希洛人管理施加控制
function.en: Oversaw the kings, directed daily administration, foreign affairs, and public order, and exercised control over education and the management of helots

function.zh: 负责审议重要法令、担任高等司法机关，并对公民大会提交议案
function.en: Deliberated major measures, served as a high judicial body, and submitted proposals to the citizen assembly

function.zh: 负责宗教祭祀、出征统帅、边境防务与战时动员
function.en: Handled royal cult functions, command in the field, frontier defense, and wartime mobilization

function.zh: 管理拉科尼亚与美塞尼亚各社区的贡赋、治安、征兵与工匠生产
function.en: Managed tribute, policing, levies, and artisanal production in the communities of Laconia and Messenia

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理主要通过斯巴达本城、周边边民社区以及对美塞尼亚与拉科尼亚乡村的监督实现，制度较简约，依赖传统、地方显族与军事控制
localAdmin.en: Local administration operated through Sparta itself, surrounding perioikic communities, and oversight of the countryside of Laconia and Messenia; it was relatively simple in form and relied on custom, local notables, and military control

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要依据莱库古式传统、习惯法与长老会及监察官裁断，刑事与政治案件带有强烈寡头与纪律性特征
legalSystem.en: Law rested chiefly on Lycurgan tradition, customary practice, and the judgments of the Gerousia and ephors, with criminal and political justice marked by oligarchic and disciplinary features

taxationSystem.zh: 斯巴达并无高度货币化的统一税制，收入主要来自附属土地收益、希洛人的实物缴纳、边民贡赋、战争战利品与盟邦贡献
taxationSystem.en: Sparta did not maintain a highly monetized uniform tax system; revenue came mainly from dependent land income, helot produce payments, perioikic contributions, war booty, and allied support

religion.zh: 希腊多神教，以阿耳忒弥斯、阿波罗、雅典娜、宙斯与地方英雄崇拜为核心，宗教与国家军事身份密切结合
religion.en: Greek polytheism centered on Artemis, Apollo, Athena, Zeus, and local hero cults, with religion closely tied to the state and military identity

philosophy.zh: 斯巴达并非希腊哲学创新中心，其政治理想强调节制、服从、勇武与集体纪律；外界常将其视为实践性政治伦理的典范或反面案例
philosophy.en: Sparta was not a major center of Greek philosophical innovation; its political ideals emphasized restraint, obedience, courage, and collective discipline, and outsiders often treated it as either a model or a cautionary case of practical civic ethics

writingSystem.zh: 使用希腊字母书写多利安希腊方言与通行希腊文
writingSystem.en: Used the Greek alphabet to write Doric Greek and broader literary Greek

culturalAchievements.zh: 以严苛军事教育、合唱诗歌传统、简约生活风格与独特政治制度闻名；虽在公元前4世纪文化创造力不及雅典，但其制度声望仍遍及希腊世界
culturalAchievements.en: Known for its rigorous military upbringing, choral poetry traditions, austere lifestyle, and distinctive constitution; though less creatively vibrant than Athens in the 4th century BCE, its institutional reputation still resonated across the Greek world

languageFamily.zh: 印欧语系希腊语族，多利安希腊语支
languageFamily.en: Indo-European, Hellenic branch, Doric Greek

gdpDescription.zh: 斯巴达经济在公元前323年已远逊其古典时代霸权水平，整体以农业地产、希洛人劳作和边民手工业为基础，货币化程度有限，但仍能维持地区性军政地位
gdpDescription.en: By 323 BCE the Spartan economy stood far below its level during the classical age of hegemony; it rested on landed agriculture, helot labor, and perioikic crafts, with limited monetization, yet it still supported a regional military and political presence

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物种植、橄榄油与葡萄酒生产、畜牧、青铜与铁器制造、武具、陶器、木工与地方贸易
mainIndustries.en: Grain farming, olive oil and wine production, pastoralism, bronze and iron work, arms manufacture, pottery, woodworking, and regional trade

tradeGoods.zh: 谷物、橄榄油、葡萄酒、金属器、武器、皮革制品、木材、羊毛与地方手工品
tradeGoods.en: Grain, olive oil, wine, metalware, weapons, leather goods, timber, wool, and local manufactures

name.zh: 德拉克马与塔兰特银计值

householdWealth.zh: 财富分布极不均衡，少数斯巴达人家族控制大部分土地，普通公民难以维持传统共餐义务，边民家庭财富中等，希洛人则几乎不拥有法定土地财富
householdWealth.en: Wealth was distributed very unevenly, with a small number of Spartiate families controlling much of the land; ordinary citizens struggled to sustain the traditional mess obligations, perioikic households held moderate means, and helots possessed little legally recognized landed wealth

economicSystem.zh: 以农业地产和依附劳动为核心的混合经济，辅以边民手工业与有限市场交换；国家意识形态重视自给与节制，抑制了商业化发展
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy centered on landed agriculture and dependent labor, supplemented by perioikic crafts and limited market exchange; state ideology prized self-sufficiency and austerity, restraining fuller commercialization

source.zh: 希洛人实物缴纳与土地收益折值
source.en: Helot produce payments and land income

source.zh: 盟助、外交馈赠与零星战利品
source.en: Allied aid, diplomatic gifts, and miscellaneous booty

category.zh: 宗教祭祀、节庆与公共礼仪
category.en: Religious rites, festivals, and public ceremony

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库规模不大，足以应对短期征召与外交开支，但无力长期支撑与马其顿式王国的持续大战
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was modest, sufficient for short-term levies and diplomatic expenses, but inadequate for sustaining prolonged warfare on the scale of Macedonian monarchies

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏保守，以维持军政核心和社会秩序为优先，依赖传统地产收入而非系统性扩张税基
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was conservative, prioritizing maintenance of the military-political core and social order, and relying on traditional landed income rather than systematic tax-base expansion

description.zh: 由完整公民组成的核心精锐，人数已较古典时代大幅下降，但仍具高声望与强纪律
description.en: The elite core composed of full citizens, much reduced in number from the classical era but still highly prestigious and disciplined

description.zh: 由边民社区提供，是斯巴达陆军的重要主体，承担多数常规作战与驻防任务
description.en: Provided by the perioikic communities, they formed a major part of the Spartan land army and handled much routine campaigning and garrison duty

description.zh: 由边缘人口、解放奴隶或附属人员构成，用于侦察、骚扰与崎岖地形作战
description.en: Drawn from marginal groups, freed dependents, or attached personnel, they were used for scouting, skirmishing, and rough-terrain warfare

description.zh: 规模较小，训练与战术传统不如马其顿骑兵发达，主要承担掩护与联络任务
description.en: Small in scale and less developed than Macedonian cavalry in training and doctrine, used mainly for screening and communication

description.zh: 负责要地驻守、乡村控制与紧急动员补充
description.en: Responsible for holding key points, controlling the countryside, and reinforcing emergency mobilizations

commanderInChief.zh: 两位斯巴达国王与监察官共同掌握最高军权
commanderInChief.en: The two Spartan kings, under the oversight of the ephors, jointly held supreme military authority

technology.zh: 装备以希腊式重装步兵武备为主，包括长矛、圆盾、胸甲与头盔；战术仍以方阵为核心，但在灵活性、攻城术和联合作战上落后于马其顿军制
technology.en: Equipment remained centered on Greek hoplite arms including spear, round shield, cuirass, and helmet; tactics still relied on the phalanx, but Sparta lagged behind Macedonian military systems in flexibility, siegecraft, and combined-arms warfare

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自马其顿在希腊的霸权、伯罗奔尼撒内部竞争、希洛人潜在不稳，以及在地区冲突中被边缘化的风险
threats.en: Major threats came from Macedonian hegemony in Greece, rivalry within the Peloponnese, potential helot unrest, and the danger of marginalization in wider regional conflicts

populationDescription.zh: 总人口约十万，包括人数极少的斯巴达人公民、数量可观的边民，以及在拉科尼亚和美塞尼亚从事农业劳动的广大希洛人
populationDescription.en: The total population was about 100,000, including a very small number of full Spartiate citizens, a substantial perioikic population, and a large body of helots laboring in Laconia and Messenia

socialClasses.zh: 上层为双王族、长老与富有斯巴达人家族；中层为一般斯巴达人公民与边民工商业者；下层为依附土地的希洛人及其他不完全自由人口
socialClasses.en: The upper stratum consisted of the two royal houses, elders, and wealthy Spartiate families; the middle stratum included ordinary Spartiates and perioikic farmers, craftsmen, and traders; the lower stratum was made up of helots tied to the land and other partially unfree populations

allies.zh: 无稳定强大盟邦网络，但在伯罗奔尼撒仍保有若干同情其反马其顿立场的城邦与派系联系
allies.en: It lacked a stable and powerful alliance network, but still retained contacts with some Peloponnesian poleis and factions sympathetic to its anti-Macedonian stance

enemies.zh: 主要对手是马其顿霸权及其在希腊的支持者；与若干伯罗奔尼撒邻邦也存在长期竞争与猜忌
enemies.en: Its principal adversary was Macedonian hegemony and its supporters in Greece; it also faced longstanding rivalry and suspicion from some neighboring Peloponnesian states

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交上力求维持独立与传统声望，反对外来霸权控制，但因资源有限，多采取谨慎观望与择机联合的策略
foreignPolicy.en: In foreign affairs Sparta sought to preserve its independence and traditional prestige, opposing external hegemonic control, but limited resources forced it toward caution, opportunism, and selective coalition-building

era.zh: 希腊化时期早期
era.en: Early Hellenistic Period

keyInnovations.zh: 斯巴达在本时期并非技术创新中心，其关键能力仍在于成熟的重装步兵训练制度、集体军事教育传统、基础金属武具制造与乡村控制组织经验
keyInnovations.en: Sparta was not a major center of innovation in this period; its key capabilities remained its mature hoplite training system, collective military upbringing, basic metal arms production, and longstanding experience in organizing rural control

strengths.zh: 拥有深厚的军事传统、强烈的城邦认同、在伯罗奔尼撒的历史威望，以及仍可动员的区域性兵力与附属人口资源
strengths.en: Its strengths lay in its deep military tradition, strong civic identity, historic prestige in the Peloponnese, and the regional manpower and dependent population resources it could still mobilize

weaknesses.zh: 公民人口锐减、贫富分化严重、经济基础狭窄、政治结构保守僵化，且面对马其顿式君主国时在财政、兵制与外交上皆处下风
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses were the severe decline in citizen numbers, sharp inequality, a narrow economic base, a conservative and rigid political structure, and inferiority in finance, military organization, and diplomacy when facing Macedonian-style monarchies

outlook.zh: 公元前323年的斯巴达仍能维持独立城邦地位并在地区事务中发声，但若无深层社会与土地改革，其长期前景将继续下滑，只能在希腊化强权之间勉力自保
outlook.en: In 323 BCE Sparta could still maintain itself as an independent polis and remain audible in regional affairs, but without deep social and land reform its long-term trajectory was downward, leaving it to struggle for survival among the Hellenistic great powers

description.zh: 昔日强权已衰落，但仍是伯罗奔尼撒的重要独立城邦。
description.en: A former great power in decline, yet still a significant independent polis in the Peloponnese.

ruler.zh: 雅典公民大会与执政官

rulerTitle.zh: 公民大会、五百人议事会与九执政官
rulerTitle.en: Assembly, Council of Five Hundred, and Nine Archons

governmentForm.zh: 受马其顿支配下保留自治制度的民主城邦
governmentForm.en: Democratic polis retaining autonomous institutions under Macedonian domination

socialStructure.zh: 社会由成年男性公民、外邦侨民、妇女、奴隶和乡村居民组成；政治权利主要集中于男性公民群体，经济与文化生活则依赖更广泛的人口结构。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of adult male citizens, resident foreigners, women, slaves, and rural inhabitants; political rights were concentrated in the male citizen body, while economic and cultural life depended on a broader population structure.

rulingClass.zh: 拥有公民资格并活跃于大会、议事会和法庭的成年男性公民，尤其是富裕家族、演说家、将领与祭司集团
rulingClass.en: Adult male citizens active in the Assembly, Council, and courts, especially wealthy families, orators, generals, and priestly groups

succession.zh: 非世袭君主制；多数重要官职通过抽签或选举产生，公民大会为最高政治权威，但关键对外事务受马其顿军政压力影响
succession.en: Not hereditary; most major offices were filled by lot or election, with the Assembly as the highest civic authority, though key external matters were constrained by Macedonian military and political pressure

structure.zh: 雅典政体以公民大会为核心，五百人议事会负责议程与日常准备，民众法庭处理司法事务，九执政官及其他年度官员承担行政和宗教职责；比雷埃夫斯的驻军与亲马其顿势力限制了其完全独立。
structure.en: The Athenian system centered on the Assembly, with the Council of Five Hundred preparing agenda and daily business, popular courts handling judicial matters, and the Nine Archons plus other annual magistrates carrying out administrative and religious functions; the garrison at Piraeus and pro-Macedonian influence limited full independence.

function.zh: 讨论和表决法律、战争、外交、财政与公民事务
function.en: Debated and voted on laws, war, diplomacy, finance, and civic matters

function.zh: 起草议程、监督官员、管理日常行政并协调部族代表
function.en: Prepared agenda, supervised magistrates, managed daily administration, and coordinated tribal representation

function.zh: 主持宗教、司法与部分礼仪行政事务
function.en: Oversaw religious affairs, judicial functions, and certain ceremonial administrative duties

function.zh: 由陪审公民审理私人和公共案件，是民主政治的重要支柱
function.en: Citizen jurors heard private and public cases, forming a major pillar of democratic politics

function.zh: 负责军事指挥、部分外交执行及紧急公共事务
function.en: Handled military command, parts of diplomatic execution, and urgent public matters

localAdmin.zh: 阿提卡划分为多个基层村社与德谟，地方祭祀、登记、征召与财产记录由地方共同体管理，并与十部族体制相衔接。
localAdmin.en: Attica was divided into local demes and communities, which managed local cults, registration, levies, and property records, integrated into the ten-tribe civic framework.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系以成文法、陪审法庭和公民诉讼为基础，强调公开辩论与陪审裁断；在323年前后仍延续古典雅典传统，但在高压外交环境下政治案件更具敏感性。
legalSystem.en: The legal system was based on written law, jury courts, and citizen litigation, emphasizing public speech and jury verdicts; by 323 BCE it still preserved classical Athenian traditions, though political cases were more sensitive under external pressure.

taxationSystem.zh: 常规收入来自港口关税、市场税、矿业与公共财产收益；公民通常不缴纳持续性的直接税，但在战争或紧急时期可征收财产税，并要求富裕公民承担礼仪捐输。
taxationSystem.en: Regular income came from port duties, market taxes, mining and public property revenues; citizens usually did not pay permanent direct taxes, but property taxes could be levied in emergencies, and wealthy citizens were expected to perform liturgies.

religion.zh: 以希腊多神教为主，崇拜雅典娜、宙斯、狄俄尼索斯、得墨忒耳和阿波罗等神祇；城邦宗教与公共节庆、戏剧竞赛和政治身份紧密相连。
religion.en: Greek polytheism predominated, with worship of Athena, Zeus, Dionysus, Demeter, Apollo, and others; civic religion was tightly bound to public festivals, dramatic competitions, and political identity.

philosophy.zh: 雅典仍是地中海最重要的哲学中心之一，学院派、逍遥学派、犬儒派和早期伊壁鸠鲁主义思想都在此活跃，伦理、政治、逻辑与自然研究并行发展。
philosophy.en: Athens remained one of the Mediterranean’s foremost centers of philosophy, where the Academy, Peripatetics, Cynics, and early Epicurean currents were active, developing ethics, politics, logic, and natural inquiry.

writingSystem.zh: 使用希腊字母书写阿提卡-伊奥尼亚希腊语方言及通行文体，碑铭、法令、戏剧、哲学著作和私人文书皆广泛采用文字。
writingSystem.en: The Greek alphabet was used to write Attic-Ionic Greek and standard literary forms, with inscriptions, decrees, drama, philosophical works, and private documents all widely employing writing.

culturalAchievements.zh: 尽管政治上受制于马其顿，雅典在哲学教育、修辞学、戏剧传统、雕塑、建筑遗产与史学记忆方面仍居希腊世界核心地位；吕克昂和学院等学术机构延续其跨地区影响。
culturalAchievements.en: Despite political subordination to Macedon, Athens remained central in the Greek world for philosophical education, rhetoric, theatrical tradition, sculpture, architectural heritage, and historical memory; institutions such as the Lyceum and Academy sustained its transregional influence.

languageFamily.zh: 印欧语系希腊语族
languageFamily.en: Indo-European, Hellenic branch

gdpDescription.zh: 雅典经济以港口贸易、手工业、银矿遗产、陶器制造、航运服务和阿提卡农业为基础。323年时其总体财富仍高于多数希腊城邦，但已不复提洛同盟时代的财政霸权。
gdpDescription.en: The Athenian economy rested on port trade, craft production, silver-mining legacy, pottery manufacture, shipping services, and Attic agriculture. In 323 BCE its overall wealth remained above that of most Greek poleis, though it no longer possessed the fiscal supremacy of the Delian League era.

mainIndustries.zh: 海运贸易、港口装卸、陶器与金属工艺、橄榄油与葡萄酒生产、建筑维修、教育与修辞教学、奴隶劳动相关产业
mainIndustries.en: Maritime trade, port handling, pottery and metalworking, olive oil and wine production, building maintenance, education and rhetorical instruction, and slave-based labor sectors

tradeGoods.zh: 出口陶器、橄榄油、银制品、工艺品和文化服务；进口粮食、木材、金属、奴隶、沥青、纸草及奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Exports included pottery, olive oil, silver goods, crafts, and cultural services; imports included grain, timber, metals, slaves, pitch, papyrus, and luxury goods

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富差距极大；富裕公民拥有土地、奴隶、船只与城市房产，中等家庭依赖小地产、工坊或服务业，贫民则常靠日工、陪审津贴、港口劳动与军役收入维生。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth varied widely; wealthy citizens held land, slaves, ships, and urban property, middling households relied on small farms, workshops, or services, while poorer people often depended on wage labor, jury pay, port work, and military service.

economicSystem.zh: 以市场交换、奴隶劳动、家庭生产与城邦调节并存的古典地中海城市经济；国家在港口、税收、宗教节庆和公共工程方面扮演重要角色。
economicSystem.en: A classical Mediterranean urban economy combining market exchange, slave labor, household production, and civic regulation; the state played an important role in ports, taxation, religious festivals, and public works.

source.zh: 公共地产、租金与矿业收益
source.en: Public lands, rents, and mining revenues

source.zh: 市场税、司法收费与杂项规费
source.en: Market taxes, legal fees, and miscellaneous charges

source.zh: 盟友性贡献、富人捐输与特别征收
source.en: Contributions, elite liturgies, and extraordinary levies

source.zh: 比雷埃夫斯相关收费与仓储收入

category.zh: 官员津贴、陪审薪给与行政

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库保持中等偏强水平，足以支持祭典、行政与短期动员，但不足以长期独立对抗马其顿。储备分散于神庙资金、公共金库及可临时调动的富人资源。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury stood at a moderately strong level, sufficient for festivals, administration, and short-term mobilization, but inadequate for sustained independent resistance to Macedon. Reserves were spread across temple funds, public treasuries, and resources that could be called from wealthy elites.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策总体谨慎，优先维持港口收入、公共秩序和宗教节庆的连续性，同时在不激怒马其顿的前提下保留一定军事和政治行动能力。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was generally cautious, prioritizing port revenue, public order, and continuity of religious festivals, while preserving some military and political capacity without provoking Macedon excessively.

description.zh: 由公民及部分装备较好的居民组成，是传统城邦军力核心，但训练与规模已不及古典时期巅峰。
description.en: Composed of citizens and some well-equipped residents, this remained the core of traditional polis military power, though training and scale were below the classical peak.

description.zh: 包括标枪兵、弓手与辅助部队，适合守城、巡逻与短期野战支援。
description.en: Included javelin-men, archers, and auxiliary troops suited for garrison duty, patrols, and short-field support.

description.zh: 雅典仍保有海军传统和有限舰艇运作能力，主要用于护航、港口控制与地区行动，而非昔日爱琴海制海权。
description.en: Athens still retained naval traditions and a limited operational fleet used mainly for escort, port control, and regional action rather than former Aegean supremacy.

description.zh: 数量较少，主要承担侦察、传令和有限机动任务。
description.en: Small in number, serving mainly for reconnaissance, dispatch, and limited mobility tasks.

commanderInChief.zh: 民选将军团受公民大会监督
commanderInChief.en: Elected strategoi under supervision of the Assembly

technology.zh: 军备以希腊式长矛、圆盾、头盔、胸甲、弓箭和攻城器械为主；海军使用三列桨战船及其后续型，但维护能力受财政和政治局势限制。
technology.en: Armament centered on Greek spears, round shields, helmets, cuirasses, bows, and siege equipment; the navy used triremes and derivative vessels, though maintenance capacity was constrained by finance and politics.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自马其顿驻军与政治干预，以及希腊内部反马其顿联盟失败后的报复风险；次要威胁包括海盗、区域动荡和贸易线受阻。
threats.en: The main threats came from Macedonian garrisons and political interference, as well as the risk of reprisal after failed anti-Macedonian coalition efforts; secondary threats included piracy, regional instability, and disruption of trade routes.

populationDescription.zh: 总人口包括雅典城、比雷埃夫斯及整个阿提卡地区居民，含男性公民、家属、外邦侨民与大量奴隶。具完整政治权利者仅占总人口一小部分。
populationDescription.en: The total population included inhabitants of Athens, Piraeus, and the wider Attic countryside, comprising male citizens, their families, resident foreigners, and a large slave population. Those with full political rights were only a small fraction of the total.

socialClasses.zh: 上层为富裕公民家族、将军、祭司与大商人；中层为小地主、工匠、船主、店主和文教从业者；下层包括贫民公民、雇工与码头劳动者；此外有无公民权的外邦侨民和人数众多的奴隶。
socialClasses.en: The upper stratum included wealthy citizen families, generals, priests, and major merchants; the middle comprised small landholders, artisans, shipowners, shopkeepers, and educational professionals; the lower included poor citizens, laborers, and dock workers; alongside them stood resident foreigners without citizenship and numerous slaves.

allies.zh: 名义上与部分希腊城邦保持传统联系，并可能寻求与埃托利亚人及其他反马其顿势力合作，但323年时缺乏可靠强大盟友。
allies.en: It maintained traditional ties with some Greek poleis and could seek cooperation with Aetolians and other anti-Macedonian forces, but in 323 BCE lacked strong dependable allies.

enemies.zh: 主要对手是支配希腊事务的马其顿政权及其在希腊的驻防力量；与斯巴达虽无持续全面战争，但存在长期竞争记忆。
enemies.en: The principal adversary was the Macedonian regime dominating Greek affairs and its garrison forces in Greece; while not in continuous full war with Sparta, there remained a long memory of rivalry.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交上采取谨慎平衡路线：一方面维护城邦尊严、海上贸易和文化声望，另一方面避免在力量不足时公开挑战马其顿；亚历山大死后，雅典内部反马其顿情绪明显上升。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy followed a cautious balancing course: preserving civic dignity, maritime trade, and cultural prestige on one hand, while avoiding open confrontation with Macedon when militarily weak on the other; after Alexander’s death, anti-Macedonian sentiment in Athens rose sharply.

era.zh: 希腊化时期早期
era.en: Early Hellenistic Period

keyInnovations.zh: 在造船、石工建筑、青铜铸造、城市供水、数学推理、逻辑分析和哲学教育制度上保持先进水平；雅典更突出的是知识生产与制度化教育，而非军事技术突破。
keyInnovations.en: It maintained advanced standards in shipbuilding, stone construction, bronze casting, urban water systems, mathematical reasoning, logical analysis, and philosophical education; Athens stood out more for knowledge production and institutionalized learning than for military technological breakthroughs.

strengths.zh: 拥有深厚的民主制度传统、活跃的港口经济、杰出的文化与教育声望，以及仍具规模的城市人口和海上经验。
strengths.en: It possessed deep democratic traditions, a lively port economy, outstanding cultural and educational prestige, and still-significant urban population and maritime experience.

weaknesses.zh: 缺乏足以单独对抗马其顿的军力，财政虽稳但有限，政治派系分裂明显，对外自主性受驻军和地区强权制约。
weaknesses.en: It lacked the military power to oppose Macedon alone, its finances were stable but limited, political factionalism was marked, and external autonomy was constrained by garrisons and regional great powers.

outlook.zh: 323年雅典处于表面稳定而潜藏激变的局面。亚历山大之死为其争取更大自治提供了短暂机会，但若转入公开反马其顿战争，凭自身资源难以长期取胜。
outlook.en: In 323 BCE Athens stood in a condition of surface stability masking potential upheaval. Alexander’s death offered a brief opening to seek greater autonomy, but if it moved into open war against Macedon, its own resources were unlikely to secure lasting victory.

description.zh: 在马其顿控制下仍保有传统城邦制度与重要文化地位。
description.en: Under Macedonian dominance but retaining its traditional polis institutions and major cultural standing.

governmentForm.zh: 由阿吉德王室名义统治、摄政大臣实际掌权的君主制，并以对希腊诸邦的霸权联盟为外部政治框架。
governmentForm.en: A monarchy ruled nominally by the Argead royal house but effectively directed by a regent, with hegemony over the Greek city-states exercised through a league structure.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以王室与贵族伙伴、地方地主、自由农民、城市居民、附庸希腊城邦公民、边地部族人口与奴隶构成；马其顿本土的乡村贵族和骑兵阶层具有特别重要的政治地位。
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of the royal house and noble companions, regional landholders, free peasants, urban inhabitants, subject Greek civic populations, frontier tribal peoples, and slaves; the rural Macedonian nobility and cavalry class held especially important political influence.

rulingClass.zh: 阿吉德王室、王廷贵族、伙伴骑兵家族、高级将领与受任命的地方总督
rulingClass.en: The Argead royal house, court nobility, Companion cavalry families, senior generals, and appointed regional governors

succession.zh: 原则上由阿吉德家族世袭，但323年前后因亚历山大大帝猝逝、合法继承人未稳固，实际依赖军队、贵族会议与摄政安排确认王位。
succession.en: In principle hereditary within the Argead family, but in 323 BCE succession was unsettled after Alexander's sudden death, and practical legitimacy depended on the army, noble assembly, and regency arrangements.

structure.zh: 政体核心为国王与王廷；在323年，安提帕特作为欧洲地区摄政掌握本土行政、军事与对希腊事务。中央机构并非高度官僚化，而是围绕宫廷、财政官、军务官、地方总督与希腊同盟监督体系运作。
structure.en: The state centered on the king and royal court; in 323 BCE Antipater, as regent in Europe, controlled homeland administration, military affairs, and Greek policy. The central apparatus was not fully bureaucratic but operated through the court, financial officers, military officials, provincial governors, and supervisory mechanisms over the Greek alliance.

function.zh: 处理诏令、使节往来、王室档案、敕令传达与摄政决策执行。
function.en: Handled decrees, diplomatic correspondence, royal records, transmission of orders, and execution of regental decisions.

function.zh: 管理王室土地收入、矿山收益、关税、附庸贡赋与军费调度。
function.en: Managed revenues from royal lands, mines, customs duties, subordinate tribute, and allocation of military funds.

function.zh: 征召军队、维护守备、发放军饷、登记兵员与组织补给。
function.en: Oversaw levies, garrisons, military pay, troop registration, and logistics.

function.zh: 监督科林斯同盟相关事务、处理希腊城邦纠纷、安置亲马其顿驻军与寡头政权。
function.en: Supervised matters related to the Corinthian League, dealt with disputes among Greek poleis, and maintained pro-Macedonian garrisons and oligarchic regimes.

function.zh: 管理上马其顿、下马其顿及边境地区，协调色雷斯、帖撒利与希腊关键据点的驻防和行政联系。
function.en: Administered Upper and Lower Macedonia and frontier regions, coordinating garrisons and administrative links in Thrace, Thessaly, and key Greek strongpoints.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理依赖王室任命官员、地方贵族、城市 magistrates 以及附庸地区的既有制度。马其顿本土分上马其顿与下马其顿诸区管理，希腊地区则通过驻军、亲马其顿政权和同盟框架维持控制。
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied on royal appointees, regional nobles, city magistrates, and preexisting institutions in subordinate territories. The Macedonian homeland was managed through the districts of Upper and Lower Macedonia, while Greece was controlled through garrisons, pro-Macedonian regimes, and the league framework.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系为王命、习惯法与希腊城邦地方法并存。马其顿本土较多依赖王权裁断、贵族仲裁与军法；希腊诸邦一般保留本地法庭，但重大安全与叛乱案件受马其顿军政干预。
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined royal decree, customary law, and local Greek civic law. In the Macedonian homeland it relied heavily on royal judgment, aristocratic arbitration, and military law; Greek cities generally retained local courts, though major security and rebellion cases faced Macedonian intervention.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政来源包括王室直辖土地收入、矿山收益、牧业与林产、贸易关税、港口税、附庸贡赋及战时特别征发。马其顿本土传统上直接税较轻，但王室经济控制力强；希腊附庸地区则更多承担贡赋与驻军负担。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue came from royal domain income, mines, pastoral and timber products, trade customs, port dues, subordinate tribute, and extraordinary wartime levies. Direct taxation in the Macedonian homeland was traditionally relatively light, but the crown exercised strong economic control; subordinate Greek regions more often bore tribute and garrison burdens.

religion.zh: 宗教以希腊多神信仰为主，崇拜宙斯、赫拉克勒斯、雅典娜、阿耳忒弥斯及地方神祇，并强调阿吉德王室与赫拉克勒斯的祖先联系。王家祭祀、英雄崇拜和军事祭典在政治生活中极其重要。
religion.en: Religion was predominantly Greek polytheism, centered on Zeus, Heracles, Athena, Artemis, and local deities, with strong emphasis on the Argead dynasty's ancestral connection to Heracles. Royal cult, hero worship, and military sacrifice were highly important in political life.

philosophy.zh: 思想文化受古典希腊传统深刻影响，亚里士多德学派、修辞教育、城邦政治理论与实用军事伦理并存。王廷比雅典更重视统治与军事效能，但仍尊重希腊知识文化的 prestige。
philosophy.en: Intellectual life was deeply shaped by classical Greek traditions, including Aristotelian thought, rhetorical education, polis political theory, and practical military ethics. The court placed greater emphasis than Athens on rule and military effectiveness, yet still respected the prestige of Greek intellectual culture.

writingSystem.zh: 使用希腊字母书写，行政与外交文本主要采用阿提卡—通用希腊文体，地方铭文亦见多种希腊方言形式。
writingSystem.en: The Greek alphabet was used for writing, and administrative and diplomatic texts mainly employed Attic and emerging koine Greek forms, while local inscriptions also reflected various Greek dialect usages.

culturalAchievements.zh: 马其顿在王家陵墓建筑、金属工艺、军事组织、宫廷艺术和希腊文化传播方面成就突出。到323年，马其顿已将希腊语文化与王权军事制度扩展到东地中海与西亚广大地区。
culturalAchievements.en: Macedon excelled in royal tomb architecture, metalwork, military organization, court art, and the spread of Greek culture. By 323 BCE it had extended Greek-language culture and Macedonian royal-military institutions across much of the eastern Mediterranean and western Asia.

languageFamily.zh: 印欧语系希腊语支
languageFamily.en: Indo-European, Hellenic branch

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以农业、牧业、林木、矿产和战争驱动的再分配体系为基础。人均产出不如最富庶的商业城邦，但王室可集中调动资源，尤其是木材、马匹、矿山和对希腊的政治控制所产生的收益。
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on agriculture, pastoralism, timber, minerals, and a war-driven redistributive system. Output per capita was below the richest commercial city-states, but the crown could concentrate resources effectively, especially from timber, horses, mines, and revenues generated by political dominance over Greece.

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物种植、葡萄酒与橄榄生产、畜牧养马、木材采伐、造船材料供应、金银矿开采、兵器制造、陶器和金属器加工
mainIndustries.en: Grain cultivation, wine and olive production, livestock and horse breeding, timber extraction, supply of shipbuilding materials, gold and silver mining, arms manufacture, and pottery and metalworking

tradeGoods.zh: 木材、沥青与树脂、马匹、奴隶、金银、谷物、葡萄酒、金属器、陶器、军需物资
tradeGoods.en: Timber, pitch and resin, horses, slaves, gold and silver, grain, wine, metal goods, pottery, and military supplies

name.zh: 德拉克马与他连得体系

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富差异很大。贵族家族拥有庄园、牲畜、战马和随从；普通自由农户多持有小块土地与有限牲畜；城市工匠和商人现金流较强但政治地位通常不及乡村贵族。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth varied greatly. Noble families held estates, herds, warhorses, and retainers; ordinary free peasants usually possessed small plots and limited livestock; urban craftsmen and traders had stronger cash income but generally less political status than rural nobles.

economicSystem.zh: 经济体系为以王室控制资源、地方自给农业与区域市场交换相结合的前现代混合经济。国家并不全面直接经营全部生产，但在矿山、林木、军需、港口和战利品再分配方面影响深远。
economicSystem.en: The economy was a premodern mixed system combining royal control of key resources, local subsistence agriculture, and regional market exchange. The state did not directly run all production, but it exerted major influence over mines, timber, military supply, ports, and redistribution of spoils.

source.zh: 关税、港口税与贸易收费
source.en: Customs, port dues, and trade charges

source.zh: 希腊附庸贡赋与驻军分摊
source.en: Greek subordinate tribute and garrison contributions

source.zh: 罚没、战利品与杂项收入

category.zh: 舰船、港口与补给线维护
category.en: Fleet, ports, and supply line maintenance

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库在323年保持稳健，但其规模远不能与亚历山大远征在亚洲掌握的巨额财宝相比。本土财政重在维持驻军、控制希腊与应对继承危机可能引发的战争。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury in 323 BCE was sound, though far smaller than the immense treasures under Alexander's control in Asia. Homeland finances were oriented toward maintaining garrisons, controlling Greece, and preparing for wars that might arise from the succession crisis.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏保守务实，优先确保军饷、边防和希腊驻军，避免对马其顿自由农民施加过重常规税负，同时通过附庸贡赋、王室产业和非常征发弥补支出。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was conservative and pragmatic, prioritizing military pay, frontier defense, and Greek garrisons while avoiding excessive routine taxation of Macedonian free peasants, relying instead on subordinate tribute, royal resources, and extraordinary levies.

description.zh: 装备长矛萨里沙的重装步兵，是马其顿野战军核心，擅长正面推进与阵地压制。
description.en: Heavy infantry armed with the sarissa, forming the core of the Macedonian field army and excelling in frontal advance and positional pressure.

description.zh: 由贵族与精锐骑士组成，承担突击、追击和统帅护卫任务，是马其顿军事优势的重要部分。
description.en: Composed of nobles and elite horsemen, they served as shock cavalry, pursuit forces, and command escorts, and were a key part of Macedonian military superiority.

description.zh: 包括佩尔塔斯特、阿格里安人式轻装兵及山地辅助部队，用于侦察、掩护、伏击和复杂地形作战。
description.en: Included peltasts, Agrianian-style light troops, and mountain auxiliaries for scouting, screening, ambush, and rough-terrain warfare.

description.zh: 由希腊附庸城邦兵员、雇佣兵和据点守军组成，负责希腊各要地与交通线控制。
description.en: Made up of troops from subordinate Greek states, mercenaries, and fortress garrisons, responsible for controlling strategic points and communications in Greece.

description.zh: 操作攻城器械、修筑营垒、架桥与道路，体现马其顿军队在技术性战争中的成熟度。
description.en: Operated siege engines, built camps, bridges, and roads, reflecting the maturity of Macedonian technical warfare.

commanderInChief.zh: 安提帕特
commanderInChief.en: Antipater

technology.zh: 军事技术处于当时地中海世界前列，结合萨里沙方阵、重骑兵协同、攻城器械、标准化营地建设和长距离补给经验。虽然主力精锐多随亚历山大远征在外，但本土仍保有成熟军制与训练传统。
technology.en: Military technology ranked among the most advanced in the Mediterranean world, combining the sarissa phalanx, heavy cavalry coordination, siege engines, standardized camp construction, and long-range logistical experience. Although many elite forces were overseas with Alexander, the homeland still retained mature institutions and training traditions.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自希腊城邦可能发动的反马其顿起义，尤其是雅典与埃托利亚势力；其次是北方色雷斯与伊利里亚边境动荡，以及亚历山大死后帝国内部继承与将领纷争可能波及欧洲本土。
threats.en: The main threats came from possible anti-Macedonian uprisings among the Greek city-states, especially Athens and Aetolian forces; secondary dangers included unrest on the Thracian and Illyrian frontiers and the possibility that succession struggles among Alexander's commanders would spill into Europe.

populationDescription.zh: 总人口包括马其顿本土居民、上马其顿山地人口、受控制的希腊地区人口以及若干边地族群。人口分布以乡村为主，真正大型城市数量有限，但佩拉、塞萨洛尼基前身区域聚落、安菲波利斯与科林斯等战略城市极为重要。
populationDescription.en: The total population included inhabitants of the Macedonian homeland, mountain populations of Upper Macedonia, controlled Greek populations, and several frontier groups. Population distribution was predominantly rural, with relatively few truly large cities, though strategic centers such as Pella, settlements in the Thermaic region, Amphipolis, and Corinth were very important.

name.zh: 德米特里亚斯前身地区港市群

socialClasses.zh: 主要阶层包括王室与宫廷、贵族伙伴阶层、自由农民、城镇工匠与商人、希腊城邦公民、雇佣兵与依附人口、奴隶。政治与军事上最关键的是拥有土地和战马的贵族及可动员的自由农民。
socialClasses.en: Major strata included the royal house and court, the noble Companion class, free peasants, urban craftsmen and merchants, Greek civic populations, mercenaries and dependents, and slaves. Politically and militarily the most crucial groups were the landholding horse-owning nobility and the mobilizable free peasantry.

allies.zh: 名义盟友包括科林斯同盟中的多数希腊城邦、亲马其顿寡头政权、帖撒利贵族集团及若干色雷斯和边地附庸首领。
allies.en: Nominal allies included most member states of the Corinthian League, pro-Macedonian oligarchic regimes, Thessalian aristocratic groups, and certain Thracian and frontier subordinate chiefs.

enemies.zh: 潜在敌人为反马其顿的雅典派系、埃托利亚人、斯巴达残余影响力、部分色雷斯与伊利里亚部族，以及任何试图利用继承危机削弱马其顿控制的势力。
enemies.en: Potential enemies included anti-Macedonian factions in Athens, the Aetolians, residual Spartan influence, certain Thracian and Illyrian tribes, and any power seeking to exploit the succession crisis to weaken Macedonian control.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以维持欧洲本土稳定、确保希腊服从、保护通往赫勒斯滂与色雷斯的战略通道，并在亚历山大死后为阿吉德王朝合法性争取时间与空间为核心。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on maintaining stability in Europe, ensuring Greek obedience, securing strategic corridors to the Hellespont and Thrace, and buying time and room for the legitimacy of the Argead dynasty after Alexander's death.

era.zh: 希腊化时期早期
era.en: Early Hellenistic Period

keyInnovations.zh: 萨里沙长矛方阵、联合兵种战法、成熟攻城工程、改进骑兵冲击战术、王室道路与补给组织、跨区域行政与铸币体系整合
keyInnovations.en: Sarissa phalanx warfare, combined-arms tactics, mature siege engineering, improved cavalry shock tactics, royal road and supply organization, and integrated supra-regional administration and coinage systems

strengths.zh: 拥有当时最成熟的陆军传统之一、强势王权军事体制、对希腊的战略控制、可观的王室资源与高政治威望。即使亚历山大在外去世，马其顿本土仍是整个帝国合法性与兵制传统的核心。
strengths.en: It possessed one of the most mature land warfare traditions of the age, a powerful royal-military system, strategic control over Greece, substantial royal resources, and immense political prestige. Even after Alexander's death abroad, the Macedonian homeland remained the core of imperial legitimacy and military tradition.

weaknesses.zh: 统治高度依赖少数将领与贵族忠诚，继承秩序脆弱；本土人口和财政基础有限，难以长期同时压制希腊、边疆与帝国继承战争；许多最精锐部队不在欧洲本土。
weaknesses.en: Rule depended heavily on the loyalty of a narrow circle of generals and nobles, and the succession order was fragile; the homeland's demographic and fiscal base was limited for sustaining simultaneous pressure in Greece, on the frontiers, and in imperial succession wars; many of the finest troops were not in Europe.

outlook.zh: 短期内局势大体稳定，安提帕特有能力维持秩序并镇压希腊叛乱；但随着亚历山大帝国分裂，马其顿将 increasingly 卷入继业者战争，稳定性将在未来数年迅速下降。
outlook.en: In the short term the situation was broadly stable, and Antipater had the capacity to maintain order and suppress Greek revolt; but as Alexander's empire fragmented, Macedon would become increasingly drawn into the Wars of the Successors, and its stability would decline rapidly in the coming years.

description.zh: 马其顿本土与希腊霸权核心，由安提帕特代表王室控制。
description.en: The Macedonian homeland and core of hegemony over Greece, controlled by Antipater on behalf of the royal house.

name.zh: 色雷斯诸部与奥德里西亚残余

ruler.zh: 塞乌特斯三世及各地部族首领

dynasty.zh: 奥德里西亚王族诸支系及其他地方王统
dynasty.en: Odrysian royal lines and other local dynasties

governmentForm.zh: 部族联盟式君主统治，夹杂地方酋邦与弱化的王权网络
governmentForm.en: Tribal confederational monarchy with local chiefdoms and a weakened royal network

socialStructure.zh: 社会以部族为核心，上层为王族、贵族骑士与武士集团，其下为自由农牧民、工匠、依附民与奴隶；各部族之间认同强于统一国家认同。
socialStructure.en: Society was organized primarily around tribes, with royal houses, nobles, mounted warriors, and martial elites at the top, followed by free farmers and pastoralists, artisans, dependents, and slaves; tribal identity remained stronger than any unified state identity.

rulingClass.zh: 奥德里西亚王室、地方王公、贵族战士家族与部族长老
rulingClass.en: Odrysian royalty, local kings, noble warrior families, and tribal elders

succession.zh: 以世袭为主，但常受贵族拥立、部族会议、武力竞争与外部马其顿干预影响
succession.en: Succession was mainly hereditary, but was often shaped by noble backing, tribal assemblies, armed contest, and Macedonian interference

structure.zh: 323年前后色雷斯并非统一国家，而是由塞乌特斯三世控制的奥德里西亚残余王权、若干半独立部族、山地酋邦和河谷据点共同构成。王权对核心谷地和王都周边控制较强，对边远部落主要依靠盟约、婚姻、贡赋和军事威慑维系。
structure.en: In 323 BCE Thrace was not a unified state, but a patchwork of the remaining Odrysian kingship under Seuthes III, semi-independent tribes, upland chiefdoms, and river-valley strongholds. Royal authority was stronger in core valleys and around the royal center, while peripheral tribes were held mainly through alliance, marriage, tribute, and military pressure.

function.zh: 管理王室居所、宴飨、使者接待、赏赐与王室财物
function.en: Managed royal residences, feasting, reception of envoys, patronage, and royal property

function.zh: 征收谷物、牲畜、金属与战利品，管理王室仓储与再分配
function.en: Collected grain, livestock, metals, and spoils, and managed royal storage and redistribution

function.zh: 组织王家护卫、召集部族兵员、维持要道与据点防务
function.en: Organized the royal guard, summoned tribal levies, and maintained key routes and strongholds

function.zh: 处理与马其顿将领、希腊城邦、邻近部族及雇佣关系的谈判
function.en: Handled negotiation with Macedonian commanders, Greek cities, neighboring tribes, and mercenary arrangements

name.zh: 地方酋长联络与司法调停
name.en: Local Chieftain Liaison and Judicial Mediation

function.zh: 传达王令、协调部族争端、监督忠诚与贡纳
function.en: Communicated royal orders, mediated inter-tribal disputes, and supervised loyalty and tribute

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理主要由部族首领、村社长老和王室任命或承认的代理人执行。河谷、交通节点与王室核心区可能有较稳定的驻守与征收体系，而广大山地与边缘地带仍以习惯统治为主。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was carried out mainly by tribal chiefs, village elders, and agents appointed or recognized by the king. River valleys, transport nodes, and the royal core probably had more stable garrisons and collection systems, while much of the uplands and frontier zones remained governed by customary authority.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以部族习惯法、贵族仲裁、血亲赔偿和王室裁断为基础。对叛乱、拒贡和背盟多以军事惩戒；对财产、婚姻和杀伤案件常通过赔偿、誓言与长老调停解决。
legalSystem.en: Law rested on tribal custom, noble arbitration, kin-based compensation, and royal judgment. Rebellion, refusal of tribute, and breach of alliance were often met by force; property, marriage, and injury cases were commonly settled through compensation, oaths, and elder mediation.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收更接近贡赋制度而非统一税制，主要以谷物、牲畜、金银器、木材、矿产、劳役和战时兵役形式征收；希腊化影响较强的区域则可能辅以市场税和道路关征。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation more closely resembled tribute than a uniform tax system, collected mainly in grain, livestock, precious metal objects, timber, minerals, labor service, and wartime military duty; in more Hellenized areas this could be supplemented by market dues and route tolls.

religion.zh: 色雷斯宗教以多神崇拜、英雄祭祀、祖先传统和山林圣所为核心，并吸收希腊宗教要素。骑士英雄、狩猎与酒神性质的神祇崇拜在贵族文化中尤为重要，亦可见与狄俄尼索斯、阿耳忒弥斯和阿波罗相关的跨文化融合。
religion.en: Thracian religion centered on polytheism, hero cult, ancestral tradition, and sanctuaries in mountains and groves, while also absorbing Greek elements. Mounted hero cults and deities associated with hunting and ecstatic rites were especially important in aristocratic culture, with cross-cultural connections to Dionysus, Artemis, and Apollo.

philosophy.zh: 色雷斯本土并无如希腊城邦式的系统哲学学派，但贵族圈层受到希腊思想、修辞和王权观念影响。社会伦理重视勇武、忠诚、宴饮、血亲纽带与战功名望。
philosophy.en: Native Thrace did not produce systematic philosophical schools like the Greek poleis, but aristocratic circles were influenced by Greek thought, rhetoric, and concepts of kingship. Social values emphasized bravery, loyalty, feasting, kinship bonds, and fame won in war.

writingSystem.zh: 本土口述传统占主导，正式书写在宫廷、外交和贸易环境中多借用希腊字母与希腊语；铭文与行政记录的覆盖面有限。
writingSystem.en: Oral tradition predominated natively, while formal writing in court, diplomatic, and trade contexts generally employed the Greek alphabet and Greek language; inscriptions and administrative record-keeping remained limited in scope.

culturalAchievements.zh: 色雷斯文化以精美金银器、王侯墓葬、骑士与狩猎图像、武器装饰、山地堡寨和受希腊影响的新兴城市营建著称。塞乌托波利斯代表了王权试图结合希腊化城市规划与本土王室中心的努力。
culturalAchievements.en: Thracian culture was noted for fine gold and silver work, elite tombs, mounted and hunting imagery, decorated weapons, hill fortifications, and emerging urban foundations shaped by Greek influence. Seuthopolis exemplified a royal attempt to combine Hellenistic urban planning with a native dynastic center.

languageFamily.zh: 色雷斯语，通常归为印欧语系古巴尔干语言；上层交流中广泛接触希腊语
languageFamily.en: Thracian, usually classified as an Indo-European Paleo-Balkan language; Greek was widely encountered in elite communication

gdpDescription.zh: 色雷斯经济以自给农业和牧业为基础，辅以采矿、林木、金属加工、掠夺性战争收益和与希腊世界的区域贸易。总体财富分布极不均衡，贵族积累大量贵金属器物与牲畜，而普通农户生活接近维持水平。
gdpDescription.en: The Thracian economy rested on subsistence farming and pastoralism, supplemented by mining, timber extraction, metalworking, gains from raiding warfare, and regional trade with the Greek world. Wealth was distributed very unevenly, with nobles accumulating precious metal vessels and herds while ordinary households lived close to subsistence.

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物种植、畜牧、葡萄酒生产、木材采伐、采矿、冶金、陶器、兵器与贵金属工艺
mainIndustries.en: Grain farming, animal husbandry, wine production, timber cutting, mining, metallurgy, pottery, weapon making, and precious metal craftsmanship

tradeGoods.zh: 谷物、葡萄酒、蜂蜜、蜡、皮革、牲畜、木材、金银器、铁器、奴隶，以及进口的陶器、油、奢侈品和希腊制成品
tradeGoods.en: Grain, wine, honey, wax, hides, livestock, timber, gold and silver vessels, iron goods, slaves, and imported pottery, oil, luxury goods, and Greek manufactures

name.zh: 希腊化钱币与金属衡量并用
name.en: Mixed Hellenistic Coinage and Metal Weight

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要体现为土地使用权、牲畜、粮仓、工具与亲族劳力；贵族财富则集中于马匹、武器、金银饮器、奴隶和对附庸村落的支配权。
householdWealth.en: Typical household wealth consisted mainly of use-rights in land, livestock, grain stores, tools, and kin labor; aristocratic wealth was concentrated in horses, weapons, gold and silver drinking vessels, slaves, and control over dependent settlements.

economicSystem.zh: 以部族再分配、贵族庄园控制、贡赋与区域市场交换并存的混合经济
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy combining tribal redistribution, aristocratic estate control, tribute, and regional market exchange

source.en: Tribal Tribute and Payment in Kind

source.zh: 战利品、赎金与掠夺收益

category.en: Gifts to Nobles and Tribal Allies

treasuryDescription.zh: 王室财政并非集中化国库，而是由王都储藏、贵金属器物、牲畜、谷物仓储和可随时向附庸征调的资源共同构成。战争状态下现金性储备偏薄，对贵族支持依赖很强。
treasuryDescription.en: Royal finance was not a fully centralized treasury, but a combination of palace stores, precious metal vessels, livestock, grain reserves, and resources that could be requisitioned from dependents. Under conditions of conflict, liquid reserves were relatively thin and heavily dependent on aristocratic support.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策重在维持战时动员和贵族忠诚，宁可通过掠夺、临时贡赋和馈赠网络弥补短缺，也难以建立稳定的普遍征税体系。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on sustaining wartime mobilization and aristocratic loyalty; shortfalls were more often met through raiding, extraordinary tribute, and gift networks than through any stable universal tax regime.

description.zh: 由王室随从和贵族骑士组成，是威望最高的兵种，适合突袭、追击和侧翼行动。
description.en: Formed by royal retainers and noble horsemen, this was the most prestigious arm and suited to raids, pursuit, and flanking action.

description.zh: 由自由民与附庸征召，装备不一，以长矛、标枪、盾牌和短刃为主，是战时主力。
description.en: Levied from freemen and dependents, unevenly equipped with spears, javelins, shields, and short blades, and formed the main wartime force.

description.zh: 善于山地与林地作战，使用标枪、弓箭和轻盾，适合伏击与骚扰。
description.en: Adept in mountain and woodland fighting, using javelins, bows, and light shields, and well suited to ambush and harassment.

description.zh: 负责王都、堡寨、道路节点与首领居所防卫，兼任使者护送与警戒任务。
description.en: Responsible for defense of the royal center, forts, route nodes, and elite residences, and also used for escort and security duties.

commanderInChief.zh: 塞乌特斯三世及各部族统兵首领
commanderInChief.en: Seuthes III and tribal war leaders

technology.zh: 军事技术属晚期铁器时代至早期希腊化水准，装备以铁制长矛、短剑、标枪、盾牌和部分头盔甲具为主。贵族武装较精良，亦受马其顿与希腊战法影响，但整体缺乏统一操练与攻城能力。
technology.en: Military technology stood at a Late Iron Age to early Hellenistic level, centered on iron spears, short swords, javelins, shields, and some helmets and armor. Aristocratic warriors were better equipped and influenced by Macedonian and Greek methods, but the overall force lacked uniform drill and strong siege capability.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自马其顿继业者势力干预、邻近色雷斯部族竞争、黑海与内陆交通线争夺，以及内部地方首领离心与叛服不定。
threats.en: Major threats came from intervention by Macedonian successor forces, rivalry among neighboring Thracian tribes, competition over Black Sea and inland routes, and the unstable loyalty or separatism of local chiefs.

populationDescription.zh: 总人口约六十万，分布在今保加利亚中东部、色雷斯平原、山地边缘和河谷聚落之中。人口多为农村村社与山地据点居民，城镇人口较少且集中于受希腊化影响的中心。
populationDescription.en: The total population was about 600,000, spread across central and eastern areas of modern Bulgaria, the Thracian plain, upland margins, and river-valley settlements. Most people lived in rural villages and hill communities, while urban population was limited and concentrated in Hellenized centers.

name.zh: 梅森布里亚周边内陆联系区

socialClasses.zh: 社会阶层包括王族与地方王公、贵族武士、自由部族民、农牧民、工匠商贩、依附民与奴隶。贵族通过宴饮、赠礼、骑兵战力和墓葬展示地位；普通民众承担农业、牧业、劳役和战时征召。
socialClasses.en: Social strata included royalty and local kings, noble warriors, free tribesmen, farmers and pastoralists, artisans and traders, dependents, and slaves. Nobles displayed status through feasting, gift exchange, cavalry power, and burial wealth, while common people bore the burden of agriculture, herding, labor service, and wartime levy.

allies.zh: 缺乏稳定统一盟友体系，主要依靠临时部族联盟、与部分希腊沿海城邦的交易关系，以及针对马其顿内乱时机形成的短期合作
allies.en: There was no stable unified alliance system; diplomacy relied mainly on temporary tribal coalitions, trading ties with some Greek coastal cities, and short-lived cooperation formed around moments of Macedonian instability

enemies.zh: 主要敌对者为试图控制色雷斯的马其顿势力、竞争性的邻近色雷斯诸王与部族，以及不时发生冲突的边境群体
enemies.en: The principal enemies were Macedonian forces seeking to control Thrace, rival neighboring Thracian kings and tribes, and border groups with whom conflict periodically erupted

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以生存和自主为核心，在323年前后亚历山大死后权力真空中，色雷斯诸首领倾向于利用继业者内斗、维护地方独立、争取贸易通道并阻止单一外来强权全面支配。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on survival and autonomy. In the power vacuum after Alexander's death in 323 BCE, Thracian leaders sought to exploit the struggles of the Successors, preserve local independence, secure trade routes, and prevent any single outside power from fully dominating the region.

era.zh: 希腊化时代早期
era.en: Early Hellenistic Period

keyInnovations.zh: 铁器兵器与工具普及、山地堡寨体系、贵金属细工、葡萄酒生产技术，以及在王都和贸易节点中吸收的希腊化城市规划、货币使用和建筑形式
keyInnovations.en: Key developments included widespread iron tools and weapons, hill-fort systems, refined precious-metal craftsmanship, wine production techniques, and the adoption in royal centers and trade nodes of Hellenistic urban planning, coin use, and architectural forms

strengths.zh: 色雷斯诸部拥有熟悉地形的战士、强悍骑兵传统、丰富木材与矿产资源、连接爱琴海与黑海内陆通道的地理优势，以及在外部帝国衰弱时迅速重组联盟的能力。
strengths.en: The Thracian tribes possessed terrain-savvy fighters, a strong cavalry tradition, rich timber and mineral resources, geographic control over routes linking the Aegean and Black Sea interiors, and the ability to rapidly re-form alliances when outside empires weakened.

weaknesses.zh: 其根本弱点在于政治分裂、王权脆弱、行政整合不足、财政依赖贡赋与掠夺、城市化有限、步兵训练不统一，并容易被马其顿或其他强邻以分化手段逐步控制。
weaknesses.en: Their fundamental weaknesses lay in political fragmentation, fragile kingship, limited administrative integration, finances dependent on tribute and raiding, low urbanization, uneven infantry training, and vulnerability to divide-and-rule tactics by Macedon or other strong neighbors.

outlook.zh: 323年前后色雷斯局势将持续动荡。塞乌特斯三世及其他首领虽有机会借马其顿继业战争扩大自主空间，但若无法建立更稳固的部族联盟与财政军事核心，地区仍将长期处于分裂、反复臣服与局部复兴并存的状态。
outlook.en: Around 323 BCE Thrace was likely to remain unstable. Seuthes III and other leaders had an opportunity to expand autonomy amid the Wars of the Successors, but without a firmer tribal coalition and a stronger fiscal-military core, the region would continue to experience fragmentation, repeated subordination, and only partial revival.

description.zh: 巴尔干东北部仍由多支色雷斯部族与地方王权分据。
description.en: Northeastern Balkans remained divided among multiple Thracian tribes and local kings.

ruler.zh: 腓力三世·阿里达伊俄斯（名义上；即将被拥立）与亚历山大四世（未出生）
ruler.en: Philip III Arrhidaeus (nominal; soon to be proclaimed) and Alexander IV (unborn)

governmentForm.zh: 马其顿君主制与亚历山大征服地总督体系相结合的个人帝国；323年时中央名义统一、实际权力由摄政者与诸将分掌
governmentForm.en: A personal empire combining Macedonian kingship with Alexander's satrapal system; in 323 BCE it remained nominally united, but real power was increasingly shared among regents and senior generals

socialStructure.zh: 社会由马其顿王室与伙伴贵族、希腊城邦精英、各地总督与军政官、祭司集团、城市居民、乡村农民、附庸社群与奴隶构成；帝国内部地区差异极大
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of the Macedonian royal house and Companion nobility, Greek civic elites, regional satraps and military administrators, priestly groups, urban populations, rural cultivators, subject communities, and slaves; conditions varied widely across the empire

rulingClass.zh: 马其顿王族、近身伙伴贵族、禁卫军统领、高级将领与保留职位的部分波斯和巴比伦地方贵族
rulingClass.en: The Macedonian royal family, Companion aristocracy, guards commanders, senior generals, and selected Persian and Babylonian local nobles retained in office

succession.zh: 原则上由阿吉德王朝世袭，但323年因亚历山大猝死、合法继承人未稳固而陷入摄政与军队拥立并行的危机
succession.en: In principle hereditary within the Argead dynasty, but in 323 BCE succession entered crisis after Alexander's sudden death, with regency and army acclamation operating together

structure.zh: 中央以国王及宫廷为核心，辅以摄政者、王家书记、财务官和近臣会议；地方沿用波斯式总督区，由总督、财政官与驻军将领相互牵制
structure.en: The center revolved around the king and court, supported by a regent, royal secretaries, treasurers, and councils of companions; provincially it retained Persian-style satrapies, with satraps, financial officers, and military commanders balancing one another

function.zh: 管理王室起居、诏令发布、宫廷礼仪、使节接待与近卫安排
function.en: Managed royal residence, promulgation of orders, court ceremony, reception of envoys, and arrangements for guards

function.zh: 起草命令、记录军政文书、保存征服地区档案并协调多语行政沟通
function.en: Drafted orders, recorded military and administrative documents, preserved archives from conquered regions, and coordinated multilingual administration

function.zh: 征收贡赋、管理金银库藏、核算军费与地方解送
function.en: Collected tribute, managed gold and silver reserves, and accounted for military costs and provincial remittances

name.zh: 总督监察与地方事务署
name.en: Satrapal Oversight and Provincial Affairs Office

function.zh: 监督各总督区、处理地方纠纷、任免部分官员并协调王命执行
function.en: Oversaw satrapies, handled provincial disputes, appointed or removed some officials, and coordinated implementation of royal commands

function.zh: 调拨军队、统筹粮秣、道路舟运、攻城器械与退伍安置
function.en: Allocated troops, organized provisions, roads and river transport, siege equipment, and veteran settlement

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政主要依总督区运行。许多旧阿契美尼德行政边界仍被保留，总督通常负责治安、征兵与政治控制，财政官独立掌税，驻军将领另掌兵权；在希腊城邦、腓尼基港市、埃及与巴比伦等地，地方传统机构仍广泛存在
localAdmin.en: Local administration functioned mainly through satrapies. Many former Achaemenid provincial boundaries were preserved; satraps usually handled order, recruitment, and political control, while financial officers separately managed taxation and military commanders held troop authority. In Greek poleis, Phoenician ports, Egypt, and Babylon, traditional local institutions remained widely active

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系高度多元：马其顿王命与军法在帝国上层有效，希腊城邦保留本地法，巴比伦、埃及、犹地亚与伊朗地区继续依习惯法、神庙法或旧帝国文书传统审理案件
legalSystem.en: The legal system was highly pluralistic: Macedonian royal orders and military law operated at the imperial upper level, Greek cities retained civic law, and Babylonian, Egyptian, Judaean, and Iranian regions continued to use customary law, temple law, or inherited imperial documentary traditions

taxationSystem.zh: 税制以地方贡赋、土地税、灌溉区农业征收、港口关税、商税、矿产收益与王室直辖地产收入为主；征收方式因地区而异，有的以银缴纳，有的以谷物、牲畜或实物折纳
taxationSystem.en: Taxation relied on provincial tribute, land tax, levies on irrigated agriculture, port customs, trade duties, mining proceeds, and income from royal estates; collection varied by region, with some payments made in silver and others in grain, livestock, or kind

religion.zh: 宗教高度多元。马其顿人与希腊人奉祀宙斯、雅典娜、阿波罗等神祇，同时亚历山大王权崇拜正在形成；帝国内部还并存埃及神庙祭祀、巴比伦传统宗教、波斯与伊朗祭仪、犹太一神信仰及地方神灵崇拜
religion.en: Religion was highly diverse. Macedonians and Greeks worshipped deities such as Zeus, Athena, and Apollo, while a royal cult around Alexander was beginning to form; alongside this stood Egyptian temple religion, Babylonian traditional worship, Persian and Iranian rites, Jewish monotheism, and local cults across the empire

philosophy.zh: 思想核心仍属古典希腊传统，包括柏拉图学园、亚里士多德学派与犬儒派影响；但在帝国扩张后，政治实践与地理知识的扩大正在推动更世界化的希腊思想环境
philosophy.en: Its intellectual core remained within the Classical Greek tradition, including the Academy, the Aristotelian school, and Cynic influence; after imperial expansion, however, wider political practice and geographical knowledge were creating a more cosmopolitan Greek intellectual world

writingSystem.zh: 官方与军政沟通 increasingly 以希腊字母书写的通用希腊语和行政希腊文为主，但帝国各地仍广泛使用阿拉米字母文、楔形文字晚期文书、埃及文字及若干本地书写体系
writingSystem.en: Official and military-administrative communication increasingly relied on Greek alphabetic writing in common and administrative Greek, but Aramaic scripts, late cuneiform documents, Egyptian scripts, and several local writing systems remained in broad use across the empire

culturalAchievements.zh: 亚历山大远征带来了前所未有的地理整合、城市创建与文化传播；希腊戏剧、雕塑、宫廷艺术、军事工程、地理记录和跨区域精英交流均达到新阶段，巴比伦、苏萨、孟斐斯等旧文明中心继续提供学术与宗教传统
culturalAchievements.en: Alexander's conquests produced unprecedented geographic integration, city foundations, and cultural transmission; Greek drama, sculpture, court art, military engineering, geographical recording, and transregional elite exchange all entered a new phase, while older centers such as Babylon, Susa, and Memphis continued to contribute scholarly and religious traditions

languageFamily.zh: 统治核心语言属印欧语系希腊语支与古马其顿语环境，行政上广泛接触闪米特语族阿拉米语、埃及语、伊朗语族诸语言及多种安纳托利亚和中亚语言
languageFamily.en: The ruling core operated in the Indo-European Greek branch and a Macedonian linguistic environment, while administration broadly interacted with Semitic Aramaic, Egyptian, Iranian languages, and multiple Anatolian and Central Asian tongues

gdpDescription.zh: 作为横跨巴尔干、安纳托利亚、埃及、美索不达米亚、伊朗高原并延伸至中亚和印度边境的征服帝国，其经济总量极大，但地区发展极不均衡。富庶核心包括尼罗河流域、两河灌溉农业带、腓尼基港口、安纳托利亚农业区和部分伊朗王家道路节点。323年经济仍强，但政治不确定性已威胁财政与贸易稳定
gdpDescription.en: As a conquest empire stretching across the Balkans, Anatolia, Egypt, Mesopotamia, the Iranian plateau, and into Central Asia and the Indian frontier, it possessed enormous aggregate output, though regional development was highly uneven. Rich core zones included the Nile valley, Mesopotamian irrigation lands, Phoenician ports, Anatolian farming regions, and key nodes along the Iranian royal roads. In 323 BCE the economy remained strong, but political uncertainty was already threatening fiscal and commercial stability

mainIndustries.zh: 灌溉农业、谷物生产、畜牧、葡萄酒与橄榄油、金银矿与金属冶炼、手工业、纺织、造船、港口贸易、神庙经济与王室地产经营
mainIndustries.en: Irrigated agriculture, grain production, pastoralism, wine and olive oil, gold and silver mining and metalworking, crafts, textiles, shipbuilding, port trade, temple economies, and management of royal estates

tradeGoods.zh: 谷物、橄榄油、葡萄酒、木材、银、金、铜、铁、马匹、奴隶、腓尼基染料、埃及纸草、香料、乳香、宝石、纺织品与奢侈器物
tradeGoods.en: Grain, olive oil, wine, timber, silver, gold, copper, iron, horses, slaves, Phoenician dyes, Egyptian papyrus, spices, frankincense, gemstones, textiles, and luxury goods

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富差异极端显著。马其顿与希腊军功贵族、总督与商港富户可积累大量贵金属与地产；普通农户多仅维持基本口粮、牲畜与农具，小农和佃农易受赋税、征发与战事冲击
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was extremely unequal. Macedonian and Greek military aristocrats, satraps, and wealthy merchant households in port cities could accumulate substantial precious metal and land, while ordinary farming families often held only basic subsistence grain, livestock, and tools; smallholders and tenants were highly vulnerable to taxation, requisition, and war

economicSystem.zh: 以农业贡赋和王室财政为基础、辅以城市市场交换和长距离贸易的混合帝国经济；国家通过军队、总督区和铸币深度介入，但地方社群、神庙和私人商人仍是生产与流通主体
economicSystem.en: A mixed imperial economy based on agrarian tribute and royal finance, supplemented by urban market exchange and long-distance trade; the state intervened heavily through the army, satrapies, and coinage, but local communities, temples, and private merchants remained the main agents of production and circulation

source.zh: 王室地产、矿山与森林收入
source.en: Royal estates, mines, and timber revenues
Tribute and remittances from dependent regions

source.zh: 罚没、战利品与特别征收

category.zh: 总督区行政与官员维持
祭祀、赠赐与临时政治开支
category.en: Cult, gifts, and emergency political expenditures

treasuryDescription.zh: 帝国继承并集中掌握了亚历山大自苏萨、波斯波利斯、巴比伦和埃克巴坦那等地夺取的巨额金银储备，323年时仍拥有古代世界极为罕见的庞大库藏，但这些财富正成为诸将争夺军心与合法性的关键资源
treasuryDescription.en: The empire inherited and concentrated immense bullion reserves seized by Alexander from Susa, Persepolis, Babylon, Ecbatana, and other centers; in 323 BCE it still held an extraordinarily large treasury by ancient standards, but that wealth was becoming the key resource in the coming struggle among the generals for military loyalty and legitimacy

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策以维持军队忠诚、保障总督区解款和动用王库应急为核心。短期内凭借巨额储备尚可支撑高额军费，但若帝国分裂，统一税收与财政调拨能力将迅速下降
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy centered on maintaining army loyalty, securing provincial remittances, and drawing on the royal treasury in emergencies. In the short term vast reserves could sustain heavy military spending, but if the empire fragmented, unified taxation and fiscal transfer capacity would quickly deteriorate

description.zh: 以长枪方阵为核心的重装步兵，是亚历山大军制的骨干，但在323年分散驻防于广阔帝国各地
description.en: Heavy infantry organized around the sarissa phalanx, the backbone of Alexander's military system, though by 323 BCE dispersed in garrisons across the vast empire

name.zh: 伙伴骑兵与马其顿精锐骑兵

description.zh: 由贵族骑兵构成的突击力量，兼具战场冲锋与政治象征作用
description.en: Shock forces composed of aristocratic cavalry, important both tactically and as symbols of political status

name.zh: 希腊同盟步兵与雇佣军

description.zh: 包括希腊重装步兵、轻装兵和长期受雇部队，是驻军与远征军的重要补充
description.en: Included Greek hoplites, light troops, and long-service mercenaries, forming a major supplement to garrisons and expeditionary forces

name.zh: 东方骑兵与地方附属军
name.en: Eastern Cavalry and Local Auxiliary Forces

description.zh: 由波斯、米底、巴克特里亚、粟特、印度边地及其他地区部队组成，提供机动、弓骑与地方作战能力
description.en: Drawn from Persian, Median, Bactrian, Sogdian, Indian frontier, and other regional forces, providing mobility, horse archery, and local campaigning capacity

name.zh: 近卫军、攻城工兵与舰队水兵
name.en: Guards, Siege Engineers, and Naval Personnel

description.zh: 包括持盾卫队、禁卫、工兵、炮械操作人员、河运与地中海舰队人员，支撑帝国的机动与围城优势
description.en: Included hypaspists or shield-bearing guards, palace troops, engineers, artillery crews, river transport personnel, and Mediterranean naval forces supporting imperial mobility and siege superiority

commanderInChief.zh: 名义上由国王统帅，实际由摄政者佩尔狄卡斯与诸大将分掌
commanderInChief.en: Nominally commanded by the king, but in practice directed by the regent Perdiccas and the senior generals

technology.zh: 军事实力建立在马其顿长枪方阵、重骑兵协同、复合兵种战术、攻城塔与扭力炮械、道路行军组织及跨区域后勤能力之上，在当时欧亚西部属顶尖水平
technology.en: Military power rested on the Macedonian sarissa phalanx, heavy cavalry coordination, combined-arms tactics, siege towers and torsion artillery, organized road marching, and transregional logistics, placing it at the forefront of warfare in the western half of Eurasia

threats.zh: 最大威胁并非外敌，而是内部继承危机与将领分裂。边疆上则有希腊本土骚乱、色雷斯与巴尔干部族、安纳托利亚与伊朗地方反叛、中亚草原骑兵，以及印度西北边境不稳定局势
threats.en: The greatest threat was not an external foe but the internal succession crisis and rivalry among the generals. On the frontiers, dangers included unrest in mainland Greece, Thracian and Balkan tribes, revolts in Anatolia and Iran, steppe horse peoples in Central Asia, and instability on the northwestern Indian frontier

populationDescription.zh: 帝国人口约三千万，涵盖巴尔干希腊人与马其顿人、小亚细亚诸民、埃及人、腓尼基人与叙利亚人、美索不达米亚居民、伊朗诸族、中亚定居与游牧社群，以及印度西北部人口，是一个空前多民族帝国
populationDescription.en: The empire's population was about 30 million, including Greeks and Macedonians of the Balkans, peoples of Asia Minor, Egyptians, Phoenicians and Syrians, inhabitants of Mesopotamia, Iranian populations, settled and nomadic communities of Central Asia, and people of the northwestern Indian regions, making it an unprecedented multiethnic empire

name.zh: 塞琉西亚-底格里斯前身驻地群

socialClasses.zh: 上层为王室、马其顿伙伴贵族、总督、将领、城邦富户与祭司精英；中层包括商人、工匠、军官、神庙管理者与地方地主；下层为广大农民、佃户、劳工、被征发社群与奴隶。不同地区的身份、税负与法权差异显著
socialClasses.en: The upper strata included the royal house, Macedonian Companion nobility, satraps, generals, wealthy civic families, and priestly elites; the middle layers included merchants, artisans, officers, temple administrators, and local landholders; the lower strata consisted of peasants, tenants, laborers, requisitioned communities, and slaves. Status, tax burdens, and legal rights differed sharply by region

allies.zh: 严格意义上缺乏平等盟友；帝国内的希腊同盟城市、腓尼基港市、埃及与巴比伦地方精英多属被统合的臣属或合作集团，而非独立盟邦
allies.en: In a strict sense it lacked equal allies; the Greek allied cities, Phoenician ports, and local elites in Egypt and Babylon were integrated subjects or cooperating groups rather than independent allied states

enemies.zh: 表面上暂无与之匹敌的统一外敌，但希腊本土反马其顿势力、巴尔干部族、帝国内离心总督及潜在地方叛乱者都是现实对手；未来敌人将主要来自帝国分裂后的继业者集团
enemies.en: On the surface it faced no unified external rival of equal scale, but anti-Macedonian forces in Greece, Balkan tribes, centrifugal satraps within the empire, and potential local rebels were real adversaries; the main future enemies would arise from the successor factions after imperial fragmentation

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策处于真空与过渡状态。亚历山大时期的扩张性世界帝国战略在323年突然失去个人核心，短期目标转为维持名义统一、安抚军队、确认继承与重新分配总督区
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy was in a state of vacuum and transition. The expansionary world-imperial strategy of Alexander suddenly lost its personal center in 323 BCE, and immediate priorities shifted to preserving nominal unity, appeasing the army, settling succession, and redistributing satrapies

era.zh: 希腊化时期初期
era.en: Early Hellenistic Period

keyInnovations.zh: 马其顿复合兵种作战、长枪方阵体系、改良攻城器械与扭力炮、跨洲道路与补给组织、大规模标准化铸币、港口与航运管理、希腊与近东知识传统的更紧密交流
keyInnovations.en: Macedonian combined-arms warfare, the sarissa phalanx system, improved siege machines and torsion artillery, transcontinental road and supply organization, large-scale standardized coinage, port and shipping management, and closer exchange between Greek and Near Eastern knowledge traditions

strengths.zh: 疆域广阔、王库丰厚、军队经验丰富、总督体系可沿用旧波斯行政基础、贸易网络覆盖东地中海至两河和伊朗；在323年仍是已知世界最强大的单一政治体之一
strengths.en: It possessed immense territory, a rich treasury, battle-hardened armies, a satrapal framework built upon former Persian administration, and trade networks spanning the eastern Mediterranean to Mesopotamia and Iran; in 323 BCE it remained one of the most powerful single political entities in the known world

weaknesses.zh: 帝国严重依赖亚历山大个人权威，缺乏稳定的继承制度与统一认同；马其顿军官集团、地方总督和被征服地区利益冲突尖锐，疆域过大导致沟通与控制成本极高
weaknesses.en: The empire depended heavily on Alexander's personal authority and lacked a stable succession mechanism or unified identity; conflicts among Macedonian officers, provincial satraps, and conquered regions were acute, and the sheer scale of the empire made communication and control extraordinarily costly

outlook.zh: 前景极不稳定。若能在巴比伦会议后建立有效摄政并维系军队忠诚，帝国或可短期保持统一；但更可能的发展是总督与名将围绕王室名义展开长期内战，最终分裂为数个希腊化王国
outlook.en: The outlook was highly unstable. If an effective regency could be established after the Babylon settlement and army loyalty maintained, the empire might remain united for a short time; more likely, however, satraps and great commanders would wage prolonged wars in the royal name, ultimately dividing it into several Hellenistic kingdoms

description.zh: 亚历山大死后帝国名义仍统一，但诸将即将分裂统治。
description.en: After Alexander's death the empire remained nominally united, but his generals were on the verge of partitioning power.

ideology.zh: 帝国统一主义——维持亚历山大帝国的完整性，以阿吉德王室名义集中权力于摄政者手中
ideology.en: Imperial unitarism — maintaining the integrity of Alexander's empire with power centralized in the regent on behalf of the Argead royal house

relationship.zh: 名义上的帝国最高权力持有者，以腓力三世与尚未出生的亚历山大四世之名摄政，试图控制财政、军队调配与总督任命，但权威不断遭到各地将领挑战。
relationship.en: The nominal supreme authority of the empire, ruling as regent in the name of Philip III and the unborn Alexander IV. Perdiccas sought to control finances, army deployments, and satrapal appointments, but his authority was increasingly challenged by generals across the empire.

description.zh: 佩尔狄卡斯是亚历山大临终时的近臣和军中最高指挥官之一，在巴比伦分封会议后被推举为帝国摄政。他坚持帝国应在王室名义下保持统一，试图掌控大军和核心财库，压制各地离心倾向。然而其专权引发普遍不满，托勒密夺取亚历山大遗体运往埃及是对其权威的直接挑衅。佩尔狄卡斯的策略是在中央维持军事优势，逐步削弱不服从的总督，但帝国疆域的广阔与诸将的个人野心使统一日益不可维持。
description.en: Perdiccas was one of Alexander's closest companions and senior military commanders, chosen as regent of the empire after the Babylon partition conference. He insisted the empire should remain united under royal authority, seeking to control the main army and central treasuries while suppressing centrifugal tendencies. His concentration of power provoked widespread resentment, and Ptolemy's seizure of Alexander's body for transport to Egypt was a direct challenge to his authority. Perdiccas's strategy was to maintain military supremacy at the center and gradually weaken non-compliant satraps, but the empire's vast scale and the personal ambitions of rival generals made unity increasingly unsustainable.

role.zh: 佩尔狄卡斯最忠诚的支持者，希腊出身的卓越军事家
role.en: Perdiccas's most loyal supporter, a brilliant Greek-born military strategist

role.zh: 军事副手与家族核心成员
role.en: Military lieutenant and core family member

ideology.zh: 分权继业主义——主张帝国应由诸将领各自独立统治既有辖区，反对中央集权摄政
ideology.en: Decentralized succession — advocating that the empire's generals should independently rule their assigned territories, opposing centralized regency

headquarters.zh: 孟菲斯（即将迁往亚历山大里亚）

relationship.zh: 名义上接受巴比伦摄政安排下的埃及总督职位，实际上迅速建立独立统治基础，劫持亚历山大灵柩运往埃及以确立自身合法性，公然挑战佩尔狄卡斯的中央权威。
relationship.en: Nominally accepting his position as satrap of Egypt under the Babylon regency arrangement, Ptolemy quickly established an independent power base, hijacked Alexander's funeral cortège to Egypt to bolster his own legitimacy, and openly challenged the central authority of Perdiccas.

description.zh: 托勒密是亚历山大最受信任的将领之一，巴比伦分封中获得埃及总督一职。他迅速认识到帝国统一已不可能，选择以富庶且地理隔绝的埃及为根基建立独立王国。托勒密最大胆的举动是劫走亚历山大遗体并将其安葬于埃及，此举既是政治宣言也是宗教合法性工程。他善于利用尼罗河谷的巨大财富与天然屏障，将埃及打造为最稳固的继业者国家。托勒密的远见在于不追求重建帝国统一，而是建立一个可持续的区域性希腊化王国。
description.en: Ptolemy was one of Alexander's most trusted generals, awarded the satrapy of Egypt in the Babylon partition. He quickly recognized that imperial unity was impossible and chose to build an independent kingdom based on Egypt's wealth and geographic isolation. His boldest move was seizing Alexander's body and burying it in Egypt — an act that was both a political declaration and a project of religious legitimation. He skillfully leveraged the immense wealth of the Nile valley and its natural barriers to make Egypt the most secure of the successor states. Ptolemy's vision lay not in restoring imperial unity but in creating a sustainable regional Hellenistic kingdom.

role.zh: 亚历山大时期的埃及财务主管，后被托勒密处决以接管财权
role.en: Alexander-era financial overseer of Egypt, later executed by Ptolemy to seize fiscal control

role.zh: 为托勒密控制利比亚西部领地
role.en: Secured western Libyan territories for Ptolemy

ideology.zh: 帝国统一复辟主义——以军事力量为后盾，声称继承亚历山大全部遗产，反对帝国分裂为诸继业者的独立王国
ideology.en: Imperial restoration — backed by military force, claiming the entirety of Alexander's legacy and opposing the fragmentation of the empire into independent successor kingdoms

relationship.zh: 安提柯在巴比伦分封中获得弗里吉亚总督之职，控制小亚细亚核心地区。他是继业者中最具野心的将领，拒绝承认佩尔狄卡斯的摄政权威，并谋求将亚历山大帝国作为统一整体置于自己控制之下。安提柯的军事才能与政治雄心使他成为所有继业者中最危险的对手。
relationship.en: Antigonus received the satrapy of Phrygia in the Babylon partition, controlling a core region of Asia Minor. He was the most ambitious of the Successors, refusing to acknowledge Perdiccas's regency authority and scheming to bring the whole of Alexander's empire under his own control. His military talent and political ambition made him the most dangerous rival among all the Successors.

description.zh: 安提柯·蒙诺夫塔尔摩斯（「独眼者」）是亚历山大麾下最年长、最富经验的将领之一，在亚历山大东征期间被委任镇守小亚细亚。323年亚历山大猝死时，安提柯已在弗里吉亚建立了牢固的权力基础，拥有训练有素的军队和充裕的财政资源。与托勒密的区域保守策略不同，安提柯坚持帝国应保持统一——当然是在他自己的领导下。他精于大规模会战与围城作战，善于利用军事优势迫使对手就范。在随后的继业者战争中，安提柯将成为最接近统一帝国的人，他的领土一度横跨从小亚细亚到巴比伦的广大地区。然而他的野心最终引发其他所有继业者的联合对抗，导致其在公元前301年伊普苏斯战役中兵败身亡。安提柯的遗产由其子德米特里一世继承，后者建立了安提柯王朝，统治马其顿直至罗马征服。
description.en: Antigonus Monophthalmus ('the One-Eyed') was one of the oldest and most experienced of Alexander's generals, entrusted with securing Asia Minor during the eastern campaigns. When Alexander died suddenly in 323 BCE, Antigonus had already built a firm power base in Phrygia, commanding well-trained armies and ample fiscal resources. Unlike Ptolemy's conservative regional strategy, Antigonus insisted the empire should remain united — under his own leadership, of course. He excelled in large-scale pitched battles and siege warfare, skillfully using military superiority to force rivals into submission. In the ensuing Wars of the Successors, Antigonus came closest to reunifying the empire, with his territory at one point stretching from Asia Minor to Babylon. Yet his ambitions ultimately provoked a coalition of all other Successors against him, leading to his defeat and death at the Battle of Ipsus in 301 BCE. Antigonus's legacy passed to his son Demetrius I, who founded the Antigonid dynasty that ruled Macedon until the Roman conquest.

role.zh: 天才军事统帅与未来马其顿国王，以攻城术闻名于世
role.en: Brilliant military commander and future king of Macedon, famed for his siege craft

role.zh: 亚历山大远征舰队的指挥官，早期与安提柯合作
role.en: Commander of Alexander's expedition fleet, early collaborator with Antigonus

governmentForm.zh: 山地部族联盟，由若干部族共同体、贵族家族与战时选出的统帅组成的松散联邦
governmentForm.en: A highland tribal confederation, a loose federation of clan communities, aristocratic lineages, and wartime-elected commanders

socialStructure.zh: 社会以氏族、部族与村寨共同体为基础，上层为武士贵族与大牧场主，中层为自由农民和牧民，下层包括依附者、债务从属者与战俘奴隶
socialStructure.en: Society was based on clans, tribes, and village communities, with a warrior aristocracy and major pastoral landholders at the top, free farmers and herders in the middle, and dependents, debt-bound persons, and war captives at the bottom

rulingClass.zh: 部族贵族、武士首领与在部族议会中有影响力的家族长老
rulingClass.en: Tribal aristocrats, warrior leaders, and influential family elders in tribal assemblies

succession.zh: 并无统一世袭制度；地方首领多由贵族家族延续影响力，战时统帅通常由联盟协商、推举或临时任命
succession.en: There was no unified hereditary succession; local chiefs often came from established noble families, while wartime commanders were usually chosen, acclaimed, or temporarily appointed by confederal agreement

structure.zh: 萨姆奈特政治结构分散，由卡乌迪尼人、彭特里人、卡拉切尼人、希尔皮尼人等部族共同体构成；日常治理主要在地方村社与部族层面进行，面对外敌时才形成更广泛的联盟决策
structure.en: Samnite political organization was decentralized, composed of tribal communities such as the Caudini, Pentri, Caraceni, and Hirpini; routine governance operated mainly at the local village and tribal level, while broader confederal decision-making emerged chiefly in response to external threats

function.zh: 讨论战争、盟约、贡赋与部族间争端，确认主要军事与外交决定
function.en: Debated war, alliances, contributions, and inter-tribal disputes, and ratified major military and diplomatic decisions

function.zh: 由显贵家族领袖组成，负责协调联盟事务、调解争端并影响统帅人选
function.en: Composed of leaders of prominent families, it coordinated confederal affairs, mediated disputes, and influenced the choice of commanders

function.zh: 在战争期间组织征召、后勤、侦察与会盟部队调度
function.en: During wartime it organized levies, logistics, reconnaissance, and the movement of allied tribal forces

function.zh: 管理村社土地、习惯法裁决、祭祀活动与地方征发
function.en: Managed village lands, customary legal judgments, ritual observances, and local levies

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政依托山地聚落、寨堡和部族领地，没有高度文书化官僚体系；首领、家族长老和祭司共同处理征兵、土地、司法与祭仪事务
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied on hill settlements, fortified communities, and tribal territories, without a highly literate bureaucratic apparatus; chiefs, family elders, and priests together handled recruitment, land, justice, and ritual matters

legalSystem.zh: 以习惯法、部族传统与长老仲裁为核心；赔偿、血亲责任、誓约与公开调解十分重要，重大战时违令可能处以严厉惩罚
legalSystem.en: Law was centered on custom, tribal tradition, and arbitration by elders; compensation, kin responsibility, oaths, and public mediation were important, while serious wartime disobedience could incur severe penalties

taxationSystem.zh: 不存在罗马式规范税制；联盟财政主要来自战时分摊、首领贡纳、战利品分配、牧业与农业实物征发，以及对附属社区的临时贡献要求
taxationSystem.en: There was no Roman-style standardized tax system; confederal finance came chiefly from wartime assessments, contributions by chiefs, division of booty, pastoral and agricultural requisitions in kind, and ad hoc demands on subordinate communities

religion.zh: 萨姆奈特宗教属于意大利半岛奥斯坎传统多神信仰，崇拜山地、泉水、树林与战神相关神灵，也与邻近拉丁、萨贝利与希腊宗教发生交流
religion.en: Samnite religion belonged to the Oscan-Italic polytheistic tradition, venerating deities associated with mountains, springs, groves, and war, while also interacting with neighboring Latin, Sabellian, and Greek religious practices

philosophy.zh: 缺乏类似希腊城邦的系统哲学学派，其思想核心体现为祖训、勇武伦理、部族荣誉、誓约神圣性与土地共同体观念
philosophy.en: They lacked systematic philosophical schools like those of the Greek poleis; their core worldview was expressed through ancestral custom, martial ethics, tribal honor, the sanctity of oaths, and ideas of communal landholding

writingSystem.zh: 主要使用奥斯坎语，书写时采用意大利地方字母，部分地区受伊特鲁里亚与希腊字母传统影响；文字使用范围有限，多见于铭文、奉献物和标记
writingSystem.en: The Samnites mainly used the Oscan language, written in local Italic alphabets influenced in part by Etruscan and Greek traditions; writing was limited in use and appeared mostly in inscriptions, dedications, and markings

culturalAchievements.zh: 萨姆奈特文化成就主要体现在山地军事传统、坚固聚落建设、青铜与铁制武器装备、特色头盔与护具、宗教圣地以及奥斯坎语铭文传统；其军事风格深刻影响了中意大利战争文化
culturalAchievements.en: Samnite cultural achievements lay chiefly in their highland martial tradition, fortified settlement building, bronze and iron arms production, distinctive helmets and armor, religious sanctuaries, and the Oscan inscriptional tradition; their military style deeply influenced the war culture of central Italy

languageFamily.zh: 奥斯坎语，属意大利语族中的萨贝利支，广义上属于印欧语系
languageFamily.en: Oscan, of the Sabellian branch of the Italic languages, broadly part of the Indo-European family

gdpDescription.zh: 萨姆奈特经济以自给性山地农业和牧业为主，货币化程度低于沿海希腊城邦与罗马。整体产出不小，但高度分散，难以转化为稳定财政能力。
gdpDescription.en: The Samnite economy rested on largely self-sufficient highland agriculture and pastoralism, with a lower degree of monetization than the coastal Greek cities or Rome. Total output was significant, but highly fragmented and difficult to convert into stable fiscal capacity.

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物种植、畜牧放牧、林业、铁器与青铜兵器制造、皮革加工、山地运输与小规模区域贸易
mainIndustries.en: Grain cultivation, livestock herding, forestry, iron and bronze arms manufacture, leatherworking, mountain transport, and small-scale regional trade

tradeGoods.zh: 牲畜、羊毛、皮革、谷物、木材、蜂蜜、盐、铁制工具与武器，以及从希腊与伊特鲁里亚地区输入的陶器、酒和奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Livestock, wool, leather, grain, timber, honey, salt, iron tools and weapons, alongside imported pottery, wine, and luxury goods from Greek and Etruscan regions

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要体现在土地使用权、牲畜数量、武器装备、家族关系网与战利品积累，而非大量铸币储蓄
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was expressed mainly through land use rights, herd size, weapons, family networks, and accumulated booty rather than large stores of coined money

economicSystem.zh: 以部族共同体和家族生产为核心的混合经济，兼具自给农业、季节性牧群迁移、战利品分配与有限市场交换
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy centered on tribal communities and household production, combining subsistence agriculture, seasonal pastoral movement, distribution of war booty, and limited market exchange

category.en: Envoys, ransom, and ad hoc compensation

treasuryDescription.zh: 萨姆奈特并无像君主国家那样集中的永久国库，所谓“国库”更接近联盟可动员的储备金、贵族家族积存、圣所财富与实物储藏的总和
treasuryDescription.en: The Samnites did not possess a permanent centralized treasury like that of a monarchy; their 'treasury' was better understood as the combined mobilizable reserves of the confederation, aristocratic family hoards, sanctuary wealth, and stored goods

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向应急与战时动员，平时征收有限，危机时依赖加重部族分摊、征发牲畜粮食和重新分配战利品
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was emergency-driven and war-oriented, with limited peacetime collection and reliance in crisis on heavier tribal assessments, requisitioning of livestock and grain, and redistribution of booty

description.zh: 军队主体，由持矛、标枪、盾牌与短剑或长矛的自由战士组成，适于山地伏击、突袭与密集近战
description.en: The main body of the army, made up of free warriors carrying spears, javelins, shields, and short swords or lances, suited for mountain ambushes, raids, and hard close combat

description.zh: 由贵族随从和经验丰富战士构成，装备较优，常担任冲击、护卫与关键阵地争夺任务
description.en: Composed of aristocratic retainers and experienced fighters with better equipment, often used for shock action, guarding leaders, and seizing key positions

description.zh: 数量有限，主要用于侦察、传令、追击和掩护，而非决定性正面冲锋
description.en: Limited in number, they were used mainly for reconnaissance, message carrying, pursuit, and screening rather than decisive frontal charges

description.zh: 驻守山地通道、村寨与堡垒，负责防御、警戒和延缓敌军推进
description.en: Stationed at mountain passes, villages, and forts, they handled defense, watch duties, and delaying enemy advances

commanderInChief.zh: 战时推举统帅与各部族酋长共同指挥
commanderInChief.en: Wartime elected commander together with tribal chiefs

technology.zh: 军备以铁制兵器、青铜头盔、椭圆或长方盾、护胸与标枪为主，擅长山地机动、伏击和利用险隘，但攻城技术与工程能力弱于罗马和希腊化军队
