technology.en: Military equipment centered on iron weapons, bronze helmets, oval or rectangular shields, cuirasses, and javelins; they excelled in mountain mobility, ambushes, and using difficult terrain, but were weaker than Rome and Hellenistic armies in siegecraft and engineering

threats.zh: 首要威胁是不断扩张的罗马共和国，其道路建设、殖民据点和持续征召能力逐步侵蚀萨姆奈特战略空间；次要威胁包括坎帕尼亚与阿普利亚边境敌对势力，以及联盟内部离心与资源衰竭
threats.en: The primary threat was the expanding Roman Republic, whose road building, colonial footholds, and sustained mobilization increasingly eroded Samnite strategic room; secondary threats included hostile forces on the Campanian and Apulian frontiers, as well as internal centrifugal tendencies and resource strain

populationDescription.zh: 人口分布于亚平宁中南部高地与山间谷地，聚居形态以村寨、山顶设防聚落和分散农牧住点为主，整体人口密度低于沿海平原地区
populationDescription.en: The population was spread across the highlands and valleys of the south-central Apennines, living mainly in villages, fortified hilltop settlements, and dispersed farming and herding sites, with a lower overall density than the coastal plains

socialClasses.zh: 主要阶层包括部族贵族与武士、自由农民与牧民、工匠和小商贩、依附家族成员、债务从属者与战俘奴隶；社会地位与家族、战功和牲畜财富密切相关
socialClasses.en: Main social strata included tribal nobles and warriors, free farmers and herders, artisans and petty traders, dependent household members, debt-bound persons, and war captives enslaved; status was closely tied to kinship, martial achievement, and livestock wealth

allies.zh: 323年时并无稳定强大盟友；萨姆奈特偶尔可与其他意大利反罗马势力、部分山地部族或局部城邦形成临时合作
allies.en: In 323 BCE they had no stable great-power ally; the Samnites could at times cooperate temporarily with other anti-Roman Italian forces, certain upland tribes, or individual local cities

enemies.zh: 罗马共和国是其最核心和长期的敌手；此外，与坎帕尼亚部分社区及若干受罗马影响的邻邦也存在敌对和竞争关系
enemies.en: The Roman Republic was their central and long-term enemy; they also had hostile and competitive relations with some Campanian communities and neighboring polities drawn into Rome's orbit

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交以维护山地自主、阻止罗马渗透、争取边缘部族支持和利用意大利半岛力量平衡为核心，但因联盟结构松散，政策持续性有限
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy was centered on preserving highland autonomy, resisting Roman penetration, seeking support among marginal tribes, and exploiting the balance of power within Italy, but the loose confederal structure limited policy consistency

era.zh: 希腊化时代早期的意大利铁器社会
era.en: An early Hellenistic-age Italic Iron Age society

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术包括山地堡寨防御、铁制武器锻造、青铜护具、牧业管理、山区道路与通道控制，以及在与罗马和希腊城邦接触中吸收的部分盔甲与战术元素
keyInnovations.en: Key technologies included hill-fort defense, iron weapon forging, bronze protective gear, pastoral management, control of mountain routes and passes, and certain armor and tactical elements absorbed through contact with Rome and Greek cities

strengths.zh: 萨姆奈特的优势在于强悍的山地战士传统、对险峻地形的熟悉、较高的部族动员能力、顽强的抵抗意志以及灵活的伏击与机动作战方式
strengths.en: Samnite strengths lay in a formidable highland warrior tradition, deep familiarity with rugged terrain, relatively strong tribal mobilization, determined resistance, and flexible methods of ambush and mobile warfare

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点包括政治分散、缺乏稳定中央财政与官僚体系、攻城和工程能力不足、难以长期维持统一战略，且在人力与后勤组织上逐渐落后于罗马
weaknesses.en: Their weaknesses included political fragmentation, the lack of a stable central treasury and bureaucracy, limited siege and engineering capacity, difficulty sustaining a unified long-term strategy, and growing inferiority to Rome in manpower and logistical organization

outlook.zh: 到323年，萨姆奈特仍是中南意大利的重要军事力量，但总体趋势已转为相对衰落。若不能形成更稳固的联盟、改善财政动员并遏制罗马殖民与道路网络，其独立空间将在未来数十年持续收缩。
outlook.en: By 323 BCE the Samnites remained a major military force in central-southern Italy, but the broader trend had turned toward relative decline. Unless they could forge a more durable alliance structure, improve fiscal mobilization, and check Roman colonies and road networks, their room for independence would continue to shrink over the coming decades.

description.zh: 山地部族联盟，虽受罗马压制，仍是意大利中南部重要力量。
description.en: A highland tribal league, pressured by Rome but still a major force in central-southern Italy.

ruler.zh: 城市贵族与地方执政官

governmentForm.zh: 分散的寡头城邦体制，在罗马影响下保留有限自治
governmentForm.en: Decentralized oligarchic city-state system retaining limited autonomy under growing Roman influence

socialStructure.zh: 社会以城市贵族家族为核心，下有自由平民、依附民、工匠、商人、农民与奴隶；宗教祭司与贵族政治紧密结合
socialStructure.en: Society centered on urban aristocratic clans, with free commoners, dependents, artisans, merchants, farmers, and slaves beneath them; priesthood and noble politics were closely intertwined

rulingClass.zh: 地方贵族家族、祭司贵族与担任年度官职的城市精英
rulingClass.en: Local noble families, priestly aristocrats, and urban elites holding annual magistracies

succession.zh: 无统一继承制度，各城邦官职通常由贵族家族选举、轮替或在寡头集团内部协商产生
succession.en: No unified succession system; offices in each city-state were usually filled through aristocratic election, rotation, or oligarchic negotiation within leading families

structure.zh: 323 BCE时伊特鲁里亚并非统一国家，而是由塔尔奎尼亚、凯雷、沃尔西尼、沃尔泰拉、佩鲁西亚等城邦组成。各城设有贵族议事会、年度执政官与祭司体系，名义上仍维持传统制度，但外交与军事空间已明显受罗马压缩
structure.en: In 323 BCE Etruria was not a unified state but a group of city-states such as Tarquinii, Caere, Volsinii, Volaterrae, and Perusia. Each city had aristocratic councils, annual magistrates, and priestly institutions; these traditional systems survived in form, but their diplomatic and military freedom had been sharply constrained by Rome

function.zh: 由贵族家族主导，决定地方财政、司法、公共工程与对外应对
function.en: Dominated by aristocratic families, it oversaw local finance, justice, public works, and external responses

function.zh: 负责城市行政、治安、税收征集和执行议事会决议
function.en: Handled urban administration, policing, tax collection, and execution of council decisions

function.zh: 主持宗教仪式、解释神意、进行占卜，并为政治决策提供神学合法性
function.en: Conducted rituals, interpreted divine signs, practiced divination, and provided sacred legitimacy for political decisions

function.zh: 处理土地、债务、财产、继承与城邦内部纠纷
function.en: Managed disputes over land, debt, property, inheritance, and internal civic conflicts

function.zh: 监管市场、港口、度量衡、关税与对外商业往来
function.en: Supervised markets, ports, weights and measures, customs, and external commerce

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理以各城邦为单位，周边乡村、庄园与圣所附属地由城市贵族、代理人和乡村小官管理。城邦联盟传统已衰弱，区域合作多属临时性
localAdmin.en: Local administration was organized city by city, with surrounding countryside, estates, and sanctuary lands managed by urban nobles, agents, and minor rural officials. The old league tradition had weakened, and regional coordination was mostly ad hoc

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法、贵族裁断、宗教规范与城邦判例为基础。伊特鲁里亚法无统一法典，私人财产、家族权利、宗教禁忌与债务关系均受重视；同时罗马法影响正在扩大
legalSystem.en: Law rested on custom, aristocratic judgment, religious norms, and civic precedent. There was no unified Etruscan code; private property, family rights, sacred prohibitions, and debt relations were important, while Roman legal influence was steadily growing

taxationSystem.zh: 财政收入主要来自土地贡赋、港口与市场税、通行费、罚金、战时征发与属地收入。税制并不高度集中，很多征收由地方贵族和城邦机构分掌
taxationSystem.en: Revenue came chiefly from land dues, port and market taxes, tolls, fines, wartime levies, and dependent territories. Taxation was not highly centralized, and much collection was controlled by local nobles and civic institutions

religion.zh: 伊特鲁里亚宗教为多神信仰，重视神意解释、祭祀秩序与宇宙分区观念。主要神祇包括蒂尼亚、乌尼、门尔瓦等；肝占、观鸟与雷兆解释尤具特色
religion.en: Etruscan religion was polytheistic and emphasized interpretation of divine will, ritual order, and a cosmological division of sacred space. Major deities included Tinia, Uni, and Menrva; haruspicy, augury, and interpretation of lightning were especially distinctive

philosophy.zh: 伊特鲁里亚文化不以抽象哲学著称，其思想核心更接近宗教仪礼、祖先记忆、贵族伦理与命运观；同时受到希腊哲学与意大利半岛多元文化环境的间接影响
philosophy.en: Etruscan culture was not primarily known for abstract philosophy; its intellectual core lay more in ritual religion, ancestral memory, aristocratic ethics, and concepts of fate, though it was indirectly influenced by Greek philosophy and the wider cultural world of Italy

writingSystem.zh: 使用伊特鲁里亚字母，源自西希腊字母体系，主要用于铭文、墓葬题记、宗教文本与所有权标记；到323 BCE时在部分地区已面临拉丁文扩张压力
writingSystem.en: The Etruscans used the Etruscan alphabet, derived from a Western Greek script, mainly for inscriptions, funerary texts, religious writings, and ownership marks; by 323 BCE it was under increasing pressure from the spread of Latin in some areas

culturalAchievements.zh: 重要成就包括拱门与城市工程传统、精致金属工艺、彩绘墓室艺术、宗教占卜学、象牙与青铜制品、贵族宴饮文化及对早期罗马宗教与政治礼制的深远影响
culturalAchievements.en: Major achievements included traditions of arches and urban engineering, fine metalwork, painted tomb art, religious divinatory science, ivory and bronze craftsmanship, aristocratic banquet culture, and a profound influence on early Roman religion and political ceremonial

languageFamily.zh: 伊特鲁里亚语，属于非印欧语言，可能与雷托语等存在远缘联系，但整体上为独特语系
languageFamily.en: Etruscan, a non-Indo-European language, possibly distantly related to Raetic and a few other languages, but overall a distinctive linguistic family

gdpDescription.zh: 323 BCE时伊特鲁里亚经济仍具区域活力，但已远不及其鼎盛期。农业、金属工艺、陶器、石材和地方贸易仍重要，然而政治分裂与罗马控制削弱了其海上商业和独立财政能力
gdpDescription.en: In 323 BCE the Etruscan economy still retained regional vitality but was far below its earlier peak. Agriculture, metalworking, ceramics, stone, and local trade remained important, yet political fragmentation and Roman control weakened maritime commerce and independent fiscal capacity

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物与葡萄种植、橄榄栽培、畜牧、陶器、青铜器、铁器加工、石材开采、木材、地方航运与港口贸易
mainIndustries.en: Grain and vine cultivation, olive growing, pastoralism, pottery, bronze work, ironworking, stone quarrying, timber, local shipping, and port trade

tradeGoods.zh: 葡萄酒、橄榄油、陶器、青铜器、铁制品、盐、木材、石材、奢侈工艺品、农产品与奴隶
tradeGoods.en: Wine, olive oil, pottery, bronzes, iron goods, salt, timber, stone, luxury craft items, agricultural produce, and slaves

name.zh: 伊特鲁里亚地方铸币与罗马铜币并用
name.en: Local Etruscan coinages alongside Roman bronze currency

householdWealth.zh: 财富高度集中于贵族庄园主、商人和祭司贵族之家。普通农户通常拥有小块土地、牲畜与基本器具，依附性债务和土地兼并使许多家庭脆弱
householdWealth.en: Wealth was highly concentrated among aristocratic estate owners, merchants, and priestly nobles. Ordinary households usually held small plots, livestock, and basic tools, while debt dependency and land concentration left many families vulnerable

economicSystem.zh: 以农业庄园、城市手工业和区域市场交换为基础的混合经济，带有贵族控制、圣所财产、地方铸币和逐步纳入罗马经济圈的特征
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy based on agricultural estates, urban craft production, and regional market exchange, characterized by aristocratic control, sanctuary property, local coinage, and gradual incorporation into the Roman economic sphere

source.zh: 罚金、司法收费与通行费
属地收入与圣所财产收益
source.en: Dependent territories and sanctuary property income

category.zh: 公共工程与城墙港口维护
category.en: Public works and maintenance of walls and harbors

treasuryDescription.zh: 各城邦分别保有库藏，并无统一国库。库藏通常包括金属储备、神庙财产、战时储粮与可动用的贵族捐输。总体财政韧性有限，难以支撑长期大规模战争
treasuryDescription.en: Each city-state maintained its own treasury rather than a unified state reserve. These treasuries usually included metal reserves, temple wealth, wartime grain stores, and mobilizable aristocratic contributions. Overall fiscal resilience was limited and insufficient for sustained large-scale war

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策以维持城邦自治、保护贵族财产和应对罗马压力为主，倾向保守征收与临时摊派，而非系统性的中央化改革
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on preserving civic autonomy, protecting aristocratic property, and managing Roman pressure, favoring conservative collection and ad hoc assessments rather than systematic centralizing reform

description.zh: 由各城邦征召的重装与中装步兵组成，装备不完全统一，主要承担城防与地区作战
description.en: Made up of heavy and medium infantry levied by individual city-states, with uneven equipment, mainly for city defense and regional warfare

description.zh: 以贵族及其随从为主，人数有限，主要用于侦察、联络和局部冲击
description.en: Comprised chiefly of nobles and their retainers, limited in number and used mainly for reconnaissance, liaison, and localized shock action

description.zh: 驻守城墙、城门和重要圣所港口，承担治安与紧急防卫任务
description.en: Stationed at walls, gates, and key sanctuaries or ports, responsible for security and emergency defense

description.zh: 在危机时由邻近城邦、意大利同盟者或个别雇佣兵补充，持续性较差
description.en: Added in times of crisis from neighboring cities, Italian allies, or individual mercenaries, with limited permanence

commanderInChief.zh: 无统一最高统帅，由各城执政官与贵族将领分别指挥
commanderInChief.en: No unified commander-in-chief; each city was led by its own magistrates and aristocratic commanders

technology.zh: 军备以意大利半岛传统重步兵装备、长矛、盾牌、头盔、胸甲、投射武器和坚固城防为主；海军能力相较古典时期已大为衰退
technology.en: Military equipment centered on the traditional Italic heavy infantry kit of spears, shields, helmets, cuirasses, missile weapons, and strong fortifications; naval capacity had greatly declined compared with earlier centuries

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自罗马共和国的政治与军事扩张，其次是萨姆奈特战争余波、内部分裂和城邦之间缺乏协同；外部海上竞争者如迦太基已不再是首要压力
threats.en: The main threat came from the political and military expansion of the Roman Republic, followed by the aftereffects of Samnite conflicts, internal fragmentation, and poor coordination among the city-states; maritime rivals such as Carthage were no longer the primary pressure

populationDescription.zh: 总人口约40万，分布于南北伊特鲁里亚的多个城邦、乡村聚落、庄园与港口。人口规模明显低于早期繁盛时代，且部分地区已加速拉丁化
populationDescription.en: The total population was about 400,000, spread across multiple city-states, rural settlements, estates, and ports in northern and southern Etruria. This was clearly below the scale of the earlier prosperous age, and parts of the region were already undergoing accelerated Latinization

socialClasses.zh: 上层为世袭贵族、祭司家族与富商；中层为自由农民、工匠、船主、小商人；下层包括佃农、依附民、债务人、劳工与奴隶。女性在贵族礼仪与家族纪念中的能见度相较希腊世界更高
socialClasses.en: The upper stratum consisted of hereditary nobles, priestly families, and wealthy merchants; the middle included free farmers, artisans, shipowners, and petty traders; the lower comprised tenants, dependents, debtors, laborers, and slaves. Elite women were more visible in ceremony and family commemoration than in much of the Greek world

allies.zh: 无稳定统一盟邦；个别城邦可能与其他伊特鲁里亚城市、拉丁社群或意大利地方势力保持临时合作
allies.en: There were no stable unified allies; individual city-states might maintain temporary cooperation with other Etruscan cities, Latin communities, or local Italian powers

enemies.zh: 罗马共和国是最主要且最持久的对手；局部地区也可能与萨姆奈特人及邻近意大利社群发生冲突或紧张关系
enemies.en: The Roman Republic was the principal and most enduring adversary; in some areas there could also be conflict or tension with the Samnites and neighboring Italian communities

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以保全地方自治、避免单独被罗马吞并、在顺从与抵抗之间寻求平衡为主。由于缺乏统一领导，整体外交呈被动、防御和地方化特征
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on preserving local autonomy, avoiding isolated absorption by Rome, and balancing accommodation with resistance. Owing to the lack of unified leadership, diplomacy was generally reactive, defensive, and highly localized

era.zh: 希腊化时期
era.en: Hellenistic Period

keyInnovations.zh: 继承并延续了拱券建筑、排水工程、青铜铸造、宗教占卜术体系、道路与城墙建设技术；但在323 BCE时更多体现为传统技术积累，而非新的前沿创新中心
keyInnovations.en: It preserved and continued traditions in arch construction, drainage engineering, bronze casting, organized divinatory practice, and road and fortification building; by 323 BCE, however, it represented accumulated traditional expertise more than a leading center of new innovation

strengths.zh: 拥有深厚城市传统、成熟宗教文化、优良工艺、肥沃农业区、若干坚固城防与长期积累的区域贸易网络；其文化对罗马仍具显著影响力
strengths.en: It possessed deep urban traditions, a mature religious culture, strong craftsmanship, fertile agricultural zones, several well-fortified cities, and long-established regional trade networks; its cultural influence on Rome remained substantial

weaknesses.zh: 最大弱点是政治分裂、缺乏统一军事指挥、财政分散、人口与海权衰退，以及在罗马扩张面前难以形成持久共同战线
weaknesses.en: Its greatest weaknesses were political fragmentation, lack of unified military command, dispersed finances, declining population and sea power, and an inability to form a lasting common front against Roman expansion

outlook.zh: 前景偏向持续衰落与逐步并入罗马体系。短期内部分城邦仍可保持名义自治和文化特色，但中长期看，伊特鲁里亚政治独立性将继续减弱，语言与制度也会逐渐被罗马同化
outlook.en: The outlook points toward continued decline and gradual incorporation into the Roman system. In the short term some city-states could preserve nominal autonomy and cultural distinctiveness, but over the medium and long term Etruscan political independence would continue to erode, and its language and institutions would increasingly be assimilated by Rome

description.zh: 曾经强盛的城邦群已大多落入罗马影响之下。
description.en: The once-powerful Etruscan city states had largely fallen under Roman influence.

ruler.zh: 罗马元老院与年度执政官
ruler.en: Roman Senate and the annual consuls

governmentForm.zh: 贵族主导的混合共和制，由公民大会、元老院与年度民选政务官共同构成
governmentForm.en: An aristocratic mixed republic composed of citizen assemblies, the Senate, and annually elected magistrates

socialStructure.zh: 社会由元老贵族、骑士与富裕平民、一般平民、自由民、盟邦共同体成员及奴隶组成；血缘、财产、兵役义务与公民资格决定政治地位
socialStructure.en: Society was structured around senatorial aristocrats, equestrians and wealthy plebeians, ordinary plebeians, freed persons, members of allied communities, and slaves; lineage, property, military obligation, and citizenship determined political standing

rulingClass.zh: 元老阶层中的贵族家族与担任高级政务官的前任官员
rulingClass.en: The senatorial class, especially noble families and former holders of high magistracies

succession.zh: 无世袭君位；高级官职由年度选举产生，国家权威在元老院传统、民众授权与同僚制之间延续
succession.en: There was no hereditary throne; high offices were filled by annual election, with state authority continuing through senatorial tradition, popular mandate, and collegial office-holding

structure.zh: 323 BCE时的罗马政体由执政官、法务官、财务官、营造官等年度政务官，元老院，以及百人团大会、特里布斯大会与平民大会等公民集会组成；在危机时可任命独裁官。元老院虽名义上为咨询机构，实则在外交、财政、战争指挥与省际盟约管理上拥有决定性影响
structure.en: In 323 BCE the Roman constitution consisted of annual magistrates such as consuls, praetors, quaestors, and aediles, the Senate, and citizen assemblies including the Centuriate Assembly, Tribal Assembly, and Plebeian Council; in emergencies a dictator could be appointed. Though formally advisory, the Senate exercised decisive influence over diplomacy, finance, war direction, and the management of interstate alliances

function.zh: 主持国家行政、召集元老院与公民大会、统率军队并执行最高年度民政与军政权力
function.en: Directed state administration, convened the Senate and assemblies, commanded armies, and exercised the highest annual civil and military authority

function.zh: 负责司法审理、程序管理，并在必要时代理军事或行政事务
function.en: Handled judicial proceedings, legal procedure, and when necessary delegated military or administrative duties

function.zh: 管理国库、军费拨付、公共财务记录及部分随军财政事务
function.en: Managed the treasury, disbursed military funds, maintained public financial records, and oversaw some campaign finance

function.zh: 监督市场、粮食供应、公共秩序、道路与祭典活动
function.en: Supervised markets, grain supply, public order, roads, and religious festivals

function.zh: 定期进行人口与财产登记、整编公民等级、审查元老院名册并管理部分公共承包
function.en: Conducted periodic census and property registration, arranged citizen classes, revised the senatorial roll, and managed some public contracts

function.zh: 保护平民免受官员侵害，提出平民决议并对若干公权行为行使否决
function.en: Protected plebeians from official abuse, proposed plebiscites, and exercised veto over certain public acts

function.zh: 审议外交、战争、财政、宗教与盟邦事务，形成国家政策连续性
function.en: Deliberated on diplomacy, war, finance, religion, and allied affairs, providing continuity in state policy

localAdmin.zh: 罗马直接统治的核心地区以城市共同体、部落区与乡村地产为基础管理；拉丁殖民地、罗马殖民地及意大利盟邦保持各自地方机构，但在兵役、条约义务与外交上受罗马支配。地方治理依赖地方贵族、家族网络、祭司组织与临时派遣的罗马官员，而非成熟的常设官僚体系
localAdmin.en: The Roman core territory was administered through urban communities, tribal districts, and rural estates; Latin colonies, Roman colonies, and Italian allied communities retained local institutions but were subordinated to Rome in military obligations, treaty commitments, and foreign relations. Local governance relied on municipal elites, family networks, priestly bodies, and temporarily assigned Roman magistrates rather than a mature permanent bureaucracy

legalSystem.zh: 法律以《十二铜表法》传统、习惯法、祭司法解释、政务官裁断与公民大会通过的法令为基础。法律强调家长权、财产权、债务关系、契约与公民程序权；贵族影响仍强，但平民争取到较多制度保障
legalSystem.en: Law rested on the tradition of the Twelve Tables, customary law, priestly interpretation, magisterial judgment, and statutes passed by citizen assemblies. It emphasized paternal authority, property rights, debt relations, contracts, and procedural rights of citizens; aristocratic influence remained strong, though plebeians had secured broader institutional protections

taxationSystem.zh: 罗马本城公民通常不承担后世意义上的固定直接税，国家主要依赖公地收益、战利品、罚没、关税、租税承包、盟邦贡赋性质的义务以及战时特别征发。意大利盟邦则以兵员、补给和地方负担形式支撑共和国扩张
taxationSystem.en: Citizens of Rome itself generally did not bear a fixed direct tax in the later provincial sense; the state relied chiefly on revenues from public land, booty, confiscations, customs dues, tax farming of rents, quasi-tributary obligations from allies, and extraordinary wartime levies. Italian allies supported expansion through troop contributions, supplies, and local burdens

religion.zh: 以古罗马多神教为核心，重视朱庇特、朱诺、密涅瓦、玛尔斯、奎里努斯、维斯塔等神祇的国家祭祀；宗教与政治密不可分，占卜、祭仪与誓言对公共决策具有重要合法性作用，同时不断吸收伊特鲁里亚和希腊宗教元素
religion.en: Religion centered on traditional Roman polytheism, with state cults devoted to Jupiter, Juno, Minerva, Mars, Quirinus, Vesta, and others; religion and politics were inseparable, and augury, ritual, and oath-taking played an important legitimizing role in public decisions, while Etruscan and Greek elements were continually absorbed

philosophy.zh: 323 BCE的罗马尚未形成成熟的本土哲学传统，公共伦理主要表现为祖制、德性观与贵族政治文化，如虔敬、勇武、节制、忠诚与荣誉；希腊哲学思想开始通过南意大利与伊特鲁里亚间接传入，但尚未主导罗马上层精神生活
philosophy.en: In 323 BCE Rome had not yet developed a mature native philosophical tradition; public ethics were expressed chiefly through mos maiorum, virtue ideals, and aristocratic political culture such as piety, courage, restraint, loyalty, and honor. Greek philosophical ideas were beginning to enter indirectly through southern Italy and Etruria, but had not yet come to dominate elite intellectual life

writingSystem.zh: 使用古拉丁字母书写拉丁语，字母体系源自伊特鲁里亚中介下的希腊字母；文字主要用于法律铭文、宗教公式、公共记录、墓志与精英交流
writingSystem.en: Rome used the Old Latin alphabet to write Latin, a script ultimately derived from Greek through Etruscan mediation; writing was used chiefly for legal inscriptions, religious formulae, public records, epitaphs, and elite communication

culturalAchievements.zh: 罗马在这一时期的成就主要体现于制度与社会整合，而非文学高峰：包括共和政体的官职与公民大会体系、军团组织、拉丁殖民网络、道路与城防建设，以及将不同意大利共同体纳入罗马主导秩序的能力
culturalAchievements.en: Roman achievements in this period lay more in institutions and social integration than in literary brilliance: the republican system of magistracies and assemblies, the legionary organization, the Latin colonial network, road and fortification building, and the ability to incorporate diverse Italian communities into a Roman-led order

languageFamily.zh: 意大利语族拉丁-法利斯坎支
languageFamily.en: Italic language family, Latin-Faliscan branch

gdpDescription.zh: 这一估算为现代折算意义上的年产出近似值，用于反映罗马及其意大利支配网络的农业、手工业、军事动员与贸易规模。经济基础高度农业化，但凭借中意大利的土地资源、战时掠夺、殖民扩张与区域市场整合，整体活力在当时西地中海诸政体中相当突出
gdpDescription.en: This estimate is an approximate annual output in modern comparative terms, intended to reflect the scale of agriculture, handicraft production, military mobilization, and trade across Rome and its Italian dominance network. The economy remained heavily agrarian, but through central Italian land resources, wartime plunder, colonial expansion, and regional market integration, it was notably vigorous among western Mediterranean polities of the time

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物种植、葡萄与橄榄栽培、畜牧、铁铜加工、陶器、盐业、建筑、武器制造、木材与运输服务
mainIndustries.en: Grain cultivation, viticulture and olive growing, pastoralism, iron and bronze working, pottery, salt production, construction, arms manufacture, timber, and transport services

tradeGoods.zh: 谷物、葡萄酒、橄榄油、盐、陶器、金属器、牲畜、木材、奴隶，以及来自南意大利和海外的奢侈品、精致陶器与希腊制品
tradeGoods.en: Grain, wine, olive oil, salt, pottery, metal goods, livestock, timber, slaves, and luxury items, fine ceramics, and Greek products from southern Italy and overseas

name.zh: 青铜阿斯及称量铜货币体系
name.en: Aes grave and bronze weight-money system

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富差异极大。元老贵族控制大片土地、公地承包、战利品分配与客户网络；普通公民多拥有小块农地、房屋与少量牲畜；盟邦与乡村贫民财富脆弱，债务与战时徭役是长期压力来源
householdWealth.en: Household wealth varied greatly. Senatorial aristocrats controlled large landed holdings, leases of public land, shares of war booty, and client networks; ordinary citizens often possessed small plots, dwellings, and limited livestock; allies and the rural poor remained economically fragile, with debt and wartime burdens as persistent pressures

economicSystem.zh: 以自耕农农业、贵族地产、盟邦供给与战时征发为核心的前现代混合经济；市场交换存在且逐步扩展，但国家能力、社会地位和军事掠夺对财富分配的影响极大
economicSystem.en: A premodern mixed economy centered on peasant farming, aristocratic estates, allied provisioning, and wartime requisition; market exchange existed and was expanding, but state capacity, social rank, and military plunder heavily shaped the distribution of wealth

source.en: Public land rents and land income

source.zh: 关税、市场收费与港口税
source.en: Customs, market dues, and port charges

source.zh: 盟邦供给折算与特别征发
source.en: Allied contributions in equivalent value and extraordinary levies

source.zh: 其他司法与宗教性收入

category.zh: 宗教祭祀、节庆与公共礼仪
category.en: Religious rites, festivals, and public ceremony

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库主要存放于农神庙相关库藏体系中，形式包括青铜、贵金属、战利品、祭器和可征发物资。罗马财政波动很大，平时积累有限，但战争胜利与盟邦供给可迅速充实库藏
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was chiefly held in the stores associated with the Temple of Saturn, in the form of bronze, precious metal, booty, cult objects, and requisitionable resources. Roman public finance was highly variable; peacetime accumulation was limited, but military victories and allied contributions could replenish the reserves rapidly

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策务实而军事化，优先保障征兵、筑路、殖民与盟邦控制；避免对核心公民施加持续重税，尽可能通过战争收益、公地经营与外部负担来支撑扩张
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was pragmatic and militarized, prioritizing recruitment, road building, colonization, and control over allies; it avoided imposing sustained heavy taxation on core citizens and sought to fund expansion through war gains, management of public land, and burdens shifted outward

description.zh: 由具备财产资格的罗马公民组成，是共和国军队核心。323 BCE时军制正处于从重装方阵向更灵活编组过渡的阶段，装备以长盾、投枪、剑与铜盔甲为主
description.en: Composed of Roman citizens with property qualifications, this was the core of the republican army. In 323 BCE the army was in a transitional stage from heavy phalanx-style fighting toward more flexible formations, equipped mainly with large shields, javelins, swords, and bronze armor

description.zh: 由拉丁人与其他意大利盟邦提供，数量常与罗马公民部队相当或更多，承担主力步战、守备与远征任务，是罗马霸权的重要支柱
description.en: Provided by Latins and other Italian allies, these forces often matched or exceeded Roman citizen contingents in number and performed mainline infantry, garrison, and expeditionary duties, forming a crucial pillar of Roman hegemony

description.zh: 包括罗马公民骑兵与盟邦骑兵，主要用于侦察、掩护、追击和侧翼作战；与后世强势骑兵国家相比规模仍有限
description.en: Included Roman citizen cavalry and allied mounted troops, used mainly for reconnaissance, screening, pursuit, and flanking; compared with later cavalry-heavy powers, its scale remained modest

description.zh: 负责殖民地、要塞、道路节点与新服从地区的守备，由轮换征发、地方驻守和临时编成部队构成
description.en: Responsible for colonies, forts, road nodes, and recently subdued areas, these forces consisted of rotating levies, local garrisons, and temporary formations

commanderInChief.zh: 两名年度执政官在各自辖区内共同行使最高统帅权，元老院负责总体战略指导
commanderInChief.en: The two annual consuls jointly exercised supreme command within their assigned spheres, while the Senate provided overarching strategic direction

technology.zh: 军事技术以铁制兵器、青铜护具、攻城器械、筑城工事、道路工程和严密营地构筑见长。罗马在组织纪律、后勤和野战营建方面尤为突出，虽在323 BCE尚未形成后期成熟的操条军团，但已表现出适应山地与多样战场的优势
technology.en: Military technology emphasized iron weapons, bronze armor, siege devices, fortification, road engineering, and highly systematic camp construction. Rome was especially strong in discipline, logistics, and field entrenchment; although the fully developed manipular legion had not yet reached its later form in 323 BCE, it already showed advantages in adapting to mountain warfare and varied battle conditions

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自萨姆奈特人及其他意大利山地部族的顽强抵抗、伊特鲁里亚与南意大利城邦可能的再度联合，以及海上强权迦太基未来在西地中海的利益冲突。更远方的马其顿势力在此时尚非直接军事威胁，但希腊世界的变动会影响南意大利局势
threats.en: The main threats came from persistent Samnite and other Italian upland resistance, the possibility of renewed alignment among Etruscans and southern Italian cities, and future strategic rivalry with Carthage in the western Mediterranean. More distant Macedonian power was not yet a direct military threat, but shifts in the Greek world could influence southern Italian affairs

populationDescription.zh: 这一人口包括罗马公民核心、拉丁殖民地及受罗马支配或结盟的广大意大利人口总和，而非单指罗马城居民。罗马城本身已是意大利最大的城市之一，但多数人口仍分布在乡村农地、山地共同体和中小城镇
populationDescription.en: This population includes the Roman citizen core, Latin colonies, and the broad Italian population under Roman dominance or alliance, not merely the inhabitants of the city of Rome. Rome itself was already one of the largest cities in Italy, but most people still lived in rural farmland, upland communities, and small to medium towns

socialClasses.zh: 社会阶层包括元老贵族、骑士与富裕地主、中小自耕农和平民、工匠与商人、自由民、意大利盟邦居民及奴隶。公民身份并不完全等同于财富，盟邦在军役与地方自治上承担重任，却通常缺乏罗马公民政治权利
socialClasses.en: Social strata included senatorial aristocrats, equestrians and wealthy landholders, middling and small peasant citizens and plebeians, artisans and traders, freed persons, inhabitants of Italian allied communities, and slaves. Citizenship did not fully correspond to wealth, and allies bore heavy military and local responsibilities while generally lacking Roman civic political rights

allies.zh: 主要盟友与附属伙伴为拉丁殖民地、部分意大利城邦与受条约约束的中意大利共同体；若干坎帕尼亚与伊特鲁里亚城市在不同时期与罗马合作
allies.en: Its principal allies and subordinate partners were the Latin colonies, certain Italian cities, and central Italian communities bound by treaty; some Campanian and Etruscan cities cooperated with Rome at various times

enemies.zh: 主要敌对力量为萨姆奈特人及其他反抗罗马扩张的意大利部族；在战略层面，南意大利希腊城邦的疑惧与迦太基在西地中海的竞争潜力也构成潜在对手关系
enemies.en: The principal hostile forces were the Samnites and other Italian peoples resisting Roman expansion; at the strategic level, suspicion from the Greek cities of southern Italy and the competitive potential of Carthage in the western Mediterranean also created latent adversarial relations

foreignPolicy.zh: 罗马外交以分层结盟、逐步吞并、殖民设防、条约约束与军事威慑为核心。其目标不是立即建立海外帝国，而是在意大利半岛通过道路、殖民地、盟邦网络与惩奖机制形成持久霸权
foreignPolicy.en: Roman foreign policy centered on layered alliances, gradual incorporation, fortified colonization, treaty control, and military deterrence. Its aim was not yet to build an overseas empire immediately, but to establish durable hegemony in the Italian peninsula through roads, colonies, allied networks, and systems of reward and punishment

era.zh: 希腊化时代早期的意大利铁器文明
era.en: Early Hellenistic-era Italic Iron Age civilization

keyInnovations.zh: 关键能力不在孤立发明，而在制度化应用：军团与盟邦联合动员、标准化营地与道路工程、殖民据点网络、城墙与排水建设、青铜和铁器制造，以及将伊特鲁里亚与希腊技术转化为共和国家能力的本领
keyInnovations.en: The key advances lay less in isolated inventions than in institutionalized application: combined Roman-allied mobilization, standardized camp and road engineering, colonial strongpoint networks, wall and drainage construction, bronze and iron manufacture, and the capacity to convert Etruscan and Greek techniques into republican state power

strengths.zh: 罗马最大的优势在于强韧的公民共同体、可扩展的盟邦体系、稳定的贵族领导层、较高的军事纪律，以及在失败后迅速重建军力的能力。其政治制度虽然竞争激烈，却能将精英荣誉追求转化为国家扩张动力
strengths.en: Rome’s greatest strengths were its resilient citizen community, scalable allied system, stable aristocratic leadership, relatively high military discipline, and ability to rebuild armed forces quickly after setbacks. Although politically competitive, its institutions turned elite pursuit of honor into a motor of state expansion

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点包括内部阶层紧张、债务与土地问题、对盟邦忠诚的持续依赖、财政对战争收益的偏重，以及政务官任期短导致的战略连贯性不足。海军能力仍有限，对南意大利希腊和海上贸易体系的掌控尚不牢固
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses included persistent internal class tensions, debt and land problems, continued reliance on allied loyalty, finances overly dependent on war gains, and short magistracies that could weaken strategic continuity. Naval capacity remained limited, and control over the Greek south of Italy and maritime trade systems was not yet secure

outlook.zh: 323 BCE的罗马正处于上升阶段。若能继续压制萨姆奈特人、稳固拉丁与意大利盟邦体系，并在南意大利扩展影响，它极可能在未来数十年完成对半岛的大体支配，并逐步卷入与迦太基及希腊化世界更广泛的竞争
outlook.en: In 323 BCE Rome was in an ascendant phase. If it could continue to suppress the Samnites, consolidate the Latin and Italian allied system, and extend influence in southern Italy, it was highly likely over the coming decades to achieve broad domination of the peninsula and gradually become drawn into wider competition with Carthage and the Hellenistic world

description.zh: 在意大利中部扩张中的共和国，正逐步建立半岛霸权。
description.en: An expanding republic in central Italy, steadily building hegemony over the peninsula.

leader.zh: 阿庇乌斯·克劳狄乌斯·凯库斯

ideology.zh: 贵族传统主义——维护元老院与贵族家族对国家事务的主导权，坚持祖制（mos maiorum），推动罗马在意大利的有序扩张
ideology.en: Patrician traditionalism — upholding senatorial and noble family dominance in state affairs, adhering to mos maiorum, and promoting Rome's orderly expansion in Italy

relationship.zh: 元老院贵族派是罗马共和国实际权力核心，通过世袭声望、庇护网络与宗教权威掌控高级政务官选举与外交决策，但正面临平民阶层日益强烈的权利诉求。
relationship.en: The patrician Senate faction formed the effective power core of the Roman Republic, controlling senior magistrate elections and foreign policy through hereditary prestige, patronage networks, and religious authority, though facing increasingly assertive demands from the plebeian order.

description.zh: 以克劳狄、科尔涅利、法比等古老贵族家族为核心的元老院集团，长期主导罗马政治。他们垄断重要祭司职位与传统权威，通过家族间联姻和庇护体系维持统治。阿庇乌斯·克劳狄乌斯·凯库斯是这一时期最具影响力的贵族政治家，不仅推动了阿庇亚大道和罗马第一条水道的建设，还大胆改革元老院构成，展现出贵族阶层内部的革新精神。在萨姆奈特战争中，贵族将领们主导了军事决策，将罗马势力逐步推向意大利半岛南部。
description.en: The senatorial bloc centered on ancient patrician families such as the Claudii, Cornelii, and Fabii dominated Roman politics for generations. They monopolized key priesthoods and traditional authority, maintaining their rule through inter-family marriage alliances and patronage systems. Appius Claudius Caecus was the most influential patrician statesman of this period — not only driving the construction of the Appian Way and Rome's first aqueduct, but also boldly reforming the Senate's composition, revealing an innovative spirit within the aristocratic class. In the Samnite Wars, patrician commanders directed military strategy, gradually extending Roman power toward southern Italy.

name.zh: 昆图斯·法比乌斯·马克西穆斯·鲁利安努斯

role.zh: 萨姆奈特战争中的核心军事指挥官，法比家族的权力奠基者
role.en: Key military commander in the Samnite Wars and power-builder of the Fabian family

name.zh: 卢基乌斯·帕皮里乌斯·库尔索

role.zh: 卓越军事统帅，在萨姆奈特战争中取得关键胜利
role.en: Outstanding military leader who achieved key victories in the Samnite Wars

ideology.zh: 平民权利扩张——通过立法手段扩大平民参政权、推动土地分配、争取平民决议的法律效力与政务官任职资格
ideology.en: Plebeian rights expansion — broadening plebeian political participation through legislation, promoting land distribution, and securing legal force for plebiscites and access to magistracies

headquarters.zh: 罗马（阿文提诺山与平民大会）
headquarters.en: Rome (Aventine Hill and the Plebeian Assembly)

relationship.zh: 平民派通过保民官否决权、撤离威胁和立法运动持续挑战贵族特权，但并非反体制力量，而是在共和框架内争取更大权利份额，双方斗争推动了罗马宪政演进。
relationship.en: The plebeian faction continually challenged patrician privileges through tribunician veto power, secession threats, and legislative campaigns, yet was not an anti-system force but rather sought a larger share of rights within the republican framework. This struggle drove the evolution of Rome's constitutional order.

description.zh: 平民改革运动是罗马共和国历史上持续数个世纪的制度性抗争。到公元前323年前后，平民已通过李锡尼-塞克斯提法案（前367年）赢得了执政官职位的开放，但土地分配、债务减免和政治平等的斗争仍在继续。保民官制度是平民最重要的制度武器，其人身不可侵犯权和否决权使其能有效制衡贵族权力。最终在前287年通过的霍腾西法将使平民大会决议获得与百人团大会同等的法律效力，标志着身份斗争的制度性解决。平民派推动的改革深刻重塑了罗马政治，使共和国从纯粹贵族统治演变为更具包容性的混合政体。
description.en: The plebeian reform movement was an institutional struggle lasting centuries in the Roman Republic. By around 323 BCE, plebeians had already won access to the consulship through the Licinian-Sextian laws (367 BCE), but struggles over land distribution, debt relief, and political equality continued. The tribunate was the plebeians' most important institutional weapon — its personal inviolability and veto power provided an effective check on patrician authority. The Lex Hortensia of 287 BCE would ultimately give plebiscites the same legal force as centuriate assembly legislation, marking an institutional resolution of the struggle of the orders. Plebeian-driven reforms profoundly reshaped Roman politics, transforming the Republic from purely aristocratic rule into a more inclusive mixed constitution.

name.zh: 盖尤斯·马尔基乌斯·鲁提卢斯

title.zh: 首位平民独裁官与监察官

role.zh: 打破贵族垄断高级职务的先驱人物
role.en: Pioneer in breaking patrician monopoly on senior offices

role.zh: 推动前339年法案，加强平民立法的法律效力
role.en: Promoted the laws of 339 BCE, strengthening the legal force of plebeian legislation

ideology.zh: 拉丁权利诉求——在承担与罗马共同的军事义务的同时，争取更多政治平等、公民权扩展与土地分配
ideology.en: Latin rights advocacy — seeking greater political equality, expansion of citizenship, and land distribution while sharing military burdens with Rome

headquarters.zh: 拉维尼乌姆（拉丁联盟宗教中心）
headquarters.en: Lavinium (religious center of the Latin League)

relationship.zh: 拉丁同盟社群是罗马霸权体系中最重要的附属力量。拉丁战争（前340-338年）结束后，旧拉丁同盟被解散，各拉丁城市被迫与罗马建立双边条约关系。拉丁人承担了与罗马公民同等甚至更重的兵役负担，却在政治权利上处于明显劣势。这种不平等的伙伴关系虽维持了军事效力，但也埋下了长期不满的种子。
relationship.en: The Latin allied communities were the most important subordinate force in Rome's hegemonic system. After the Latin War (340-338 BCE), the old Latin League was dissolved and individual Latin cities were forced into bilateral treaty relationships with Rome. Latins bore military burdens equal to or greater than Roman citizens, yet held significantly inferior political rights. This unequal partnership maintained military effectiveness but sowed seeds of lasting resentment.

description.zh: 拉丁同盟社群是罗马在意大利扩张中最核心的盟友群体。这些社群包括数十个拉丁殖民地和旧拉丁城市，其居民与罗马人共享语言、宗教传统和文化纽带。公元前338年拉丁战争结束后，罗马解散了拉丁同盟的集体组织，将其改造为以罗马为中心的双边条约网络。拉丁人保留了商法权（ius commercii）和通婚权（ius conubii），但失去了集体决策权和完整的罗马公民身份。他们在每次战争中提供大量步兵和骑兵，数量往往与罗马军团相当甚至更多，是罗马军事霸权不可或缺的支柱。然而拉丁社群对于不平等待遇的不满日益积累，最终将在公元前91年的同盟者战争中全面爆发。在公元前323年，这种制度虽仍在有效运作，但拉丁人对更平等地位的渴望已是一个持续的政治暗流。
description.en: The Latin allied communities were the most critical allied group in Rome's Italian expansion. These communities comprised dozens of Latin colonies and old Latin towns whose inhabitants shared language, religious traditions, and cultural bonds with the Romans. After the Latin War ended in 338 BCE, Rome dissolved the Latin League's collective organization and restructured it into a bilateral treaty network centered on Rome. Latins retained commercial rights (ius commercii) and intermarriage rights (ius conubii) but lost collective decision-making power and full Roman citizenship. In every war they supplied large contingents of infantry and cavalry, often matching or exceeding Roman legion numbers, forming an indispensable pillar of Roman military hegemony. Yet Latin resentment at unequal treatment steadily accumulated, ultimately erupting in the Social War of 91 BCE. In 323 BCE the system still functioned effectively, but Latin aspirations for greater equality remained a persistent political undercurrent.

role.zh: 拉丁同盟中最重要的城市之一的代表，维持与罗马的合作关系
role.en: Representatives of one of the most important Latin communities, maintaining cooperative relations with Rome

role.zh: 拉丁战争后被降格为附庸的重要拉丁城市，象征拉丁人的不满情绪
role.en: A major Latin city demoted to subordinate status after the Latin War, symbolizing Latin discontent

ruler.zh: 以塔拉斯等城邦的公民机构、将军与年度执政官为主
ruler.en: Civic institutions, strategoi, and annually elected magistrates in cities such as Taras

rulerTitle.zh: 将军、执政官与公民大会官员

governmentForm.zh: 由多个希腊城邦组成的寡头—公民混合政体，部分城市保留僭主传统
governmentForm.en: A multi-polis oligarchic-civic system in which Greek city-states were governed by mixed oligarchic and citizen institutions, with some cities retaining tyrannical traditions

socialStructure.zh: 社会由公民、侨民、自由民、乡村居民、工匠、商人、奴隶与意大利本地附属群体构成；政治权力主要集中于拥有土地、舰船与重装步兵装备的公民阶层
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of citizens, resident foreigners, freedpersons, rural populations, artisans, merchants, slaves, and subordinate Italic groups; political power lay chiefly with citizen strata able to furnish land, ships, and hoplite equipment

rulingClass.zh: 城邦公民精英、地主家族、商人船主与高级军政官员
rulingClass.en: Citizen elites, landed families, merchant-shipowners, and senior military-civic officeholders

succession.zh: 无统一世袭继承；各城邦通过选举、轮任、贵族协商或非常时期拥立强人掌权
succession.en: There was no unified hereditary succession; individual poleis used election, rotation, aristocratic negotiation, or in exceptional times the elevation of strongmen

structure.zh: 大希腊并非统一国家，而是以塔拉斯、叙拉古、克罗顿、列吉翁、洛克里等希腊城邦为核心的区域文明网络。各城邦通常设公民大会、议事会、执政官、将军和财政官，战时可能集中于少数统帅或僭主式人物手中。
structure.en: Magna Graecia was not a unitary state but a regional civilization centered on Greek poleis such as Taras, Syracuse, Croton, Rhegion, and Locri. Individual cities typically maintained a citizens' assembly, council, magistrates, generals, and financial officials, while wartime authority could become concentrated in a few commanders or tyrannical figures.

function.zh: 决定战争、同盟、法律、殖民、授予公民权及重要财政事项
function.en: Decided war, alliances, laws, colonization, grants of citizenship, and major fiscal matters

function.zh: 统率陆海军、组织征召、守备港口并处理对外军事行动
function.en: Commanded land and naval forces, organized levies, guarded ports, and managed external military operations

function.zh: 管理关税、港口税、公共地产收益、战争基金与银币铸造
function.en: Managed customs duties, harbor taxes, public land revenue, war funds, and silver coinage

function.zh: 主持法庭、仲裁商业纠纷、维护城邦秩序并执行罚金
function.en: Oversaw courts, arbitrated commercial disputes, maintained civic order, and enforced fines

function.zh: 管理码头、仓储、度量衡、船只登记与外来商人事务
function.en: Managed quays, storage, weights and measures, ship registration, and affairs concerning foreign merchants

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理依附各城邦及其乡村腹地展开。城市内分部族、区或公民登记单位管理；城外则通过附属乡镇、神庙地产、殖民据点与同盟社区实施影响。大希腊整体上缺乏统一常设官僚体系。
localAdmin.en: Local administration operated through each polis and its surrounding chora. Within cities, tribes, districts, or citizen registration units handled governance; outside them, influence was exercised through dependent towns, temple estates, colonial outposts, and allied communities. Magna Graecia as a whole lacked a unified permanent bureaucracy.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以希腊城邦成文法、习惯法与公民陪审审理为基础，并在商贸事务中高度重视契约、债务、海运争议及财产界定。不同城邦法制并不完全一致。
legalSystem.en: The legal system rested on Greek civic written law, customary practice, and citizen adjudication, with strong emphasis in commercial matters on contracts, debt, maritime disputes, and property definition. Legal practice varied from polis to polis.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政主要依靠港口关税、贸易税、市场费、公共土地收益、矿场与盐场收益、非常时期财产征敛，以及同盟贡赋或附属社区供给。平时对公民直接税常较有限，战争时期则增加临时征收。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue depended chiefly on harbor customs, trade duties, market fees, income from public lands, mines and saltworks, extraordinary property levies in emergencies, and tribute or provisions from allied and dependent communities. Direct taxation on citizens was often limited in peacetime but increased during war.

religion.zh: 信奉希腊多神教，崇拜雅典娜、阿波罗、赫拉、阿耳忒弥斯、得墨忒耳、狄俄尼索斯与地方英雄，同时与意大利本地和西西里宗教传统有所交融。
religion.en: Religion was Greek polytheism, venerating Athena, Apollo, Hera, Artemis, Demeter, and Dionysus along with local heroes, while also showing interaction with native Italic and Sicilian religious traditions.

philosophy.zh: 大希腊是希腊哲学重要区域，延续毕达哥拉斯学派、自然哲学、政治思辨与修辞教育传统。到前323年，经典希腊思想与新兴希腊化世界的实用政治文化并存。
philosophy.en: Magna Graecia was a major zone of Greek philosophy, carrying forward Pythagorean traditions, natural philosophy, political reflection, and rhetorical education. By 323 BCE, classical Greek thought coexisted with the pragmatic political culture of the emerging Hellenistic world.

writingSystem.zh: 使用希腊字母的西希腊地方变体，铭文、法令、商业记载与铸币铭文广泛采用希腊文。
writingSystem.en: The civilization used western local variants of the Greek alphabet, and Greek was widely employed in inscriptions, decrees, commercial records, and coin legends.

culturalAchievements.zh: 以神庙建筑、陶器绘饰、青铜工艺、戏剧传播、竞技文化、城市规划、铸币艺术和哲学传统著称；塔拉斯陶器、南意大利红绘陶与各城宗教建筑尤为重要。
culturalAchievements.en: It was known for temple architecture, decorated ceramics, bronze craftsmanship, the transmission of drama, athletic culture, urban planning, coin artistry, and philosophical traditions; Tarentine wares, South Italian red-figure pottery, and civic sanctuaries were especially notable.

languageFamily.zh: 印欧语系希腊语支，兼有与奥斯坎语、墨萨庇语等本地语言长期接触
languageFamily.en: Indo-European, Greek branch, with long contact alongside local languages such as Oscan and Messapic

gdpDescription.zh: 经济高度商业化，以港口贸易、粮食与葡萄酒生产、陶器制造、渔盐业、金属加工和海运服务为核心。按古代标准属富庶地区，但财富分布极不均衡，且城邦之间差异显著。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was highly commercialized, centered on port trade, grain and wine production, pottery manufacture, fisheries and salt, metalworking, and maritime services. By ancient standards it was a prosperous region, though wealth was very unevenly distributed and differed markedly from one polis to another.

mainIndustries.zh: 农业、葡萄酒与橄榄油生产、陶器、青铜与铁器加工、造船、港口贸易、渔业、盐业与纺织
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture, wine and olive oil production, pottery, bronze and iron working, shipbuilding, port commerce, fishing, salt production, and textiles

tradeGoods.zh: 谷物、葡萄酒、橄榄油、陶器、金属器、木材制品、盐、鱼制品、奴隶与奢侈工艺品
tradeGoods.en: Grain, wine, olive oil, pottery, metal goods, timber products, salt, processed fish, slaves, and luxury crafts

name.zh: 希腊银币与地方城邦铸币
name.en: Greek silver coinage and local civic issues

householdWealth.zh: 城市精英家庭拥有地产、奴隶、船只和放贷资本，普通公民则多依赖小地产、渔业、手工业和军役收入；奴隶与无产者财产有限。
householdWealth.en: Elite urban households held land, slaves, ships, and lending capital, while ordinary citizens relied more on small holdings, fishing, crafts, and military service income; slaves and the propertyless possessed little or no wealth.

economicSystem.zh: 以城邦市场经济、奴隶劳动、海上贸易和公民土地所有为基础的混合经济；国家干预主要体现在港务、铸币、战争供给和关键粮食安全上。
economicSystem.en: The economy was a mixed polis market system based on slave labor, maritime trade, and citizen landholding; state intervention was concentrated in harbors, coinage, war supply, and the safeguarding of key grain provision.

source.zh: 市场费、司法罚金与杂项收入
source.en: Market fees, judicial fines, and miscellaneous income

source.zh: 盟邦贡赋与附属社区供给折算
source.en: Tribute and assessed provisions from allied or dependent communities

source.zh: 非常时期财产征敛与战时筹款
source.en: Extraordinary property levies and wartime fundraising

category.zh: 公共工程、港口与城墙维护
category.en: Public works, harbors, and wall maintenance

category.zh: 祭祀、节庆与 civic 补贴

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库并非统一大希腊金库，而是以塔拉斯等主要城邦的现金储备、神庙财富、金银器与紧急征发能力构成。稳定状态下足以维持港防和短期战争，但若同时面对罗马、萨姆奈特或迦太基压力则会迅速吃紧。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was not a single Magna Graecian central reserve, but rather the combined ready cash, temple wealth, bullion objects, and emergency levy capacity of major cities such as Taras. In stable conditions this was sufficient for harbor defense and short wars, but simultaneous pressure from Rome, the Samnites, or Carthage would strain it quickly.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策总体偏审慎，重视保留银币储备、维护贸易通道和在危机时动员富裕公民承担临时负担。城邦倾向于在和平期依赖商业税收，在战争期扩大征敛与借贷。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was generally cautious, emphasizing the maintenance of silver reserves, protection of trade routes, and the mobilization of wealthy citizens for temporary burdens in crises. Poleis tended to rely on commercial revenues in peace and expand levies and borrowing in war.

description.zh: 由公民重装步兵、较轻装步兵及守城民兵组成，是城邦陆战核心，装备质量取决于城市财力与公民财富。
description.en: Composed of citizen hoplites, lighter infantry, and garrison militia, this was the core of land warfare; equipment quality depended on urban finances and citizen wealth.

description.zh: 塔拉斯以轻骑兵著称，其机动侦察、袭扰和掩护能力在希腊世界享有声誉，并影响后世“塔兰廷骑兵”用法。
description.en: Taras was famous for its light cavalry, whose mobility, scouting, skirmishing, and screening abilities were renowned across the Greek world and informed the later concept of 'Tarentine cavalry'.

description.zh: 各主要港口维持三列桨战船及较轻舰只，承担护航、反海盗、封锁与近岸作战任务，是大希腊自保最关键的军种。
description.en: Major ports maintained triremes and lighter vessels for escort, anti-piracy, blockade, and coastal operations, making the navy the most critical branch for Magna Graecian self-defense.

description.zh: 包括意大利本地步兵、弓兵、投石兵及临时受雇希腊战士，通常在战争升级时补充兵力。
description.en: Included local Italic infantry, archers, slingers, and temporarily hired Greek fighters, typically used to reinforce manpower when wars escalated.

commanderInChief.zh: 无统一总司令；战时由塔拉斯等主要城邦的将军会议或首席将军统领
commanderInChief.en: No single supreme commander; in wartime leadership fell to councils of strategoi or a leading general from major cities such as Taras

technology.zh: 军事技术属晚期古典至早期希腊化水平，拥有成熟的重装步兵装备、攻城器械基础、三列桨舰战术、铸造青铜武具和较发达的港防工事。
technology.en: Military technology stood at a late Classical to early Hellenistic level, with mature hoplite equipment, basic siege machinery, trireme tactics, bronze arms production, and relatively developed harbor fortifications.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自意大利半岛内陆强权，尤其是罗马扩张与萨姆奈特压力；西西里方向则长期需警惕迦太基势力及岛内城邦冲突。内部方面，城邦分裂、派系斗争与对雇佣兵依赖削弱共同防务。
threats.en: The chief threats came from inland powers in Italy, especially Roman expansion and Samnite pressure; from the Sicilian direction, Carthaginian power and inter-polis conflicts remained long-term concerns. Internally, division among the cities, factionalism, and dependence on mercenaries weakened collective defense.

populationDescription.zh: 人口包括南意大利与西西里希腊城邦居民、乡村农民、侨民商人、奴隶及与希腊城邦密切往来的意大利本地群体。塔拉斯是最重要中心之一，但人口并不集中于单一都市。
populationDescription.en: The population included residents of Greek cities in southern Italy and Sicily, rural farmers, resident foreign merchants, slaves, and local Italic groups closely connected with the Greek poleis. Taras was among the most important centers, but population was not concentrated in a single metropolis.

socialClasses.zh: 上层为公民贵族、地主与富商船主；中层为小地产公民、工匠、店主与桨手；下层包括雇工、无产者、外来侨民中较贫困者与大量奴隶。另有与希腊城邦通婚、从军或纳贡的意大利本地社群。
socialClasses.en: The upper stratum consisted of citizen aristocrats, landowners, and wealthy merchant-shipowners; the middle included smallholding citizens, artisans, shopkeepers, and rowers; the lower comprised laborers, the propertyless, poorer resident foreigners, and many slaves. There were also local Italic communities linked through intermarriage, military service, or tribute.

allies.zh: 各城邦联盟关系流动不定，常与其他希腊城邦、部分意大利部族及在需要时的希腊化君主建立临时合作；塔拉斯尤其倾向寻求外部希腊军事援助以平衡意大利内陆强权。
allies.en: Alliances among the cities were fluid, often involving other Greek poleis, some Italic tribes, and when necessary Hellenistic rulers in temporary cooperation; Taras in particular tended to seek external Greek military support to balance inland Italic powers.

enemies.zh: 罗马文明、部分萨姆奈特或其他意大利部族竞争者、迦太基在西西里的扩张利益，以及彼此敌对的希腊城邦派系
enemies.en: Roman civilization, certain Samnite and other Italic rivals, Carthaginian expansionary interests in Sicily, and hostile factions among the Greek cities themselves

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以维持海上贸易、安全港口、阻止内陆势力吞并和在希腊世界中维持自治声望为核心。由于缺乏统一中央，外交多为各城邦自行主导，区域合作时常受派系竞争限制。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on preserving maritime trade, securing ports, preventing annexation by inland powers, and maintaining autonomous prestige within the Greek world. Because there was no unified center, diplomacy was largely directed by individual poleis, and regional cooperation was often constrained by factional rivalry.

era.zh: 希腊化时期早期
era.en: Early Hellenistic Period

keyInnovations.zh: 成熟的三列桨舰海战体系、发达的港口与城墙工程、标准化铸币、南意大利红绘陶工艺、改良骑兵战术以及毕达哥拉斯传统影响下的数学与音乐理论传播
keyInnovations.en: Key innovations included mature trireme warfare systems, developed harbor and wall engineering, standardized coinage, South Italian red-figure ceramic production, refined cavalry tactics, and the transmission of mathematical and musical theory under the influence of Pythagorean traditions

strengths.zh: 海洋贸易发达、城市文化繁荣、港口网络密集、铸币与商业制度成熟、骑兵与海军有特色，并与更广阔的希腊世界保持紧密文化联系。
strengths.en: Its strengths were advanced maritime trade, flourishing urban culture, dense port networks, mature coinage and commercial institutions, distinctive cavalry and naval capabilities, and close cultural ties to the wider Greek world.

weaknesses.zh: 缺乏统一政权、城邦间内耗严重、陆上纵深有限、易受罗马等内陆强国威胁，且战时常需依赖雇佣兵和外来援军。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses were the lack of political unity, severe inter-city rivalry, limited inland depth, vulnerability to inland powers such as Rome, and frequent wartime reliance on mercenaries and outside aid.

outlook.zh: 前323年时大体稳定且仍具财富与文化活力，但长期前景取决于能否协调城邦共同防务。若无法形成更稳固联盟，随着罗马在意大利的扩张推进，大希腊诸城的自治空间将逐步收缩。
outlook.en: In 323 BCE the region was broadly stable and still wealthy and culturally dynamic, but its long-term outlook depended on whether the cities could coordinate common defense. Without a stronger federation, the autonomy of the Magna Graecian poleis would gradually contract as Roman expansion advanced in Italy.

description.zh: 南意与西西里希腊殖民城邦仍保持活跃的海上与商业文化。
description.en: The Greek colonial cities of southern Italy and Sicily retained vibrant maritime and commercial cultures.

ruler.zh: 迦太基元老院与苏费特

governmentForm.zh: 由寡头贵族主导的共和国，兼具公民机构、元老院与执政官式年度长官制度
governmentForm.en: An oligarchic republic dominated by aristocratic lineages, with civic institutions, a senate, and annually elected chief magistrates

socialStructure.zh: 社会由城邦贵族、富商船主、自由民工匠与农民、利比亚与其他属地居民、雇佣兵与奴隶构成；核心公民权主要集中于迦太基本城及部分腓尼基殖民城市
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of urban aristocrats, wealthy merchants and shipowners, free craftsmen and farmers, Libyan and other subject populations, mercenaries, and slaves; full civic privilege was concentrated mainly in Carthage itself and some Phoenician colonial cities

rulingClass.zh: 以大商人家族、土地贵族和长期控制政务与祭祀职位的旧族为统治阶层
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted of great mercantile families, landed aristocrats, and old lineages that dominated magistracies and priestly offices

succession.zh: 非世袭君主制；苏费特与多数高级官员由公民共同体中的精英按年推举或选举，重大事务受元老院与百人委员会监督
succession.en: Not a hereditary monarchy; the suffetes and most senior magistrates were selected or elected annually from the civic elite, while major affairs were overseen by the senate and the Council of the Hundred

structure.zh: 国家以元老院为政策核心，双苏费特为年度最高民政长官，另有百人委员会负责审计与问责，公民大会在特定议题上发挥作用；海外属地与附庸城市通过总督、驻军指挥官及地方盟约进行间接统治
structure.en: The state was centered on the senate as the main policy body, with two suffetes serving as annual chief civil magistrates, alongside a Council of the Hundred responsible for oversight and accountability; the citizen assembly played a role on certain issues. Overseas territories and dependent cities were governed indirectly through governors, military commanders, and local treaties

function.zh: 制定对外政策、财政大政、战争与和约方针，并协调高级官员任命
function.en: Set foreign policy, major fiscal policy, war and peace strategy, and coordinated senior appointments

function.zh: 主持政务、司法程序与公民会议，执行元老院决议
function.en: Presided over administration, judicial procedures, and civic assemblies, and executed senatorial decisions

function.zh: 监督将领与高级官员，审查财政与战时责任，防止个人权力过大
function.en: Supervised generals and senior officials, reviewed finances and wartime responsibility, and restrained excessive personal power

function.zh: 征收港税、商业税、属地贡赋及农产收入，管理国库与国家付款
function.en: Collected harbor dues, commercial taxes, territorial tribute, and agricultural revenues, and managed the treasury and state payments

function.zh: 管理军港、商港、船坞、舰只建造、航路巡逻与海军动员
function.en: Managed military and commercial harbors, dockyards, ship construction, sea-lane patrols, and naval mobilization

function.zh: 统筹非洲腹地、撒丁、科西嘉、西西里据点与伊比利亚贸易站的驻军、贡赋与盟约
function.en: Coordinated garrisons, tribute, and treaties in the African hinterland, Sardinia, Corsica, Sicilian positions, and Iberian trading stations

function.zh: 保存城邦档案、契约、法令与贸易记录，协助审判与仲裁
function.en: Kept civic archives, contracts, decrees, and trade records, and assisted trials and arbitration

localAdmin.zh: 本土核心区由城邦机构直接管理，周边利比亚农业区和腓尼基殖民城市则依附于税务官、驻军指挥官或地方盟邦精英；海上属地常保持内部自治，但须缴税、供舰或接受迦太基外交节制
localAdmin.en: The core homeland was administered directly through city institutions, while surrounding Libyan agricultural zones and Phoenician colonial cities were tied to tax officials, garrison commanders, or allied local elites; overseas possessions often retained internal autonomy but paid dues, supplied ships, or accepted Carthaginian diplomatic control

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系结合腓尼基习惯法、商法、契约法与城邦判例，重视财产、航运、债务、合伙与贸易仲裁；高级官员受问责机制约束
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined Phoenician customary law, commercial law, contract law, and civic precedent, with strong emphasis on property, shipping, debt, partnership, and trade arbitration; senior magistrates were subject to accountability mechanisms

taxationSystem.zh: 财政主要依赖港口关税、贸易税、属地贡赋、农业租税与战时特别征发；利比亚农民与属地人口承担较重赋税，而本城富裕家族也通过船队、公共工程和军费分摊承担义务
taxationSystem.en: Revenue depended chiefly on port customs, trade taxes, provincial tribute, agricultural rents and taxes, and extraordinary wartime levies; Libyan cultivators and subject populations bore heavy taxation, while wealthy urban families also contributed through fleets, public works, and military financing

religion.zh: 以迦南—腓尼基多神信仰为主，崇拜巴力·哈蒙与塔尼特等神祇，并融合北非本土与地中海其他地区宗教元素；神庙、祭司与公共祭祀在国家生活中地位重要
religion.en: Religion was predominantly Canaanite-Phoenician polytheism, centered on deities such as Baal Hammon and Tanit, while incorporating North African and wider Mediterranean religious elements; temples, priests, and public rites held an important place in state life

philosophy.zh: 迦太基并非以哲学学派著称，但政治文化强调务实、商贸理性、家族荣誉、航海经验与对传统法度的尊重；同时受希腊思想圈的间接影响
philosophy.en: Carthage was not famous for formal philosophical schools, but its political culture emphasized pragmatism, mercantile rationality, family honor, seafaring experience, and respect for inherited norms; it was also indirectly influenced by the Greek intellectual world

writingSystem.zh: 使用腓尼基字母体系的布匿文书写，主要用于行政、碑铭、宗教奉献与商业记录；希腊语在对外贸易和外交接触中亦有实际用途
writingSystem.en: The Punic form of the Phoenician alphabet was used for administration, inscriptions, religious dedications, and commercial records; Greek was also practically useful in foreign trade and diplomacy

culturalAchievements.zh: 在远航、造船、港口工程、农业组织、商业网络经营和跨文化贸易方面成就突出；迦太基军港、商港及其双港布局是古代地中海最先进的海事设施之一
culturalAchievements.en: Carthage excelled in long-distance navigation, shipbuilding, harbor engineering, agricultural organization, commercial networking, and cross-cultural trade; its military and commercial dual-harbor complex was one of the most advanced maritime installations in the ancient Mediterranean

languageFamily.zh: 闪米特语系西北支，以布匿语为主，并广泛接触利比亚诸语言、希腊语和伊比利亚地方语言
languageFamily.en: Northwest Semitic, with Punic as the principal language, in broad contact with Libyan languages, Greek, and local Iberian tongues

unit.en: silver drachma equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 作为西地中海最发达的海上商业国家之一，迦太基经济高度多元，兼具农业腹地、手工业作坊、属地贡赋和远程贸易利润。其总量在古代属于第一梯队，但财富分配极不均衡，商业与国家财政紧密相连
gdpDescription.en: As one of the most developed maritime commercial states in the western Mediterranean, Carthage had a highly diversified economy combining an agricultural hinterland, workshops, provincial tribute, and profits from long-distance trade. Its aggregate output ranked among the foremost in the ancient world, though wealth distribution was highly unequal and commerce was closely tied to state finance

mainIndustries.zh: 海运贸易、造船、港口服务、金属转运、农业种植、橄榄油与葡萄酒生产、紫染与纺织、陶器、木材加工、奴隶贸易与雇佣兵招募中介
mainIndustries.en: Maritime trade, shipbuilding, harbor services, metal redistribution, agriculture, olive oil and wine production, purple dye and textiles, pottery, timber processing, slave trading, and mercenary recruitment brokerage

tradeGoods.zh: 白银、铜、铅、锡、金、象牙、奴隶、盐、谷物、橄榄油、葡萄酒、腌鱼、木材、布匹、染料、陶器、奢侈品与再出口的东方商品
tradeGoods.en: Silver, copper, lead, tin, gold, ivory, slaves, salt, grain, olive oil, wine, preserved fish, timber, textiles, dyes, pottery, luxury goods, and re-exported eastern products

name.zh: 布匿银币与金银衡重体系

householdWealth.zh: 本城贵族与大商人拥有极高财富，常兼有船队、地产与海外投资；普通自由民生活水平中等，属地农民负担较重，奴隶与雇佣兵收入不稳定
householdWealth.en: Urban aristocrats and major merchants possessed very high wealth, often combining fleets, estates, and overseas investments; ordinary free citizens had moderate living standards, subject farmers bore heavier burdens, and slaves and mercenaries had unstable incomes

unit.en: silver drachma equivalent per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 以市场交换和商业资本为核心、由国家保护海权和通商据点的寡头商业经济；农业、贡赋和海外贸易共同支撑国家实力
economicSystem.en: An oligarchic commercial economy centered on market exchange and mercantile capital, with the state protecting naval power and trading stations; agriculture, tribute, and overseas trade jointly sustained state power

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库较为充裕，得益于长期海贸利润、属地贡赋和谨慎的寡头财政管理；但其稳定性取决于海上航路安全、属地服从和雇佣兵薪饷的持续支付
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was relatively strong, benefiting from long-term maritime profits, provincial tribute, and cautious oligarchic fiscal management; however, its stability depended on secure sea lanes, obedient territories, and continuous payment of mercenary wages

fiscalPolicy.zh: 总体上采取保守而重商的财政政策，优先维持舰队、商路和战略据点，并避免过度动摇本城商业精英利益；在危机时则倾向加强贡赋与临时征发
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was generally conservative and mercantile, prioritizing fleets, trade routes, and strategic strongpoints while avoiding excessive disruption to the interests of urban commercial elites; in crisis it tended toward increased tribute and extraordinary levies

description.zh: 国家军力核心，由桨帆战舰船员、陆战队和港务后勤组成，能够保护商路、封锁海峡并快速投送兵力至西西里、撒丁与伊比利亚沿岸
description.en: The core of state power, consisting of war-galley crews, marines, and harbor logistics personnel, capable of protecting trade routes, blockading straits, and rapidly projecting forces to Sicily, Sardinia, and the Iberian coast

description.zh: 由迦太基本城公民部队、利比亚步兵及驻守非洲腹地和城防体系的常备部队组成，负责首都安全与税赋控制
description.en: Composed of Carthaginian citizen contingents, Libyan infantry, and standing troops guarding the African hinterland and defensive system, responsible for capital security and tax control

description.zh: 包括伊比利亚剑士、巴利阿里投石手、努米底亚骑兵、凯尔特战士和希腊化世界招募兵，是迦太基远征和岛屿战争的重要力量
description.en: Included Iberian swordsmen, Balearic slingers, Numidian cavalry, Celtic warriors, and recruits from the Hellenistic world, forming a crucial arm for expeditions and island warfare

description.zh: 由附庸城邦、盟友提供的舰船、守军和临时征发部队构成，用于补充战时消耗与要地防守
description.en: Consisted of ships, garrisons, and temporary levies supplied by dependent cities and allies, used to cover wartime losses and defend key positions

commanderInChief.zh: 元老院任命的将军与海军统帅，受苏费特与百人委员会监督
commanderInChief.en: Generals and naval commanders appointed by the senate, under the oversight of the suffetes and the Council of the Hundred

technology.zh: 海军技术处于古代世界前列，擅长多列桨战舰建造、军港设施、航海组织与海上补给；陆军装备多样，依赖各族雇佣兵专长，常使用长矛步兵、骑兵、投石兵和攻城器械
technology.en: Naval technology ranked among the best in the ancient world, excelling in the construction of multibanked galleys, military harbor facilities, navigational organization, and maritime supply; land forces were diverse and relied on the specialties of various mercenary peoples, commonly using spear infantry, cavalry, slingers, and siege equipment

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自西西里希腊城邦的竞争、意大利中部新兴罗马势力、属地叛乱、雇佣兵忠诚不足，以及海上贸易线遭受敌对封锁或劫掠
threats.en: Major threats came from rivalry with the Greek cities of Sicily, the rising Roman power in central Italy, provincial revolts, uncertain mercenary loyalty, and the danger of hostile blockades or raids against maritime trade lines

populationDescription.zh: 总人口包括迦太基本城公民、北非腓尼基殖民城市居民、利比亚农业人口、撒丁与科西嘉居民、西西里据点人口、伊比利亚贸易据点人口以及大量非公民属民。真正享有完整迦太基公民权者仅占少数
populationDescription.en: The total population included citizens of Carthage proper, inhabitants of North African Phoenician colonial cities, Libyan agricultural populations, residents of Sardinia and Corsica, people in Sicilian strongholds, populations at Iberian trading stations, and large numbers of non-citizen subjects. Those enjoying full Carthaginian civic rights formed only a minority

name.zh: 莫提亚遗址周边与西西里布匿据点
name.en: Mothya region and Punic Sicilian strongholds

socialClasses.zh: 上层为元老贵族、祭司家族和大商人；中层为船主、工匠、商贩和自由农民；下层为属地农民、港口劳工、契约雇工、雇佣兵及奴隶。族群和法律身份差异常与阶层紧密重合
socialClasses.en: The upper stratum consisted of senatorial aristocrats, priestly families, and great merchants; the middle included shipowners, artisans, traders, and free farmers; the lower included subject cultivators, port laborers, hired workers, mercenaries, and slaves. Ethnic and legal-status distinctions often overlapped closely with class divisions

allies.zh: 与部分腓尼基殖民城市、北非地方酋邦、努米底亚部族首领及若干伊比利亚沿海社群保持盟约或互利关系；在希腊化世界中亦维持务实贸易外交
allies.en: Carthage maintained alliances or mutually beneficial ties with certain Phoenician colonial cities, North African local chieftains, Numidian tribal leaders, and some Iberian coastal communities; it also pursued pragmatic commercial diplomacy with the Hellenistic world

enemies.zh: 在西西里长期与希腊城邦处于竞争和间歇战争状态，并密切警惕意大利中部罗马势力的扩张；部分属地与被征服部族也是潜在敌对力量
enemies.en: It remained in long-standing competition and intermittent warfare with the Greek cities of Sicily, while closely watching the expansion of Roman power in central Italy; some subject territories and conquered tribal groups were also potential enemies

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以保护西地中海贸易网络、控制海峡与港口、维持岛屿据点、避免东方强权直接介入西部海域为核心；更偏好通过条约、金钱、代理盟友和海军威慑达成目标，而非持续的大规模本土征服
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy was centered on protecting the western Mediterranean trade network, controlling straits and ports, maintaining island strongholds, and preventing direct eastern great-power intervention in western waters; it generally preferred treaties, money, proxy allies, and naval deterrence over sustained large-scale territorial conquest in the homeland

era.zh: 希腊化时期
era.en: Hellenistic Period

keyInnovations.zh: 双港体系与圆形军港设计、多列桨战舰建造、远程航海组织、港务后勤、北非集约农业管理、贸易会计与跨海商路调度
keyInnovations.en: Dual-harbor systems and circular military harbor design, construction of multibanked war galleys, long-distance navigation management, harbor logistics, intensive North African agricultural management, commercial accounting, and transmarine trade-route coordination

strengths.zh: 迦太基的最大优势在于海权、财富、商业网络和灵活的雇佣兵体系。其首都位置优越，能够联结北非、西西里、撒丁、伊比利亚与更远的贸易区；国库充实，海军强大，政体虽寡头化但决策经验丰富
strengths.en: Carthage’s greatest strengths lay in sea power, wealth, commercial networks, and a flexible mercenary system. Its capital occupied an excellent strategic position linking North Africa, Sicily, Sardinia, Iberia, and more distant trading zones; the treasury was strong, the navy powerful, and the polity, though oligarchic, possessed considerable governing experience

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点在于公民基础较窄，对属地贡赋和外籍雇佣兵依赖过深，陆上统合能力不如海上控制力；一旦战争拖长、薪饷不足或属地反叛，国家承受能力会迅速下降
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses lay in a narrow citizen base, deep dependence on provincial tribute and foreign mercenaries, and weaker territorial integration on land than control at sea; if wars became prolonged, wages went unpaid, or provinces revolted, the state’s resilience could deteriorate rapidly

outlook.zh: 公元前323年的迦太基仍处于繁荣与强盛阶段，短期内将继续主导西地中海贸易与海军均势。但随着罗马在意大利的扩张、西西里竞争持续以及属地治理压力上升，未来数十年其将面临越来越严峻的战略挑战
outlook.en: In 323 BCE Carthage remained prosperous and powerful, and in the near term would continue to dominate western Mediterranean trade and naval equilibrium. Yet with Rome expanding in Italy, competition in Sicily continuing, and pressures of provincial control increasing, it would face mounting strategic challenges in the decades ahead

description.zh: 控制北非沿海、西地中海贸易与若干属地的海上强权。
description.en: A maritime power controlling the North African coast, western Mediterranean trade, and several dependent territories.

ideology.zh: 军事扩张主义——主张通过积极的陆上军事行动扩大迦太基势力范围，尤其在伊比利亚建立领土基础，以对抗罗马的崛起
ideology.en: Military expansionism — advocating aggressive land-based military operations to extend Carthaginian power, particularly establishing a territorial base in Iberia to counter Rome's rise

headquarters.zh: 迦太基（后转至伊比利亚新迦太基城）
headquarters.en: Carthage (later shifting to New Carthage in Iberia)

relationship.zh: 巴卡家族与元老院主导的和平派长期对立。他们主张以军事力量而非纯粹商业手段确保迦太基的地中海霸权，通过在伊比利亚建立半独立的军事领地积累战争资源，准备与罗马的最终对决。
relationship.en: The Barcid family was in long-standing rivalry with the Senate-led peace faction. They advocated securing Carthaginian Mediterranean hegemony through military force rather than purely commercial means, building a semi-independent military domain in Iberia to accumulate war resources for the eventual confrontation with Rome.

description.zh: 巴卡家族是迦太基历史上最杰出的军事世家。哈米尔卡·巴卡在第一次布匿战争中展现了卓越的军事才能，但战后因元老院未能及时支付雇佣兵薪饷而爆发的残酷雇佣兵战争使他对国内寡头政治深感失望。哈米尔卡将目光转向伊比利亚，率军征服半岛南部和东部大片区域，利用当地丰富的银矿和人力资源建立了一个事实上的巴卡家族领地。他的伊比利亚战略不仅是为了弥补迦太基在第一次布匿战争中的领土损失，更是在为与罗马的第二次较量积蓄力量。哈米尔卡据传曾让年幼的汉尼拔在祭坛前宣誓永远仇视罗马，这一传说深刻揭示了巴卡家族的战略执念。
description.en: The Barcid family was the most distinguished military dynasty in Carthaginian history. Hamilcar Barca demonstrated exceptional military talent during the First Punic War, but the brutal Mercenary War that erupted afterward due to the Senate's failure to pay mercenary wages left him deeply disillusioned with domestic oligarchic politics. Hamilcar turned his attention to Iberia, leading campaigns that conquered vast stretches of the southern and eastern peninsula, exploiting local silver mines and manpower to build what was effectively a Barcid family domain. His Iberian strategy aimed not merely to compensate for Carthage's territorial losses in the First Punic War but to accumulate strength for a second confrontation with Rome. Hamilcar reportedly made the young Hannibal swear eternal enmity against Rome at the altar — a tradition that vividly reveals the Barcid family's strategic obsession.

title.zh: 哈米尔卡之婿与继任指挥官

role.zh: 哈米尔卡死后接管伊比利亚军事行动，建立新迦太基城
role.en: Took over Iberian military operations after Hamilcar's death, founded New Carthage

role.zh: 未来将成为古代最伟大的军事统帅之一，此时随父在伊比利亚受训
role.en: Would become one of the greatest military commanders of antiquity, currently training with his father in Iberia

ideology.zh: 商业寡头和平主义——优先以贸易、外交与财富积累而非军事冒险维护迦太基利益，主张与罗马共处并开发非洲腹地
ideology.en: Commercial oligarchic pacifism — prioritizing trade, diplomacy, and wealth accumulation over military adventurism, advocating coexistence with Rome and development of the African hinterland

relationship.zh: 汉诺派系控制着元老院多数席位，主导迦太基国家政策。他们将巴卡家族在伊比利亚的扩张视为危险的军事冒险，担心会引发与罗马的新战争。汉诺主张巩固非洲领地并通过商业手段维持影响力。
relationship.en: Hanno's faction controlled the majority of Senate seats and dominated Carthaginian state policy. They viewed the Barcid expansion in Iberia as a dangerous military adventure that risked provoking a new war with Rome. Hanno advocated consolidating African territories and maintaining influence through commercial means.

description.zh: 汉诺大帝是迦太基元老院中最有权势的政治人物，代表了城市商业寡头阶层的核心利益。他坚信迦太基的繁荣应建立在海上贸易与非洲农业开发之上，而非代价高昂的军事远征。在第一次布匿战争的惨痛教训后，汉诺派系极力避免与罗马的新冲突，认为迦太基应利用和平时期积累财富、发展非洲腹地并巩固地中海贸易网络。汉诺与巴卡家族的对立不仅是政策之争，更反映了迦太基社会中商业精英与军事贵族之间的根本矛盾。他多次在元老院阻止对伊比利亚巴卡军队的资金拨付，试图限制哈米尔卡的独立行动能力。
description.en: Hanno the Great was the most powerful political figure in the Carthaginian Senate, representing the core interests of the city's commercial oligarchy. He firmly believed Carthage's prosperity should rest on maritime trade and African agricultural development rather than costly military expeditions. After the painful lessons of the First Punic War, Hanno's faction strove to avoid new conflict with Rome, arguing that Carthage should use peacetime to accumulate wealth, develop the African hinterland, and consolidate its Mediterranean trade network. The rivalry between Hanno and the Barcids was not merely a policy dispute but reflected the fundamental tension between commercial elites and military aristocrats in Carthaginian society. He repeatedly blocked Senate funding for the Barcid army in Iberia, seeking to limit Hamilcar's capacity for independent action.

role.zh: 反巴卡派系领导人，主导迦太基和平与商业政策
role.en: Leader of the anti-Barcid faction, directing Carthage's peace and commercial policies

ideology.zh: 腓尼基商业共和传统——维护迦太基城邦的宪制平衡，在军事派与和平派之间充当调停力量，确保国家决策服务于整体商业利益
ideology.en: Phoenician commercial republican tradition — maintaining Carthage's constitutional balance, mediating between the military and peace factions, and ensuring state decisions served overall commercial interests

relationship.zh: 苏费特作为迦太基年度选举的最高行政官，在巴卡军事派与汉诺和平派之间扮演平衡角色。他们执行元老院决议，主持司法与行政事务，代表了腓尼基商业城邦的古老治理传统。
relationship.en: As Carthage's annually elected chief magistrates, the suffetes played a balancing role between the Barcid military faction and Hanno's peace faction. They implemented senatorial decisions, presided over judicial and administrative affairs, and represented the ancient Phoenician commercial city-state governing tradition.

description.zh: 苏费特制度是迦太基从腓尼基母城继承的核心政治遗产。每年选举产生的两位苏费特相当于罗马的执政官，负责主持国家行政、司法和宗教事务，但不直接指挥军队。苏费特集团代表了迦太基宪制中的稳定力量，在百人委员会的监督和公民大会的参与下运作。他们的存在体现了腓尼基商业文明对制度化治理的重视——国家权力不应集中于单一家族或军事统帅之手。在巴卡派与汉诺派的激烈斗争中，苏费特和百人委员会试图维持某种制度平衡，确保商业航线安全、属地贡赋稳定和城市基础设施的维护。苏费特制度也象征着迦太基与其腓尼基母城推罗的文化联系。
description.en: The suffete system was a core political legacy inherited from Carthage's Phoenician mother cities. The two annually elected suffetes were comparable to Roman consuls, responsible for presiding over state administration, justice, and religious affairs, though they did not directly command the military. The suffete establishment represented the stabilizing force in Carthage's constitution, operating under the oversight of the Council of the Hundred and with participation of the citizen assembly. Their existence embodied Phoenician commercial civilization's emphasis on institutionalized governance — that state power should not be concentrated in any single family or military commander. Amid the fierce struggle between the Barcid and Hanno factions, the suffetes and the Council of the Hundred sought to maintain constitutional balance, ensuring the security of commercial sea lanes, stability of provincial tribute, and maintenance of urban infrastructure. The suffete system also symbolized Carthage's cultural connection to its Phoenician mother city of Tyre.

role.zh: 执行元老院政策，主持司法与公民大会
role.en: Implementing senatorial policy and presiding over courts and civic assemblies

name.en: Members of the Council of the Hundred

role.zh: 监督将领与高级官员，审查财政与军事责任
role.en: Supervising generals and senior officials, reviewing financial and military accountability

name.zh: 伊比利亚诸部族与塔尔特索斯后继政体
伊比利亚人与凯尔特伊比利亚人部族世界

governmentForm.zh: 由部族、氏族贵族、战士首领和设防城镇共同构成的分散式部族政体，部分南部与东部社群受迦太基势力影响
governmentForm.en: A decentralized tribal political order composed of tribes, clan aristocracies, warrior leaders, and fortified towns, with some southern and eastern communities under Carthaginian influence

socialStructure.zh: 社会以部族、氏族和地方共同体为核心，贵族战士阶层居上，自由民农牧者与手工业者为主体，另有依附者、奴隶和战俘
socialStructure.en: Society was centered on tribes, clans, and local communities, with a warrior aristocracy at the top, free farmers, herders, and artisans forming the main body, and dependents, slaves, and captives beneath them

rulingClass.zh: 部族贵族、战士精英、地方酋长与掌控设防聚落的家族集团
rulingClass.en: Tribal nobles, warrior elites, local chieftains, and family groups controlling fortified settlements

succession.zh: 通常在首领家族内部继承，但常须经贵族、战士集会或部族认可；部分地区首领地位更具竞争性而非严格世袭
succession.en: Usually hereditary within leading families, but often requiring recognition by nobles, warrior assemblies, or the tribe; in some regions leadership was more competitive than strictly dynastic

structure.zh: 不存在统一中央政府。各部族和城镇各自治理，事务由酋长、长老议事会、贵族家族及战士集会共同处理；沿海贸易区和南部矿业区在外交与财政上更容易受迦太基代理人或盟约影响
structure.en: There was no unified central government. Each tribe and town governed itself, with affairs managed by chieftains, councils of elders, noble families, and warrior assemblies; coastal trade zones and southern mining districts were more susceptible to Carthaginian agents or treaty influence in diplomacy and finance

name.zh: 酋长家廷与部族议事会

function.zh: 协调盟约、裁决争端、组织战争与分配贡赋
function.en: Coordinated alliances, judged disputes, organized warfare, and allocated tribute

function.zh: 管理设防聚落、储粮、道路关口、防务与地方征收
function.en: Managed fortified settlements, grain storage, road passes, defense, and local collection

function.zh: 主持祭祀、维护圣地、见证誓约与祖先礼仪
function.en: Conducted rites, maintained sacred places, witnessed oaths, and preserved ancestral observances

function.zh: 监管矿区、市场交换、对外商人往来及向强势部族或外邦缴纳的物资
function.en: Oversaw mining zones, market exchange, dealings with foreign merchants, and goods rendered to stronger tribes or external powers

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理以部族领地、设防山城和河谷聚落为单位，由酋长、长老、贵族家族及武装随从实施；行政边界并不固定，往往随婚盟、战争和牧场控制而变化
localAdmin.en: Local administration was organized around tribal territories, fortified hilltowns, and riverine settlements, exercised by chieftains, elders, noble families, and armed retainers; boundaries were not fixed and often shifted with marriage alliances, warfare, and pasture control

legalSystem.zh: 以习惯法为主，强调血亲、赔偿、誓约、客友关系与复仇规范；严重案件可能由长老与贵族裁决，南部受腓尼基—布匿接触地区在商业纠纷中更可能采用书面契约或标准衡量
legalSystem.en: Law was chiefly customary, emphasizing kinship, compensation, oaths, guest-friendship, and norms of vengeance; serious cases might be judged by elders and nobles, while southern zones exposed to Phoenician-Punic contact were more likely to use written agreements or standardized measures in commercial disputes

taxationSystem.zh: 不存在统一全国税制。主要财政形式包括部族贡赋、战利品分配、矿产抽成、关隘收费、牲畜和谷物征收，以及个别地区对迦太基盟约义务的缴纳
taxationSystem.en: There was no unified national tax system. Main fiscal forms included tribal tribute, distribution of war booty, mining shares, tolls at passes, levies in livestock and grain, and in some regions payments due under Carthaginian alliances

religion.zh: 宗教以地方多神崇拜、祖先崇拜、山川泉林圣地祭祀和战神、护城神、生育神崇拜为主；沿海和南部地区还受腓尼基—布匿神祇传统影响
religion.en: Religion was based on local polytheism, ancestor veneration, and sacred rites at hills, rivers, springs, and groves, with cults of war, protection, and fertility deities; coastal and southern regions were also influenced by Phoenician-Punic divine traditions

philosophy.zh: 无类似希腊系统哲学，思想主要体现为部族荣誉、战士伦理、祖先传统、誓约忠诚与地方祭司性知识；与地中海商贸接触地区开始吸收外来观念
philosophy.en: There was no philosophy in the Greek systematic sense; thought was expressed mainly through tribal honor, warrior ethics, ancestral tradition, oath loyalty, and localized priestly knowledge, while regions engaged in Mediterranean trade began to absorb foreign ideas

writingSystem.zh: 文字使用不均。伊比利亚东部和南部存在本地文字系统，如东北伊比利亚文字与东南伊比利亚文字；塔尔特索斯—西南传统在部分地区留下痕迹，而许多凯尔特伊比利亚社群仍主要依赖口传
writingSystem.en: Writing was unevenly distributed. Eastern and southern Iberian areas used local scripts such as the Northeastern and Southeastern Iberian scripts; the Tartessian-Southwestern tradition left traces in some regions, while many Celtiberian communities remained predominantly oral

culturalAchievements.zh: 重要成就包括山城防御建筑、精美金属工艺、铁制武器、雕塑与墓葬纪念物、纺织与陶器传统，以及融合本地与地中海风格的物质文化；著名作品类型包括石雕动物像和贵族武装装备
culturalAchievements.en: Major achievements included hillfort defensive architecture, fine metalworking, iron weaponry, sculpture and funerary monuments, textile and ceramic traditions, and material culture blending local and Mediterranean styles; notable forms included stone animal sculptures and aristocratic martial equipment

languageFamily.zh: 语言多样：伊比利亚语属于前印欧或未定语系，凯尔特伊比利亚语属凯尔特语支，西部与北部还有其他印欧及非印欧地方语言并存
languageFamily.en: The linguistic landscape was diverse: Iberian belonged to a pre-Indo-European or otherwise uncertain family, Celtiberian was Celtic, and other local Indo-European and non-Indo-European languages were also present in the west and north

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以农业、畜牧、矿产和区域交换为基础，整体富庶程度因地区差异极大。南部和东部沿海较多参与地中海贸易，内陆高地则更依赖混合农牧与部族再分配
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on agriculture, pastoralism, mining, and regional exchange, with prosperity varying greatly by region. The south and east coasts participated more actively in Mediterranean trade, while the inland uplands relied more on mixed farming, herding, and tribal redistribution

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物种植、葡萄与橄榄种植的早期发展、畜牧、铁器锻造、青铜与贵金属加工、银铜铅矿开采、陶器、纺织、盐业及地方贸易
mainIndustries.en: Grain cultivation, early viticulture and olive growing, pastoralism, ironworking, bronze and precious-metal craft, extraction of silver, copper, and lead, pottery, textiles, salt production, and local trade

tradeGoods.zh: 出口金属矿产、银、铜、铅、铁器、盐、皮革、羊毛、牲畜、蜂蜜和部分奴隶；进口酒、精陶、奢侈器物、首饰、外来武器样式及地中海商品
tradeGoods.en: Exports included ores, silver, copper, lead, iron goods, salt, leather, wool, livestock, honey, and some slaves; imports included wine, fine pottery, luxury goods, jewelry, foreign weapon styles, and Mediterranean merchandise

unitName.zh: 舍客勒、德拉克马及地方金属值
unitName.en: shekels, drachmae, and local metal values
