householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要体现在土地使用权、牲畜、储粮、武器、金属饰物和亲族网络，而非统一货币储蓄；沿海与矿区精英的财富明显高于内陆普通农牧家庭
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was expressed mainly through access to land, livestock, stored grain, weapons, metal ornaments, and kin ties rather than uniform monetary savings; elites in coastal and mining zones were notably wealthier than ordinary inland farming and herding households

economicSystem.zh: 以部族再分配、家庭生产和区域市场交换并存的混合经济；南部与东部沿海已与迦太基商路和更广泛的西地中海网络相连
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy combining tribal redistribution, household production, and regional market exchange; the south and east coasts were already connected to Carthaginian routes and the wider western Mediterranean network

source.zh: 战利品、盟贡与附属贡献
source.en: Booty, Allied Payments, and Dependent Contributions

category.zh: 酋长家廷、馈赠与盟约维系
category.en: Chieftainly Households, Gift Distribution, and Alliance Maintenance

category.zh: 城寨防务、储粮与地方工程
category.en: Hillfort Defense, Storage, and Local Works

category.zh: 对外贸易使节与外部义务
category.en: External Trade Missions and Foreign Obligations

treasuryDescription.zh: 不存在统一国库。财富以各部族储粮、金属储备、战利品、牲畜和贵族私藏形式分散保存；南部与矿区的金属财富最集中
treasuryDescription.en: There was no unified state treasury. Wealth was held in dispersed form as tribal grain stores, metal reserves, booty, livestock, and aristocratic hoards; metallic wealth was most concentrated in the south and mining regions

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政以维持武装追随者、稳固部族忠诚和确保粮食与矿产控制为核心，通常偏向分散征收与再分配，而非官僚化预算管理
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice focused on maintaining armed followers, securing tribal loyalty, and ensuring control over food and mineral resources, favoring decentralized collection and redistribution rather than bureaucratic budgeting

description.zh: 以持矛、剑、标枪和圆盾的步兵为主，是各部族作战核心，装备与训练水平依地区和财富而异
description.en: Foot warriors armed with spears, swords, javelins, and round shields formed the core of tribal warfare, with equipment and training varying by region and wealth

description.zh: 由贵族和富裕战士组成，机动性强，适合突袭、追击和展示身份，是伊比利亚与凯尔特伊比利亚军事的重要精锐
description.en: Composed of nobles and wealthy warriors, these horsemen were mobile and suited to raiding, pursuit, and status display, forming an important elite arm of Iberian and Celtiberian warfare

description.zh: 包括投石手、标枪手和轻装山地战士，擅长崎岖地形、伏击和骚扰战
description.en: Included slingers, javelin throwers, and lightly equipped hill fighters skilled in rough terrain, ambush, and harassment

description.zh: 负责守卫设防聚落、储藏地和关键通道，多由酋长近随及地方守军组成
description.en: Guarded fortified settlements, storage sites, and key routes, often made up of chieftains' retainers and local garrison forces

commanderInChief.zh: 无统一最高统帅，由各部族酋长在战时分别统率
commanderInChief.en: No unified commander-in-chief; tribal chieftains led their own forces in war

technology.zh: 军事技术以铁制剑矛、标枪、盾牌、锁子甲或皮甲的有限使用、马战传统和山城防御工事为核心；未形成希腊化军队那样的标准化攻城与方阵体系
technology.en: Military technology centered on iron swords and spears, javelins, shields, limited use of mail or leather armor, equestrian traditions, and hillfort defenses; it did not develop the standardized siege and phalanx systems of Hellenistic armies

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自部族间冲突、南部沿海与矿区的迦太基扩张、争夺商路的地方战争，以及未来来自地中海强国更深度介入的风险
threats.en: Main threats came from intertribal conflict, Carthaginian expansion in southern coasts and mining regions, local wars over trade routes, and the longer-term risk of deeper intervention by Mediterranean great powers

populationDescription.zh: 人口分布极不均衡，主要集中于河谷、沿海平原、矿区周边与设防山城网络中；南部和东部人口密度较高，内陆高原与北部山区相对分散
populationDescription.en: Population was very unevenly distributed, concentrated in river valleys, coastal plains, mining belts, and networks of fortified hilltowns; the south and east were more densely settled, while the inland plateau and northern mountains were relatively dispersed

socialClasses.zh: 主要阶层包括部族贵族与骑士、自由战士、农民与牧民、手工业者、商人、依附民、奴隶和战俘；在沿海地区，商贸精英与受外来文化影响的城镇上层更为突出
socialClasses.en: Main social strata included tribal nobles and horsemen, free warriors, farmers and pastoralists, artisans, merchants, dependents, slaves, and war captives; in coastal areas, mercantile elites and urban upper groups influenced by foreign culture were more prominent

allies.zh: 无统一盟友体系；各部族按地缘利益结盟，部分南部与东部社群与迦太基保持贸易或从属关系，另一些内陆部族则维持相对独立
allies.en: There was no unified alliance system; tribes aligned according to local interests, some southern and eastern communities maintained trade or subordinate relations with Carthage, while other inland tribes remained relatively independent

enemies.zh: 无统一共同敌人，但部族间敌对频繁；在受压地区，迦太基的政治与经济渗透也可能被视为威胁
enemies.en: There was no single common enemy, but intertribal hostility was frequent; in pressured areas, Carthaginian political and economic penetration could also be seen as a threat

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策高度地方化，以保持部族自主、争取贸易利益、利用外部强权平衡邻近竞争者为主；沿海社群更主动参与西地中海外交与商业网络
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy was highly localized, aiming to preserve tribal autonomy, secure trade advantages, and use outside powers to balance neighboring rivals; coastal communities were more active participants in western Mediterranean diplomacy and commerce

era.zh: 铁器时代晚期至希腊化时代接触期
era.en: Late Iron Age with Hellenistic Contact

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术包括铁器锻造、山城筑防、贵族骑战、矿石冶炼、地方文字系统、纺织与陶器工艺，以及在沿海吸收的地中海式度量、贸易与航海知识
keyInnovations.en: Key technologies included ironworking, hillfort fortification, aristocratic cavalry warfare, ore smelting, local writing systems, textile and ceramic crafts, and Mediterranean-derived knowledge of weights, trade, and seafaring in coastal zones

strengths.zh: 优势在于地形复杂、部族战士勇悍、骑兵与轻装兵适应性强、矿产资源丰富、地方共同体韧性高，并能吸收地中海贸易与技术影响
strengths.en: Strengths included difficult terrain, hardy tribal warriors, adaptable cavalry and light troops, rich mineral resources, resilient local communities, and an ability to absorb Mediterranean trade and technical influences

weaknesses.zh: 弱点是政治分裂、缺乏统一指挥、财政与行政分散、部族间长期争斗、城市化和制度整合有限，面对组织严密的外来国家时容易被各个击破
weaknesses.en: Weaknesses were political fragmentation, lack of unified command, dispersed finance and administration, chronic intertribal conflict, and limited urban and institutional integration, making the region vulnerable to divide-and-conquer by organized external states

outlook.zh: 323年前后总体稳定，但这种稳定建立在分散均势之上。若迦太基进一步深入控制南部矿区和海岸，或未来有更强大的地中海国家介入，伊比利亚与凯尔特伊比利亚诸部将面临更大整合压力与战争风险
outlook.en: Around 323 BCE the region was broadly stable, but that stability rested on a decentralized balance of power. If Carthage deepened its control over southern mines and coasts, or if stronger Mediterranean states intervened in the future, the Iberian and Celtiberian tribes would face rising pressure toward conflict and political realignment

description.zh: 伊比利亚半岛由多支部族、城邑与地区政权并存，部分受迦太基影响。
description.en: The Iberian Peninsula was divided among tribes, towns, and regional polities, some under Carthaginian influence.

name.zh: 不列颠古凯尔特部族文化

governmentForm.zh: 部族分治的酋邦体系，由多个亲族、战士贵族与宗教权威共同构成政治秩序
governmentForm.en: A decentralized tribal chiefdom system in which multiple kin-groups, warrior aristocracies, and religious authorities together formed political order

socialStructure.zh: 社会以氏族、部族与附庸关系为基础，上层由酋长家族、战士贵族与德鲁伊等宗教人士组成，下层为自由农民、工匠、牧民及一定数量的依附民与奴隶
socialStructure.en: Society was organized around clans, tribes, and client relationships, with chiefdom families, warrior nobles, and religious specialists such as druids at the top, and free farmers, craftsmen, herders, and a smaller number of dependents and slaves below

rulingClass.zh: 部族酋长家族、战士贵族与德鲁伊祭司阶层
rulingClass.en: Tribal chieftain families, warrior nobility, and the druidic priestly class

succession.zh: 通常由酋长家族内部继承或由贵族会议推举，重视血缘、军事威望与追随者支持，缺乏统一成文规则
succession.en: Leadership usually passed within chiefly families or was chosen by assemblies of nobles, emphasizing kinship, military prestige, and follower support, without a single codified rule

structure.zh: 不列颠不存在统一国家机构，政治单位主要是各自独立的部族与丘堡中心；决策由酋长、亲族首领、战士随从与宗教权威协商完成，重大事务常通过部族集会处理
structure.en: Britain had no unified state apparatus; the main political units were autonomous tribes and hillfort centers. Decisions were made through consultation among chieftains, kin leaders, warrior retinues, and religious authorities, while major matters were often handled through tribal assemblies

function.zh: 管理酋长领地、宴飨、贡赋储藏、随从与对外馈赠
function.en: Managed the chieftain's estate, feasting, tribute stores, retainers, and external gift exchange

function.zh: 负责护卫、征战、劫掠、征收贡物与维持酋长威望
function.en: Provided protection, warfare, raiding, tribute collection, and maintenance of chiefly prestige

function.zh: 主持祭祀、口述法律传统、仲裁争端、教育贵族子弟并维护跨部族文化联系
function.en: Oversaw ritual, preserved oral legal tradition, arbitrated disputes, educated elite youth, and sustained inter-tribal cultural links

function.zh: 管理丘堡防务、谷仓、畜群集散和手工业作坊
function.en: Managed hillfort defense, granaries, livestock movement, and craft workshops

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理依托氏族、村落、农庄与丘堡网络进行，各部族核心区域由酋长亲族和附庸贵族控制，边缘地区则依赖盟约、婚姻与贡赋联系维系
localAdmin.en: Local administration operated through networks of clans, villages, farmsteads, and hillforts. Tribal core territories were controlled by chiefly kin and client nobles, while peripheral areas were held together by alliances, marriage ties, and tribute obligations

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要是口述习惯法，由酋长、长老和德鲁伊解释执行；赔偿、和解、誓言与血亲责任比正式监禁更常见
legalSystem.en: Law was chiefly customary and oral, interpreted and enforced by chieftains, elders, and druids; compensation, settlement, oaths, and kin responsibility were more common than formal imprisonment

taxationSystem.zh: 不存在统一税制，酋长主要通过贡赋、战利品分配、牲畜征集、宴飨义务与对附庸的劳役要求获取资源
taxationSystem.en: There was no unified tax system; chieftains chiefly drew resources from tribute, division of war booty, livestock levies, feasting obligations, and labor demands imposed on dependents

religion.zh: 以凯尔特多神信仰为核心，崇拜与自然、战争、丰饶、泉水、树林和祖先相关的神灵，祭祀活动常在圣林、河流、沼泽和高地进行，德鲁伊具有重要宗教权威
religion.en: Religion centered on Celtic polytheism, venerating deities associated with nature, war, fertility, springs, groves, and ancestors. Ritual activity often took place in sacred groves, rivers, wetlands, and high places, and druids held major religious authority

philosophy.zh: 缺乏类似地中海城邦的成体系书面哲学，思想传统主要体现在德鲁伊的宇宙观、口传知识、祖先记忆、荣誉伦理与英雄价值观之中
philosophy.en: There was no formal written philosophy comparable to Mediterranean city-states; intellectual tradition was expressed through druidic cosmology, oral knowledge, ancestral memory, honor ethics, and heroic values

writingSystem.zh: 323 BCE时不列颠凯尔特社会基本以口述传统为主，尚无本土普遍使用的文字行政体系；与高卢和地中海贸易中可能接触到希腊或伊特鲁里亚字母影响，但未形成广泛本地书写制度
writingSystem.en: In 323 BCE British Celtic society remained primarily oral and lacked a native widely used written administrative system. Contacts with Gaul and the Mediterranean may have exposed elites to Greek or Etruscan alphabetic influences, but these had not become a broad local writing tradition

culturalAchievements.zh: 突出成就在于丘堡建设、青铜与早期铁器工艺、车辆与战车传统、精美首饰与武器装饰、口头诗歌与英雄叙事、宴飨政治以及跨海贸易网络的形成
culturalAchievements.en: Major achievements included hillfort construction, bronze and early ironworking, vehicle and chariot traditions, finely decorated jewelry and weapons, oral poetry and heroic narrative, feasting politics, and the development of maritime trade networks

languageFamily.zh: 凯尔特语族，主要为不列颠凯尔特语的早期形态
languageFamily.en: Celtic language family, chiefly early forms of Brittonic Celtic

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以自给农业和畜牧业为主，辅以手工业、地方交换和有限的跨海贸易。财富更多以土地、牲畜、粮食储备、金属器与人际依附关系体现，而非统一货币总量
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based mainly on subsistence agriculture and pastoralism, supplemented by craft production, local exchange, and limited maritime trade. Wealth was expressed more through land, livestock, grain stores, metal objects, and client relationships than through a unified money supply

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物种植、畜牧养殖、铁器锻造、青铜器制作、盐生产、纺织、木工、制陶与战车制造
mainIndustries.en: Grain cultivation, livestock herding, iron forging, bronze working, salt production, textiles, woodworking, pottery, and chariot construction

tradeGoods.zh: 输出或交换牲畜、兽皮、羊毛、盐、金属制品、奴隶及可能的锡；输入酒、精致陶器、装饰品、少量奢侈金属器与地中海风格商品
tradeGoods.en: Goods exported or exchanged included livestock, hides, wool, salt, metal goods, slaves, and possibly some tin; imports included wine, fine pottery, ornaments, small quantities of luxury metalwork, and Mediterranean-style prestige goods

unitName.en: head of livestock and metal pieces

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要体现在耕地使用权、牛羊数量、谷仓存粮、工具武器以及与酋长或贵族的依附关系。贵族家庭拥有更大畜群、宴飨能力和奢侈金属器
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was mainly measured by access to arable land, numbers of cattle and sheep, grain in storage, tools and weapons, and ties to chieftains or nobles. Elite households possessed larger herds, greater feasting capacity, and luxury metal goods

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 以氏族土地占有、家庭生产、酋长再分配和礼物交换为核心的部族经济，市场交换存在但规模有限且区域差异很大
economicSystem.en: A tribal economy centered on clan landholding, household production, chiefly redistribution, and gift exchange, with market exchange present but limited in scale and highly regional

category.zh: 宗教祭祀与德鲁伊支持
category.en: Ritual activity and support for druids

treasuryDescription.zh: 并无统一国库，财富分散储存在各部族酋长的谷仓、畜群、金属器藏品与丘堡仓储中。所谓财政储备更像是可动员资源总量，而非集中铸币库藏
treasuryDescription.en: There was no unified state treasury; wealth was dispersed among tribal chieftains in granaries, herds, metal hoards, and hillfort stores. Fiscal reserves were therefore more a pool of mobilizable resources than a centralized coin treasury

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政重在维持酋长威望与追随者忠诚，通过再分配、宴飨、馈赠和战利品分赏实现政治整合，平时积累粮食与牲畜以应对歉收和战争
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice focused on maintaining chiefly prestige and follower loyalty through redistribution, feasting, gift-giving, and the sharing of booty, while accumulating grain and livestock to face poor harvests and warfare

description.zh: 由自由民和战士组成，装备长矛、盾牌、短剑或刀，作战方式以密集冲锋、伏击和地方防御为主
description.en: Made up of freemen and warriors, armed with spears, shields, and short swords or knives, fighting mainly through mass charges, ambush, and local defense

description.zh: 由酋长和贵族亲兵构成，装备较优，承担核心突击、护卫与威望展示功能
description.en: Composed of the personal followers of chieftains and nobles, better equipped and used for decisive assault, guard duty, and displays of prestige

description.zh: 战车在不列颠早期凯尔特军事文化中具有重要象征与机动用途，用于贵族作战、侦察、快速投射与仪式化展示
description.en: Chariots held important symbolic and mobile roles in early British Celtic warfare, serving elite combat, scouting, rapid movement, and ritualized display

description.zh: 负责丘堡据守、警戒和粮仓保护，在部族冲突和外敌登陆威胁时迅速集结
description.en: Responsible for holding hillforts, keeping watch, and protecting storage sites, mobilizing quickly during tribal conflicts or threats of seaborne attack

commanderInChief.zh: 无统一最高统帅；战时由各部族酋长分别领军
commanderInChief.en: No unified commander-in-chief; in war each tribe was led by its own chieftain

technology.zh: 军事技术以铁制矛头、长剑、盾牌、皮革与木制防具、战车和丘堡防御工事为主，尚未形成地中海式攻城体系或正规常备军制度
technology.en: Military technology centered on iron spearheads, swords, shields, leather and wooden protection, chariots, and hillfort fortifications, without Mediterranean-style siege systems or a formal standing army structure

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自部族间竞争、跨海袭掠、爱尔兰海和北海沿岸冲突，以及与高卢部族互动带来的迁徙和战争压力；323 BCE时尚未面临罗马直接军事入侵
threats.en: The main threats came from inter-tribal rivalry, seaborne raiding, conflict along the Irish Sea and North Sea coasts, and migration or warfare pressures arising from interaction with Gaulish tribes; in 323 BCE Britain did not yet face direct Roman military invasion

populationDescription.zh: 人口分布稀疏且区域不均，主要集中在英格兰南部和东部较适宜农业的地带，以及若干河谷、沿海平原与丘堡周边地区；苏格兰高地、威尔士山区与偏远地带人口较少
populationDescription.en: The population was sparse and unevenly distributed, concentrated mainly in the more arable regions of southern and eastern England, as well as certain river valleys, coastal plains, and areas around hillforts; the Scottish Highlands, Welsh uplands, and remote districts were more lightly settled

name.zh: 斯坦威克前身区域中心

socialClasses.zh: 大体可分为酋长与贵族、德鲁伊与祭司、战士、自由农民与牧民、工匠和商旅、依附民及奴隶。社会地位与血缘、武勇、馈赠能力和宗教威望密切相关
socialClasses.en: Society can broadly be divided into chieftains and nobles, druids and priests, warriors, free farmers and herders, craftsmen and traders, dependents, and slaves. Social standing was closely tied to kinship, martial ability, gift-giving capacity, and religious prestige

allies.zh: 无统一盟友体系；各部族与高卢凯尔特部族保持最密切的跨海联系，并在婚姻、贸易和军事上形成临时联盟
allies.en: There was no unified alliance system; individual tribes maintained the closest overseas links with Celtic Gaulish tribes and formed temporary alliances through marriage, trade, and warfare

enemies.zh: 无共同国家级敌人，主要对手是相邻不列颠部族与偶发海上袭掠者；对遥远的地中海强国仅有间接认知，并无直接战争关系
enemies.en: There was no common state-level enemy; the main rivals were neighboring British tribes and occasional seaborne raiders. Mediterranean great powers were only indirectly known and not in direct war with Britain

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以部族利益为中心，依赖贸易、婚姻、人质交换、馈赠与武力威慑维持平衡。跨海联系主要面向高卢海岸，而非直接介入地中海政治
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy was centered on tribal interests and relied on trade, marriage, hostage exchange, gifts, and military deterrence to maintain balance. Overseas relations were directed chiefly toward the Gaulish coast rather than direct involvement in Mediterranean politics

era.zh: 铁器时代晚期，不列颠前罗马凯尔特阶段
era.en: Late Iron Age, pre-Roman British Celtic phase

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术与实践包括铁器锻造、丘堡防御体系、轮车与战车使用、改良木工和纺织、谷物储藏设施、盐业生产及区域性金属装饰工艺
keyInnovations.en: Key technologies and practices included ironworking, hillfort defensive systems, wheeled vehicles and chariots, improved woodworking and textiles, grain storage facilities, salt production, and regional metal decorative craftsmanship

strengths.zh: 优势在于部族社会适应性强、丘堡网络便于地方防御、战士文化凝聚力高、农业与畜牧基础稳固，并与高卢保持海上联系，能够持续获得外来奢侈品和文化影响
strengths.en: Strengths included the adaptability of tribal society, hillfort networks suited for local defense, strong cohesion within warrior culture, a solid agricultural and pastoral base, and maritime links with Gaul that brought prestige goods and cultural influence

weaknesses.zh: 弱点在于政治高度分裂、缺乏统一税制和常备军、行政记录不足、跨部族协调困难，面对组织严密的大型国家时容易处于劣势
weaknesses.en: Weaknesses lay in extreme political fragmentation, the absence of a unified tax system and standing army, limited administrative record-keeping, and difficulty in inter-tribal coordination, leaving Britain at a disadvantage against tightly organized large states

outlook.zh: 323 BCE时整体局势稳定，但未来几个世纪中，不列颠将继续受到高卢文化、贸易网络与可能的人口流动影响，部分南部部族会逐渐强化权力中心与对外交换，为后来的更复杂部族政体和最终与罗马世界接触奠定基础
outlook.en: In 323 BCE the overall situation was stable, but over the coming centuries Britain would continue to be shaped by Gaulish culture, trade networks, and possible population movements. Some southern tribes would gradually strengthen power centers and external exchange, laying the groundwork for more complex tribal polities and eventual contact with the Roman world

description.zh: 不列颠群岛由众多凯尔特部族和山寨中心分布控制。
description.en: The British Isles were controlled by numerous Celtic tribes and hillfort centers.

governmentForm.zh: 分散的部族君长制与贵族议会并存，各部落拥有独立统治者与战士贵族集团
governmentForm.en: A decentralized tribal monarchy with aristocratic councils, in which each tribe maintained its own ruler and warrior nobility

socialStructure.zh: 社会由贵族武士、德鲁伊祭司、自由民、依附民与奴隶构成，部族与氏族纽带强于统一国家身份
socialStructure.en: Society was organized around aristocratic warriors, Druids, free commoners, dependents, and slaves, with tribal and clan ties stronger than any common state identity

rulingClass.zh: 部族王族、贵族战士家族与祭司阶层共同构成统治精英
rulingClass.en: The ruling elite consisted of royal lineages, noble warrior families, and the priestly class

succession.zh: 继承通常在贵族家族内部进行，但需得到战士贵族与部族集会认可，兼具世袭与选举色彩
succession.en: Succession generally occurred within noble families but required acceptance by warrior aristocrats and tribal assemblies, combining hereditary and elective elements

structure.zh: 高卢并非统一国家，而是由众多部族政体组成；每个部族通常由国王或首领、贵族议会、战士随从与祭司网络共同治理
structure.en: Gaul was not a unified state but a mosaic of tribal polities; each tribe was commonly governed by a king or chief together with a noble council, warband retainers, and priestly networks

function.zh: 负责首领护卫、宴飨分配、使者接待、贡赋储存与对内协调
function.en: Handled the ruler's protection, feast distribution, reception of envoys, storage of tribute, and internal coordination

function.zh: 审议战争、盟约、土地争端、继承与跨氏族事务，是部族政治决策核心
function.en: Deliberated on war, alliances, land disputes, succession, and inter-clan affairs, forming the core of tribal political decision-making

function.zh: 主持宗教仪式、口述法传统、仲裁纠纷、监督誓约与部族间神圣规范
function.en: Oversaw religious rites, oral legal tradition, arbitration, oath supervision, and sacred norms between tribes

function.zh: 征集牲畜、谷物、金属与战利品，维持首领财富再分配与节庆供给
function.en: Collected livestock, grain, metals, and spoils, sustaining elite redistribution and festival provisioning

function.zh: 负责召集战士、传递号令、整合附庸部落与组织远征
function.en: Called up warriors, transmitted commands, integrated dependent tribes, and organized expeditions

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理以部落、次部族、氏族与聚落为单位，缺乏统一文官体系，依赖地方贵族、族长与德鲁伊维持秩序
localAdmin.en: Local administration operated through tribes, sub-tribes, clans, and settlements, lacking a unified bureaucracy and relying on local nobles, kin leaders, and Druids to maintain order

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要为习惯法与口传判例，由德鲁伊和贵族仲裁；重视赔偿、誓言、亲族责任与荣誉，而非成文法典
legalSystem.en: Law was chiefly customary and orally transmitted, arbitrated by Druids and nobles; it emphasized compensation, oaths, kin responsibility, and honor rather than written codes

taxationSystem.zh: 无统一全国税制，部族首领通过贡赋、战利品分配、牲畜征收、节庆馈赠与对附庸部众的义务征集获取资源
taxationSystem.en: There was no unified national tax system; tribal rulers drew resources through tribute, distribution of war booty, livestock levies, ceremonial gifts, and obligations imposed on dependents

religion.zh: 多神信仰，以自然圣地、林苑、河流与战神、生育神、工艺神崇拜为核心，德鲁伊在祭祀与神圣知识中具有重要地位
religion.en: Polytheistic religion centered on sacred groves, rivers, natural sanctuaries, and the worship of deities associated with war, fertility, and craft, with Druids holding major ritual authority

philosophy.zh: 高卢社会缺乏希腊式成体系书面哲学，但具有由德鲁伊传承的宇宙观、灵魂观、记忆传统与道德训诫
philosophy.en: Gaul lacked a Greek-style formal written philosophy, but possessed Druidic traditions of cosmology, beliefs about the soul, mnemonic learning, and moral instruction

writingSystem.zh: 以口传为主，正式知识尤其宗教法理多避免书写；南部边缘地区在与地中海接触中偶有采用希腊字母或伊特鲁里亚相关书写习惯
writingSystem.en: Culture was predominantly oral, and formal knowledge especially in religion and law often avoided writing; in southern fringe regions, contact with the Mediterranean occasionally brought use of Greek letters or related Italic scripts

culturalAchievements.zh: 以精湛金工、武器装饰、马具、珠饰、扭环、复杂几何与曲线纹艺术见长，并发展出盛宴文化、英雄诗传统与高水平口述记忆体系
culturalAchievements.en: The Gauls excelled in fine metalwork, decorated weapons, horse gear, beads, torcs, and intricate curvilinear art, while also sustaining feast culture, heroic poetic traditions, and highly developed oral memory systems

languageFamily.zh: 凯尔特语族，大体属于大陆凯尔特语支中的高卢语诸方言
languageFamily.en: Celtic language family, broadly represented by Gaulish dialects of the Continental Celtic branch

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以农业、畜牧、地方手工业与部族间交换为主，南高卢与阿尔卑斯通道地区因连接地中海贸易而较富庶，其余广大地区仍以地方自给和贡赋再分配为主
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based chiefly on agriculture, pastoralism, local craft production, and intertribal exchange; southern Gaul and Alpine corridor regions were wealthier through Mediterranean connections, while much of the broader territory remained oriented toward local subsistence and tribute redistribution

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物种植、畜牧养马、冶铁、青铜与金工、陶器、木工、盐业、织造、武器制造
mainIndustries.en: Grain cultivation, livestock raising and horse breeding, ironworking, bronze and goldsmithing, pottery, woodworking, salt production, weaving, and weapon manufacture

tradeGoods.zh: 盐、奴隶、皮革、毛织品、金属锭、铁器、武器、马匹、琥珀转运、葡萄酒进口、奢侈陶器进口
tradeGoods.en: Salt, slaves, hides, wool textiles, metal ingots, iron goods, weapons, horses, transit amber, imported wine, and imported fine pottery

name.zh: 以称量金属与实物交换为主，局部使用外来钱币
name.en: Predominantly weighed metal and barter, with limited use of foreign coinage

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要体现为土地使用权、牲畜、谷仓储备、武器、金属饰物与亲族庇护关系，贵族财富远超普通自由民
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was measured mainly in access to land, livestock, grain stores, weapons, metal ornaments, and kin protection, with noble households far wealthier than ordinary freemen

economicSystem.zh: 部族再分配经济与地方市场交换并存，财富通过贡赋、首领馈赠、战利品与婚盟流动，尚未形成统一财政市场体系
economicSystem.en: A mixed system of tribal redistribution and local market exchange prevailed, with wealth moving through tribute, elite gift-giving, war booty, and marriage alliances rather than a unified fiscal-market order

source.en: Gifts from dependents and allied groups

source.zh: 矿盐、金属与工艺品收入

category.zh: 首领馈赠与宴飨再分配
宗教仪式与德鲁伊支持
外交婚盟、赎买与使节往来
category.en: Diplomatic marriage alliances, ransom, and embassies

treasuryDescription.zh: 不存在统一国库，财富分散储存在各大部族首领的仓储、金属器窖藏、牲畜群与神圣供奉之中；贵族可迅速调动财富，但难以长期集中管理
treasuryDescription.en: There was no single central treasury; wealth was dispersed among major tribal rulers in storehouses, metal hoards, herds, and sacred deposits, allowing rapid elite mobilization but limiting sustained central control

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政以维持首领威望和战士忠诚为核心，重在再分配而非积累；稳定时期略有盈余，但战争与部族竞争会迅速消耗储备
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice centered on sustaining elite prestige and warrior loyalty, emphasizing redistribution over accumulation; stable periods could generate modest surpluses, but war and intertribal rivalry quickly consumed reserves

description.zh: 由贵族与其随从构成，是部族威望与机动力量核心，适合突击、追击与展示身份
description.en: Formed by nobles and their retainers, this was the core of tribal prestige and mobility, suited for shock action, pursuit, and status display

description.zh: 由自由民和战士集团组成，装备长矛、剑、盾牌，作战方式强调勇武冲锋与部族集群作战
description.en: Made up of freemen and warrior groups, these infantrymen carried spears, swords, and shields, fighting with an emphasis on ferocious charges and tribal mass action

description.zh: 来自较贫弱自由民与边缘附庸，使用标枪、投石索与轻盾，用于骚扰和前哨作战
description.en: Drawn from poorer freemen and marginal dependents, they used javelins, slings, and light shields for harassment and screening

description.zh: 部分地区仍保留战车传统，更多具有贵族仪仗与机动投送功能，在不列颠方向影响更大，在高卢本土已非主力
description.en: Some regions still preserved chariot traditions, though these increasingly served aristocratic display and mobile transport roles; they were more prominent toward Britain and no longer the main arm in Gaul proper

commanderInChief.zh: 无统一统帅，由各部族国王、战首领与贵族联军议决统兵
commanderInChief.en: No unified commander-in-chief; armies were led by individual tribal kings, war leaders, and coalition decisions among nobles

technology.zh: 处于欧洲铁器时代成熟阶段，擅长锻铁长剑、长矛、盾牌、锁子甲早期形式、骑兵装备与木土防御工事，但攻城技术与后勤组织弱于地中海国家
technology.en: The Gauls stood in the mature European Iron Age, skilled in forged iron swords, spears, shields, early mail, cavalry gear, and timber-earth fortifications, though they lagged behind Mediterranean states in siegecraft and logistics

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自部族间竞争、阿尔卑斯以南意大利势力扩张、莱茵以东日耳曼部族压力，以及与地中海贸易线相关的冲突；323年前后尚未面临统一外来征服者，但边疆摩擦不断
threats.en: Major threats came from intertribal competition, the expanding powers south of the Alps in Italy, pressure from eastern Rhine Germanic groups, and conflicts tied to Mediterranean trade routes; around 323 BCE there was not yet a single conquering external power, but frontier friction was constant

populationDescription.zh: 人口分布广泛，集中于河谷、农耕平原、盐矿区与交通廊道，整体呈低密度乡村聚居格局，南部与大河流域人口较为稠密
populationDescription.en: The population was widely spread, concentrated in river valleys, arable plains, salt-producing areas, and transport corridors, with an overall low-density rural settlement pattern and denser populations in the south and major river basins

name.zh: 马西利亚周边高卢聚落带
比布拉克特前身区域性山堡中心
name.en: Proto-regional hillfort center of the future Bibracte area
Tribal center in the Toulouse region

name.zh: 塞纳河上游山堡群中心
name.en: Hillfort cluster center in the upper Seine region

socialClasses.zh: 上层为王族与贵族武士，其次为德鲁伊与其他祭司专业群体；中层为自由农民、牧民、工匠与商旅；下层为依附民、债役者与奴隶
socialClasses.en: At the top stood royal families and noble warriors, followed by Druids and other specialist ritual groups; the middle consisted of free farmers, herders, artisans, and traders; below them were dependents, debt-bondsmen, and slaves

allies.zh: 无统一对外盟友体系，常见的是部族间临时同盟；南高卢部分部落与马西利亚希腊商人保持贸易合作，并与不列颠及伊比利亚凯尔特群体维持跨海峡和陆路联系
allies.en: There was no unified external alliance system; temporary tribal coalitions were common. Some southern Gaulish groups maintained trade cooperation with the Greek merchants of Massalia and kept cross-Channel and overland ties with British and Iberian Celtic peoples

enemies.zh: 主要敌对对象并非单一文明，而是相互竞争的邻近部族、阿尔卑斯以南的伊特拉斯坎人与早期罗马及萨姆奈特势力，以及局部边境上的日耳曼群体
enemies.en: The principal enemies were not a single civilization but rival neighboring tribes, powers south of the Alps such as Etruscans and early Rome together with Samnite forces, and Germanic groups along certain frontiers

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交以婚盟、互市、结盟、劫掠报复与人质交换为基本手段，缺乏长期统一大战略，各部族按自身地缘利益行动
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy relied on marriage alliances, exchange markets, coalitions, retaliatory raiding, and hostage exchange, without a long-term unified grand strategy; each tribe acted according to its own regional interests

era.zh: 欧洲晚期铁器时代，处于拉坦文化扩展阶段，并与早期希腊化世界接触
era.en: Late European Iron Age, in the expansion phase of La Tene culture and in contact with the early Hellenistic world

keyInnovations.zh: 高质量铁器锻造、装饰性金工、改进骑兵装备、木土山堡防御、盐业开采与长距离部族交换网络
keyInnovations.en: High-quality iron forging, decorative metalwork, improved cavalry equipment, timber-earth hillfort defenses, salt extraction, and long-distance intertribal exchange networks

strengths.zh: 人口可观，战士传统强盛，贵族骑兵机动性高，铁器与金工技术出色，部族社会在地方层面具有韧性，且控制从大西洋到阿尔卑斯前地的广阔资源带
strengths.en: The Gaulish world possessed a large population, strong warrior traditions, highly mobile noble cavalry, impressive iron and metalworking skills, resilient local tribal structures, and access to a broad resource zone stretching from the Atlantic to the Alpine forelands

weaknesses.zh: 缺乏统一国家与持续行政体系，财政分散，后勤和攻城能力有限，部族内斗频繁，对外难以长期协调，易被组织更严密的国家逐步分化与征服
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses lay in the absence of a unified state and durable administration, dispersed finances, limited logistics and siege capacity, frequent intertribal rivalry, and difficulty sustaining coordinated external policy, making it vulnerable to more organized states

outlook.zh: 323年前后的高卢总体稳定，但这种稳定建立在松散均势之上。短期内其部族体系仍将延续并保持活力；长期看，随着地中海国家扩张、跨区域贸易深化与更大规模战争出现，缺乏统一政治整合将成为决定性弱点
outlook.en: Around 323 BCE Gaul was broadly stable, but this stability rested on a loose balance of tribal powers. In the short term its tribal order would remain vigorous and durable; in the longer term, however, the expansion of Mediterranean states, deeper interregional trade, and larger-scale warfare would turn the lack of political integration into a decisive vulnerability

description.zh: 西欧广泛分布的凯尔特部族社会，尚未形成统一国家。
description.en: A broad Celtic tribal world across western Europe, not yet consolidated into a single state.

name.zh: 早期日耳曼与北欧部落文化
name.en: Early Germanic and Nordic Tribal Cultures

governmentForm.zh: 分散的部落酋邦与氏族议事制度，权力建立在亲族、战士追随者与地方习惯法之上
governmentForm.en: Dispersed tribal chiefdoms with clan assemblies, where authority rested on kinship, warrior followings, and local customary law

socialStructure.zh: 社会以家族、氏族、部落为核心，自由民占主体，另有酋长家族、战士随从、工匠、祭司职能人物以及一定数量的奴隶或被俘依附者
socialStructure.en: Society was centered on households, clans, and tribes, with free commoners forming the majority alongside chieftainly families, warrior retinues, artisans, religious specialists, and a limited number of slaves or captives

rulingClass.zh: 各部落酋长家族、显贵战士与集会中有影响力的长老
rulingClass.en: Chieftainly families, prominent warriors, and influential elders in tribal assemblies

succession.zh: 酋长继承通常偏向家族世袭，但需获得氏族首领、战士追随者或部落集会认可，并非严格长子继承
succession.en: Succession generally favored hereditary transmission within leading families, but required acceptance by clan leaders, warrior followers, or the tribal assembly rather than strict primogeniture

structure.zh: 不存在统一国家机构；政治层级通常为家族、村落、氏族与部落。重大事务由自由民集会、长老协商和酋长裁断共同决定，战时酋长权威增强，平时地方自治较强
structure.en: There was no unified state apparatus; political organization generally operated through household, village, clan, and tribe. Major matters were decided through assemblies of free men, councils of elders, and the judgment of chieftains, with stronger chiefly authority in war and strong local autonomy in peacetime

function.zh: 管理酋长住所、储藏、馈赠、护卫、传令与对外接待，是最接近“行政核心”的组织
function.en: Managed the chieftain's residence, storage, gift distribution, guard duties, communications, and reception of outsiders, serving as the closest equivalent to an administrative core

function.zh: 就争端、婚盟、迁徙、祭仪与对外战争进行协商，为酋长提供建议并约束其权力
function.en: Deliberated on disputes, marriages, migration, ritual matters, and external war, advising the chieftain and limiting his power

function.zh: 召集自由民集会、传达决议、主持宣誓和仲裁程序，在大型部落中承担协调职能
function.en: Convened assemblies of free men, communicated decisions, and supervised oaths and arbitration procedures, performing coordination roles in larger tribes

name.zh: 祭司与神圣场所管理者
name.en: Priests and custodians of sacred sites

function.zh: 主持祭祀、占卜、圣林或沼泽祭场维护，并在部分部落中参与司法宣誓与公共仪式
function.en: Conducted sacrifices, divination, and maintenance of sacred groves or wetland cult sites, and in some tribes participated in judicial oaths and public rituals

function.zh: 在较稳定的酋邦中征集粮食、牲畜、皮毛和琥珀等贡赋，并管理宴飨与战时储备
function.en: In more stable chiefdoms, collected tribute in grain, livestock, furs, and amber, and managed feasting supplies and wartime reserves

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理以村落和氏族首领为主，无职业化官僚体系；权力通过亲族网络、口头协定与集体习惯维系
localAdmin.en: Local administration was conducted by village heads and clan leaders, without a professional bureaucracy; authority was maintained through kinship networks, oral agreements, and collective custom

legalSystem.zh: 以习惯法、赔偿原则、血亲调解和公开集会裁决为主。重罪可能引发流放、复仇或以牲畜和贵重物赔偿；誓言、见证人与神意占断具有重要作用
legalSystem.en: Law was based on custom, compensation, kin mediation, and public assembly judgments. Serious offenses could lead to exile, feud, or compensation in livestock and valuables; oaths, witnesses, and divinatory signs played important roles

taxationSystem.zh: 无统一常设税制，酋长主要依靠自有地产、战利品、宴飨回馈、附庸贡品以及在战争或祭典时向自由民征集物资
taxationSystem.en: There was no unified permanent tax system; chieftains relied mainly on personal estates, war booty, reciprocal feasting, tribute from dependents, and occasional levies of goods from free households during war or major rituals

religion.zh: 多神信仰与自然崇拜并存，崇敬天空、雷电、战斗、生育、海洋与祖灵，圣林、湖沼、岩石和墓葬地点具有宗教意义，祭品包括牲畜、武器和贵重物
religion.en: Polytheistic belief and nature worship coexisted, venerating powers of sky, thunder, war, fertility, sea, and ancestral spirits. Sacred groves, bogs, stones, and burial places held religious significance, and offerings included livestock, weapons, and valuables

philosophy.zh: 尚无成体系文字哲学，世界观主要体现为口传传统、英雄伦理、亲族责任、荣誉观念、战士忠诚和对命运与神意的敬畏
philosophy.en: There was no systematic written philosophy; worldview was expressed through oral tradition, heroic ethics, kin obligation, honor culture, warrior loyalty, and reverence for fate and divine will

writingSystem.zh: 尚未形成成熟本土文字体系，信息传承主要依赖口述、记忆、象征器物与装饰符号；卢恩文字要到更晚时期才出现
writingSystem.en: No mature native writing system yet existed; transmission relied mainly on oral memory, symbolic objects, and decorative marks. Runic writing would emerge only in a later period

culturalAchievements.zh: 在青铜时代晚期与早期铁器时代传统基础上，形成船只制造、木工、纺织、金属饰物、琥珀加工、口头史诗与纪念性墓冢文化；北欧岩画与礼仪性埋藏传统延续其象征体系
culturalAchievements.en: Building on Late Bronze Age and Early Iron Age traditions, these societies developed boatbuilding, woodworking, textiles, metal ornament production, amber crafting, oral heroic tradition, and monumental burial mounds; Scandinavian rock art legacies and ritual deposit practices preserved symbolic systems

languageFamily.zh: 主要为前原始日耳曼语方言及北方相关印欧语支，斯堪的纳维亚与日德兰地区方言差异显著但仍属近缘口语连续体
languageFamily.en: Primarily pre-Proto-Germanic dialects and related northern Indo-European speech forms, with notable variation across Scandinavia and Jutland but still forming a closely related spoken continuum

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以自给农业、牧养、渔猎与家庭手工业为主，市场交换和长距离贸易规模有限但并非缺席。以银当量估算的总产出较低，因大部分财富不经货币化流通，而以粮食、牲畜、木材和劳役形式存在
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based mainly on subsistence farming, herding, fishing, hunting, and household craft production, with limited but real market exchange and long-distance trade. Estimated in silver equivalent, total output was low because most wealth did not circulate monetarily but existed in grain, livestock, timber, and labor

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物种植、大麦与小麦耕作、畜牧、渔业、狩猎、木材采集、造船、纺织、制皮、骨角加工、铁器冶炼与琥珀采集
mainIndustries.en: Grain cultivation, barley and wheat farming, livestock raising, fishing, hunting, timber extraction, boatbuilding, textile production, leatherworking, bone and antler craft, ironworking, and amber collection

tradeGoods.zh: 琥珀、皮毛、奴隶、蜂蜡、蜂蜜、牲畜、兽角、羽毛、木材、鱼干以及少量铁器；输入品包括青铜器、精制武器、玻璃珠、酒、盐和地中海奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Trade goods included amber, furs, slaves, beeswax, honey, livestock, antler, feathers, timber, dried fish, and limited iron goods; imports included bronze items, fine weapons, glass beads, wine, salt, and Mediterranean luxury goods

name.zh: 无统一货币，兼用称量贵金属与物物交换
name.en: No unified currency; barter and weighed valuables

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要表现为耕地使用权、牲畜数量、谷仓储备、船只、武器、首饰和亲族关系，而非硬币储蓄
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was expressed mainly in access to arable land, livestock numbers, stored grain, boats, weapons, ornaments, and kin connections rather than coin savings

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 以家庭生产和部落再分配为基础的低货币化经济，酋长通过宴飨、馈赠和战利品分配维持政治关系，区域间交换沿河流、海岸和琥珀路线展开
economicSystem.en: A low-monetized economy based on household production and tribal redistribution, in which chieftains sustained political ties through feasting, gift exchange, and distribution of booty, while interregional exchange moved along rivers, coasts, and amber routes

source.zh: 酋长自有地产和畜群产出
source.en: Output from chiefly estates and herds

treasuryDescription.zh: 不存在统一国库；财富分散保存在酋长厅舍、谷仓、畜群、武器贮藏、琥珀和首饰中。所谓“财政储备”更多是地方性财富与可再分配物资的集合
treasuryDescription.en: There was no unified state treasury; wealth was dispersed among chieftains' halls, granaries, herds, weapon stores, amber, and ornaments. The effective 'treasury' was a collection of local stores and redistributable valuables

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政运作重在维持酋长威望、战士忠诚与部落团结，而非积累抽象货币盈余；稳定时期偏向储粮与馈赠，紧张时期则加强征集与掠夺
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice aimed at maintaining chiefly prestige, warrior loyalty, and tribal cohesion rather than accumulating abstract monetary surplus; in stable times it favored food storage and gift distribution, while in tense times it shifted toward heavier levies and raiding

description.zh: 由酋长维持的较核心武装，装备较好，承担护卫、报复袭击、示威和战时中坚任务
description.en: The core armed element maintained by chieftains, generally better equipped and used for protection, retaliatory raids, displays of power, and as the wartime backbone

description.zh: 主要由自由民组成，以长矛、盾牌、斧和猎弓作战，动员迅速但训练不统一
description.en: Composed mainly of free men fighting with spears, shields, axes, and hunting bows, rapidly mobilized but unevenly trained

description.zh: 以小型船队进行沿岸巡行、渡河、突袭和运输，尚未形成后世意义上的专业海军
description.en: Small boat forces used for coastal movement, river crossings, raids, and transport, not yet a professional navy in the later sense

commanderInChief.zh: 无统一最高统帅；各部落战时由酋长或临时推举的战首领指挥
commanderInChief.en: No single commander-in-chief; in war each tribe was led by its chieftain or a temporarily chosen war leader

technology.zh: 军备处于早期铁器时代水平，以木盾、长矛、铁刀、短剑、斧与少量锁甲或青铜、铁盔为主。战斗方式偏重步战冲突、伏击、袭掠和地形利用
technology.en: Military technology was at an Early Iron Age level, centered on wooden shields, spears, iron knives, short swords, axes, and limited mail or bronze and iron helmets. Warfare emphasized infantry clashes, ambush, raiding, and use of terrain

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自部落间竞争、资源压力、与凯尔特邻群的冲突、北海和波罗的海沿岸争夺，以及严酷气候和歉收引发的人口流动
threats.en: Main threats came from intertribal rivalry, pressure on resources, conflict with neighboring Celtic groups, competition along the North Sea and Baltic coasts, and migration pressures caused by harsh climate and poor harvests

populationDescription.zh: 人口分散在日德兰半岛、丹麦群岛、斯堪的纳维亚南部、波罗的海南岸部分地区及北海沿岸定居点，多为村落和农庄型聚居，尚无真正意义上的大型城市
populationDescription.en: The population was dispersed across Jutland, the Danish islands, southern Scandinavia, parts of the southern Baltic coast, and North Sea coastal settlements, mostly in villages and farmsteads rather than true large cities

name.zh: 无固定大型城市，最大聚落为区域性酋长中心
name.en: No fixed major cities; the largest settlements were regional chiefly centers

name.zh: 日德兰南部大型聚落群

socialClasses.zh: 社会大体可分为酋长与显贵家族、战士随从、自由农民、工匠与贸易者、宗教职能人物，以及奴隶、债役者和战俘依附者
socialClasses.en: Society may broadly be divided into chieftains and noble families, warrior retainers, free farmers, artisans and traders, religious specialists, and slaves, debt dependents, or war captives

allies.zh: 无统一对外盟友；各部落与邻近凯尔特群体、波罗的海沿岸部落和不列颠部族存在临时贸易与婚盟关系
allies.en: There were no unified external allies; individual tribes maintained temporary trade ties and marriage alliances with neighboring Celtic groups, Baltic coastal peoples, and British tribal communities

enemies.zh: 无统一国家级敌人，但与周边竞争部落、部分凯尔特部族及争夺贸易通道的沿海群体时有冲突
enemies.en: There was no single state-level enemy, but conflicts were frequent with rival neighboring tribes, some Celtic peoples, and coastal groups competing for trade routes

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外关系以近距离部落政治、互市、馈赠、婚盟和报复性袭击为主；尚未形成统一、持续的跨区域外交战略，但已通过琥珀贸易间接接入地中海世界
foreignPolicy.en: External relations were governed mainly by local tribal politics, barter, gift exchange, marriage alliances, and retaliatory raiding; no unified long-range diplomatic strategy existed, though amber trade had already linked them indirectly to the Mediterranean world

era.zh: 欧洲北部早期铁器时代，处于赫勒尼主义时期同时代的非国家部落发展阶段
era.en: Northern European Early Iron Age, contemporaneous with the Hellenistic Period but at a non-state tribal stage of development

keyInnovations.zh: 铁器工具和武器的逐步普及、改良木制与板材船、羊毛纺织、沼铁利用、长屋建筑、谷物储藏技术以及沿海与内河航行经验
keyInnovations.en: Key developments included wider use of iron tools and weapons, improved wooden and plank-built boats, wool textile production, use of bog iron, longhouse construction, grain storage techniques, and accumulated knowledge of coastal and inland navigation

strengths.zh: 社会韧性强，适应寒冷环境与低密度定居；自由民战斗传统鲜明；海岸与河网交通促进区域联系；琥珀和北方物产提供对外交换价值
strengths.en: These societies were resilient, adapted to cold environments and low-density settlement; free men maintained strong martial traditions; coastal and river networks supported regional contact; amber and northern products provided exchange value abroad

weaknesses.zh: 政治高度分散，缺乏统一指挥、书写行政和稳定税制；农业产出易受气候波动影响；内部争斗频繁，难以形成大规模持久国家能力
weaknesses.en: They were highly politically fragmented and lacked unified command, written administration, and stable taxation; agricultural output was vulnerable to climate variation; frequent internal conflict limited the emergence of durable large-scale state capacity

outlook.zh: 323年前后整体处于稳定但分散的状态。未来数世纪内，随着铁器扩散、人口增长、贸易深化以及与凯尔特和地中海世界接触增加，部分部落将向更强的酋邦和族群联盟演化
outlook.en: Around 323 BCE these cultures were broadly stable but fragmented. Over the coming centuries, as iron use spread, populations grew, trade deepened, and contact with Celtic and Mediterranean worlds increased, some tribes would evolve into stronger chiefdoms and wider ethnic confederations

description.zh: 北海、波罗的海与斯堪的纳维亚一带由分散部族社会占据。
description.en: The North Sea, Baltic, and Scandinavian zones were occupied by dispersed tribal societies.

governmentForm.zh: 名义上隶属亚历山大帝国继承体系、实际上由马其顿军政精英控制的总督制君主政体
governmentForm.en: A satrapal monarchy nominally within the Alexandrian imperial succession but in practice controlled by a Macedonian military-administrative elite

socialStructure.zh: 社会由马其顿—希腊军政上层、埃及神庙祭司集团、地方书吏与官员、城市居民、乡村农民、工匠、雇佣兵与奴隶构成；传统埃及村社结构仍占主体，而新到来的希腊—马其顿统治层控制军政与财政枢纽。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of a Macedonian-Greek military and administrative upper layer, Egyptian temple priesthoods, local scribes and officials, urban inhabitants, rural peasants, artisans, mercenaries, and slaves; traditional Egyptian village structures remained dominant, while the incoming Greek-Macedonian ruling stratum controlled the military, administrative, and fiscal centers.

rulingClass.zh: 马其顿将领集团、希腊裔军官、宫廷亲信以及与之合作的埃及高级祭司与文书官僚
rulingClass.en: The Macedonian command elite, Greek officers, court retainers, and cooperating Egyptian high priests and scribal bureaucrats

succession.zh: 323年时尚无独立王朝法理；权力基础来自亚历山大任命、驻军支持与对埃及财政和粮仓的控制，实际继承依赖军队拥立与宫廷政治。
succession.en: In 323 BCE there was not yet a fully independent dynastic legality; authority rested on Alexander's appointment, support from the garrison, and control of Egypt's treasury and grain reserves, with practical succession depending on army backing and court politics.

structure.zh: 中央政权以总督府为核心，依托孟斐斯宫廷、马其顿驻军系统、埃及原有税收与文书网络运作。希腊—马其顿高层负责军事、财政总控和对外事务，埃及地方机构继续管理土地丈量、灌溉、税簿、神庙事务和乡村治安。
structure.en: The central regime was organized around the satrapal court, operating through Memphis, the Macedonian garrison system, and the inherited Egyptian tax and scribal network. Greek-Macedonian leaders handled military affairs, overall fiscal control, and foreign relations, while Egyptian local institutions continued to manage land measurement, irrigation, tax registers, temple administration, and rural order.

function.zh: 统筹政令发布、宫廷管理、与将领和地方官的协调
function.en: Coordinated decrees, palace management, and communication with generals and provincial officials

function.zh: 管理土地税、仓储、货币与贵金属征收，以及国家支出
function.en: Managed land taxes, granaries, monetary and bullion collections, and state expenditures

function.zh: 统辖驻军、雇佣军、舰船、要塞与边防
function.en: Supervised garrisons, mercenaries, ships, fortresses, and frontier defense

function.zh: 负责尼罗河洪泛后土地丈量、灌溉渠道维护和农业征税基础
function.en: Handled post-inundation land surveys, maintenance of irrigation channels, and the agrarian basis of taxation

function.zh: 监督神庙财产、祭祀关系、地方文书、契约与人口记录
function.en: Oversaw temple property, cult relations, local records, contracts, and population registration

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政沿用埃及州县化结构，各诺姆由本地官员、书吏和神庙网络运作，上层则受马其顿总督及其委任的军事和财政官监督。乡村一级仍以村社、灌溉共同体和税役登记为治理核心。
localAdmin.en: Local administration retained the Egyptian nome-based structure, with local officials, scribes, and temple networks running provincial affairs under supervision from the Macedonian satrap and his appointed military and fiscal officers. At the village level, governance remained centered on rural communities, irrigation cooperation, and tax-labor registration.

legalSystem.zh: 法律呈并行体系：埃及民事与土地习惯法、神庙司法传统、王室敕令以及希腊—马其顿军法并存。不同族群和不同案件常适用不同程序，国家优先保障税收、治安和土地控制。
legalSystem.en: The legal order was pluralistic: Egyptian customary civil and land law, temple judicial traditions, royal decrees, and Greek-Macedonian military law coexisted. Different populations and cases often followed different procedures, while the state prioritized revenue, order, and control of land.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政主要依靠土地税、谷物征收、灌溉相关税役、神庙与王室地产收入、关税和港口收费。征税高度依赖尼罗河洪水后的土地测量与仓储体系，实物税和银重计价并行。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue relied chiefly on land taxes, grain collection, irrigation-related labor dues, income from temple and royal estates, customs, and port charges. Taxation depended heavily on post-inundation land measurement and the granary system, with payment in kind coexisting with valuation in silver weight.

religion.zh: 宗教生活以古埃及多神信仰为主体，阿蒙、卜塔、拉、欧西里斯、伊西斯、荷鲁斯等神庙体系仍具核心地位；同时马其顿与希腊统治者奉行宙斯、雅典娜、狄俄尼索斯等希腊神祇崇拜。323年时两种传统开始共存，但系统性的王朝宗教融合尚在形成前夜。
religion.en: Religious life remained centered on ancient Egyptian polytheism, with temple systems devoted to Amun, Ptah, Ra, Osiris, Isis, Horus, and others retaining central importance; at the same time the Macedonian and Greek ruling stratum practiced the worship of Zeus, Athena, Dionysus, and other Greek deities. In 323 BCE the two traditions coexisted, though systematic dynastic religious fusion was still in its early stage.

philosophy.zh: 本土思想以玛阿特所代表的秩序、公正与王权神圣观为核心；希腊方面则携带柏拉图、亚里士多德及修辞学、自然哲学传统进入宫廷与军政精英圈。
philosophy.en: Native thought was centered on ma'at, the ideals of order, justice, and sacral kingship; the Greek side brought Platonic and Aristotelian currents, rhetoric, and natural philosophy into court and elite military-administrative circles.

writingSystem.zh: 书写体系并用圣书体、僧侣体与通俗体埃及文，行政中也逐渐出现希腊文书写。神庙、税册和土地记录仍主要依靠埃及书吏传统。
writingSystem.en: Writing systems included hieroglyphic, hieratic, and especially demotic Egyptian scripts, while Greek writing was increasingly used in administration. Temples, tax registers, and land records still depended primarily on the Egyptian scribal tradition.

culturalAchievements.zh: 埃及长期积累的神庙建筑、石雕、墓葬艺术、历法、测量学、灌溉工程和行政文书制度极为成熟。323年这一政体的文化特色在于把成熟的法老时代国家传统与马其顿军事贵族政治结合起来，为后来的亚历山大里亚时代奠定基础。
culturalAchievements.en: Egypt's long-developed temple architecture, stone sculpture, funerary art, calendar science, surveying, irrigation engineering, and administrative record-keeping remained highly advanced. The cultural significance of this polity in 323 BCE lay in combining mature pharaonic state traditions with Macedonian military aristocratic rule, laying the groundwork for the later Alexandrian age.

languageFamily.zh: 主体语言为古埃及语晚期形态，属亚非语系埃及语支；统治层同时使用希腊语，属印欧语系希腊语族。
languageFamily.en: The main population spoke Late Egyptian forms belonging to the Egyptian branch of the Afroasiatic language family; the ruling stratum also used Greek, part of the Indo-European Hellenic branch.

gdpDescription.zh: 埃及是东地中海最富庶的农业核心区之一，经济基础在于尼罗河高产谷物农业、亚麻与纸草生产、神庙地产、工匠制造和连接地中海、红海与非洲内陆的贸易。尽管323年尚未形成后期托勒密王朝那样高度集中和货币化的经济机器，但其资源密度已极高。
gdpDescription.en: Egypt was one of the wealthiest agrarian core regions of the eastern Mediterranean, with an economy based on high-yield Nile grain agriculture, flax and papyrus production, temple estates, craft manufacturing, and trade linking the Mediterranean, the Red Sea, and inner Africa. Although in 323 BCE it had not yet developed the highly centralized and monetized economic machinery of the later Ptolemaic kingdom, its resource density was already exceptionally high.

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物农业、亚麻与纺织、纸草加工、啤酒与面包生产、石材开采、金矿采掘、河运、神庙工坊和港口贸易
mainIndustries.en: Grain agriculture, flax and textiles, papyrus processing, beer and bread production, stone quarrying, gold mining, river transport, temple workshops, and port trade

tradeGoods.zh: 小麦、大麦、亚麻布、纸草、玻璃与陶器、油脂、黄金、石材、香料转口品、象牙、黑檀木和奴隶
tradeGoods.en: Wheat, barley, linen, papyrus, glass and pottery, oils, gold, stone, re-exported aromatics, ivory, ebony, and slaves

name.zh: 阿提卡标准银币与银重单位
name.en: Attic-standard silver coinage and bullion weights

householdWealth.zh: 农村家庭财富主要体现为耕地使用权、牲畜、谷物储备和农具；城市与神庙附属家庭则拥有工艺收入、金属器具和商业库存。财富分布极不均衡，神庙与官僚—军政上层控制大量可征收剩余。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth in the countryside was chiefly embodied in access to arable land, livestock, grain stores, and tools; urban and temple-affiliated households also held craft income, metal goods, and trading stock. Wealth was highly unequal, with temples and the bureaucratic-military elite controlling much of the collectible surplus.

economicSystem.zh: 以灌溉农业和土地征收为核心的再分配经济，辅以神庙地产、王室仓储、地方市场交换和区域性货币贸易；国家对关键资源流通拥有强大干预能力。
economicSystem.en: A redistributive economy centered on irrigated agriculture and land extraction, supplemented by temple estates, royal granaries, local market exchange, and regional monetary trade; the state possessed strong capacity to intervene in key resource flows.

source.zh: 关税、港口税与贸易收费
source.en: Customs, port dues, and trade charges

source.zh: 矿产、采石场与附属贡赋

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库规模较大，得益于埃及长期积累的粮仓、贵金属储备与稳定税基。托勒密在夺取并稳固埃及时尤其重视掌握财政与仓储，因此323年其财力在继业者集团中极具分量。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was substantial, benefiting from Egypt's long-standing granary system, bullion reserves, and stable tax base. Ptolemy placed special emphasis on securing finance and storage when taking and consolidating Egypt, making his resources exceptionally important among the Successor elites in 323 BCE.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏保守务实：优先确保谷物征收、军费支付和地方合作，不急于激烈改造埃及传统征收结构，而是通过控制要塞、港口、国库和神庙关系来逐步建立独立财权。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was conservative and pragmatic: it prioritized grain collection, military payment, and local cooperation, avoiding a rapid overhaul of traditional Egyptian revenue structures while gradually building independent financial authority through control of fortresses, ports, the treasury, and relations with temples.

description.zh: 由马其顿与希腊重装步兵构成，是政权核心野战力量，擅长正面会战与要地防守。
description.en: Composed of Macedonian and Greek heavy infantry, this was the regime's core field force, suited for pitched battle and defense of key positions.

description.zh: 数量可观，用于驻防、远征和镇压地方叛乱，忠诚更多依赖军饷。
description.en: Present in significant numbers, they were used for garrison duty, expeditions, and suppressing unrest, with loyalty tied largely to pay.

description.zh: 包括马其顿伴随骑兵传统残余、希腊与本地骑兵，负责机动突击、侦察和追击。
description.en: Included remnants of the Macedonian companion cavalry tradition, along with Greek and local mounted troops, used for maneuver, reconnaissance, and pursuit.

name.zh: 埃及地方守备与轻步兵
name.en: Egyptian Local Garrison and Light Infantry

description.zh: 承担河谷要塞、仓库、治安和辅助作战任务，装备较轻，适合本地环境。
description.en: Handled valley fortresses, storehouses, internal security, and auxiliary combat roles; more lightly equipped and suited to local conditions.

description.zh: 包括船员、海军陆战与港口守军，用于控制尼罗河航线和地中海东部沿岸交通。
description.en: Included crews, marines, and port garrisons, used to control Nile navigation and communications along the eastern Mediterranean coast.

commanderInChief.zh: 托勒密一世
commanderInChief.en: Ptolemy I

technology.zh: 军事实力建立在晚期马其顿战争体系上，包括长枪方阵、复合兵种协同、攻城器械、要塞驻军和成熟的后勤组织。埃及提供粮食、河运和工事基础，使军队持续作战能力很强。
technology.en: Military strength rested on the late Macedonian system of warfare, including sarissa phalanxes, combined arms coordination, siege equipment, fortress garrisons, and mature logistics. Egypt provided food supplies, river transport, and fortification infrastructure, giving the army strong staying power.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自亚历山大继业者之间的内战与权力重组，尤其是佩尔狄卡斯及其他马其顿强人可能试图重新夺回埃及；此外还需防备昔兰尼、叙利亚方向竞争者及南方努比亚边境波动。
threats.en: The principal threats came from the wars and power struggles among Alexander's Successors, especially the possibility that Perdiccas or other Macedonian strongmen would attempt to reassert direct control over Egypt; additional concerns included rivalry from Cyrenaica and Syria and fluctuations on the southern Nubian frontier.

populationDescription.zh: 人口高度集中于尼罗河谷与三角洲，绝大多数为从事农业的埃及居民。少量但政治上举足轻重的马其顿人与希腊人分布在驻军、宫廷、港口和行政中心，另有利比亚人、犹太人、腓尼基人和其他东地中海移民社群。
populationDescription.en: The population was heavily concentrated in the Nile Valley and Delta, with the overwhelming majority consisting of Egyptian agricultural communities. A small but politically influential Macedonian and Greek population was concentrated in garrisons, the court, ports, and administrative centers, alongside Libyans, Jews, Phoenicians, and other eastern Mediterranean migrant groups.

socialClasses.zh: 上层包括马其顿总督集团、军官、希腊侨民精英和埃及大祭司；中间层包括书吏、神庙人员、商人、工匠和退役军人；底层为佃农、小农、劳役者、码头工、矿工和奴隶。社会身份常受族群、法律地位和与国家机器的关系影响。
socialClasses.en: Upper strata included the Macedonian satrapal elite, officers, Greek expatriate notables, and Egyptian high priests; middle strata included scribes, temple personnel, merchants, artisans, and veterans; lower strata consisted of tenant cultivators, smallholders, laborers, dockworkers, miners, and slaves. Social standing was shaped by ethnicity, legal status, and proximity to the state apparatus.

allies.zh: 323年尚无稳固长期盟友体系；托勒密主要依靠与部分马其顿将领的临时合作、与埃及祭司集团的政治协调，以及对昔兰尼和东地中海港口势力的机会性接触。
allies.en: In 323 BCE there was not yet a fixed long-term alliance system; Ptolemy relied mainly on temporary cooperation with some Macedonian commanders, political coordination with the Egyptian priesthood, and opportunistic dealings with Cyrenaican and eastern Mediterranean port interests.

enemies.zh: 潜在敌人首先是任何试图恢复中央帝国权威、收回埃及财政与军权的继业者派系，尤其是佩尔狄卡斯一方；也需提防周边总督区和海上竞争者的干预。
enemies.en: The foremost potential enemies were any Successor factions seeking to restore central imperial authority and reclaim Egypt's fiscal and military resources, especially the camp of Perdiccas; neighboring satrapies and maritime competitors also posed risks.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以守住埃及、避免被继业者联盟孤立、控制东地中海海运节点、稳住昔兰尼方向并维护南部边境为核心。整体倾向防御性现实主义，同时为未来独立王权预作布局。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on holding Egypt, avoiding isolation among the Successors, controlling eastern Mediterranean maritime points, stabilizing the Cyrenaican direction, and securing the southern frontier. It was broadly a defensive realist strategy while laying groundwork for future independent kingship.

era.zh: 希腊化时期早期
era.en: Early Hellenistic Period

keyInnovations.zh: 结合了埃及成熟的灌溉、测量、粮仓与石工技术，以及马其顿的攻城术、方阵装备、军事组织和更广泛的地中海航运经验。虽然亚历山大里亚的学术黄金时代尚未开始，但这一时期已具备吸纳和整合多文明技术传统的条件。
keyInnovations.en: It combined Egypt's mature irrigation, surveying, granary, and stoneworking technologies with Macedonian siegecraft, phalanx equipment, military organization, and broader Mediterranean maritime experience. Although Alexandria's great scholarly age had not yet begun, the polity already possessed the conditions to absorb and integrate multiple technological traditions.

strengths.zh: 最大优势在于尼罗河农业财富、庞大人口、成熟官僚体系、稳固粮仓与可观财政储备。地理上埃及易守难攻，托勒密本人又是经验丰富、政治审慎的马其顿将领，能够把传统埃及国家机器与希腊化军事统治有效结合。
strengths.en: Its greatest strengths were Nile-based agricultural wealth, a large population, a mature bureaucratic structure, secure granaries, and considerable fiscal reserves. Geographically Egypt was defensible, and Ptolemy himself was an experienced and politically cautious Macedonian commander capable of effectively combining the traditional Egyptian state machine with Hellenistic military rule.

weaknesses.zh: 弱点在于统治合法性仍不稳固，希腊—马其顿统治层人数有限，依赖雇佣军与高额军饷；本地埃及社会与外来军政精英之间存在文化隔阂。若继业者发动大规模入侵或内部军政分裂，政权仍可能迅速承压。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses lay in still-fragile legitimacy, the small size of the Greek-Macedonian ruling stratum, dependence on mercenaries and high military pay, and cultural distance between native Egyptian society and the foreign military-administrative elite. If the Successors launched a major invasion or internal military divisions emerged, the regime could come under severe pressure.

outlook.zh: 323年展望总体乐观。只要托勒密能够守住埃及、掌握国库并避免在继业者斗争中遭到集中围攻，这一政体极有可能从总督区逐步演化为独立而持久的希腊化王国，并成为东地中海最富强的国家之一。
outlook.en: The outlook in 323 BCE was broadly favorable. If Ptolemy could hold Egypt, retain the treasury, and avoid concentrated assault in the Successor struggles, this polity was highly likely to evolve from a satrapy into an independent and durable Hellenistic kingdom, becoming one of the wealthiest and strongest states in the eastern Mediterranean.

description.zh: 尼罗河流域仍极富庶，已由托勒密掌握并趋向独立。
description.en: The Nile valley remained immensely wealthy and was already under Ptolemy's control, moving toward autonomy.

name.zh: 西非萨赫勒与森林诸部族
name.en: West African Sahelian and Forest Tribes

ruler.en: Local chiefs and councils of elders

governmentForm.zh: 以酋长、族长与长老会议共同治理的分散部族政体。
governmentForm.en: A decentralized tribal polity governed by chiefs, lineage heads, and councils of elders.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以氏族、血缘群体和村落共同体为核心，身份多由家族、年龄等级与战士资格界定。农业居民、牧民、猎采者和手工业者并存，不同生态带之间存在互补关系。
socialStructure.en: Society was centered on clans, kin groups, and village communities, with status shaped by lineage, age grades, and warrior standing. Farmers, pastoralists, foragers, and craft specialists coexisted, with strong complementarities between the Sahel and forest zones.

rulingClass.zh: 统治层主要由地方酋长、宗教祭司、资深长老和精英战士组成。其权威依赖血缘声望、财富分配能力与祭祀合法性。
rulingClass.en: The ruling stratum consisted mainly of local chiefs, ritual specialists, senior elders, and elite warriors. Their authority rested on lineage prestige, control of redistribution, and ritual legitimacy.

succession.zh: 继承通常遵循家族或氏族传统，可为父系、旁系或经长老认可的择贤制。酋长更替往往需要长老会与祭司共同确认。
succession.en: Succession generally followed lineage custom, whether patrilineal, collateral, or elder-approved selection. A new chief often required recognition by both the elders and ritual authorities.

structure.zh: 政治结构高度地方化，多数事务由村落议会与酋长主持，跨地区协调较弱。较大的部族联盟只在贸易、迁徙或战争时形成临时联合。
structure.en: Political organization was highly localized, with most affairs handled by village councils and chiefs, and only limited supra-local coordination. Larger tribal confederations tended to form temporarily for trade, migration, or warfare.

function.zh: 裁决纠纷、确认继承、协调村落间事务与传统礼仪。
function.en: Resolved disputes, confirmed succession, and coordinated inter-village affairs and customary rites.

function.zh: 征集粮食、牲畜与手工贡品，并在饥荒或战时进行再分配。
function.en: Collected grain, livestock, and craft tribute and redistributed them in famine or wartime.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以村社为核心，由村长、家族首领与长老共同管理。边远地带更多依靠习惯法和亲族网络维持秩序。
localAdmin.en: Local administration centered on the village community, managed jointly by headmen, lineage leaders, and elders. In remote areas, order depended more on customary law and kinship networks.

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要是不成文习惯法，重视赔偿、调解与宗教誓言。严重冲突可导致逐出氏族、血债报复或贡罚。
legalSystem.en: Law was primarily unwritten customary law emphasizing compensation, mediation, and ritual oath-taking. Serious conflicts could lead to clan expulsion, feud, or tribute penalties.

taxationSystem.zh: 不存在统一税制，通常以谷物、牲畜、劳役和节庆贡物形式向酋长缴纳。贸易节点可能征收过路贡或市集费。
taxationSystem.en: There was no uniform tax system; dues were usually paid to chiefs in grain, livestock, labor, and festival offerings. Trade nodes could also collect transit tribute or market fees.

religion.zh: 泛灵论
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 已形成多样化的口述史诗、鼓乐传统、木雕与纤维工艺，并掌握适应草原与森林环境的农业知识。铁器技术在部分地区开始传播，推动工具和武器改进。
culturalAchievements.en: They developed diverse oral epics, drumming traditions, wood carving, and fiber crafts, along with agricultural knowledge suited to both savanna and forest environments. Ironworking was beginning to spread in some areas, improving tools and weapons.

languageFamily.zh: 以尼日尔-刚果语系、尼罗-撒哈拉语系及部分亚非语系语言为主
languageFamily.en: Primarily Niger-Congo, Nilo-Saharan, and some Afroasiatic languages

philosophy.zh: 以祖灵崇拜、自然神灵信仰和口述传统为核心，强调氏族延续、土地神圣性与长老权威。
philosophy.en: Centered on ancestor veneration, nature spirits, and oral tradition, with emphasis on clan continuity, the sacredness of land, and elder authority.

writingSystem.zh: 尚无普遍使用的本土文字，知识主要通过口传、歌谣与仪式记忆保存。
writingSystem.en: No widely used indigenous writing system; knowledge was preserved mainly through oral transmission, song, and ritual memory.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以自给农业、畜牧、采集和地方交换为主，货币化程度很低。萨赫勒与森林地带之间的盐、粮食、牲畜与手工品交换构成主要增值来源。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based mainly on subsistence farming, pastoralism, foraging, and local exchange, with very low monetization. Trade in salt, grain, livestock, and craft goods between the Sahel and forest zones formed the main source of added value.

mainIndustries.zh: 农耕、畜牧、狩猎采集、铁器初炼、陶器、织物与木工
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture, pastoralism, hunting and gathering, early ironworking, pottery, textiles, and woodworking

tradeGoods.zh: 盐、谷物、牲畜、蜂蜜、象牙、染料、皮革、木材、金砂与手工器物
tradeGoods.en: Salt, grain, livestock, honey, ivory, dyes, leather, timber, gold dust, and crafted goods

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要表现为土地使用权、粮仓储备、牲畜数量、婚聘资源和亲族支持网络。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was measured mainly in access to land, grain stores, livestock, marriage resources, and kinship support networks.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以家户生产、酋长再分配和区域实物交换为核心的部族经济。
economicSystem.en: A tribal economy centered on household production, chiefly redistribution, and regional barter.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

treasuryDescription.zh: 储备多以粮仓、牲畜群、盐块与少量金砂形式保存，而非铸币金库。
treasuryDescription.en: Reserves were held mainly as granary stocks, herds, salt blocks, and small amounts of gold dust rather than coined treasure.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政以维持酋长威望和灾荒再分配为主，征收弹性较大，随丰歉和战事而变动。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on sustaining chiefly prestige and redistribution in times of scarcity, with collections varying according to harvests and war.

description.zh: 以矛、盾、木棒、弓箭和少量铁制武器作战，适于村落防御与突袭。
description.en: Fought with spears, shields, clubs, bows, and some iron weapons, suitable for village defense and raiding.

description.zh: 在较干燥的萨赫勒地带承担侦察、追击与长途联络任务。
description.en: Operated mainly in the drier Sahel, handling scouting, pursuit, and long-distance communication.

description.zh: 由成年男子临时动员，负责守卫耕地、粮仓与聚落。
description.en: Temporarily mobilized adult men tasked with guarding fields, granaries, and settlements.

commanderInChief.zh: 各部族最高酋长或战时盟主
commanderInChief.en: Each tribe's paramount chief or a wartime confederate leader

technology.zh: 军备以木、皮革、石骨与逐渐扩散的铁器为主，缺乏攻城技术和统一后勤体系。
technology.en: Armament relied on wood, leather, bone and stone, plus gradually spreading iron tools, with little siege capability and no unified logistics system.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近部族争地、劫掠、牲畜争夺与生态压力造成的迁徙冲突。
threats.en: Main threats came from neighboring tribes over land, raiding, livestock disputes, and migration conflicts driven by ecological pressures.

populationDescription.zh: 人口广泛分布于河谷、草原边缘和森林村落，聚落规模普遍较小。人口增长受降雨、疫病、粮食储备和迁徙条件影响明显。
populationDescription.en: The population was widely dispersed across river valleys, savanna margins, and forest villages, with generally small settlements. Growth was strongly affected by rainfall, disease, food storage, and migration conditions.

name.zh: 尼日尔河弯曲部集市聚落

socialClasses.zh: 酋长与长老、祭司与巫师、战士、自由农牧民、工匠与边缘依附群体。
socialClasses.en: Chiefs and elders, priests and ritual specialists, warriors, free farmers and pastoralists, artisans, and marginal dependent groups.

allies.zh: 以邻近姻亲部族、贸易伙伴与临时战时联盟为主，无长期统一盟约。
allies.en: Allies were mainly neighboring affinal tribes, trade partners, and temporary wartime coalitions, with no permanent general alliance.

enemies.zh: 敌对者多为争夺水源、牧场、耕地与商路的近邻部族。
enemies.en: Enemies were usually nearby tribes competing over water, pasture, farmland, and trade routes.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策重在维持区域交换、婚盟和平衡冲突，必要时通过祭礼、赔偿或袭击解决争端。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy prioritized regional exchange, marriage alliances, and conflict balancing, with disputes settled through ritual, compensation, or raids when necessary.

era.zh: 西非早期铁器与晚期新石器过渡阶段
era.en: West African early Iron Age and late Neolithic transition

keyInnovations.zh: 铁器冶炼的局部传播、适应季风环境的混合农牧技术、木鼓与纤维编织工艺。
keyInnovations.en: Local diffusion of iron smelting, mixed agro-pastoral techniques adapted to monsoon environments, and advanced drumming and fiber-weaving crafts.

strengths.zh: 生态适应力强、社会韧性高、区域交换网络灵活，并具较大的动员潜力。
strengths.en: Strong ecological adaptability, high social resilience, flexible exchange networks, and considerable mobilization potential.

weaknesses.zh: 政治分散、缺乏统一军政机构、财政集中度低，难以维持长期大规模作战。
weaknesses.en: Political fragmentation, lack of centralized military-administrative institutions, and low fiscal concentration made sustained large-scale warfare difficult.

outlook.zh: 若贸易节点与铁器技术继续扩展，未来可能出现更稳定的酋邦与早期王国。
outlook.en: If trade nodes and iron technology continued to expand, more stable chiefdoms and early kingdoms could emerge in the future.

description.zh: 尼日尔河与西非森林地带分布着众多农业、牧业与聚落网络。
description.en: The Niger basin and West African forest zones were home to numerous farming, pastoral, and settlement networks.

ruler.zh: 地方希臘菁英與總督行政官
ruler.en: Local Greek elites and satrapal administration

dynasty.zh: 巴提亞德王朝已終結；現為後王朝寡頭與總督治理
dynasty.en: Battiad dynasty ended; now governed by post-dynastic oligarchic and satrapal rule

governmentForm.zh: 受馬其頓繼業者體系影響的希臘寡頭城邦聯合行政
governmentForm.en: Greek oligarchic city-state administration under the influence of the Macedonian successor order

socialStructure.zh: 社會以希臘公民地主為核心，上有地方名門與官員，中有商人、工匠與定居農民，下有利比亞原住民勞動者、佃戶與奴隸；沿海希臘城市與內陸部族世界並存。
socialStructure.en: Society was centered on Greek citizen landholders, with local notables and officials at the top, merchants, artisans, and settled farmers in the middle, and Libyan indigenous laborers, tenants, and slaves below; the Greek coastal cities coexisted with an inland tribal world.

rulingClass.zh: 昔蘭尼、巴爾卡與阿波羅尼亞的希臘地主家族、富商與受任命官員
rulingClass.en: Greek landholding families, wealthy merchants, and appointed officials of Cyrene, Barca, and Apollonia

succession.zh: 無世襲君主；地方官由城邦名門選任、推舉或在強權干預下任命，政權更替取決於城市菁英協商與外部馬其頓勢力支持。
succession.en: There was no hereditary monarch; local officials were selected, elected, or appointed under the influence of powerful interests, and political succession depended on negotiation among urban elites and backing from external Macedonian power.

structure.zh: 昔蘭尼地區由城市議會、執政官、財政官與司法官共同治理，並受外來總督或軍政代表監督。其政治核心在昔蘭尼城，但阿波羅尼亞等港市保有自身地方機構。
structure.en: The Cyrenaean region was governed by city councils, magistrates, financial officers, and judicial officials, under the supervision of an external governor or military-administrative representative. Its political center was Cyrene, while port cities such as Apollonia retained their own local institutions.

name.zh: 公民議會與長老議事機構

function.zh: 處理立法、選官、外交諮議與地方政治協調
function.en: Handled legislation, selection of officials, diplomatic consultation, and coordination of local politics

function.zh: 徵收土地稅、港口稅、市場稅與管理公產收入
function.en: Collected land taxes, harbor dues, market taxes, and managed income from public property

function.zh: 主持民事、商事與刑事訴訟，維護城邦法與希臘習慣法
function.en: Oversaw civil, commercial, and criminal cases, and upheld city law and Greek customary practice

function.zh: 管理阿波羅尼亞等港口、徵收關稅並監督糧食與出口品流通
function.en: Managed ports such as Apollonia, collected customs, and supervised the flow of grain and export goods

function.zh: 統籌公民兵、傭兵、守備隊與同內陸利比亞部族的安全關係
function.en: Coordinated citizen militia, mercenaries, garrisons, and security relations with inland Libyan tribes

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以昔蘭尼五城傳統網絡為基礎，主要城市各設議事機構與官職，鄉間由地主、村社首領與稅務承辦人協助治理。
localAdmin.en: Local administration rested on the traditional network of the Cyrenaean Pentapolis, with major cities maintaining councils and magistracies, while the countryside was managed with the aid of landowners, village leaders, and tax contractors.

legalSystem.zh: 法律體系以希臘城邦成文法、法令決議與習慣法為基礎，商業案件重視契約與仲裁；涉及鄉間部族與非公民人口時，常採彈性處理。
legalSystem.en: The legal system was based on Greek city-state written law, decrees, and customary law, with commercial cases emphasizing contracts and arbitration; matters involving rural tribes and non-citizens were often handled pragmatically.

taxationSystem.zh: 稅制以農地徵收、港口關稅、市場交易稅、租地收入與特別徵發為主；戰時或政局緊張時，富裕家族可能被要求承擔臨時公共支出。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation relied mainly on agricultural land assessments, harbor customs, market transaction taxes, land rents, and special levies; in wartime or periods of political tension, wealthy families could be required to bear extraordinary public expenses.

religion.zh: 宗教以希臘多神信仰為主，尤崇阿波羅、宙斯與得墨忒耳；昔蘭尼亦有地方化祭儀，並與利比亞及埃及宗教元素互動。
religion.en: Religion was predominantly Greek polytheism, with particular devotion to Apollo, Zeus, and Demeter; Cyrene also maintained localized cult practices and interacted with Libyan and Egyptian religious elements.

philosophy.zh: 昔蘭尼以昔蘭尼學派聞名，其思想傳統重視感官經驗與快樂問題；至323年時，雖不再處於創始高峰，仍保有希臘教育與哲學辯論風氣。
philosophy.en: Cyrene was known for the Cyrenaic school, whose intellectual tradition emphasized sensation and the question of pleasure; by 323 BCE it was no longer at its founding height, but it still retained a lively Greek culture of education and philosophical debate.

writingSystem.zh: 使用希臘字母書寫，行政、商業、碑銘與宗教奉獻多以希臘文記錄。
writingSystem.en: The Greek alphabet was used, and administration, commerce, inscriptions, and religious dedications were mostly recorded in Greek.

culturalAchievements.zh: 昔蘭尼在醫學、哲學、詩歌與宗教聖所建築上頗具聲望，並因香草植物西爾菲烏姆及其形象在錢幣與地方認同中的重要地位而聞名。
culturalAchievements.en: Cyrene enjoyed a reputation in medicine, philosophy, poetry, and the architecture of religious sanctuaries, and was famous for the plant silphium and its importance in coinage imagery and local identity.

languageFamily.zh: 印歐語系希臘語支，並與利比亞語言環境長期接觸
languageFamily.en: Indo-European, Greek branch, in long-term contact with Libyan linguistic environments

gdpDescription.zh: 昔蘭尼經濟以高價值農產、地中海貿易與港口轉運為核心，在希臘世界屬中等偏富的區域型城市經濟。其財富來自穀物、橄欖油、葡萄酒、馬匹、畜產與昔蘭尼特產西爾菲烏姆，但後者在此時可能已呈衰退趨勢。
gdpDescription.en: The Cyrenaean economy was centered on high-value agriculture, Mediterranean trade, and port redistribution, making it a moderately prosperous regional urban economy by Greek standards. Its wealth came from grain, olive oil, wine, horses, livestock, and the famed local product silphium, though the latter was likely already in decline by this time.

mainIndustries.zh: 農業、畜牧、香草與藥材採集、港口貿易、陶器加工、橄欖油與葡萄酒生產
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture, pastoralism, herb and medicinal collection, port trade, pottery production, and olive oil and wine manufacture

tradeGoods.zh: 穀物、橄欖油、葡萄酒、馬匹、羊毛、皮革、西爾菲烏姆、陶器，以及自外地輸入的木材、金屬器與奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Grain, olive oil, wine, horses, wool, leather, silphium, pottery, and imported timber, metal goods, and luxury items

name.zh: 希臘式銀幣與阿提卡重量體系貨幣
name.en: Greek silver coinage under the Attic weight standard

householdWealth.zh: 城市公民家庭財富差距顯著，地主與商人擁有莊園、奴隸與流動資本；一般農戶多自耕或佃作，富裕程度受收成與市場波動影響很大。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth varied sharply among urban citizens, with landowners and merchants holding estates, slaves, and liquid capital; ordinary farming households were often owner-cultivators or tenants, and their prosperity depended heavily on harvests and market conditions.

economicSystem.zh: 以私人土地所有、市場交換與港口商業為基礎的希臘城邦經濟，並受地方寡頭、公產、稅契與外部強權政治影響。
economicSystem.en: A Greek city-state economy based on private landholding, market exchange, and port commerce, shaped by local oligarchs, public property, tax farming, and the politics of external great powers.

source.zh: 公產、租地與王室舊產收益
source.en: Public estates, land rents, and former royal properties

source.zh: 罰金、司法收入與臨時捐輸
source.en: Fines, judicial income, and extraordinary contributions

treasuryDescription.zh: 國庫規模中等，足以支應港口維護、地方守軍與短期危機，但若遭遇大規模戰事或外部強權徵發，財政承受力有限。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was moderate in size, sufficient to support port maintenance, local garrisons, and short-term crises, but its fiscal resilience was limited in the event of large-scale war or heavy demands from external great powers.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 財政政策偏保守，重視維持港口稅收與農業基盤，避免過度壓榨公民地主；必要時藉由臨時捐輸、借貸或向富商徵派公共義務補足支出。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was relatively conservative, emphasizing the preservation of harbor revenues and the agricultural base while avoiding excessive pressure on citizen landowners; when necessary, expenses were covered through extraordinary contributions, borrowing, or public obligations imposed on wealthy merchants.

description.zh: 以希臘公民與定居者為核心，為城邦主要戰鬥兵種，負責保衛城市與平原農地。
description.en: Centered on Greek citizens and settlers, this was the principal combat arm of the city-state, responsible for defending the cities and the agricultural plain.

description.zh: 多由地方居民、附庸人口與傭兵構成，適合邊地巡防與機動作戰。
description.en: Often composed of local inhabitants, dependent populations, and mercenaries, they were suited to frontier patrol and mobile warfare.

description.zh: 昔蘭尼素有良馬傳統，騎兵在地區防衛與對付內陸部族襲擾時尤為重要。
description.en: Cyrene had a long tradition of fine horses, and cavalry was especially important for regional defense and dealing with inland tribal incursions.

description.zh: 規模有限，以港口巡邏船與守備人員為主，主要保護阿波羅尼亞與沿岸航線。
description.en: Limited in scale, consisting mainly of harbor patrol craft and guards, primarily protecting Apollonia and nearby coastal routes.

description.zh: 戰時由公民預備隊與短期雇用傭兵補充，用於應對突發內亂或外敵威脅。
description.en: In wartime these were supplemented by citizen reserves and short-term hired mercenaries to address sudden unrest or external threats.

commanderInChief.zh: 地方總督或受任命的最高將官
commanderInChief.en: Local governor or appointed senior military commander

technology.zh: 軍事技術屬典型晚期古典至早期希臘化水準，使用重裝步兵裝備、騎兵、攻城器械基本形式與城牆防禦工事，但缺乏大型王國那種持久攻城與野戰資源。
technology.en: Military technology was at a typical Late Classical to early Hellenistic level, employing hoplite equipment, cavalry, basic siege engines, and defensive walls, but lacking the sustained siege and field resources of larger kingdoms.

threats.zh: 主要威脅來自內陸利比亞部族衝突、地方派系鬥爭、港口安全問題，以及馬其頓繼業者體系變動下可能出現的外部干預；西方亦須留意迦太基海上勢力。
threats.en: Primary threats came from conflict with inland Libyan tribes, local factional struggles, harbor security concerns, and possible intervention arising from shifts in the Macedonian successor order; to the west, Carthaginian maritime power also required attention.

populationDescription.zh: 人口包括沿海希臘城邦公民、家屬、外來商人、工匠、鄉村農民、利比亞本地居民與奴隸。都市與鄉間差異顯著，希臘化程度主要集中於五城與港口地帶。
populationDescription.en: The population included Greek city-state citizens, their families, foreign merchants, artisans, rural farmers, indigenous Libyan inhabitants, and slaves. The contrast between urban and rural zones was marked, and Hellenization was concentrated above all in the Pentapolis and port districts.

name.zh: 優厄斯佩里得斯／貝勒尼基前身

socialClasses.zh: 主要階層包括希臘公民地主與貴族、富商與船主、中小農與工匠、非公民居民、利比亞部族民眾、依附農民與奴隸。政治權利集中於少數希臘男性公民。
socialClasses.en: The main social strata included Greek citizen landowners and aristocrats, wealthy merchants and shipowners, small farmers and artisans, non-citizen residents, Libyan tribal populations, dependent cultivators, and slaves. Political rights were concentrated in a minority of Greek male citizens.

allies.zh: 無固定強盟，通常依附或傾向與馬其頓繼業者政權、鄰近埃及方向的希臘化勢力保持合作；與其他希臘城市存在文化與商業聯繫。
allies.en: There were no permanent major allies; Cyrenaea generally aligned with or cooperated with the Macedonian successor powers and Hellenistic forces in the direction of Egypt, while maintaining cultural and commercial ties with other Greek cities.

enemies.zh: 無明確全面戰爭中的宿敵，但與部分內陸利比亞部族、競爭港口勢力及可能的外來干預者關係緊張；對迦太基保持戒備。
enemies.en: It had no clearly defined mortal enemy in full-scale war, but relations were tense with some inland Libyan tribes, rival port interests, and potential external interveners; it remained wary of Carthage.

foreignPolicy.zh: 對外政策以維持自治、保護港口貿易、在馬其頓繼業者鬥爭中審慎選邊，以及平衡埃及、愛琴海與北非內陸關係為主。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on preserving autonomy, protecting port commerce, choosing sides cautiously in the struggles of the Macedonian successors, and balancing relations with Egypt, the Aegean, and the North African interior.

era.zh: 希臘化時代初期
era.en: Early Hellenistic Period

keyInnovations.zh: 成熟的希臘石造建築、城市規劃、銀幣流通、地中海航海技術、橄欖與葡萄加工、醫學與哲學教育傳統，以及基本攻城與防禦工事技術。
keyInnovations.en: Mature Greek stone architecture, urban planning, silver coin circulation, Mediterranean seafaring techniques, olive and grape processing, traditions of medical and philosophical education, and basic siege and fortification technology.

strengths.zh: 農業條件優越，城市文化成熟，港口與地中海貿易連結良好，騎兵與馬產具特色，且具希臘世界文化聲望。
strengths.en: It possessed favorable agricultural conditions, a mature urban culture, strong connections through ports and Mediterranean trade, distinctive cavalry and horse-breeding strengths, and cultural prestige within the Greek world.

weaknesses.zh: 政治上缺乏強而穩定的王權整合，易受寡頭內鬥與外部強權干預；軍事與財政規模有限，對內陸控制力不均，特產西爾菲烏姆亦可能衰退。
weaknesses.en: Politically it lacked a strong and stable monarchy to unify the region, making it vulnerable to oligarchic infighting and outside intervention; its military and fiscal scale was limited, its control of the interior uneven, and its prized silphium product may also have been declining.

outlook.zh: 323年時昔蘭尼地區大體穩定且富庶，但其未來將高度取決於亞歷山大死後希臘化世界的權力重組。若能在繼業者競爭中維持自治並守住港口與農地，仍可延續區域繁榮；反之則可能被更大的希臘化王國吸納。
outlook.en: In 323 BCE Cyrenaea was broadly stable and prosperous, but its future depended heavily on the post-Alexander reordering of the Hellenistic world. If it could preserve autonomy amid the Successor struggles and hold its ports and farmlands, it could continue as a prosperous regional center; otherwise it was likely to be absorbed by a larger Hellenistic kingdom.

description.zh: 北非东部的希腊殖民区域，处于马其顿继业体系影响下。
