description.en: A Greek colonial region in eastern North Africa under the influence of the Macedonian successor order.

name.zh: 前阿克苏姆与阿高—库施特文化圈

ruler.zh: 地方酋长与高地统治者

governmentForm.zh: 分散的部族酋邦与高地首领联盟，沿海与内陆聚落各自自治，部分地区受祭司家族与贵族氏族共同影响。
governmentForm.en: A decentralized network of tribal chiefdoms and highland lordships, with coastal and inland settlements largely self-governing; in some areas authority was shared by priestly families and noble lineages.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以氏族、血缘群体和地方部落为基础，上层为酋长家族、战士贵族与祭司，中层为自由农牧民和商旅，下层包括依附者、债务劳工与战俘。
socialStructure.en: Society was organized around clans, kin groups, and local tribes. The upper strata consisted of chiefly families, warrior nobles, and ritual specialists; the middle strata were free farmers, herders, and merchants; the lower strata included dependents, debt laborers, and captives.

rulingClass.zh: 高地贵族氏族、地方酋长家族、沿海贸易首领与宗教祭司集团。
rulingClass.en: Highland noble lineages, local chiefly houses, coastal trade leaders, and religious priestly groups.

succession.zh: 多为氏族内部的世袭继承，但常需获得长老、战士集团或祭司认可；若继承有争议，可能由实力最强的支系夺位。
succession.en: Succession was usually hereditary within a clan, but often required the approval of elders, warrior groups, or priests; disputed succession could be settled by the strongest branch of the lineage.

structure.zh: 不存在统一国家中枢；政治权力分布于多个高地酋邦、农业聚落与红海沿岸贸易据点。重要事务由酋长、长老会、祭司与武装随从共同处理。
structure.en: There was no single state center; political authority was spread among multiple highland chiefdoms, agricultural settlements, and Red Sea trade stations. Major matters were handled jointly by chiefs, councils of elders, priests, and armed retainers.

name.en: Clan and Council of Elders Administration

function.zh: 协调部族纠纷、土地与放牧权、婚盟、赔偿和继承事务。
function.en: Coordinated tribal disputes, land and grazing rights, marriage alliances, compensation, and inheritance matters.

function.zh: 征收谷物、牲畜、蜂蜜、皮革和劳役，并管理地方仓储与再分配。
function.en: Collected grain, livestock, honey, hides, and labor obligations, and managed local storage and redistribution.

function.zh: 维护祭祀场所、组织节庆、主持誓约和祖先祭礼，并保管宗教性贡物。
function.en: Maintained ritual sites, organized festivals, oversaw oaths and ancestral rites, and safeguarded sacred offerings.

function.zh: 管理内陆到沿海的商路、商队保护、度量衡习惯与港口交易惯例。
function.en: Managed inland-to-coastal trade routes, caravan protection, customary weights and measures, and port exchange practices.

function.zh: 组织酋长亲兵、召集部落战士、保卫聚落、护送商队并应对边境袭扰。
function.en: Organized chiefly bodyguards, summoned tribal warriors, defended settlements, escorted caravans, and responded to frontier raiding.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理依托村社、氏族长老和区域酋长。高地农耕区通常由固定聚落首领管理，低地和半游牧地区则更依赖部族首领与季节性协商。
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied on village communities, clan elders, and regional chiefs. Highland farming zones were usually managed by settlement leaders, while lowland and semi-pastoral regions depended more on tribal headmen and seasonal negotiation.

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要是习惯法，以赔偿、调解、誓约与血亲责任为核心。严重案件如杀人、偷牛、破坏婚盟或亵渎圣所，往往由长老与祭司共同裁断。
legalSystem.en: Law was primarily customary, centered on compensation, mediation, oaths, and kin responsibility. Serious cases such as homicide, cattle theft, violation of marriage alliances, or sacrilege were often judged jointly by elders and priests.

taxationSystem.zh: 没有统一的定额税制，主要采取贡赋与劳役形式。农牧民向酋长提供谷物、牲畜、蜂蜜、金属制品、象牙或运输服务，沿海商贸点则抽取交易分成与通行费。
taxationSystem.en: There was no uniform assessed tax system; revenue came mainly through tribute and labor service. Farmers and herders provided grain, livestock, honey, metal goods, ivory, or transport duties to chiefs, while coastal trade points collected transaction shares and passage dues.

religion.zh: 以本地库施特宗教、祖先崇拜、圣山圣树信仰与祭司主持的献祭传统为主，部分沿海地区已与南阿拉伯宗教符号和观念发生接触。
religion.en: Religion was centered on local Cushitic cults, ancestor veneration, sacred mountains and trees, and priest-led sacrificial traditions; some coastal areas had already come into contact with South Arabian religious symbols and ideas.

philosophy.zh: 尚无成体系文字哲学，社会伦理重视祖先传统、氏族荣誉、誓约、互惠、待客之道以及人与土地、牲畜和神灵的平衡关系。
philosophy.en: There was no formal written philosophy, but social ethics emphasized ancestral custom, clan honor, oath-keeping, reciprocity, hospitality, and balance among people, land, livestock, and the divine.

writingSystem.zh: 大多数地区仍以口述传统为主；沿海贸易节点可能有限接触南阿拉伯字母传统，但尚未在整个文化圈普及。
writingSystem.en: Most regions still relied on oral tradition; coastal trade nodes may have had limited contact with South Arabian alphabetic practices, but writing was not yet widespread across the cultural sphere.

culturalAchievements.zh: 代表成就包括高地梯田与混合农牧技术、石质聚落与防御性建筑、长距离商路组织、珠饰与金属工艺、蜂蜜酒与祭礼文化，以及强大的口头诗歌与谱系记忆传统。
culturalAchievements.en: Notable achievements included highland terracing and mixed agro-pastoral techniques, stone-built settlements and defensive works, long-distance route organization, bead and metal craft production, mead and ritual culture, and strong traditions of oral poetry and genealogical memory.

languageFamily.zh: 以阿高诸语言和其他库施特语支为主，沿海与贸易区可能并存南阿拉伯语接触现象与多语交流。
languageFamily.en: The sphere was dominated by Agaw and other Cushitic languages, while coastal and trade areas likely showed South Arabian contact and multilingual exchange.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以自给性农牧业为基础，剩余产品有限但稳定。高地出产谷物、豆类、牲畜与蜂蜜，沿海与通商节点通过象牙、皮革、香料转运和海陆交换创造额外价值。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on subsistence agro-pastoral production with limited but stable surplus. Highlands produced cereals, pulses, livestock, and honey, while coastal and transit nodes generated additional value through ivory, hides, aromatics, and maritime-overland exchange.

mainIndustries.zh: 农耕、牧业、养蜂、皮革加工、陶器、金属小工艺、石材建筑、商队运输和沿海转口贸易。
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture, pastoralism, beekeeping, leatherworking, pottery, small-scale metal craft, stone construction, caravan transport, and coastal entrepôt trade.

tradeGoods.zh: 谷物、牲畜、蜂蜜、蜂蜡、皮革、象牙、盐、黑曜石、金属器、珠饰，以及输入的纺织品、酒类、奢侈器物和部分南阿拉伯商品。
tradeGoods.en: Grain, livestock, honey, beeswax, hides, ivory, salt, obsidian, metal tools, and beads, alongside imported textiles, wine, prestige objects, and some South Arabian goods.

name.zh: 以物易物与金属衡量并用

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要以牲畜数量、谷仓储备、土地使用权、婚姻联盟和金属器物衡量。高地首领和沿海贸易家族明显富于普通农牧民。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was measured chiefly in livestock numbers, grain stores, land-use rights, marriage alliances, and metal goods. Highland chiefs and coastal trading families were clearly wealthier than ordinary farmers and herders.

economicSystem.zh: 以部族再分配、自给生产与区域交换相结合的混合经济。酋长通过贡赋和宴飨巩固权威，贸易利润集中在沿海据点和商路控制者手中。
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy combining tribal redistribution, subsistence production, and regional exchange. Chiefs reinforced authority through tribute and feasting, while trade profits concentrated in coastal stations and among those controlling caravan routes.

source.en: Trade Route Tolls and Transit Dues

category.en: Feasting, Gift Distribution, and Alliance Maintenance

treasuryDescription.zh: “国库”并非集中于单一都城，而是分散储存在酋长谷仓、牲畜群、祭司保管的贵重物与沿海贸易据点库存中。财富以实物与贵重交换品为主，而非铸币。
treasuryDescription.en: The 'treasury' was not centralized in one capital, but dispersed among chiefly granaries, herds, priest-held valuables, and stocks at coastal trade stations. Wealth was held mainly in goods and prestige exchange items rather than coined money.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政目标偏向维持稳定、储备余粮和巩固联盟，而不是大规模扩张。丰年积储、灾年再分配是首领合法性的关键来源。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on stability, surplus storage, and alliance maintenance rather than large-scale expansion. Storing in good years and redistributing in bad years was a key source of chiefly legitimacy.

description.zh: 由酋长家族供养的常备武装，负责护卫首领、保卫核心聚落与押送贡赋和商队。
description.en: Full-time armed followers maintained by chiefly households, used to guard leaders, defend core settlements, and escort tribute and caravans.

description.zh: 由自由农牧民临时征集，装备长矛、盾牌、短刀和投射武器，适于山地防御和突袭战。
description.en: Temporarily mobilized free farmers and herders armed with spears, shields, knives, and missile weapons, suited to hill fighting and raiding.

description.zh: 来自低地与半游牧群体，擅长侦察、追击、护送与边境袭扰，机动性较强。
description.en: Drawn from lowland and semi-pastoral groups, these warriors specialized in scouting, pursuit, escort, and frontier raiding, with relatively strong mobility.

description.zh: 驻守沿海节点和交通要地，保护货栈、淡水点与港湾交易场所。
description.en: Stationed at coastal nodes and strategic passages to protect depots, water sources, and harbor market areas.

commanderInChief.zh: 无统一最高统帅，由各地酋长分别统领
commanderInChief.en: No single commander-in-chief; forces were led by local chiefs

technology.zh: 军事技术以铁器和青铜器并用的长矛、刀剑、皮革盾和简易弓投枪为主，不具备希腊化国家那样的攻城器与重装方阵传统，但非常适应高地地形和局部冲突。
technology.en: Military technology centered on iron and bronze spears, blades, leather shields, simple bows, and javelins. It lacked the siege equipment and heavy phalanx traditions of Hellenistic states, but was well adapted to highland terrain and local conflict.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近部族竞争、草场与水源冲突、商路争夺，以及红海两岸势力变化带来的贸易扰动；暂无来自希腊化世界的直接征服压力。
threats.en: Primary threats came from neighboring tribal competition, disputes over pasture and water, struggles over trade routes, and trade disruptions caused by shifting powers around the Red Sea; there was no immediate direct conquest pressure from the Hellenistic world.

populationDescription.zh: 人口分散于埃塞俄比亚—厄立特里亚高地、山间谷地、红海沿岸据点与若干低地牧场。整体密度不高，但高地农业区已形成较稳定聚落网络。
populationDescription.en: The population was dispersed across the Ethiopian-Eritrean highlands, intermontane valleys, Red Sea coastal points, and some lowland pasture zones. Overall density was modest, but the highland agricultural zones had already developed relatively stable settlement networks.

name.zh: 叶海一带宗教—政治聚落
马塔拉前身型高地聚落群
阿杜利斯前身型沿海贸易点
阿克勒古扎伊高地聚落群
特克泽—高地边缘集市聚落

socialClasses.zh: 主要阶层包括酋长与贵族氏族、祭司和圣所管理者、武装随从、自由农民、牧民、工匠、商旅、依附民与战俘。社会地位常与血缘、牲畜占有、土地控制和宗教威望相关。
socialClasses.en: Main social strata included chiefly and noble lineages, priests and sanctuary keepers, armed retainers, free cultivators, herders, artisans, merchants, dependents, and captives. Status was closely tied to kinship, livestock ownership, land control, and religious prestige.

allies.zh: 无固定统一盟友；实际关系以邻近部族婚盟、与南阿拉伯海上商人合作，以及同部分库施特和尼罗特边缘群体的临时协定为主。
allies.en: There were no fixed unified allies; in practice, relations consisted of marriage alliances with neighboring tribes, cooperation with South Arabian seaborne merchants, and temporary agreements with some Cushitic and Nilotic frontier groups.

enemies.zh: 无统一国家级宿敌，但与周边争夺水源、放牧地和商路的部族常发生冲突；对外需警惕红海贸易竞争和北方尼罗河流域势力间接影响。
enemies.en: There was no single state-level archenemy, but conflicts were frequent with neighboring groups competing for water, grazing land, and trade routes; externally, Red Sea commercial rivalry and indirect influence from northern Nile powers required caution.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交以务实为主，重在维持商路、换取外来奢侈品和金属资源、避免被更强外部势力卷入，同时通过婚盟与馈赠稳固地区平衡。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy was pragmatic, aimed at preserving trade routes, obtaining imported prestige goods and metals, avoiding entanglement with stronger outside powers, and stabilizing the region through marriage alliances and gift exchange.

era.zh: 铁器时代早期至中期的高地—红海过渡阶段，属希腊化时代同时代的东北非地方发展谱系
era.en: An early-to-middle Iron Age highland-Red Sea transitional stage, part of a local Northeast African developmental trajectory contemporary with the Hellenistic era

keyInnovations.zh: 梯田农业、混合农牧体系、养蜂与蜂蜜酒生产、石基建筑、区域商路组织、早期跨红海交流，以及有限的南阿拉伯文字和建筑风格输入。
keyInnovations.en: Terraced agriculture, mixed agro-pastoral systems, beekeeping and mead production, stone-based architecture, regional caravan organization, early cross-Red Sea exchange, and limited importation of South Arabian scripts and architectural styles.

strengths.zh: 优势在于高地生态适应力强、农牧结合提高韧性、贸易位置优越、氏族与宗教网络能快速动员人力，并具备未来国家形成的社会基础。
strengths.en: Strengths included strong adaptation to the highland environment, resilience from mixed farming and herding, an advantageous trade position, clan and religious networks capable of mobilizing labor quickly, and social foundations for future state formation.

weaknesses.zh: 弱点是政治高度分散、缺乏统一税制与常备官僚机构、军力难以持续集中、书写和行政记录有限，导致大型工程和长期对外战争能力不足。
weaknesses.en: Weaknesses were extreme political fragmentation, the absence of a unified tax system and permanent bureaucracy, difficulty sustaining concentrated military force, and limited writing and record-keeping, all of which constrained large projects and prolonged foreign warfare.

outlook.zh: 323年前后总体稳定，若红海贸易继续扩展且高地首领能够整合宗教权威、商路与农业核心区，未来数世纪内有望向更集中化的前阿克苏姆政权演化。
outlook.en: Around 323 BCE the sphere was generally stable. If Red Sea trade continued to expand and highland rulers were able to integrate religious authority, trade routes, and agricultural core zones, it could evolve over the coming centuries toward a more centralized proto-Aksumite polity.

description.zh: 非洲之角存在多支高地与沿海社群，尚未形成统一帝国。
description.en: The Horn of Africa contained multiple highland and coastal societies, not yet unified into a single empire.

governmentForm.zh: 以君主为核心、结合宫廷贵族、祭司集团与地方总督的努比亚世袭王国
governmentForm.en: A hereditary Nubian kingdom centered on the monarch, combining court nobility, priestly institutions, and provincial governors

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王室与贵族、祭司、军政官员、工匠与商人、农民牧民以及依附劳役人口构成；尼罗河谷定居农业与草原牧业并存。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of the royal house and nobility, priests, military and administrative officials, artisans and merchants, peasants and pastoralists, and dependent laborers; settled Nile agriculture coexisted with savanna pastoralism.

rulingClass.zh: 麦罗埃王室、地方贵族、大神庙祭司与高级军政官员
rulingClass.en: The Meroitic royal family, provincial nobility, major temple priesthoods, and senior military-administrative officials

succession.zh: 王位通常在王族内部世袭，继承受王室血统、宫廷支持与宗教合法性共同影响，王太后与王族女性常具重要作用。
succession.en: Succession was generally hereditary within the royal line, shaped by royal blood, court support, and religious legitimacy; queen mothers and royal women often played important roles.

structure.zh: 王廷设于麦罗埃，由国王统领中央宫廷、神庙体系与地方行政。国家通过尼罗河沿岸中心、要塞、税站和地方官管理核心地区，对边缘地区则依赖附属首领与驻军。
structure.en: The court at Meroë directed the central palace, temple system, and provincial administration. The state governed the core regions through Nile centers, fortresses, toll stations, and local officials, while relying on subordinate chiefs and garrisons in peripheral zones.

function.zh: 管理王室诏令、贡赋调配、宫廷仓储、使节接待与内廷事务
function.en: Managed royal decrees, tribute allocation, palace storage, reception of envoys, and internal court affairs

function.zh: 主持阿蒙等神祇祭祀、管理神庙地产、祭司任命与宗教节庆
function.en: Oversaw the worship of Amun and other deities, temple estates, priestly appointments, and religious festivals

function.zh: 征收谷物、牲畜、黄金、铁器与商税，并维持国家仓廪与储备
function.en: Collected grain, livestock, gold, iron goods, and trade tolls, and maintained state granaries and reserves

function.zh: 监督诺巴提亚、尼罗河中上游及草原地带的地方官、要塞与附属首领
function.en: Supervised local officials, forts, and subordinate chiefs in Nobatia, the Middle and Upper Nile, and adjoining savanna zones

function.zh: 管理驻军、征召、边境哨所、战象与弓箭兵补给
function.en: Managed garrisons, mobilization, frontier posts, and the supply of war elephants and archers

function.zh: 监管尼罗河运输、商队通行、红海与内陆贸易线路及关卡征费
function.en: Supervised Nile transport, caravan passage, Red Sea and inland trade routes, and customs collection

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理以河谷城镇、要塞和神庙中心为骨架。各地区由总督、堡寨长官、税吏与神庙书记共同管理；更南方和沙漠边缘地区则常通过盟属部族首领间接统治。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was built around riverine towns, forts, and temple centers. Provinces were managed by governors, fortress commanders, tax officials, and temple scribes; farther southern and desert-edge areas were often ruled indirectly through allied tribal chiefs.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以王命、习惯法与神庙司法并行。土地、贡赋、婚姻、继承和贸易争端多由地方官与祭司裁断，重大案件由王廷裁决。
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined royal command, customary law, and temple justice. Land, tribute, marriage, inheritance, and trade disputes were usually judged by local officials and priests, while major cases were decided at court.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收主要以实物征收，包括谷物、牲畜、金砂、铁器、手工业品和劳役；贸易节点征收通行税与关税，附属地区则输送贡品。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation was collected mainly in kind, including grain, livestock, gold dust, iron goods, craft products, and labor service; trade points levied tolls and customs, while subordinate regions sent tribute.

religion.zh: 国家宗教融合努比亚传统与埃及化神祇崇拜，以阿蒙、伊西斯、奥西里斯、阿佩德马克等神最为重要；王权被视为受神明认可。
religion.en: State religion blended Nubian traditions with Egyptianized cults, with Amun, Isis, Osiris, and Apedemak among the most important deities; kingship was regarded as divinely sanctioned.

philosophy.zh: 库施并无如希腊式系统哲学学派，其思想核心在于王权神授、祖先传统、神庙秩序、土地丰饶与尼罗河周期所体现的宇宙和谐。
philosophy.en: Kush did not possess systematic philosophical schools in the Greek sense; its intellectual outlook centered on divine kingship, ancestral tradition, temple order, fertility of the land, and the cosmic harmony reflected in the Nile cycle.

writingSystem.zh: 本时期仍广泛使用埃及圣书体、僧侣体以及地方行政中的埃及语书写传统；麦罗埃文字正处于形成与早期发展阶段。
writingSystem.en: This period still made broad use of Egyptian hieroglyphic and hieratic writing traditions in formal and administrative contexts; the Meroitic script was in its formative and early developmental stage.

culturalAchievements.zh: 麦罗埃时期在金字塔墓葬、神庙建筑、铁器冶炼、王室艺术、象牙与金工制品方面成就突出，并延续了努比亚—埃及混合的高等级礼仪文化。
culturalAchievements.en: The Meroitic age excelled in pyramid burials, temple construction, ironworking, royal art, and ivory and gold craftsmanship, while sustaining an elite culture that fused Nubian and Egyptian traditions.

languageFamily.zh: 宫廷与宗教书写深受埃及语影响，民间语言属于东北非语境中的努比亚—库施地方语言群；麦罗埃语为本土王国语言。
languageFamily.en: Court and religious writing was heavily influenced by Egyptian, while spoken languages belonged to local Nubian-Kushitic linguistic environments of Northeast Africa; Meroitic was the native language of the kingdom.

gdpDescription.zh: 库施经济在上尼罗河地区属高度发达的区域性王国经济，依托农业、牧业、黄金、铁冶炼、象牙与过境贸易。与地中海诸国相比总量有限，但在东北非内陆具有显著实力。
gdpDescription.en: The Kushite economy was a highly developed regional kingdom economy in the Upper Nile, based on agriculture, pastoralism, gold, iron smelting, ivory, and transit trade. Its total output was modest compared with major Mediterranean powers, but substantial within inland Northeast Africa.

mainIndustries.zh: 灌溉与洪泛农业、牲畜牧养、金矿采集、铁器冶炼、陶器制造、石工建筑、象牙与乌木加工、河运与商队贸易
mainIndustries.en: Floodplain and irrigated agriculture, livestock herding, gold extraction, iron smelting, pottery production, stone construction, ivory and ebony working, river transport, and caravan trade

tradeGoods.zh: 黄金、铁器、牲畜、谷物、象牙、乌木、豹皮、奴隶、羽毛、香料与来自非洲内陆的珍稀物产；输入酒、油、精制陶器、奢侈品与部分地中海制成品。
tradeGoods.en: Gold, iron goods, livestock, grain, ivory, ebony, leopard skins, slaves, feathers, aromatics, and rare products from inner Africa; imports included wine, oil, fine pottery, luxuries, and some Mediterranean manufactures.

name.zh: 金属重量与实物交换体系

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富多体现为谷仓存粮、牛羊、农具、住房与亲族网络；精英财富则集中于黄金、土地、奴仆、祭祀地产与奢侈器物。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth was held mainly in stored grain, cattle and sheep, tools, housing, and kinship networks; elite wealth was concentrated in gold, land, dependents, temple estates, and luxury goods.

economicSystem.zh: 以王室再分配、神庙地产、地方贡赋和市场交换并存的混合经济；国家在矿产、长途贸易节点和大规模仓储中具有强大影响力。
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy combining royal redistribution, temple estates, provincial tribute, and market exchange; the state exercised strong influence over minerals, long-distance trade nodes, and large storage systems.

source.en: Gold, mineral, and iron production income

source.zh: 附属地区贡品与战利收入
source.en: Tribute from subordinate regions and occasional war gains

category.en: Irrigation, river transport, and public works
Diplomacy, gift exchange, and route maintenance

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库主要以谷物储备、牲畜、金砂、金属库存、象牙和神庙及王室仓储形式存在，而非完全以铸币或银锭保存。麦罗埃作为都城兼工匠中心，能维持较强财政韧性。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury existed mainly as grain reserves, livestock, gold dust, metal stocks, ivory, and palace and temple stores rather than entirely as coined money or bullion. As both capital and craft center, Meroë gave the kingdom notable fiscal resilience.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏重储粮、维持边防、支持神庙与王权工程，并在丰年积累库存以应对旱灾、贸易中断或边疆动荡。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy emphasized stockpiling grain, maintaining the frontiers, supporting temples and royal works, and building reserves in good years to withstand drought, trade disruption, or border unrest.

description.zh: 由长矛兵、盾兵与部分佩剑步卒组成，是守城、列阵和护卫补给线的主力。
description.en: Composed of spearmen, shield-bearers, and some sword-armed infantry, forming the main force for garrison duty, line battle, and supply protection.

description.zh: 以射术闻名，是库施军最具代表性的兵种，适合河谷防御、伏击与机动远射。
description.en: Famed for their marksmanship, they were the signature arm of Kushite warfare, well suited to riverine defense, ambushes, and mobile missile combat.

description.zh: 数量不大，主要用于侦察、边境巡逻、追击与沙漠交通线护卫。
description.en: Limited in number, they were used chiefly for reconnaissance, frontier patrols, pursuit, and protection of desert routes.

description.zh: 战象数量有限，多用于威慑、王家象征与特定战役；近卫军负责护卫国王与都城核心。
description.en: War elephants were few and used for prestige, intimidation, and selected campaigns; the guard protected the king and the core of the capital.

description.zh: 部署于尼罗河节点、南方通道和沙漠边缘据点，维持治安、征税与通商安全。
description.en: Stationed at Nile nodes, southern corridors, and desert-edge posts to maintain order, enforce taxation, and secure commerce.

commanderInChief.zh: 阿尔卡马尼一世国王
commanderInChief.en: King Arqamani I

technology.zh: 军备以复合地方传统的弓、矛、盾、皮甲和部分金属甲具为主，熟悉尼罗河行军与要塞防御；铁器制造能力增强了武器供给，但整体仍非地中海式重装军。
technology.en: Military equipment centered on bows, spears, shields, leather armor, and some metal protection, supported by expertise in Nile campaigning and fortress defense; growing iron production improved weapon supply, though the army was not a Mediterranean-style heavy force.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自北方埃及—马其顿势力可能的南侵、沙漠与边疆部族袭扰、商路争夺以及南方附属地区离心。323 BCE时亚历山大死后北方政局不稳，既带来风险也提供缓冲。
threats.en: Main threats came from possible southern pressure by the Egyptian-Macedonian sphere, raiding by desert and frontier tribes, struggles over trade routes, and centrifugal tendencies in southern subordinate regions. In 323 BCE, the instability following Alexander's death brought both danger and breathing space.

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中在尼罗河两岸可耕带、麦罗埃周边及若干宗教与军事中心，另有大量分散于草原与半干旱地区的牧民群体。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated mainly in the cultivable Nile corridor, around Meroë, and in several religious and military centers, with substantial pastoral groups dispersed across savanna and semi-arid zones.

socialClasses.zh: 上层为王室、贵族与大神庙祭司；中层为军官、书记、工匠首领、商人和地方官；下层为农民、牧民、普通工匠与劳役人口，另有奴隶与战俘构成的依附群体。
socialClasses.en: The upper stratum consisted of the royal house, nobility, and major temple priests; the middle included officers, scribes, master artisans, merchants, and local officials; the lower comprised peasants, pastoralists, ordinary craftsmen, and laborers, along with dependent slaves and war captives.

allies.zh: 库施较少拥有固定意义上的远程正式盟友，更多依靠与南方部族、红海通道社群及部分边缘首领的朝贡—互惠关系。
allies.en: Kush had few fixed long-distance formal allies, relying instead on tributary-reciprocal relationships with southern tribes, Red Sea corridor communities, and various frontier chiefs.

enemies.zh: 潜在敌对者主要是控制埃及的马其顿化政权、争夺商路的沙漠部族，以及不稳定的边疆附属群体。
enemies.en: Its principal potential enemies were the Macedonian-controlled power in Egypt, desert peoples competing over trade routes, and unstable frontier dependents.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以控制上尼罗河、维持黄金与象牙贸易、避免被北方强权压制、并通过馈赠、婚姻、驻军和神庙威望维系周边秩序为核心。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on controlling the Upper Nile, sustaining the gold and ivory trade, avoiding domination by northern great powers, and maintaining regional order through gifts, marriage ties, garrisons, and temple prestige.

era.zh: 希腊化时代早期的东北非铁器王国阶段
era.en: An early Hellenistic Northeast African Iron Age kingdom phase

keyInnovations.zh: 高水平铁冶炼、洪泛农业管理、石砌与砖砌建筑、长途商队组织、河运补给体系，以及逐渐成形的麦罗埃文字与本土行政书写传统。
keyInnovations.en: Advanced iron smelting, floodplain agricultural management, stone and brick construction, long-distance caravan organization, river transport logistics, and the emerging Meroitic script and native administrative writing tradition.

strengths.zh: 地处上尼罗河与非洲内陆贸易枢纽，拥有黄金、铁器和牲畜资源；王权、神庙与地方贵族结合形成较稳定统治；弓箭兵传统强，边疆适应力高。
strengths.en: Kush occupied a strategic Upper Nile and inner African trade nexus, possessed gold, iron, and livestock resources, and was stabilized by cooperation among kingship, temples, and provincial elites; its archer tradition and frontier adaptability were major strengths.

weaknesses.zh: 人口与财政规模有限，行政整合度不及更官僚化的大帝国；对尼罗河与商路依赖显著；边远地区控制多靠间接统治，易受北方强权与气候波动影响。
weaknesses.en: Its demographic and fiscal scale was limited, administrative integration lagged behind more bureaucratic empires, dependence on the Nile and trade routes was high, and peripheral control often rested on indirect rule, leaving it vulnerable to northern great powers and climatic fluctuations.

outlook.zh: 323 BCE时库施正处于繁荣上升阶段。若能利用亚历山大死后埃及与东地中海权力重组的空隙，维持北部边境并扩大贸易控制，麦罗埃王国在随后数代仍有望持续强盛。
outlook.en: In 323 BCE Kush was in a phase of rising prosperity. If it could exploit the power reordering in Egypt and the eastern Mediterranean after Alexander's death, preserve its northern frontier, and expand control over trade, the kingdom of Meroë could remain strong for generations.

description.zh: 上尼罗河的重要王国，以麦罗埃为中心延续努比亚国家传统。
description.en: A major Upper Nile kingdom centered on Meroë, continuing the Nubian state tradition.

governmentForm.zh: 以绿洲都城为核心的部族君主制，王权依托宗族贵族、绿洲控制与商道征收而运作。
governmentForm.en: A tribal monarchy centered on an oasis capital, with royal power sustained through clan nobility, oasis control, and toll collection on caravan routes.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王室与贵族家族、武装随从、定居农民、工匠与商旅，以及被支配的劳役人口构成；绿洲居民与沙漠游动群体之间保持紧密联系。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of the royal house and noble lineages, armed retainers, settled farmers, artisans and caravan merchants, and subordinate laboring populations; oasis dwellers remained closely tied to mobile desert groups.

rulingClass.zh: 王室、主要氏族首领、绿洲贵族与控制水源和车队通道的地方精英。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class comprised the royal family, leading clan chiefs, oasis nobility, and local elites who controlled water sources and caravan passages.

succession.zh: 推定为世袭君主制，王位大体在王族内部传承，并需获得主要氏族与地方贵族承认。
succession.en: Succession was likely hereditary within the royal house, with the new king needing recognition from major clans and local nobles.

structure.zh: 政权组织较为简朴，中央宫廷直接统辖都城及核心绿洲，通过地方首领、亲族代理人与贡赋网络管理外围聚落和井泉地带。
structure.en: The state structure was comparatively simple: the central court directly controlled the capital and core oases, while peripheral settlements and well-zones were managed through local chiefs, royal kinsmen, and tribute networks.

function.zh: 处理王令、接待使者、组织祭祀、分配贡赋与协调各氏族关系。
function.en: Handled royal orders, received envoys, organized rituals, allocated tribute, and coordinated relations among clans.

function.zh: 维护地下引水渠道、井泉、农地分配与灌溉秩序，是国家生存的关键部门。
function.en: Maintained underground water channels, wells, land allocation, and irrigation order; it was essential to state survival.

function.zh: 监管穿越费赞地区的商旅、征收通行贡税、保障驿站与补给点运行。
function.en: Supervised caravans crossing the Fezzan region, collected transit dues, and maintained stations and supply points.

function.zh: 征收谷物、枣椰、牲畜与手工业品，储备王室与军队所需物资。
function.en: Collected grain, dates, livestock, and craft goods, and stored supplies for the court and military.

function.zh: 组织战车、骑乘与步战力量，巡护井泉、商道与臣属部落地区。
function.en: Organized chariot, mounted, and infantry forces and patrolled wells, caravan routes, and dependent tribal zones.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以绿洲聚落为单位，由王室任命的代表、附庸首领或地方贵族管理；偏远地区更多依赖盟约、贡纳和人质关系而非严格官僚控制。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was organized by oasis settlements under royal representatives, subordinate chiefs, or local nobles; distant areas relied more on alliance, tribute, and hostage ties than on strict bureaucratic control.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法、王室裁决和氏族仲裁为基础，涉及水权、土地使用、贡赋、婚姻与商旅纠纷；重大案件由国王或贵族议断。
legalSystem.en: The legal system rested on customary law, royal judgment, and clan arbitration, covering water rights, land use, tribute, marriage, and caravan disputes; major cases were decided by the king or noble councils.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收主要以实物和劳役形式征收，包括农产贡赋、牲畜贡、商队通行费、水源使用义务及对附属群体的周期性征发。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation was collected chiefly in kind and labor, including agricultural tribute, livestock dues, caravan tolls, water-use obligations, and periodic levies on subordinate groups.

religion.zh: 宗教以本地撒哈拉信仰为主，崇敬祖先、地方神灵、泉源与墓葬圣地；同时与地中海世界接触，可能吸收部分利比亚—柏柏尔及迦太基宗教元素。
religion.en: Religion was primarily indigenous Saharan, venerating ancestors, local deities, springs, and funerary sacred places; contact with the Mediterranean likely introduced some Libyan-Berber and Carthaginian elements.

philosophy.zh: 不存在成体系的哲学学派，思想传统主要体现为部族习俗、祖先威望、王权正当性、水与土地秩序观念，以及对商旅信誉的重视。
philosophy.en: There was no formal philosophical school; intellectual tradition was expressed through tribal custom, ancestral prestige, royal legitimacy, concepts of water and land order, and the high value placed on caravan trustworthiness.

writingSystem.zh: 书写使用有限，可能采用早期利比亚字母或借助腓尼基—布匿文化接触形成的记号体系，但行政与文化传承主要依靠口述。
writingSystem.en: Writing was limited, possibly using early Libyan script or marking systems influenced through Phoenician-Punic contact, though administration and cultural transmission remained predominantly oral.

culturalAchievements.zh: 最突出的成就是在极端干旱环境中发展绿洲农业与地下引水工程，建立跨撒哈拉商路节点，并形成独特的墓葬、聚落与战车传统。
culturalAchievements.en: Its greatest achievements were the development of oasis agriculture and underground water-engineering in an extremely arid environment, the creation of trans-Saharan trade nodes, and distinctive funerary, settlement, and chariot traditions.

languageFamily.zh: 语言大体属于古代利比亚—柏柏尔相关语支，具体形态不明，并与周边撒哈拉和北非语言群持续接触。
languageFamily.en: The language likely belonged to an ancient Libyan-Berber related branch, though its exact form is uncertain, and it remained in contact with surrounding Saharan and North African speech communities.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济规模在地中海大国面前较小，但在撒哈拉腹地属于高度组织化的绿洲贸易经济。财富来源并非大面积农耕，而是灌溉农业、牲畜、区域中转与商路控制的结合。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was small compared with Mediterranean great powers but highly organized for an oasis trade state in the Sahara. Wealth came not from broad arable plains, but from a combination of irrigated agriculture, livestock, regional exchange, and control of caravan routes.

mainIndustries.zh: 枣椰与谷物农业、畜牧、皮革与纺织、盐与地方矿产转运、车队服务、陶器及简单金属加工。
mainIndustries.en: Date and grain agriculture, pastoralism, leatherwork and textiles, transit of salt and local minerals, caravan services, pottery, and simple metalworking.

tradeGoods.zh: 输出枣椰、牲畜、皮革、盐、半宝石与撒哈拉转运货物；输入金属器、精制陶器、织物、酒、橄榄油及其他地中海制成品。
tradeGoods.en: Exports included dates, livestock, leather, salt, semiprecious stones, and Saharan transit goods; imports included metal tools, fine pottery, textiles, wine, olive oil, and other Mediterranean manufactures.

name.zh: 称量银与杂用外来钱币

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富以椰枣园使用权、牲畜、粮食储备、井泉份额和亲族关系体现，而非大量铸币储蓄。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was measured mainly in access to date groves, livestock, grain stores, shares in wells, and kinship ties rather than in large coin savings.

economicSystem.zh: 以宫廷与部族精英控制水利和商道的再分配经济为核心，同时存在家庭农业、牧养和长距离交换；市场机制存在但受地理与权力结构强烈约束。
economicSystem.en: The economy centered on a redistributive system in which the court and tribal elites controlled water and caravan routes, alongside household farming, herding, and long-distance exchange; market behavior existed but was strongly constrained by geography and power structures.

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库以实物储备和贵金属并存，包含粮仓、枣椰储藏、牲畜、外来银器与少量钱币。对沙漠国家而言，稳定水利与食物储备比铸币存量更重要。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury combined stores in kind with precious metal holdings, including granaries, date reserves, livestock, imported silverware, and limited coinage. For a desert state, secure waterworks and food reserves mattered more than large monetary stockpiles.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏保守，重视维持绿洲灌溉、保障商路安全与积蓄灾年储备；在稳定年份保持小幅盈余，以应对旱灾或部族冲突。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was conservative, emphasizing oasis irrigation, caravan security, and reserve accumulation for bad years; in stable years the kingdom sought modest surpluses to prepare for drought or tribal conflict.

description.zh: 由都城与核心绿洲征发的步战力量组成，负责守卫聚落、井泉、仓储与近程作战，装备以矛、盾和短兵器为主。
description.en: Composed of troops levied from the capital and core oases, this force guarded settlements, wells, and stores and handled close-range fighting, armed mainly with spears, shields, and short weapons.

description.zh: 延续加拉曼特传统的精锐机动力量，用于沙漠追击、侦察、袭扰与控制开阔地带，兼具象征王权的作用。
description.en: An elite mobile arm continuing Garamantian traditions, used for desert pursuit, reconnaissance, raiding, and control of open terrain, while also symbolizing royal authority.

description.zh: 由地方首领与附庸群体提供，承担商队护送、哨站值守和边远绿洲巡逻任务。
description.en: Supplied by local chiefs and dependent groups, these troops escorted caravans, manned outposts, and patrolled distant oases.

commanderInChief.zh: 加拉曼特人之王
commanderInChief.en: King of the Garamantes

technology.zh: 军事技术以北非铁器时代水平为主，善于沙漠机动、井泉利用和远程穿越，但缺乏地中海大国式重装攻城能力。
technology.en: Military technology was typical of North Africa's Iron Age, with strengths in desert mobility, use of wells, and long-range movement, but lacking the heavy siege capabilities of major Mediterranean powers.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自撒哈拉内部部族竞争、商道争夺、旱灾引发的资源冲突，以及北方沿海强权势力间接影响内陆贸易格局。
threats.en: Main threats came from rival Saharan tribes, competition over caravan routes, resource conflicts triggered by drought, and indirect disruption of inland trade by powerful states on the northern coast.

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要分布在费赞绿洲带及其周边，密集聚居于可灌溉区域，另有相当部分人口保持半流动或季节性迁徙生活方式。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated in the Fezzan oasis belt and its surroundings, densely settled where irrigation was possible, while a significant share lived semi-mobile or seasonally migratory lives.

socialClasses.zh: 社会阶层包括王室与贵族、地方首领、自由农牧民、工匠与商人、依附民及奴役或被征服人口。水源控制权是区分地位的重要标准。
socialClasses.en: Social classes included the royal family and nobility, local chiefs, free farmers and pastoralists, artisans and merchants, dependents, and enslaved or conquered populations. Control over water sources was a key marker of status.

allies.zh: 无固定意义上的远程正式盟友；更常见的是与邻近绿洲群体、撒哈拉部族及北非商人维持务实合作关系，并与迦太基势力圈保持间接贸易联系。
allies.en: There were no formal long-distance allies in the strict sense; more typical were pragmatic ties with neighboring oasis groups, Saharan tribes, and North African merchants, along with indirect trade links to the Carthaginian sphere.

enemies.zh: 无持续性的单一国家宿敌，但与争夺井泉、贡纳和商道的沙漠群体时常发生冲突；在北方，可能与利比亚沿海势力关系时紧时松。
enemies.en: There was no single permanent state enemy, but conflict was frequent with desert groups competing for wells, tribute, and caravan routes; to the north, relations with Libyan coastal powers were variable.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以控制商道、确保绿洲安全和维持贸易中立为核心，避免卷入地中海大国争霸，同时通过贡礼、婚盟和护商协议扩展影响力。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on controlling caravan routes, securing oasis zones, and preserving trade neutrality, while avoiding entanglement in Mediterranean great-power struggles and extending influence through gifts, marriages, and caravan-protection agreements.

era.zh: 希腊化时期
era.en: Hellenistic Period

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术在于地下引水渠道、井泉管理、干旱环境农艺、长距离沙漠通行经验及适应沙漠地形的车乘与畜力组织。
keyInnovations.en: Key innovations lay in underground water channels, well management, arid-environment agriculture, expertise in long-distance desert travel, and the organization of chariotry and animal transport suited to desert terrain.

strengths.zh: 最大优势是对撒哈拉绿洲和交通节点的掌控、适应极端环境的水利技术，以及作为内陆贸易中介所积累的区域影响力。
strengths.en: Its greatest strengths were control of Saharan oases and transit nodes, water-management techniques suited to extreme conditions, and regional influence built through inland trade intermediation.

weaknesses.zh: 弱点在于人口与资源基础有限、政治整合依赖部族忠诚、书面行政能力较弱，并高度受气候波动与商路转移影响。
weaknesses.en: Weaknesses included a limited population and resource base, political integration dependent on tribal loyalty, weak written administrative capacity, and strong vulnerability to climatic fluctuation and shifts in trade routes.

outlook.zh: 公元前323年的加拉曼特王国总体稳定，只要水利系统和商路收益得以维持，便能继续作为中撒哈拉的重要绿洲国家存在；但其长期前景取决于环境承载力与北非贸易格局变化。
outlook.en: In 323 BCE the Garamantian kingdom was broadly stable, and if its water systems and caravan revenues were maintained it could continue as a significant oasis state of the central Sahara; its long-term prospects, however, depended on environmental capacity and changes in North African trade patterns.

description.zh: 撒哈拉中部绿洲国家，经营跨沙漠交通与灌溉农业。
description.en: An oasis state in the central Sahara sustained by trans-Saharan routes and irrigated agriculture.

governmentForm.zh: 名义上隶属亚历山大帝国、由马其顿军政精英控制并沿用巴比伦官僚体系的总督制王国
governmentForm.en: A satrapal kingdom nominally within Alexander's empire, controlled by Macedonian military-administrative elites while retaining the Babylonian bureaucratic system

socialStructure.zh: 社会由马其顿—希腊军事统治层、巴比伦与亚述城市祭司及书记官群体、地方地主、依附农民、城市工匠与商人，以及多族裔驻军与移民组成。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of a Macedonian-Greek military ruling stratum, Babylonian and Assyrian urban priestly and scribal groups, local landholders, dependent cultivators, urban craftsmen and merchants, and multiethnic garrisons and migrants.

rulingClass.zh: 马其顿将领、骑兵贵族、王家伙伴官员，以及与其合作的巴比伦神庙精英和高级书记官
rulingClass.en: Macedonian generals, cavalry aristocrats, royal companion officials, and cooperating Babylonian temple elites and senior scribes

succession.zh: 总督职位原则上由帝国中央或军队会议任命，实际上取决于马其顿诸将权力斗争与驻军支持，并非稳定世袭
succession.en: The satrapy was in principle assigned by the imperial center or army council, but in practice depended on struggles among Macedonian commanders and support of the garrison, rather than stable hereditary succession

structure.zh: 政体以巴比伦城为行政中枢，上层由马其顿总督、军队指挥官和王室财政代理人掌权，下层则沿用阿契美尼德与新巴比伦传统的行省、城市、神庙与地方税务网络。
structure.en: The polity was centered administratively on Babylon, with authority held at the top by the Macedonian satrap, military commanders, and royal fiscal agents, while lower administration continued through Achaemenid and Neo-Babylonian provincial, urban, temple, and local tax networks.

function.zh: 处理政令、任命、行省文书、与各城及地方官员的往来
function.en: Handled decrees, appointments, provincial documentation, and correspondence with cities and local officials

function.zh: 征收贡赋、管理银锭与谷物储备、监督铸币与仓储
function.en: Collected tribute, managed bullion and grain reserves, and supervised minting and storage

function.zh: 协调大神庙地产、祭祀供给、楔形文字档案与地方契约登记
function.en: Coordinated major temple estates, cultic provisioning, cuneiform archives, and registration of local contracts

function.zh: 管理驻军、补给、征调、城防与河道军事运输
function.en: Managed garrisons, supplies, levies, fortifications, and military transport along waterways

function.zh: 监督田赋、运河维护、劳役组织与农业统计
function.en: Supervised land taxation, canal maintenance, labor organization, and agricultural records

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理依靠城市长官、地区税吏、神庙管理者和乡村首领共同运作；巴比伦、波尔西帕、尼普尔、乌鲁克等城市保持较强的传统自治与文书连续性。
localAdmin.en: Local administration functioned through city governors, district tax officials, temple managers, and village headmen; cities such as Babylon, Borsippa, Nippur, and Uruk retained strong traditions of local autonomy and documentary continuity.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系呈并行状态：王室与总督命令、阿契美尼德行政惯例、城市习惯法、神庙法庭裁决以及以阿卡德语和阿拉米语记录的契约法同时适用；马其顿军人则更多受军法与王命约束。
legalSystem.en: The legal system was pluralistic: royal and satrapal decrees, Achaemenid administrative practice, urban customary law, temple court judgments, and contract law recorded in Akkadian and Aramaic all remained in force; Macedonian soldiers were more directly subject to military law and royal command.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政收入主要来自田赋、灌溉农业产出、神庙与王室地产收益、商税、关津、牲畜税及非常时期军需征发；征税常以谷物、银锭和实物并行。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue derived chiefly from land tax, irrigated agricultural output, temple and royal estate income, trade dues, transit tolls, livestock taxes, and extraordinary wartime requisitions; taxation was collected in grain, bullion, and other goods.

religion.zh: 宗教生活以传统美索不达米亚多神信仰为主，马尔杜克、那波、伊什塔尔、沙马什等神祇仍受崇拜；同时希腊—马其顿军人带来宙斯、雅典娜、赫拉克勒斯等崇拜，形成有限并存与对应。
religion.en: Religious life remained predominantly Mesopotamian polytheistic, with continued worship of Marduk, Nabu, Ishtar, Shamash, and other deities; Greek-Macedonian soldiers also introduced cults of Zeus, Athena, and Heracles, producing limited coexistence and identification between traditions.

philosophy.zh: 本地思想传统以祭司天文学、神学注释、 omen 学和古典文书记忆为核心；希腊理性探究、地理与医学知识开始进入巴比伦行政与精英交流圈，但尚未完全融合。
philosophy.en: Local intellectual life centered on priestly astronomy, theological commentary, omen scholarship, and preservation of classical texts; Greek rational inquiry, geography, and medical knowledge were beginning to enter Babylonian administrative and elite circles, but had not yet fully fused.

writingSystem.zh: 文字体系并行使用楔形文字与帝国阿拉米字母；官方财税和地方契约多用阿拉米语，神庙学术和传统档案仍大量使用阿卡德语楔形文，希腊语在军政圈逐渐出现。
writingSystem.en: The writing system was dual, employing both cuneiform and Imperial Aramaic script; official fiscal business and many local contracts used Aramaic, while temple scholarship and traditional archives still relied heavily on Akkadian cuneiform, with Greek gradually appearing in military-administrative circles.

culturalAchievements.zh: 巴比伦仍是近东最重要的学术中心之一，在天文观测、历法计算、神庙建筑维护、数学表册编制和长期档案保存方面继续表现突出，并成为希腊人与美索不达米亚知识接触的关键节点。
culturalAchievements.en: Babylon remained one of the Near East's foremost intellectual centers, notable for astronomical observation, calendrical calculation, temple architectural maintenance, mathematical table-making, and long-term archival preservation, while serving as a key point of contact between Greek and Mesopotamian knowledge.

languageFamily.zh: 语言环境多元：阿卡德语属东闪米特语支，阿拉米语属西北闪米特语支，希腊语属印欧语系；行政与商业逐渐向阿拉米语和希腊语倾斜。
languageFamily.en: The linguistic environment was diverse: Akkadian belonged to the East Semitic branch, Aramaic to the Northwest Semitic branch, and Greek to the Indo-European family; administration and commerce increasingly favored Aramaic and Greek.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济高度发达于古代标准之上，依托两河灌溉农业、城市手工业、神庙地产和长距离贸易。323年因继业者冲突初起，经济基础仍强，但政治不稳已开始影响流通和税收。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was highly developed by ancient standards, based on Mesopotamian irrigated agriculture, urban crafts, temple estates, and long-distance trade. In 323 BCE, as the Successor struggles began, the economic base remained strong, though political instability was already affecting circulation and taxation.

mainIndustries.zh: 大麦与小麦种植、椰枣园经营、畜牧、纺织、制陶、金属加工、沥青与芦苇制品、建筑、神庙地产管理、河运与商贸中转
mainIndustries.en: Barley and wheat cultivation, date-palm orchards, pastoralism, textiles, pottery, metalworking, bitumen and reed products, construction, temple estate management, river transport, and commercial transit

tradeGoods.zh: 谷物、椰枣、羊毛织物、油脂、啤酒、陶器、沥青、芦苇制品、银锭、铜锡制品，以及来自伊朗高原、黎凡特和印度边境的奢侈品与原料
tradeGoods.en: Grain, dates, wool textiles, oils, beer, pottery, bitumen, reed products, silver bullion, copper and tin goods, and luxury items and raw materials from the Iranian plateau, the Levant, and the Indian frontier

name.zh: 德拉克马与银锭并用体系

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富差距显著。神庙、地主与商人积累大量土地、奴仆、牲畜和银锭，而普通农户主要拥有农具、牲畜、少量余粮与债务负担。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was highly unequal. Temples, landholders, and merchants accumulated land, dependents, livestock, and bullion, while ordinary farming households typically possessed tools, animals, limited surplus grain, and significant debt burdens.

economicSystem.zh: 这是一个由王室、神庙和私人地主并存的再分配—市场混合经济，农业剩余通过税收、地租和神庙网络集中，再通过军费、工程和贸易流通重新分配。
economicSystem.en: This was a mixed redistributive-market economy in which royal, temple, and private landed sectors coexisted; agricultural surplus was concentrated through taxation, rents, and temple networks, then redistributed through military spending, public works, and trade.

source.en: Land tax and irrigated agricultural dues

treasuryDescription.zh: 巴比伦长期是帝国财库重地，拥有巨额金银储备、谷仓与神庙库存。323年虽仍富庶，但各派将领争夺此地，使财库既是优势也是危险来源。
treasuryDescription.en: Babylon had long been a major imperial treasury center, holding substantial stocks of gold, silver, grain, and temple reserves. In 323 BCE it remained wealthy, but competition among rival commanders made its treasure both an asset and a source of danger.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向维持军队忠诚、确保运河与粮食系统运转，并通过吸纳神庙与地方精英来稳定统治；在冲突背景下，短期内更依赖征发与库存动用而非长期改革。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy prioritized military loyalty, the functioning of canals and grain systems, and political stability through accommodation with temple and local elites; under conflict conditions, the regime relied more on requisitions and treasury drawdowns than on long-term reform.

description.zh: 核心重装步兵，以长矛方阵为主，是总督控制城市与野战的骨干力量。
description.en: Core heavy infantry armed in phalanx formation with long pikes, forming the backbone of urban control and field operations.

description.zh: 由马其顿贵族骑兵、地方骑手和部分雇佣骑兵组成，用于侦察、冲击与追击。
description.en: Composed of Macedonian noble cavalry, local horsemen, and some mercenary riders, used for reconnaissance, shock action, and pursuit.

description.zh: 包括投石手、标枪兵、弓箭手和地方辅助部队，适合守城、巡逻与复杂地形作战。
description.en: Included slingers, javelin-men, archers, and local auxiliaries suited to siege defense, patrol, and fighting in difficult terrain.

description.zh: 分散于巴比伦及两河要地，负责城门、仓库、财库、运河节点与交通线安全。
description.en: Dispersed across Babylon and key Mesopotamian sites, responsible for city gates, depots, treasuries, canal nodes, and communication lines.

description.zh: 由地方征发农民、附庸与临时雇佣者构成，战斗力有限，主要承担守备和运输任务。
description.en: Made up of levied cultivators, dependents, and temporary hires, with limited combat value and mainly used for guarding and transport.

commanderInChief.zh: 总督兼巴比伦军政长官
commanderInChief.en: The Satrap as military and civil commander of Babylon

technology.zh: 军备体现晚期古典至早期希腊化过渡特征：使用萨里沙长矛方阵、复合弓、攻城器械、河运补给与成熟城防工程；地方仍保留近东传统后勤与筑城技术。
technology.en: Military technology reflected the transition from the late Classical to early Hellenistic era: sarissa phalanxes, composite bows, siege engines, riverine logistics, and mature fortification works, alongside enduring Near Eastern traditions of supply and construction.

threats.zh: 最大威胁来自亚历山大死后继业者诸将的争夺、邻近行省总督的干预、驻军忠诚不稳，以及两河地方势力在权力真空中的动员能力。
threats.en: The greatest threats came from struggles among the Successor generals after Alexander's death, interference by neighboring satraps, uncertain garrison loyalty, and the mobilization capacity of local Mesopotamian forces in a power vacuum.

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中于幼发拉底河与底格里斯河灌溉带、城市节点与神庙地产周边。族群包括巴比伦人、亚述人、阿拉米人、马其顿人与希腊人，以及来自帝国各地的军人、商人和工匠。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated mainly in the irrigated belts of the Euphrates and Tigris, urban nodes, and temple-estate zones. Ethnic groups included Babylonians, Assyrians, Aramaeans, Macedonians and Greeks, along with soldiers, merchants, and craftsmen from across the empire.

name.zh: 塞琉西亚前身地区聚落群
name.en: Settlements in the future Seleucia area

socialClasses.zh: 上层为马其顿军政贵族、神庙祭司、大地主与富商；中层为书记官、工匠、自由农、船主与地方官；下层为佃农、债务劳工、雇工、奴仆及战俘。城市与乡村、军人与本地居民之间差异明显。
socialClasses.en: The upper strata consisted of Macedonian military-administrative nobles, temple priests, major landholders, and wealthy merchants; the middle strata included scribes, craftsmen, free cultivators, boat owners, and local officials; the lower strata comprised tenant farmers, debt laborers, hired workers, servants, and war captives. Clear divisions existed between city and countryside and between soldiers and local inhabitants.

allies.zh: 形式上仍与亚历山大帝国其他马其顿行省保持共同体关系，并可能与部分忠于帝国中央的将领合作；但真正稳固的盟友极少。
allies.en: Formally it remained in common cause with other Macedonian satrapies of Alexander's empire and could cooperate with commanders still loyal to the imperial center, but it had few truly reliable allies.

enemies.zh: 敌对者主要是争夺巴比伦财库与战略地位的继业者诸将、可能南下或西进的邻近总督势力，以及任何试图控制两河交通与税源的军阀集团。
enemies.en: Its enemies were primarily the Successor commanders seeking Babylon's treasury and strategic position, neighboring satrapal powers moving south or west, and any warlord coalition attempting to control Mesopotamian routes and revenues.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以守住巴比伦、维持河道与粮道、在诸将之间周旋并通过财库和合法性争取支持为核心，属于高度机会主义且防御性的权力平衡策略。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on holding Babylon, maintaining river and grain routes, maneuvering among rival commanders, and using treasury resources and claims of legitimacy to secure support; it was a highly opportunistic and defensive balance-of-power strategy.

era.zh: 希腊化时代初期
era.en: Early Hellenistic Period

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术优势包括成熟的大规模灌溉与运河管理、精确天文观测与历法、标准化度量衡、楔形文数学表、城防与攻城工程，以及将希腊军事组织与两河行政技术结合的实践。
keyInnovations.en: Key technological strengths included mature large-scale irrigation and canal management, precise astronomical observation and calendrics, standardized weights and measures, cuneiform mathematical tables, fortification and siege engineering, and the practical combination of Greek military organization with Mesopotamian administrative techniques.

strengths.zh: 巴比伦—马其顿政体的优势在于财富雄厚、地处交通枢纽、农业基础强、官僚传统深厚，并拥有能够支撑大军的财库与粮仓体系。
strengths.en: The strengths of the Babylonian-Macedonian polity lay in its great wealth, central strategic location, strong agricultural base, deep bureaucratic traditions, and treasury-granary system capable of supporting large armies.

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点在于统治合法性脆弱、马其顿军人与本地社会整合有限、权力依赖驻军忠诚，且在323年后极易卷入继业者战争，财政与人口都可能因长期内战受损。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses were fragile legitimacy, limited integration between Macedonian soldiers and local society, dependence on garrison loyalty, and extreme vulnerability to the Wars of the Successors after 323 BCE, which could damage both finances and population through prolonged civil conflict.

outlook.zh: 短期前景不稳。巴比伦仍会在数年内保持近东核心地位，但随着继业者冲突升级，该政体大概率被更大规模的希腊化王朝机器吸收，其地方自主性将逐步下降。
outlook.en: The short-term outlook was unstable. Babylon would remain a central Near Eastern power center for some years, but as the Successor conflicts intensified, this polity would likely be absorbed into a larger Hellenistic dynastic apparatus, with its local autonomy gradually diminishing.

description.zh: 两河流域仍是帝国行政中枢，但已成为继业者争夺焦点。
description.en: Mesopotamia remained an imperial administrative center, but was becoming a focal point of successor struggles.

leaderTitle.en: Satrap of Babylon (soon to be appointed)

ideology.zh: 马其顿-波斯融合帝国主义——在亚历山大遗产基础上建立东方希腊化帝国，推行马其顿军事贵族与波斯行政传统的深度融合
ideology.en: Macedonian-Persian fusion imperialism — building an eastern Hellenistic empire on Alexander's legacy, promoting deep integration of Macedonian military aristocracy with Persian administrative traditions

relationship.zh: 塞琉古在巴比伦分封中获得重要职位，并将于公元前312年正式夺取巴比伦总督权。他致力于在两河流域和东方总督区建立稳固的个人权力基础，善于与当地巴比伦祭司阶层和波斯贵族合作，同时维持马其顿军事核心的忠诚。
relationship.en: Seleucus gained an important position in the Babylon partition and would formally seize the satrapy of Babylon by 312 BCE. He sought to build a firm personal power base in Mesopotamia and the eastern satrapies, skillfully cooperating with the local Babylonian priestly class and Persian nobility while maintaining the loyalty of the Macedonian military core.

description.zh: 塞琉古·尼卡托尔是亚历山大的伙伴骑兵军官和近卫指挥官之一，在巴比伦分封中被分配了一个相对次要的职位。然而他凭借出色的政治直觉和军事才能，逐步将自己的权力扩展到从两河流域到中亚和印度河的广大地区。塞琉古最突出的特点是他对马其顿-波斯文化融合的热情——他是唯一保持了与波斯贵族阿帕玛婚姻的继业者将领，其后代继承了希腊与伊朗双重血统。公元前312年被视为塞琉古纪元的起点，当他击败对手重新控制巴比伦时。此后他东征至印度河流域，与旃陀罗笈多达成和约并获得五百头战象，又回师西进在伊普苏斯战役中击败安提柯。塞琉古帝国最终成为继业者国家中疆域最广的一个，从安纳托利亚延伸至中亚，是希腊化文明向东方传播的主要载体。
description.en: Seleucus Nicator was one of Alexander's Companion cavalry officers and bodyguard commanders, assigned a relatively minor role in the Babylon partition. Yet through exceptional political instinct and military skill, he gradually extended his power from Mesopotamia to Central Asia and the Indus River. Seleucus's most distinctive trait was his enthusiasm for Macedonian-Persian cultural fusion — he was the only successor general to maintain his marriage to the Persian noblewoman Apama, and his descendants inherited a dual Greco-Iranian heritage. The year 312 BCE is regarded as the start of the Seleucid Era, when he defeated rivals and regained control of Babylon. He then campaigned east to the Indus valley, concluded a peace treaty with Chandragupta Maurya in exchange for 500 war elephants, and returned west to defeat Antigonus at the Battle of Ipsus. The Seleucid Empire ultimately became the most extensive of the successor states, stretching from Anatolia to Central Asia and serving as the primary vehicle for transmitting Hellenistic civilization eastward.

role.zh: 粟特-巴克特里亚贵族之女，塞琉古王朝的王后，象征马其顿-波斯融合
role.en: Daughter of Sogdian-Bactrian nobility, queen of the Seleucid dynasty, symbolizing Macedonian-Persian integration

name.zh: 安提奥库斯一世·索忒尔

role.zh: 未来的塞琉古帝国第二位国王，继承其父东方帝国的治理
role.en: Future second king of the Seleucid Empire, inheriting governance of his father's eastern domains

ideology.zh: 波斯行政延续主义——在新的马其顿统治下保持波斯贵族的社会地位、地方治理权力和文化传统
ideology.en: Persian administrative continuity — preserving the social status, local governance authority, and cultural traditions of Persian nobility under the new Macedonian rule

headquarters.zh: 巴比伦、苏萨及各地方总督府
headquarters.en: Babylon, Susa, and various provincial governor seats

relationship.zh: 波斯总督贵族是两河流域和前波斯帝国各行省地方治理的实际执行者。马其顿征服者需要依靠这些经验丰富的波斯官僚和贵族来维持税收、灌溉和行政运转。他们以顺从换取特权保留，形成了与马其顿军事统治层的合作关系。
relationship.en: The Persian satrapal aristocracy were the practical executors of local governance across Mesopotamia and former Persian provinces. Macedonian conquerors needed these experienced Persian bureaucrats and nobles to maintain taxation, irrigation, and administrative operations. They traded compliance for the preservation of privileges, forming a cooperative relationship with the Macedonian military ruling class.

description.zh: 阿契美尼德帝国灭亡后，大量波斯贵族家族选择与马其顿征服者合作而非抵抗。这些家族世代掌握地方治理的实际经验——从灌溉渠道的维护、税收的征集到司法裁断和宗教祭祀，两河流域的行政运转离不开他们。亚历山大本人就曾大量保留波斯总督和地方官员，鼓励马其顿将领与波斯贵族通婚。到323年，这种波斯-马其顿共治模式已成为帝国行政的常态。波斯贵族保留了大量庄园、牧场和商业利益，在社会地位上仅次于马其顿军事贵族。他们中的许多人适应了希腊文化的某些方面，同时坚守波斯传统——这种文化二元性将成为塞琉古帝国的显著特征。波斯总督贵族的合作态度对于两河流域的政治稳定至关重要，任何忽视或冒犯这一群体的统治者都将面临严重的行政瘫痪风险。
description.en: After the fall of the Achaemenid Empire, numerous Persian noble families chose to cooperate with the Macedonian conquerors rather than resist. These families possessed generations of practical governance experience — from maintaining irrigation canals and collecting taxes to administering justice and conducting religious ceremonies. The administrative functioning of Mesopotamia was impossible without them. Alexander himself had retained many Persian satraps and local officials, encouraging intermarriage between Macedonian generals and Persian nobility. By 323 BCE, this Persian-Macedonian co-governance model had become the norm of imperial administration. Persian nobles retained extensive estates, pastures, and commercial interests, ranking in social status just below the Macedonian military aristocracy. Many adopted certain aspects of Greek culture while preserving Persian traditions — this cultural duality would become a defining feature of the Seleucid Empire. The cooperative attitude of the Persian satrapal aristocracy was crucial for Mesopotamian political stability; any ruler who ignored or offended this group risked severe administrative paralysis.

role.zh: 最成功地保持权力的波斯总督，后来建立了独立的阿特罗帕特尼王国
role.en: The most successful Persian satrap in retaining power, later founding the independent kingdom of Atropatene

title.zh: 帕提亚和希尔卡尼亚总督

role.zh: 忠于马其顿权威的波斯总督，协助维持东方行省的行政秩序
role.en: A Persian satrap loyal to Macedonian authority, helping maintain administrative order in the eastern provinces

ideology.zh: 希腊化殖民主义——在东方建立希腊式城市生活，传播希腊语言、法律、教育和宗教，同时为马其顿军事力量提供驻防基地
ideology.en: Hellenistic colonialism — establishing Greek-style urban life in the East, spreading Greek language, law, education, and religion, while providing garrison bases for Macedonian military power

headquarters.zh: 两河流域新建希腊城市
headquarters.en: Newly founded Greek cities in Mesopotamia

relationship.zh: 希腊殖民城市是马其顿统治东方的文化和军事支柱。这些城市由退役希腊和马其顿士兵以及新移民组成，作为军事驻防点控制战略要地，同时推动希腊文化在近东和中亚的传播。殖民者的利益与马其顿军事统治层紧密一致。
relationship.en: Greek settler colonies were the cultural and military pillars of Macedonian rule in the East. Composed of retired Greek and Macedonian soldiers and new settlers, these cities served as military garrison points controlling strategic locations while promoting the spread of Greek culture across the Near East and Central Asia. The settlers' interests were closely aligned with the Macedonian military ruling class.

description.zh: 亚历山大大帝在东征过程中沿途建立了数十座希腊式城市——从埃及的亚历山大里亚到中亚的最远亚历山大里亚。这些城市按照希腊城邦模式组织，设有广场（阿哥拉）、体育场、剧院和希腊神庙，居民享有一定程度的城市自治。到323年，这些殖民城市已形成一个横跨前波斯帝国的希腊人定居点网络。殖民者大多是退伍军人和来自希腊本土的移民，他们在当地获得土地、经营商业，并将希腊生活方式移植到异域环境中。这些城市兼具多重功能：军事上作为驻军基地控制周边地区，经济上作为贸易节点连接希腊世界与东方，文化上作为希腊化的桥头堡影响当地社会。殖民者群体虽然在数量上远少于当地居民，但凭借军事优势和政治特权在各城市中占据统治地位。亚历山大死后，这些殖民城市面临新的不确定性——部分东方殖民者甚至在亚历山大在世时就曾企图集体返回希腊，反映出在遥远异乡生活的压力。
description.en: Alexander the Great founded dozens of Greek-style cities during his eastern campaigns — from Alexandria in Egypt to Alexandria Eschate in Central Asia. These cities were organized on the Greek polis model, with agoras, gymnasia, theaters, and Greek temples, their inhabitants enjoying a degree of municipal self-governance. By 323 BCE, they had formed a network of Greek settlements stretching across the former Persian Empire. Most colonists were military veterans and immigrants from the Greek mainland who received land grants, engaged in commerce, and transplanted Greek lifestyles into foreign settings. These cities served multiple functions: militarily as garrison bases controlling surrounding territories, economically as trade nodes linking the Greek world with the East, and culturally as bridgeheads of Hellenization influencing local societies. Though far outnumbered by indigenous populations, the colonists occupied a dominant position in each city through military advantage and political privilege. After Alexander's death, these colonies faced new uncertainty — some eastern colonists had even attempted a mass return to Greece during Alexander's lifetime, reflecting the pressures of life in distant lands.

role.zh: 底格里斯河畔塞琉西亚城的早期建设者，这座城市将成为塞琉古帝国的首都之一
role.en: Early builders of Seleucia on the Tigris, which would become one of the capitals of the Seleucid Empire

role.zh: 负责各殖民城市防务与当地秩序维护的马其顿军官
role.en: Macedonian officers responsible for defense and maintaining local order in colonial cities

name.zh: 第二圣殿时期犹太社会

ruler.zh: 大祭司辖下的祭司领导层
ruler.en: The High Priesthood under the High Priest

governmentForm.zh: 以圣殿为中心、受帝国行省体系约束的祭司神权共同体
governmentForm.en: A Temple-centered priestly theocratic community operating within an imperial provincial framework

socialStructure.zh: 社会围绕圣殿、祭司、利未人、长老、自由农民、手工业者与朝圣网络组织；宗教律法塑造公共生活与身份。
socialStructure.en: Society was organized around the Temple, priests, Levites, elders, free cultivators, artisans, and pilgrimage networks; religious law shaped public life and collective identity.

rulingClass.zh: 耶路撒冷祭司贵族、主要祭司家族、圣殿官员与地方长老
rulingClass.en: The Jerusalem priestly aristocracy, leading priestly families, Temple officials, and local elders

succession.zh: 大祭司职通常在祭司名门内部承袭，但亦受帝国当局承认、地方政治协商与家族竞争影响。
succession.en: The high priesthood was usually transmitted within leading priestly lineages, but it was also shaped by imperial recognition, local political bargaining, and family rivalry.

structure.zh: 耶路撒冷以大祭司与圣殿机构为核心，辅以祭司议事、长老权威和地方社群治理；对外则从属于亚历山大帝国瓦解后的马其顿统治框架与地方总督体系。
structure.en: Jerusalem was governed through the High Priest and Temple institutions, supported by priestly deliberation, elder authority, and local community administration; externally it remained subject to the Macedonian imperial framework and provincial governors following Alexander's collapse.

function.zh: 管理日常献祭、节期礼仪、祭司班次与祭坛运作。
function.en: Managed daily offerings, festival rites, priestly rotations, and altar operations.

function.zh: 保管十一税、奉献、银器、谷物与圣殿公款，并监督支出。
function.en: Held tithes, offerings, silver vessels, grain, and Temple funds, and supervised expenditures.

function.zh: 组织利未人执勤、门卫、歌咏与圣殿辅助事务。
function.en: Organized Levite service, gatekeeping, chant, and supporting Temple duties.

function.zh: 处理宗教律法争议、誓约、纯洁条例与民间纠纷调解。
function.en: Handled disputes in religious law, oaths, purity regulations, and mediation of civil quarrels.

function.zh: 与犹地亚乡村、宗族长老和城镇会众沟通，征收贡赋与协调朝圣。
function.en: Maintained ties with Judean villages, clan elders, and town congregations, collecting dues and coordinating pilgrimage.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理依赖宗族长老、城镇首领、祭司代表与文书记员。耶路撒冷以外的犹地亚聚落通常维持较强自治，但在税赋、律法与圣殿义务上受中心约束。
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied on clan elders, town heads, priestly representatives, and scribes. Settlements outside Jerusalem in Judea generally retained strong self-management, but were tied to the center through taxation, law, and Temple obligations.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以《妥拉》、祭司传统、习惯法与长老裁断为基础。刑事与宗教案件多在社群与祭司层面处理，重大政治权力仍受帝国官员限制。
legalSystem.en: Law rested on the Torah, priestly tradition, customary practice, and elder judgment. Criminal and religious cases were usually handled at the communal and priestly level, while major political authority remained limited by imperial officials.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政来源包括十一税、初熟之物、各类圣殿奉献、牲畜与谷物贡纳，以及向帝国体系承担的地租和赋税。宗教性缴纳与世俗性税负并存。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue came from tithes, firstfruits, various Temple offerings, livestock and grain contributions, as well as land dues and taxes owed within the imperial system. Religious obligations and secular taxation coexisted.

religion.zh: 耶和华一神信仰居于核心，圣殿献祭、洁净条例、安息日和年度节期构成社会节律。
religion.en: The worship of Yahweh stood at the center, and Temple sacrifice, purity rules, the Sabbath, and annual festivals structured social life.

philosophy.zh: 此时思想重心不是希腊式哲学体系，而是盟约神学、律法诠释、先知传统与智慧文学。对外来希腊文化已有接触，但尚未全面主导本地观念。
philosophy.en: The intellectual focus was not on Greek-style philosophical systems but on covenant theology, interpretation of the Law, prophetic tradition, and wisdom literature. Contact with Hellenic culture had begun, but it did not yet dominate local thought.

writingSystem.zh: 主要使用帝国阿拉米字母书写阿拉米语与希伯来语文本；希伯来语保留重要礼仪与经文地位，文书活动由抄写员承担。
writingSystem.en: Imperial Aramaic script was chiefly used for writing Aramaic and Hebrew texts; Hebrew retained major liturgical and scriptural prestige, and scribes carried out documentary activity.

culturalAchievements.zh: 第二圣殿礼仪体系的持续运作、经文抄传、律法共同体身份的巩固、朝圣节期秩序以及耶路撒冷作为宗教中心的强化，是这一时期最显著的文化成就。
culturalAchievements.en: The continued functioning of the Second Temple ritual system, transmission of sacred texts, consolidation of Law-centered communal identity, orderly pilgrimage festivals, and the strengthening of Jerusalem as a religious center were the period's chief cultural achievements.

languageFamily.zh: 西北闪米特语族；日常以阿拉米语广泛通行，希伯来语保有宗教与文学权威。
languageFamily.en: Northwest Semitic; Aramaic was widely used in daily life, while Hebrew retained religious and literary authority.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济规模在古代近东属于小型区域共同体水平，以农业、圣殿再分配与朝圣消费为主。耶路撒冷的宗教中心地位提高了服务、畜产与贸易活动，但整体仍以乡村生产为根基。
gdpDescription.en: Its economy was that of a small regional Near Eastern community, driven by agriculture, Temple redistribution, and pilgrimage consumption. Jerusalem's role as a religious center stimulated services, livestock, and trade, but the economy remained fundamentally rural.

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物种植、葡萄与橄榄栽培、牧羊与山羊畜养、陶器、纺织、石工、圣殿相关手工业与朝圣服务
mainIndustries.en: Grain cultivation, viticulture and olive growing, sheep and goat herding, pottery, textiles, stonework, Temple-related crafts, and pilgrimage services

tradeGoods.zh: 谷物、葡萄酒、橄榄油、羊毛、皮革、陶器、盐、香料转运品、金属器与祭祀用牲畜
tradeGoods.en: Grain, wine, olive oil, wool, leather, pottery, salt, transit aromatics, metal goods, and sacrificial animals

householdWealth.zh: 大多数家庭财富有限，主要体现为土地使用权、橄榄树、葡萄园、小型畜群、粮食储备与家用器具。祭司贵族和圣殿相关家族掌握较多流动财富与贵金属。
householdWealth.en: Most households possessed modest wealth, mainly in land-use rights, olive trees, vineyards, small herds, grain stores, and household goods. Priestly aristocrats and Temple-connected families controlled more movable wealth and precious metal.

economicSystem.zh: 以自给农业为基础，辅以圣殿征纳与再分配、地方市场交换以及纳入更大帝国商业网络的小规模区域贸易。
economicSystem.en: A subsistence agrarian economy supplemented by Temple collection and redistribution, local market exchange, and modest regional trade integrated into wider imperial commercial networks.

source.en: Temple offerings and commuted sacrificial value
Market dues, vow payments, and miscellaneous receipts

category.zh: 祭司、利未人与官员供养
category.en: Support for priests, Levites, and officials

treasuryDescription.zh: 圣殿库藏既具宗教性质，也具有公共财政功能，储存银衡、器皿、谷物与奉献财物。其规模对一个小型社会而言可观，但并非可随意用于世俗扩张的国家金库。
treasuryDescription.en: The Temple treasury was both sacred and public in character, holding silver by weight, vessels, grain, and dedicated property. It was substantial for a small society, but not a fully flexible state treasury available for secular expansion.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏重维持圣殿礼仪、社会稳定与储备，审慎保守，避免过度抽取乡村生产者，同时确保对上层帝国秩序的义务得以履行。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy emphasized sustaining Temple ritual, social stability, and reserves, remaining cautious and conservative, avoiding excessive extraction from rural producers while ensuring obligations to the wider imperial order were met.

description.zh: 负责圣殿秩序、门禁、库藏安全与节期人流管制，兼具宗教与治安职能。
description.en: Maintained Temple order, gate control, treasury security, and festival crowd management, combining sacred and policing functions.

description.zh: 驻守城门、城墙与要点，在紧急情况下保护首都与圣殿山。
description.en: Held gates, walls, and key points, protecting the capital and Temple Mount in emergencies.

description.zh: 由乡村自由民在危机时动员，装备简朴，以矛、盾、弓和农具改制武器为主。
description.en: Mobilized from rural free men in times of danger, lightly equipped mainly with spears, shields, bows, and adapted agricultural implements.

commanderInChief.zh: 大祭司及其委任的祭司长与地方首领
commanderInChief.en: The High Priest together with appointed chief priests and local leaders

technology.zh: 军备以铁制矛、短剑、弓箭、皮革或青铜护具和简易城防为主，不具马其顿式专业方阵能力。防务重点在守城与维持秩序，而非远征作战。
technology.en: Armament consisted chiefly of iron spears, short swords, bows, leather or bronze protection, and simple fortifications, without a professional Macedonian-style phalanx capability. Defense focused on guarding the city and maintaining order rather than campaigning.

threats.zh: 主要风险来自帝国继承战争造成的行省权力变动、邻近总督或驻军压力、撒马利亚及地方边界纠纷，以及内部派系斗争对耶路撒冷稳定的影响。
threats.en: The main risks came from the imperial succession wars altering provincial authority, pressure from nearby governors or garrisons, Samaritan and local boundary disputes, and internal factional tensions affecting Jerusalem's stability.

populationDescription.zh: 人口以犹地亚乡村居民为主，耶路撒冷在节期时会因朝圣者大量涌入而显著膨胀。常住人口规模有限，但宗教中心功能使其影响超出地理范围。
populationDescription.en: The population was primarily rural Judean, while Jerusalem swelled markedly during festivals because of pilgrims. Its permanent population was limited, but its religious centrality gave it influence beyond its size.

socialClasses.zh: 上层为祭司贵族、主要家族与大地主；中层为普通祭司、利未人、书吏、商人和手工业者；下层为小农、佃户、牧民、雇工与贫民。另有少量奴仆与依附人口。
socialClasses.en: The upper stratum consisted of priestly aristocrats, leading families, and large landholders; the middle included ordinary priests, Levites, scribes, merchants, and artisans; the lower included small farmers, tenants, herders, laborers, and the poor. There were also smaller numbers of servants and dependents.

allies.zh: 无完全独立意义上的正式盟国；主要依赖与所属马其顿—希腊化行省权力保持合作，并与周边犹太社群维持宗教纽带。
allies.en: It had no formal allies in the sense of a fully sovereign state; it relied chiefly on cooperation with the ruling Macedonian-Hellenistic provincial authorities and maintained religious ties with surrounding Jewish communities.

enemies.zh: 无持续性的全面外敌，但与撒马利亚社群存在长期宗教与政治紧张，并易受帝国军政斗争波及。
enemies.en: It faced no permanent all-out foreign enemy, but had enduring religious and political tensions with Samaritan communities and was vulnerable to wider imperial military and political struggles.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以谨慎顺从、保存宗教自治、维护圣殿利益与避免卷入大国战争为主。其目标不是扩张，而是在更大帝国体系中守住律法与祭司权威。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy was based on cautious accommodation, preserving religious autonomy, protecting Temple interests, and avoiding entanglement in great-power wars. Its aim was not expansion, but safeguarding the Law and priestly authority within a larger imperial order.

era.zh: 希腊化时代早期的铁器时代近东技术水平
era.en: Early Hellenistic Near Eastern Iron Age technological level

keyInnovations.zh: 石造与灰泥建筑、蓄水池与山地农业梯田、铁制农具、橄榄与葡萄加工技术、标准化文书抄写及圣殿礼仪组织化管理。
keyInnovations.en: Stone and plaster construction, cisterns and hill-country terracing, iron agricultural tools, olive and wine processing techniques, standardized scribal copying, and organized Temple ritual administration.

strengths.zh: 宗教凝聚力强，耶路撒冷圣殿具有高度象征与经济中心作用；社会组织稳定，律法与传统提供持久身份认同；在乡村基础上具备一定财政韧性。
strengths.en: It possessed strong religious cohesion, with the Jerusalem Temple serving as a major symbolic and economic center; social organization was stable, and the Law and tradition provided enduring identity; its rural base gave it a measure of fiscal resilience.

weaknesses.zh: 政治主权有限，军事力量薄弱，经济规模较小且高度依赖农业收成与圣殿秩序；祭司家族竞争及外部帝国更替可能迅速破坏平衡。
weaknesses.en: It had limited political sovereignty, weak military power, and a small economy highly dependent on harvests and Temple order; rivalry among priestly families and changes in external imperial rule could quickly upset the balance.

outlook.zh: 公元前323年时局总体稳定，但亚历山大死后继业者冲突即将重塑整个近东。只要圣殿运作、税赋负担与帝国容忍度维持平衡，犹太社会可继续稳定；若外来统治强化干预，则内部张力将上升。
outlook.en: In 323 BCE the situation was broadly stable, but the conflicts of Alexander's successors were about to reshape the Near East. As long as Temple operations, tax burdens, and imperial tolerance remained in balance, Jewish society could remain stable; if outside rule became more intrusive, internal tensions would rise.

description.zh: 在帝国行省框架内，耶路撒冷仍以圣殿与祭司阶层为核心。
description.en: Within the imperial provincial framework, Jerusalem remained centered on the Temple and priestly leadership.

name.zh: 安纳托利亚—马其顿诸政体

governmentForm.zh: 马其顿征服后形成的总督辖区君主制，各地由受马其顿王权委任或自立的总督、将领与地方寡头共同治理
governmentForm.en: A satrapal monarchical order formed after the Macedonian conquest, in which regions were governed by satraps, generals, and local oligarchies appointed by or acting independently from Macedonian royal authority.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由马其顿—希腊军事统治者、希腊化城市公民、安纳托利亚本地贵族、神庙阶层、商人、农民、牧民与奴隶构成，沿海与内陆差异显著
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of Macedonian-Greek military rulers, citizens of Hellenized cities, indigenous Anatolian nobles, temple personnel, merchants, peasants, pastoralists, and slaves, with strong contrasts between coastal and inland regions.

rulingClass.zh: 马其顿裔军政精英联合部分希腊城邦寡头与地方豪族
rulingClass.en: Macedonian military-administrative elites together with some Greek civic oligarchs and local aristocratic houses.

succession.zh: 名义上由亚历山大帝国的王室与摄政体系授予总督职位，实际上常依赖军队拥戴、个人权势与继业者战争中的胜负而更替
succession.en: In theory satrapies were granted by the royal house and regency of Alexander's empire, but in practice succession depended on army backing, personal power, and outcomes in the Wars of the Successors.

structure.zh: 政体高度分散。西部沿海希腊城邦保留议事机构与地方自治，内陆与战略要地由总督驻军控制；税收、司法与军务常由总督属官、城市官员和旧有神庙机构并行处理
structure.en: The polity was highly decentralized. Greek cities on the western coast retained assemblies and local self-government, while inland and strategic centers were controlled by satrapal garrisons; taxation, justice, and military affairs were handled in parallel by satrapal officials, civic magistrates, and older temple institutions.

function.zh: 统筹政令、任命属官、管理档案、发布军政命令
function.en: Coordinated decrees, appointed subordinates, managed records, and issued civil and military orders.

function.zh: 征收地税、关税、贡赋与王室地产收入，管理银币与实物储备
function.en: Collected land taxes, customs, tribute, and royal estate revenue, and managed coin and in-kind reserves.

function.zh: 维持驻军、军粮、要塞、防务与雇佣兵支付
function.en: Maintained garrisons, provisions, fortresses, defense, and mercenary payments.

function.zh: 管理王室土地、牧场、仓储与征发运输
function.en: Managed royal lands, pastures, storage facilities, and requisitioned transport.

function.zh: 协调城邦法、王令、地方习惯法与纠纷裁决
function.en: Coordinated civic law, royal ordinances, local customary law, and dispute settlement.

function.zh: 维护干道、驿站、渡口、港口税卡与官方通信
function.en: Maintained roads, relay stations, crossings, port toll stations, and official communications.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政因地而异：爱奥尼亚与赫勒斯滂地区多由希腊城邦自治官员管理；弗里吉亚、吕基亚、卡里亚、帕夫拉戈尼亚等地则由总督、副总督、要塞长官与地方豪族共同治理；乡村基层依赖村社、神庙与税吏网络
localAdmin.en: Local administration varied by region: in Ionia and the Hellespont many Greek cities were governed by autonomous civic magistrates; in Phrygia, Lycia, Caria, and Paphlagonia rule was shared by satraps, sub-satraps, fortress commanders, and local aristocrats; the countryside relied on village communities, temples, and tax collectors.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系混合了马其顿王令、希腊城邦法、阿契美尼德时期行省惯例以及安纳托利亚地方习惯法。商业与港口事务多按希腊城邦法处理，土地、贡赋和治安则常由总督裁断
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined Macedonian royal ordinances, Greek civic law, Achaemenid-era provincial practice, and Anatolian local custom. Commercial and port matters were often handled under Greek civic law, while land, tribute, and security issues were frequently decided by the satrap.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政以土地税、贡赋、关税、港口税、矿山与林木收益、王室地产租佃收入及战时征发为主。部分地区继续沿用波斯式按区摊派制度，城市则以关税与商业税见长
taxationSystem.en: Revenue came mainly from land tax, tribute, customs duties, port tolls, mines and timber, rents from royal estates, and wartime requisitions. Some areas retained Persian-style district assessments, while cities relied more heavily on customs and commercial taxation.

religion.zh: 宗教呈多元并存状态。希腊诸神崇拜与安纳托利亚本土神祇、山地与母神传统、波斯祭祀遗存并行，重要圣所兼具经济与政治功能
religion.en: Religion was highly pluralistic. Worship of the Greek gods coexisted with indigenous Anatolian deities, mountain and mother-goddess traditions, and residual Persian cult practices; major sanctuaries served economic and political roles as well.

philosophy.zh: 沿海希腊城市受古典希腊哲学影响，修辞、城邦政治思想与伦理讨论活跃；内陆地区则更重实用统治、王权忠诚和地方传统。323年正值亚里士多德时代尾声与犬儒、早期希腊化思想扩散之际
philosophy.en: The coastal Greek cities were influenced by classical Greek philosophy, with active rhetoric, civic political thought, and ethical debate; inland areas emphasized practical rule, royal loyalty, and local tradition. In 323 BCE the region stood at the end of Aristotle's age and the spread of Cynic and early Hellenistic thought.

writingSystem.zh: 主要使用希腊字母书写通用希腊语与地方行政文书，部分内陆地区仍保留阿拉米文行政遗风及安纳托利亚地方语言口传传统
writingSystem.en: The Greek alphabet was the principal script for koine-like Greek and administrative writing, while some inland areas retained Aramaic administrative habits and oral traditions in local Anatolian languages.

culturalAchievements.zh: 该地区以希腊化城市生活、铸币、港口商业、神庙建筑、要塞工程和多族文化融合见长。安纳托利亚沿海继续维持剧场、体育场与祭典传统，内陆则保存古老圣地与地方王侯纪念文化
culturalAchievements.en: The region was notable for Hellenizing urban life, coinage, port commerce, temple building, fortress engineering, and multiethnic cultural blending. The Anatolian coast maintained theaters, gymnasia, and festival traditions, while the interior preserved ancient sanctuaries and local dynastic memorial cultures.

languageFamily.zh: 以希腊语（印欧语系希腊语族）为统治与城市通用语言，并有弗里吉亚语、吕底亚语、卡里亚语、吕基亚语等安纳托利亚印欧语言，以及部分阿拉米语行政使用
languageFamily.en: Greek, an Indo-European Hellenic language, served as the main language of rule and urban life, alongside Anatolian Indo-European languages such as Phrygian, Lydian, Carian, and Lycian, with some administrative use of Aramaic.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济总量在地中海东部属中上水平，依托肥沃内陆、高原畜牧、木材矿产与爱琴海港市贸易。由于继业者争战、驻军消耗和政权分裂，经济潜力未能完全发挥
gdpDescription.en: Overall output was moderate to strong by eastern Mediterranean standards, based on fertile inland agriculture, plateau pastoralism, timber and minerals, and Aegean port trade. Owing to Successor conflict, garrison burdens, and fragmented authority, the region's full economic potential was not realized.

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物种植、葡萄与橄榄、畜牧、木材采伐、金属采矿、陶器、纺织、造船材料供应、铸币与区域转运贸易
mainIndustries.en: Grain cultivation, viticulture and olives, pastoralism, timber extraction, metal mining, pottery, textiles, shipbuilding materials supply, minting, and regional transit trade.

tradeGoods.zh: 粮食、葡萄酒、橄榄油、木材、沥青、铁铜银、羊毛、皮革、陶器、染料、奴隶与雇佣兵服务
tradeGoods.en: Grain, wine, olive oil, timber, bitumen, iron, copper, silver, wool, leather, pottery, dyes, slaves, and mercenary services.

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富差异极大。城市富商、放债者、承包商与地方豪族拥有土地、奴隶、仓储与铸币收益；普通农户多为小规模自耕或佃作，易受征发、战乱与歉收冲击
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was highly unequal. Urban merchants, lenders, contractors, and local aristocrats held land, slaves, storage, and mint-related gains; ordinary farming households were usually smallholders or tenants vulnerable to requisitioning, warfare, and poor harvests.

economicSystem.zh: 以农业为基础、商业化程度较高的混合经济。王室地产、总督财政、城邦税制与私人商贸并存，货币经济在沿海与军需网络中尤为重要
economicSystem.en: A mixed agrarian economy with substantial commercialization. Royal estates, satrapal finance, civic taxation, and private trade coexisted, with monetary exchange especially important on the coast and in military supply networks.

source.en: Customs, port dues, and market taxes

source.zh: 附庸贡纳、罚金与临时征发

category.zh: 军队军饷、驻防与雇佣兵

treasuryDescription.zh: 库藏主要分散保存在总督驻地、要塞、神庙金库和若干沿海城市铸币中心，流动性受战争与地方分裂限制
treasuryDescription.en: Treasury resources were dispersed among satrapal residences, fortresses, temple vaults, and several coastal minting centers, with liquidity constrained by war and regional fragmentation.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向战时汲取型：优先保障军饷、要塞与运输，必要时加征临时赋税、要求城市贷款或征收实物。和平地区则鼓励港口通商和恢复农业以扩大税基
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was wartime-extractive: priority went to troop pay, fortresses, and transport, with extraordinary taxes, city loans, and in-kind levies imposed when necessary. In peaceful districts authorities encouraged port trade and agrarian recovery to broaden the tax base.

description.zh: 核心重装步兵，装备长枪、圆盾与头盔，是总督军的主力野战部队
description.en: Core heavy infantry armed with sarissae, shields, and helmets, forming the main field force of the satraps.

description.zh: 来自爱琴海世界的职业士兵，常用于守城、攻城与补充野战阵列
description.en: Professional soldiers from the Aegean world, often used for garrison duty, sieges, and reinforcement of field armies.

description.zh: 包括马其顿伙伴式骑兵、地方贵族骑兵与轻骑侦察部队，适合追击与侧翼作战
description.en: Included Macedonian-style companion cavalry, local aristocratic horsemen, and light scouting cavalry for pursuit and flank operations.

description.zh: 由标枪兵、弓箭手、投石兵及山地部族兵组成，适合崎岖地形与骚扰战
description.en: Consisted of javelinmen, archers, slingers, and mountain tribal troops suited to rough terrain and skirmishing.

description.zh: 规模有限，依赖沿海城市舰船与港口驻军，主要负责补给线和近岸控制
description.en: Limited in scale, relying on ships from coastal cities and port garrisons, mainly to secure supply lines and nearby waters.

description.zh: 负责攻城器械、筑垒、架桥与道路开通，继承亚历山大时代工程传统
description.en: Handled siege machines, entrenchments, bridging, and route clearance, inheriting the engineering tradition of Alexander's age.

description.zh: 由城市民兵与乡村征发人员组成，战力不均，主要承担守备和后方任务
description.en: Made up of civic militias and levied rural men, uneven in quality and mainly used for guard and rear-area duties.

commanderInChief.zh: 各地总督兼最高统帅，如安提柯一世
commanderInChief.en: The satrap acting as supreme commander, such as Antigonus I

technology.zh: 军技处于晚期古典至早期希腊化高水平，拥有长枪方阵、复合弓、攻城塔、弩炮与抛石机等技术，并善用要塞体系和道路补给
technology.en: Military technology stood at a high late-classical to early Hellenistic level, including sarissa phalanxes, composite bows, siege towers, bolt-shooters, and stone-throwers, supported by fortifications and road-based logistics.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自继业者内战中的敌对总督、欧洲马其顿本部的权力斗争、沿海城邦的叛离、山地部族骚扰，以及埃及或巴比伦方向继业者势力对小亚细亚的争夺
threats.en: Main threats came from rival satraps in the Wars of the Successors, power struggles involving the Macedonian homeland, defections of coastal poleis, mountain tribal raiding, and contests for Asia Minor by Successor powers based in Egypt or Babylonia.

populationDescription.zh: 人口约五百万，分布不均。爱奥尼亚沿海、赫勒斯滂周边与部分河谷平原人口较密集，安纳托利亚高原则较为分散，兼有定居农业与牧业迁徙人口
populationDescription.en: The population was about five million and unevenly distributed. The Ionian coast, the Hellespontine zone, and some river plains were relatively dense, whereas the Anatolian plateau was more dispersed, mixing settled agriculture with pastoral mobility.

socialClasses.zh: 上层为马其顿将领、总督亲信、希腊城邦富裕公民和本地豪族；中层包括商人、工匠、军官、神庙人员与较富农户；下层为普通农民、佃户、牧民、港口工人、雇佣兵家属与奴隶
