It consisted of nomadic households, dependent settled groups, and residents of frontier trade nodes, with high mobility and marked regional variation.

name.zh: 无固定都城；主要集中点为季节性王庭营地
name.en: No fixed capital; main centers were seasonal royal encampments

name.zh: 锡尔河沿线萨迦营地群
name.en: Saka Camp Clusters along the Syr Darya

name.zh: 七河地区季节性交换中心
阿拉木图山前与绿洲边缘营市

socialClasses.zh: 社会阶层包括王族、部落贵族、骑士战士、自由牧民、工匠与商旅中介、附庸农耕人口以及奴仆。女性在某些贵族与武装群体中拥有较高能见度，希腊文献所述“女战士”传统虽带夸饰，但反映了草原社会较高的女性骑乘与武装参与度。
socialClasses.en: Social strata included royal houses, tribal aristocrats, mounted warriors, free pastoralists, artisans and trade intermediaries, dependent agricultural populations, and slaves or servants. Women had notable visibility in some noble and armed groups; the 'Amazon' traditions reported in Greek sources were exaggerated, but they reflect comparatively high female participation in riding and, at times, bearing arms.

allies.zh: 无持久统一盟友体系；通常与邻近部族、部分黑海希腊城邦商人、草原盟部及中亚边境交换网络保持临时合作关系。
allies.en: There was no permanent unified alliance system; temporary cooperative relations were commonly maintained with neighboring tribes, merchants of some Black Sea Greek city-states, allied steppe groups, and Central Asian frontier exchange networks.

enemies.zh: 敌对对象随地区而变化，常包括竞争草场的草原部族、拒绝纳贡的定居社群，以及试图控制边境贸易与通道的马其顿化或伊朗系政权。
enemies.en: Enemies varied by region, often including rival steppe tribes competing for pasture, settled groups refusing tribute, and Macedonianized or Iranian polities seeking control of frontier trade and corridors.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以维持草原均势、保护迁徙路线、利用贸易与劫掠双重手段获取资源、并通过婚盟、馈赠和威慑维系部族联盟为核心。对于定居帝国，通常采取边贸、雇佣、袭扰和间接控制并用的灵活策略。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on preserving steppe balance, protecting migration routes, obtaining resources through both trade and raiding, and maintaining confederational ties through marriage alliances, gifts, and deterrence. Toward settled empires, they typically used a flexible mix of frontier trade, mercenary service, raiding, and indirect control.

era.zh: 希腊化时期的欧亚草原铁器时代
era.en: Eurasian Steppe Iron Age of the Hellenistic Period

keyInnovations.zh: 复合弓骑射体系、改良马具与鞍垫、可移动毡帐营地、动物纹金工、远距离草原补给与侦察体系、适应多生态边疆的游牧作战组织。
keyInnovations.en: Composite-bow mounted warfare, improved horse gear and saddle padding, mobile felt-tent encampments, animal-style goldworking, long-range steppe supply and scouting systems, and nomadic military organization adapted to multiple frontier ecologies.

strengths.zh: 最大优势在于广域机动、优秀骑射、适应草原生态、联盟弹性和控制马匹资源。其社会虽缺乏中央官僚体系，却能迅速集结战士并在欧亚草原建立跨区域联系。
strengths.en: Its greatest strengths lay in wide-ranging mobility, excellent mounted archery, adaptation to steppe ecology, confederational flexibility, and control of horse resources. Though lacking a central bureaucracy, its society could rapidly assemble warriors and maintain transregional connections across the Eurasian steppe.

weaknesses.zh: 主要弱点是政治分裂、财政分散、对草场与气候高度依赖、攻城能力有限，以及在面对组织严密、资源深厚的定居帝国时难以长期占领城市与农耕腹地。
weaknesses.en: Its main weaknesses were political fragmentation, dispersed finance, strong dependence on pasture and climate, limited siege capacity, and difficulty in permanently holding cities and agrarian heartlands against well-organized, resource-rich settled empires.

outlook.zh: 公元前323年前后，该文明整体处于稳定但分散的状态。亚历山大去世后，希腊化世界东部权力重组将为草原部族提供新的贸易、雇佣与劫掠机会；同时，中亚边疆竞争也会加剧，促使萨迦与其他东伊朗草原集团继续迁徙、重组并向南方和东方施加影响。
outlook.en: Around 323 BCE, this civilization was stable overall but politically fragmented. The power reconfiguration of the eastern Hellenistic world after Alexander's death would create new opportunities for trade, mercenary service, and raiding; at the same time, intensifying competition on the Central Asian frontier would push Saka and other Eastern Iranian steppe groups to continue migrating, reorganizing, and exerting influence southward and eastward.

description.zh: 从黑海北岸到中亚草原分布广泛的伊朗语游牧联盟与部族群。
description.en: Iranian-speaking nomadic confederations and tribes ranged from the north Black Sea to the Central Asian steppe.

governmentForm.zh: 高度集权的世袭君主制帝国，以宫廷、财政官僚与地方总督共同治理广阔的恒河流域
governmentForm.en: A highly centralized hereditary monarchical empire governed through the royal court, fiscal bureaucracy, and provincial governors across the Ganges basin

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王室与贵族、婆罗门、军政官员、城市商人、乡村农民、工匠以及地位较低的劳作者构成，并受瓦尔那与种姓差异深刻影响
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of the royal house and elites, Brahmins, military and administrative officials, urban merchants, rural cultivators, artisans, and lower-status laborers, with varna and caste distinctions exerting strong influence

rulingClass.zh: 以难陀王室、宫廷近臣、高级税务与军事官员以及依附王权的地方精英为统治核心
rulingClass.en: The ruling core consisted of the Nanda royal house, court intimates, senior fiscal and military officials, and local elites tied to royal authority

succession.zh: 王位实行王朝世袭，通常在王族内部传承，但宫廷斗争、权臣与军队态度会显著影响继承稳定
succession.en: The throne followed dynastic hereditary succession, usually within the royal family, though court intrigue, powerful ministers, and army support strongly affected stability

structure.zh: 政权以国王为最高权威，中央朝廷负责财政、军务、仓储、工程与司法；地方则通过省级总督、城邑官员与乡村首长征税与维持秩序
structure.en: The state centered on the king as supreme authority, with the central court overseeing revenue, military affairs, granaries, public works, and justice; the provinces were managed through governors, urban officials, and village headmen who collected taxes and maintained order

function.zh: 起草王令、传递政令、保存朝廷文书与地方报告
function.en: Drafted royal orders, transmitted decrees, and maintained court records and provincial reports

function.zh: 统计土地、征收农税、商业税与贡赋，管理现金与实物收入
function.en: Surveyed land, collected agrarian taxes, trade levies, and tribute, and managed both cash and in-kind revenues

function.zh: 保管金银、钱币、谷物与军需，调配中央储备
function.en: Held gold, silver, coin, grain, and military supplies, and allocated central reserves

function.zh: 管理军队编制、补给、征发、战象与边防驻军
function.en: Managed army organization, supplies, levies, war elephants, and frontier garrisons

name.zh: 公共工程与道路水利署
name.en: Public Works, Roads, and Irrigation Department

function.zh: 修筑道路、渡口、堤坝、运河、防御工事与王室建筑
function.en: Built roads, ferries, embankments, canals, fortifications, and royal structures

function.zh: 处理诉讼、刑罚、治安与对地方官吏和可疑势力的监督
function.en: Handled litigation, punishments, public order, and oversight of provincial officers and suspect elements

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政依托旧有摩揭陀核心区与新并入地区的分层治理体系，设省级总督、县邑官吏、税吏及村社首领。王权对核心区控制较强，对边缘地区则常通过驻军、贡赋和承认地方首领来维系统治。
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied on a layered system spanning the old Magadhan heartland and annexed territories, with provincial governors, district and town officers, tax collectors, and village headmen. Royal control was stronger in the core regions, while peripheral areas were often held through garrisons, tribute, and the recognition of local chiefs.

legalSystem.zh: 法律并非成文法典化统一体系，而是由王令、惯例、社会规范、种姓秩序与行政裁断共同构成。刑罚可以相当严厉，涉及罚金、没收、劳役与体罚；税务、治安与叛乱问题尤其受到国家重视。
legalSystem.en: The legal system was not a fully unified written code but a combination of royal decree, custom, social norms, caste order, and administrative judgment. Punishments could be severe, including fines, confiscation, labor obligations, and corporal penalties; revenue, public order, and rebellion received particular state attention.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政基础主要是农业地税，同时征收灌溉税、商业关税、手工业税、矿产与林产收入、畜牧税以及附庸贡赋。国家既征收钱币，也大量征收谷物、牲畜、纺织品和劳役。
taxationSystem.en: The fiscal base rested primarily on agrarian land tax, supplemented by irrigation dues, trade customs, artisan taxes, mining and forest income, livestock taxes, and tribute from subordinate territories. The state collected both coin and substantial in-kind payments in grain, animals, textiles, and labor service.

religion.zh: 宗教生活多元，婆罗门教传统居于主导地位，但佛教、耆那教以及各地民间崇拜在恒河平原也很活跃。王室虽以现实政治和财政为先，却必须与婆罗门祭祀传统及沙门教团保持互动。
religion.en: Religious life was plural. Brahmanical traditions remained dominant, but Buddhism, Jainism, and local cults were also active across the Gangetic plain. The royal court prioritized practical politics and revenue, yet had to maintain relations with Brahmanical ritual authority and the śramaṇa movements.

philosophy.zh: 这一时期思想环境极为活跃，既有吠陀—奥义书传统，也有佛教、耆那教和其他沙门学派对业、轮回、伦理与政治秩序的讨论。宫廷政治更偏重现实主义治国而非纯粹祭仪思想。
philosophy.en: The age was intellectually vibrant, shaped by Vedic-Upanishadic traditions as well as Buddhist, Jain, and other śramaṇa debates on karma, rebirth, ethics, and political order. Court politics leaned toward practical statecraft rather than purely ritual ideology.

writingSystem.zh: 书写使用尚处发展阶段的早期婆罗米书写传统以及其他地区性记写方式，行政记录相当可能同时依赖口述、书记与易腐材料。梵语具有宗教声望，俗语则广泛用于日常交流，其中摩揭陀地区的中古印度-雅利安语尤为重要。
writingSystem.en: Writing employed early Brahmi-related practices still in development, along with other regional recording methods; administration likely depended heavily on dictation, scribes, and perishable materials. Sanskrit held ritual prestige, while vernacular Middle Indo-Aryan speech was widely used in daily life, especially the dialects of the Magadhan region.

culturalAchievements.zh: 华氏城作为当时南亚最宏大的都市之一，体现出都城规划、木构宫殿、防御工事与河运网络的成就。都市商业、学术辩论、宗教流派竞争和跨区域交通使摩揭陀成为北印度文化重心。
culturalAchievements.en: Pataliputra, one of the greatest cities of South Asia at the time, embodied achievements in urban planning, timber architecture, fortifications, and riverine transport. Urban commerce, intellectual debate, sectarian competition, and long-distance communication made Magadha a major cultural center of northern India.

languageFamily.zh: 印度-雅利安语族为主，并与东部与南部其他语言群体长期接触
languageFamily.en: Predominantly Indo-Aryan, with long-term contact with eastern and southern language groups

gdpDescription.zh: 摩揭陀帝国拥有当时欧亚大陆最强大的农业税基之一。其经济以稻作与多种粮食生产为核心，辅以密集的人口、河运、城市市场、手工业和国家掌控下的财政汲取能力。估值高度近似，但其规模无疑位居当时世界最富庶国家之列。
gdpDescription.en: The Magadhan Empire possessed one of the strongest agrarian tax bases in the Eurasian world. Its economy rested on rice cultivation and diverse grain production, supported by dense population, riverine transport, urban markets, craft industries, and the state's ability to extract revenue. The estimate is necessarily approximate, but its overall scale clearly placed it among the wealthiest states of its age.

mainIndustries.zh: 农业、灌溉农耕、谷物储运、纺织、金属加工、木工、战象养护、采矿、盐业与区域贸易
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture, irrigated cultivation, grain storage and transport, textiles, metalworking, carpentry, elephant management, mining, salt production, and regional trade

tradeGoods.zh: 稻米、小麦、大麦、豆类、甘蔗制品、棉布、染织品、象牙、木材、金属器、盐、香料、宝石、战象与手工制品
tradeGoods.en: Rice, wheat, barley, pulses, sugarcane products, cotton cloth, dyed textiles, ivory, timber, metal goods, salt, spices, gemstones, war elephants, and crafted wares

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富差异极大。核心农区的小农家庭通常拥有少量土地、牲畜、农具与谷物储备；城市商人与官员家庭则持有更多金属货币、珠宝、奴婢或仆役、仓储和贸易资本。国家重税意味着农户虽普遍有生产能力，但积累空间受限。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth varied sharply. Smallholding families in the core agrarian zones typically possessed limited land, livestock, tools, and grain stores; urban merchant and official households held larger reserves of metal currency, jewelry, servants or dependents, warehouses, and trade capital. Heavy state exactions meant peasant households were often productive but had limited room for accumulation.

economicSystem.zh: 这是一个以农业为主、由国家强力汲取剩余并部分干预流通与资源的帝国经济。市场交换、商人网络与货币使用相当发达，但国家在土地收益、交通节点、仓储和军需调度上扮演关键角色。
economicSystem.en: This was an agrarian imperial economy in which the state forcefully extracted surplus and partially intervened in circulation and resources. Market exchange, merchant networks, and coin use were substantial, but the state played a crucial role in land revenue, transport chokepoints, storage, and military provisioning.

source.zh: 矿产、林产、盐业与王室产业
source.en: Mines, forests, salt, and crown domains

category.en: Court, royal household, and elite stipends
Granaries, famine relief, and reserve stock

category.zh: 宗教施舍、祭祀与外交馈赠
category.en: Religious patronage, ritual, and diplomatic gifts

treasuryDescription.zh: 古代文献普遍强调难陀王朝拥有巨额国库，虽具体数字难以验证，但其以苛密征敛和广阔领土闻名，储备显然远高于多数同时代国家。国库既包括银币与贵金属，也包括大型粮仓、军需物资、牲畜与象队。
treasuryDescription.en: Ancient literary traditions consistently emphasize the immense Nanda treasury. Although exact figures cannot be verified, the dynasty was famous for stringent extraction and extensive territory, and its reserves were clearly exceptional by the standards of the age. The treasury included not only silver coin and precious metal but also large granaries, military supplies, livestock, and elephant corps.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策以最大化可征收剩余为核心，强调稳定农税、扩大对贸易和资源的控制，并维持巨大储备以支持战争、镇压和饥荒应对。这种高汲取模式增强了国家实力，也加剧了地方与民间的不满。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy centered on maximizing extractable surplus, stabilizing land revenue, expanding control over trade and resources, and maintaining large reserves for war, coercion, and famine response. This high-extraction model strengthened the state but also intensified local and popular resentment.

description.zh: 步兵是军队主体，多为征发兵与常备驻军混合，装备长矛、弓、盾与刀剑，用于野战、守城与地方镇压。
description.en: Infantry formed the core of the army, combining levied men and standing garrisons, armed with spears, bows, shields, and swords for field warfare, sieges, and internal suppression.

description.zh: 骑兵数量相对较少，但在侦察、追击与机动作战中重要，部分来自西北地区传统更强的骑乘文化圈。
description.en: Cavalry was comparatively limited in number but important for reconnaissance, pursuit, and mobility, with some elements drawn from regions where mounted traditions were stronger, especially toward the northwest.

description.zh: 战车仍具威望和战场作用，但其军事重要性已较早期下降，更多在平坦地带发挥冲击与指挥平台功能。
description.en: Chariots still retained prestige and battlefield use, though their military importance had declined from earlier times, serving mainly in flatter terrain for shock and command functions.

description.zh: 战象是摩揭陀军力的标志性兵种，用于冲击敌阵、震慑对手和支撑统帅威望，对同时代诸国构成显著心理与战术优势。
description.en: War elephants were the signature arm of Magadhan power, used to break enemy formations, intimidate opponents, and enhance royal and general prestige, giving the state a major psychological and tactical advantage.

description.zh: 负责城防、营垒、架桥、渡河、攻城器械和后勤线维护，是庞大帝国军队不可缺少的组成部分。
description.en: These troops handled fortress defense, camp construction, bridging, river crossings, siege equipment, and maintenance of supply lines, making them indispensable to a large imperial army.

commanderInChief.zh: 陀那难陀大王
commanderInChief.en: Maharaja Dhana Nanda

technology.zh: 军事技术以铁制兵器、大型弓箭、战象运用、城防工事、河流后勤与传统战车体系为特色。与马其顿式方阵相比，摩揭陀军队在组织纪律上未必同样标准化，但在人力、象队与资源持续供给方面极具优势。
technology.en: Military technology featured iron weaponry, large bows, war elephant deployment, fortified strongholds, riverine logistics, and a continuing chariot tradition. Compared with Macedonian-style phalanx warfare, Magadhan forces may have been less uniformly standardized in drill, but they enjoyed major advantages in manpower, elephant corps, and sustained resource supply.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自西北边疆的不稳定局势、旁遮普与印度河地区政权、可能东进的希腊—马其顿势力，以及国内因重税和王朝不受欢迎而引发的叛乱风险。
threats.en: The chief threats came from instability on the northwestern frontier, the polities of Punjab and the Indus region, any future eastward movement by Greek-Macedonian forces, and internal revolt risk stemming from heavy taxation and the dynasty's unpopularity.

populationDescription.zh: 人口极其庞大，集中于恒河中下游的高产农业带，核心区聚落密度高，河流与冲积平原支持持续扩张。如此规模使摩揭陀在兵员、税源与劳动力方面拥有同时代少见的优势。
populationDescription.en: The population was exceptionally large, concentrated in the highly productive agrarian belt of the middle and lower Ganges. Settlement density was high in the core regions, and rivers and alluvial plains supported sustained expansion. This scale gave Magadha unusual advantages in manpower, taxation, and labor by the standards of the age.

socialClasses.zh: 上层包括王室、贵族、婆罗门、高级军政官员与富商；中层包括地方首领、文吏、工匠与中小商人；底层则以农民、佃作者、劳工、仆役和受压迫群体为主。瓦尔那与种姓差异深刻影响婚姻、职业、礼仪和法律待遇。
socialClasses.en: Upper strata included the royal family, nobles, Brahmins, senior military-administrative officials, and wealthy merchants; the middle ranks included local notables, scribes, artisans, and lesser traders; the lower strata consisted largely of peasants, tenant cultivators, laborers, servants, and oppressed groups. Varna and caste distinctions strongly shaped marriage, occupation, ritual standing, and legal treatment.

allies.zh: 摩揭陀更依赖附庸、朝贡关系与对邻近小邦的威慑，而非稳定意义上的平等盟友。其外围通常存在承认难陀霸权、定期输纳贡赋或在军事上服从的地方政权。
allies.en: Magadha relied more on subordinates, tributary arrangements, and intimidation of neighboring states than on stable equal alliances. Around it were local polities that acknowledged Nanda supremacy, sent tribute, or accepted military subordination.

enemies.zh: 潜在敌手包括西北的旁遮普与印度河流域政权、与马其顿征服余波相连的边疆势力，以及任何试图摆脱难陀控制的地方邦国和部族集团。
enemies.en: Potential enemies included the polities of Punjab and the Indus basin to the northwest, frontier forces linked to the aftermath of Macedonian conquest, and any local kingdoms or tribal groups seeking to escape Nanda control.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策以维持恒河流域霸权、压制周边独立势力、控制交通与贡赋路线为核心。对于遥远的地中海世界暂无直接系统外交，但西北战局与亚历山大东征的余绪已使摩揭陀开始面对更广阔的欧亚政治联系。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on preserving hegemony over the Ganges basin, suppressing surrounding independent powers, and controlling transport and tribute routes. There was not yet a systematic direct diplomacy with the distant Mediterranean world, but the northwestern situation and the aftermath of Alexander's eastern campaign had already begun to place Magadha in a wider Eurasian political context.

era.zh: 铁器时代晚期至早期古典帝国时代
era.en: Late Iron Age to Early Classical Imperial Age

keyInnovations.zh: 铁制农具与兵器的广泛应用、冲积平原高强度农业开发、成熟的河运与仓储体系、大规模战象军事化、穿孔标记银币流通、木构都城防御与行政组织能力
keyInnovations.en: Widespread use of iron agricultural tools and weapons, intensive exploitation of alluvial agriculture, developed river transport and storage systems, large-scale militarization of war elephants, circulation of punch-marked silver coinage, and advanced timber-built urban fortification and administrative capacity

strengths.zh: 摩揭陀最突出的优势是庞大人口、肥沃农田、恒河水运、强力财政汲取和巨额国库，这些共同支撑了当时世界上最庞大的军队之一。华氏城的政治集中度与核心区整合能力，使其具备向全北印度扩张的现实基础。
strengths.en: Magadha's greatest strengths were its immense population, fertile farmland, Ganges transport network, powerful fiscal extraction, and enormous treasury, all of which supported one of the largest armies in the world at the time. The political concentration of Pataliputra and the integration of the core region gave it a real basis for expansion across northern India.

weaknesses.zh: 其主要弱点在于王朝声望不足、重税激起不满、地方控制在边缘地带并不均衡，以及庞大军政机器的维持成本极高。若宫廷失序或将领倒向反对者，国家虽富强却可能迅速失稳。
weaknesses.en: Its principal weaknesses lay in limited dynastic prestige, resentment caused by heavy taxation, uneven control in peripheral regions, and the immense cost of maintaining a vast military-administrative machine. If the court fell into disorder or commanders shifted to the opposition, the wealthy state could destabilize quickly.

outlook.zh: 323年时，难陀帝国仍处于强盛顶点，足以压倒多数印度诸邦，并对西北来者形成威慑。然而其内部合法性脆弱，若出现有力挑战者并联合不满的军政精英，王朝更替的可能性很高。总体前景是国力强大但政权根基并不稳固。
outlook.en: In 323 BCE, the Nanda Empire remained at the height of its power, strong enough to dominate most Indian states and deter forces from the northwest. Yet its internal legitimacy was fragile, and if a capable challenger united dissatisfied military and administrative elites, dynastic overthrow was highly possible. The overall outlook was one of great state power resting on politically insecure foundations.

description.zh: 恒河流域最强大的国家，以摩揭陀为核心并维持庞大财政与军队。
description.en: The most powerful state of the Ganges basin, centered on Magadha and sustaining immense fiscal and military resources.

ideology.zh: 中央集权帝国主义——推翻腐败的难陀王朝，建立统一的北印度帝国，以强有力的行政与军事体制重塑次大陆政治秩序
ideology.en: Centralized imperialism — overthrowing the corrupt Nanda dynasty to establish a unified North Indian empire, reshaping subcontinental political order through strong administrative and military institutions

headquarters.zh: 华氏城外围（正在集结力量）

relationship.zh: 旃陀罗笈多正在集结西北边疆力量与国内不满势力，准备推翻难陀王朝。他已在西北地区击败马其顿驻军残部，正率军东进恒河流域，代表了一种新兴的、充满活力的帝国建设力量。
relationship.en: Chandragupta was assembling northwestern frontier forces and domestic discontents to overthrow the Nanda dynasty. He had already defeated remnants of Macedonian garrisons in the northwest and was marching east toward the Ganges basin, representing a new and vigorous empire-building force.

description.zh: 旃陀罗笈多出身低微但雄心勃勃，据传曾在年轻时目睹亚历山大的军队并受到启发。在考底利耶的辅佐下，他于公元前324-322年间从西北边疆起兵，先收复了马其顿人撤离后的印度河流域地区，随后向东挺进，击败了庞大但内部腐败的难陀帝国。旃陀罗笈多的成功依靠三个要素：考底利耶的政治谋略、西北边疆社群对马其顿统治的反抗力量，以及难陀朝廷内部对暴虐统治的广泛不满。他建立的孔雀帝国将成为南亚历史上第一个统一大部分次大陆的帝国，其行政体制之完善在古代世界堪称典范。
description.en: Chandragupta rose from humble origins but harbored vast ambitions. Tradition held that he witnessed Alexander's army as a youth and was inspired. With Kautilya's guidance, he launched his campaign from the northwestern frontier around 324-322 BCE, first recovering the Indus valley regions after Macedonian withdrawal, then pushing eastward to overthrow the vast but internally corrupt Nanda Empire. Chandragupta's success rested on three elements: Kautilya's political genius, the resistance of northwestern frontier communities against Macedonian rule, and widespread resentment within the Nanda court against tyrannical governance. The Maurya Empire he founded would become the first to unify most of the Indian subcontinent, with an administrative system regarded as exemplary in the ancient world.

role.zh: 帝国建设的总策划者，《利论》的作者，旃陀罗笈多最重要的政治导师
role.en: Master architect of empire-building, author of the Arthashastra, and Chandragupta's most important political mentor

title.en: Chief of the Mountain Tribal Alliance

role.zh: 为旃陀罗笈多提供西北部族军事力量支持
role.en: Provided northwestern tribal military support for Chandragupta's campaigns

ideology.zh: 难陀王朝复辟——反对孔雀家族的篡位，维护难陀王室的正统合法性，企图恢复旧王朝统治
ideology.en: Nanda restoration — opposing the Maurya usurpation, upholding the legitimate authority of the Nanda royal house, and seeking to restore the old dynasty's rule

headquarters.en: Peripheral regions of the Ganges basin

relationship.zh: 难陀残余势力由被推翻的难陀王朝支持者组成，散布于帝国边缘地带。他们对旃陀罗笈多的篡位充满怨恨，在地方上维系旧王朝的忠诚网络，伺机反扑。尽管其军事实力已大为削弱，但在部分省份仍保有潜在的颠覆能力。
relationship.en: The Nanda remnants consisted of supporters of the overthrown dynasty, scattered across the empire's periphery. Filled with resentment at Chandragupta's usurpation, they maintained loyalty networks to the old dynasty in the provinces and awaited opportunities to strike back. Though their military strength had been greatly diminished, they retained latent subversive potential in some provinces.

description.zh: 难陀王朝曾是恒河流域最强大的帝国，统治北印度长达数十年，以强大的军事力量和严苛的税收制度著称。当旃陀罗笈多在考底利耶的辅佐下推翻陀那难陀后，大量难陀王朝的支持者并未彻底消失。部分前朝将领带着武装逃往帝国边缘地区，在恒河上游、德干高原北部和东部边疆建立了地下抵抗网络。这些残余势力虽然缺乏统一领导和充足资源，却在地方精英中保有相当影响力——尤其是那些在新政权下失去特权的旧难陀官僚和军事贵族。他们代表了对孔雀帝国合法性的持续挑战，迫使旃陀罗笈多在即位初期不得不投入大量军事和情报资源来维持内部稳定。难陀残余势力的存在也是考底利耶在《利论》中特别强调间谍网络和内部安全的重要原因之一。
description.en: The Nanda dynasty had been the most powerful empire in the Ganges basin, ruling northern India for decades and renowned for its formidable military and harsh taxation. When Chandragupta overthrew Dhana Nanda with Kautilya's guidance, many Nanda supporters did not simply disappear. Some former generals fled with armed retinues to the empire's margins, establishing underground resistance networks in the upper Ganges region, the northern Deccan, and the eastern frontier. Though lacking unified leadership and adequate resources, these remnants retained considerable influence among local elites — particularly former Nanda bureaucrats and military aristocrats who had lost privileges under the new regime. They represented a persistent challenge to Maurya legitimacy, forcing Chandragupta to devote substantial military and intelligence resources to internal stability during his early reign. The existence of Nanda remnants was also a key reason Kautilya emphasized spy networks and internal security in the Arthashastra.

role.zh: 难陀王朝残存的王室血脉，作为复辟运动的象征性领袖
role.en: Surviving Nanda royal bloodline, serving as symbolic leaders of the restoration movement

role.zh: 拒绝向孔雀王朝效忠的前难陀官员，在边缘地区维持武装抵抗
role.en: Former Nanda officials who refused allegiance to the Maurya dynasty, maintaining armed resistance in peripheral areas

ideology.zh: 非暴力与苦修主义——强调极端的不杀生（ahimsa）、严格的苦行修炼、灵魂解脱与世俗权力的节制
ideology.en: Non-violence and asceticism — emphasizing radical ahimsa (non-harm), strict ascetic practice, spiritual liberation, and restraint of worldly power

headquarters.zh: 摩揭陀地区耆那教寺院
headquarters.en: Jain monasteries in the Magadha region

relationship.zh: 耆那教僧团对旃陀罗笈多及其宫廷具有深远的精神影响。据传旃陀罗笈多晚年退位后成为耆那教修行者，在南印度绝食而终。耆那教的非暴力理念对帝国统治哲学形成了独特的制衡。
relationship.en: The Jain monastic order exerted profound spiritual influence over Chandragupta and his court. Tradition held that Chandragupta abdicated in later life to become a Jain ascetic, fasting to death in South India. Jain ideals of non-violence provided a unique counterbalance to imperial governing philosophy.

description.zh: 耆那教是与佛教同时期兴起的重要沙门传统，在摩揭陀地区拥有深厚根基。耆那教僧侣以极端的苦修和严格的非暴力戒律闻名，其教义吸引了包括王室成员在内的众多信众。在难陀-孔雀王朝过渡时期，耆那教僧侣不直接参与政治斗争，但通过精神指导和道德教化对统治者产生了重要影响。旃陀罗笈多与耆那教圣人巴德罗巴胡（Bhadrabahu）的关系尤为著名——据传他在帝国晚期追随巴德罗巴胡南行至施罗瓦那贝拉戈拉，最终以耆那教苦行者的方式结束生命。耆那教的非暴力理念、素食传统和对生命的尊重为古印度社会提供了一种与帝国扩张截然不同的价值取向。
description.en: Jainism was a major shramana tradition that arose alongside Buddhism, with deep roots in the Magadha region. Jain monks were renowned for their extreme asceticism and strict non-violence vows, and their doctrines attracted numerous followers including members of the royal family. During the Nanda-Maurya transition, Jain monks did not directly participate in political struggles but exercised important influence through spiritual guidance and moral instruction. Chandragupta's relationship with the Jain saint Bhadrabahu was particularly famous — tradition held that in his later years he followed Bhadrabahu south to Shravanabelagola and ultimately ended his life through the Jain practice of fasting unto death. Jain ideals of non-violence, vegetarianism, and reverence for life provided ancient Indian society with a value orientation starkly different from imperial expansion.

role.zh: 旃陀罗笈多的精神导师，据传引导其走向耆那教修行之路
role.en: Chandragupta's spiritual mentor, traditionally credited with guiding him toward the Jain ascetic path

title.zh: 耆那教学者与僧团领袖

role.zh: 在巴德罗巴胡南行后维持北印度耆那教传统
role.en: Maintained the Jain tradition in North India after Bhadrabahu's southward migration

governmentForm.zh: 以部族贵族和王室权威为基础的君主制，受马其顿承认的地区王国
governmentForm.en: A monarchy based on tribal aristocracy and royal authority, functioning as a regional kingdom recognized by the Macedonians

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王室、战士贵族、地方首领、祭司、农民、牧民、工匠、商人及依附人口组成，兼具印度河流域农业社会与旁遮普部族传统特征
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of the royal household, warrior aristocracy, local chiefs, priests, peasants, pastoralists, artisans, merchants, and dependent populations, combining Indus agricultural society with Punjabi tribal traditions

rulingClass.zh: 王室、战士贵族、象兵与骑兵领主、地方部族首领
rulingClass.en: The royal family, warrior nobles, lords commanding elephants and cavalry, and local tribal chiefs

succession.zh: 以王族世袭为主，通常由成年男性宗室继承，但须获得贵族和武装首领支持
succession.en: Primarily hereditary within the royal family, usually passing to an adult male dynast, though requiring support from nobles and military chiefs

structure.zh: 国家政权以国王为核心，王廷统辖军事、贡赋和司法；地方由附属首领、城邑官员与村社长老共同管理
structure.en: State authority centered on the king, with the court overseeing military affairs, tribute, and justice; local governance was shared by subordinate chiefs, town officials, and village elders

function.zh: 管理王宫、王族财产、宫廷仪礼、使节接待与国王近侍
function.en: Managed the palace, royal property, court ceremony, reception of envoys, and the king's personal attendants

function.zh: 负责征兵、象兵与骑兵调度、边防部署和战时补给
function.en: Handled recruitment, deployment of elephants and cavalry, frontier defense, and wartime supply

function.zh: 征收土地税、实物贡赋、畜产税和贸易关卡收入，并管理粮仓
function.en: Collected land taxes, payments in kind, livestock dues, and customs revenues, and managed granaries

function.zh: 协调附属首领、监督村社义务、传达王令并处理地方争端
function.en: Coordinated subordinate chiefs, supervised village obligations, conveyed royal orders, and handled local disputes

function.zh: 处理贵族纠纷、土地界限、赔偿、宣誓与王命裁决
function.en: Handled noble disputes, land boundaries, compensation, oaths, and judgments issued in the king's name

function.zh: 管理河渡、道路商税、外来商队与区域市场秩序
function.en: Managed ferries, road tolls, foreign caravans, and order in regional markets

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理依赖河谷城镇、要塞据点与村社网络。国王通过封臣首领、税吏、仓吏和村长实施统治，边缘地带往往采取间接统治。
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied on riverine towns, fortified sites, and village networks. The king ruled through vassal chiefs, tax collectors, granary officials, and village headmen, with indirect rule common in peripheral areas.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法、王室裁断、赔偿原则和宗教誓约为基础。严重案件由王廷或贵族会议裁决，村社内部事务多按地方传统解决。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on customary practice, royal adjudication, compensation principles, and sacred oaths. Serious cases were judged by the royal court or noble councils, while village affairs were often settled according to local custom.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收以农产实物征收为主，包括谷物、牲畜、劳役和地方贡品；贸易税与渡口费对王室现金和贵金属收入尤为重要。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation was collected mainly in kind, including grain, livestock, labor service, and local tribute; trade taxes and ferry dues were especially important for the crown's cash and precious-metal income.

religion.zh: 宗教生活以早期婆罗门传统、地方神祇崇拜、祖先祭祀与河流崇拜并存，祭司与地方圣所共同维系宗教秩序
religion.en: Religious life combined early Brahmanical traditions, local deity cults, ancestor worship, and river veneration, maintained by priests and local sanctuaries

philosophy.zh: 思想上受北印度婆罗门礼制、王权伦理和地方英雄传统影响，同时与西北地区更广泛的沙门与思辨风气有所接触
philosophy.en: Intellectual life was shaped by North Indian Brahmanical ritual order, royal ethics, and local heroic traditions, while also touching the wider śramaṇa and speculative currents of the northwest

writingSystem.zh: 行政和高层文化可能同时依赖口述传统与有限的书写实践；阿拉米文在西北交流中仍有影响，印度本地文字使用尚不统一
writingSystem.en: Administration and elite culture likely relied on both oral tradition and limited writing practices; Aramaic still had influence in northwestern exchange, while indigenous Indian scripts were not yet standardized in use

culturalAchievements.zh: 旁遮普地区以战争象队、骑兵文化、河谷农业定居、口头史诗传统和跨区域贸易接触著称；与马其顿人交锋后，其军事声望尤为突出
culturalAchievements.en: The Punjab was noted for war elephants, cavalry culture, settled riverine agriculture, oral heroic traditions, and long-distance trade contacts; after its clash with the Macedonians, its military prestige became especially prominent

languageFamily.zh: 印度-雅利安语支，伴随旁遮普地方方言与多语接触环境
languageFamily.en: Indo-Aryan, accompanied by Punjabi regional dialects and a multilingual contact environment

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以灌溉与季风农业为基础，辅以畜牧、木材、象只、手工业和区域贸易。整体富于农业剩余，但货币化程度仍低于地中海核心王国。
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on irrigated and monsoon agriculture, supplemented by herding, timber, elephants, crafts, and regional trade. It was rich in agrarian surplus overall, though less monetized than core Mediterranean kingdoms.

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物种植、畜牧、战象供养、木材采运、棉织与纺织、陶器、金属加工、河运与商贸
mainIndustries.en: Grain cultivation, animal husbandry, war-elephant maintenance, timber extraction, cotton weaving and textiles, pottery, metalworking, river transport, and trade

tradeGoods.zh: 谷物、棉布、牲畜、木材、象只、皮革、盐、金属器、香料与来自更东部地区的奢侈品转运货物
tradeGoods.en: Grain, cotton cloth, livestock, timber, elephants, leather, salt, metal goods, spices, and transit luxury goods from farther eastern regions

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要体现在土地使用权、牲畜、粮食储备、农具和亲族网络；贵族财富则集中于土地、贡赋、战马与象只。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth lay mainly in land-use rights, livestock, grain reserves, tools, and kinship networks; aristocratic wealth was concentrated in land, tribute, warhorses, and elephants.

economicSystem.zh: 以王室征贡和村社农业为核心的混合型经济，市场交换与长途贸易虽重要，但大部分产出仍通过实物、劳役和地方互惠关系流通
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy centered on royal tribute and village agriculture, in which market exchange and long-distance trade were important, but most output still circulated through payments in kind, labor obligations, and local reciprocity

source.zh: 商税、渡口费与关卡收入
source.en: Trade taxes, ferry dues, and customs

category.en: Granaries, water control, and ferry facilities

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库由贵金属、谷物储备、牲畜、武器和王室可调动贡赋构成。战后因与马其顿关系稳定，财政压力有所缓解，积蓄处于较稳健水平。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury consisted of precious metals, grain reserves, livestock, weapons, and tribute available for royal mobilization. After the war, more stable relations with the Macedonians eased fiscal pressure, leaving reserves at a fairly sound level.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向稳健保守，优先保障军力、王室威望和粮仓储备，同时避免对地方首领与村社课征过重，以维持战后稳定。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was cautious and conservative, prioritizing military power, royal prestige, and granary reserves, while avoiding excessive burdens on local chiefs and village communities in order to preserve postwar stability.

description.zh: 由持矛步兵、盾兵和地方征召兵组成，是军队主体，适合河谷平原作战与守御
description.en: Composed of spearmen, shield-bearers, and locally levied troops, forming the main body of the army and suited to warfare and defense in the river plains

description.zh: 由贵族与其随从构成，机动性强，是王国最有威望的兵种之一
description.en: Made up of nobles and their retainers, highly mobile and one of the kingdom's most prestigious arms

description.zh: 战象是王权象征与战场冲击力量，数量不大但战略与心理价值极高
description.en: War elephants were symbols of kingship and shock forces on the battlefield, small in number but of great strategic and psychological value

description.zh: 战车的重要性下降，但仍具礼仪与部分战术功能；王家护卫负责近身保卫和核心阵线
description.en: Chariots were declining in importance but still retained ceremonial and some tactical roles; royal guards protected the king and held key positions in battle

description.zh: 驻守渡口、要塞和边界道路，负责警戒、治安和快速动员
description.en: Stationed at ferries, forts, and border roads, responsible for surveillance, security, and rapid mobilization

commanderInChief.zh: 波鲁斯国王
commanderInChief.en: King Porus

technology.zh: 军备以长矛、弓、盾、剑、战车和战象为主，擅长在河流、泥泞和平原环境作战；与马其顿交战使其更了解密集步兵与联合兵种战法
technology.en: Military equipment centered on spears, bows, shields, swords, chariots, and war elephants, with strengths in riverine, muddy, and plains warfare; conflict with the Macedonians exposed it to dense infantry and combined-arms tactics

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自西北马其顿继承势力的变动、其他旁遮普首领的竞争以及来自草原与边境地区的骑马部落袭扰；短期内因与马其顿达成承认关系而相对安全。
threats.en: Main threats came from shifts in Macedonian successor power in the northwest, rivalry with other Punjabi rulers, and raids by horse-borne groups from frontier zones; in the short term it was relatively secure because of its recognized relationship with the Macedonians.

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中于旁遮普河流冲积平原、渡口城镇和农业村社，整体分布较分散，但沿主要河道形成较密集居住带
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated chiefly in the alluvial plains, ferry towns, and agricultural villages of the Punjab, dispersed overall but denser along major river corridors

socialClasses.zh: 上层为王室、贵族、祭司和大地主；中间层包括武士、商人、富农和工匠首领；下层为普通农民、佃作者、牧民、劳役者和家内依附人口。
socialClasses.en: The upper strata consisted of the royal house, nobles, priests, and major landholders; the middle included warriors, merchants, prosperous farmers, and master artisans; the lower strata comprised ordinary peasants, tenant cultivators, pastoralists, laborers, and household dependents.

allies.zh: 与马其顿方面维持承认与合作关系，并可能联合部分旁遮普附属首领以巩固地位
allies.en: Maintained a relationship of recognition and cooperation with the Macedonians, and likely worked with some subordinate Punjabi chiefs to consolidate his position

enemies.zh: 潜在敌手包括敌对旁遮普部族、未完全臣服的地方首领，以及未来可能进入该地区的更强大印度王国
enemies.en: Potential enemies included rival Punjabi tribes, not fully submissive local chiefs, and stronger Indian kingdoms that might later push into the region

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策以战后平衡为核心：对马其顿采取务实合作以确保王位与领地，对周边首领则以威慑、联姻、贡纳和军事展示并用。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on postwar balancing: pragmatic cooperation with the Macedonians to secure throne and territory, and a mix of deterrence, marriage alliances, tribute arrangements, and military display toward neighboring chiefs.

era.zh: 希腊化时代早期的南亚西北铁器时代
era.en: Northwestern South Asian Iron Age in the early Hellenistic era

keyInnovations.zh: 铁制兵器与农具、战象系统化使用、河渡交通管理、棉纺织技术、粮仓储藏与区域灌溉农业
keyInnovations.en: Iron weapons and tools, systematic use of war elephants, management of river crossings, cotton textile production, granary storage, and regional irrigated agriculture

strengths.zh: 王国拥有富庶河谷、稳定农业剩余、强大的骑兵与象兵传统，以及波鲁斯个人声望。其战后获马其顿承认，使其在旁遮普地区保持重要地位。
strengths.en: The kingdom possessed fertile river valleys, stable agrarian surplus, strong cavalry and elephant traditions, and the personal prestige of Porus. Its recognition after the war by the Macedonians helped preserve its major position in the Punjab.

weaknesses.zh: 政权对个人领袖、贵族忠诚和地方首领合作依赖较深，行政整合程度有限；货币化不足也限制了财政和长期常备军能力。
weaknesses.en: The regime depended heavily on personal leadership, noble loyalty, and cooperation from local chiefs, while administrative integration remained limited; low monetization also constrained finance and long-term standing military capacity.

outlook.zh: 323年前后局势总体稳定，但亚历山大死后西北权力格局可能迅速变化。若能维持对地方首领的控制并妥善应对马其顿继业者与恒河流域强国的压力，王国仍可延续一段时期。
outlook.en: Around 323 BCE the situation was broadly stable, but the northwestern balance of power could shift rapidly after Alexander's death. If it maintained control over local chiefs and responded effectively to pressures from Macedonian successors and stronger Gangetic powers, the kingdom could endure for some time.

description.zh: 希达斯佩斯河战后获马其顿承认，仍是旁遮普重要地方王国。
description.en: Recognized by the Macedonians after the Hydaspes battle, it remained a major local kingdom in the Punjab.

name.zh: 印度河—希腊化边疆诸政体

ruler.zh: 培松（阿革诺耳之子）等马其顿总督与地方统治者
ruler.en: Peithon son of Agenor and other Macedonian satraps with local rulers

governmentForm.zh: 马其顿军事占领下的总督制边疆王国，结合希腊驻军与本地附庸王公
governmentForm.en: A satrapal frontier kingdom under Macedonian military occupation, combining Greek garrisons with local client rulers

socialStructure.zh: 社会由马其顿—希腊驻军与官员、本地王公与贵族、城市商人与工匠、乡村农民、部族社群及奴仆构成；不同地区保留强烈地方性。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of Macedonian-Greek garrison troops and officials, local kings and nobles, urban merchants and artisans, rural cultivators, tribal communities, and dependents or slaves; strong regional variation persisted.

rulingClass.zh: 马其顿军政精英、受承认的印度地方王公、税吏与城邦化城市中的上层家族
rulingClass.en: The ruling class comprised Macedonian military-administrative elites, recognized Indian local princes, tax officials, and leading families in urbanized centers

succession.zh: 名义上由亚历山大帝国任命总督，实际上依赖军队支持、继业者权力斗争及与地方统治者的协商；地方王位多按本地世袭习惯传承。
succession.en: Formally, satraps were appointed by Alexander's empire; in practice, succession depended on army backing, Successor struggles, and negotiation with local rulers, while local thrones generally followed indigenous hereditary custom.

structure.zh: 政体呈分层混合结构：塔克西拉的总督中枢统辖主要驻军、税收与道路节点；下辖若干地区由马其顿军官、希腊文书和本地王公共同治理，边远地带多通过附庸关系维持。
structure.en: The polity had a layered hybrid structure: a satrapal center at Taxila controlled major garrisons, revenue, and road junctions; below it, districts were governed jointly by Macedonian officers, Greek scribes, and local princes, while remote zones were held mainly through tributary relationships.

function.zh: 处理政令发布、文书、使节往来与总督日常行政
function.en: Handled decrees, records, diplomatic correspondence, and the satrap's daily administration

function.zh: 征收地税、贡赋、关税与牧场税，管理仓储与银款
function.en: Collected land tax, tribute, customs dues, and pastoral levies, and managed granaries and bullion

function.zh: 统筹驻军补给、边防调动、兵员名册与要塞维护
function.en: Coordinated garrison supply, frontier deployments, troop rolls, and fortress maintenance

function.zh: 维护河渡、道路、驿站与军用通信
function.en: Maintained ferries, roads, relay stations, and military communications

function.zh: 协调地方王公、部族首领、城市议事群体与总督府关系
function.en: Managed relations among local princes, tribal chiefs, urban councils, and the satrapal center

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政因地而异：塔克西拉及主要通道由驻军与官吏直接控制；旁遮普河谷和山前地带多由保留权位的地方王公、城邑首领与部族酋长在缴纳贡赋和提供兵员条件下自治。
localAdmin.en: Local administration varied widely: Taxila and key corridors were directly controlled by garrisons and officials, while much of the Punjab river valleys and piedmont was left to local princes, town leaders, and tribal chiefs who retained authority in return for tribute and military support.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系并不统一，马其顿军法、希腊行政习惯、本地成文与习惯法并行；涉及驻军、叛乱、贡赋与道路安全的案件由总督及军官优先裁断，土地、婚姻与社群事务多依地方传统处理。
legalSystem.en: The legal system was not uniform: Macedonian military law, Greek administrative practice, and indigenous written or customary law operated side by side; cases involving garrisons, revolt, tribute, and road security were decided primarily by the satrap and officers, while land, marriage, and community matters were generally settled by local custom.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政主要依靠土地税、定额贡赋、城市商税、道路与渡口关税、战时征发及附庸供给；税收常以谷物、牲畜、织物、金属和银两混合形式缴纳。
taxationSystem.en: Fiscal extraction rested chiefly on land tax, fixed tribute, urban commercial dues, road and ferry customs, wartime requisitions, and client contributions; payment was often made in mixed forms including grain, livestock, textiles, metals, and silver.

religion.zh: 宗教高度多元，主要为吠陀—地方祭祀传统、早期婆罗门宗教实践、地方神灵崇拜，并夹杂希腊人对宙斯、雅典娜、赫拉克勒斯等神祇的祭礼；也可能接触到沙门传统与苦行团体。
religion.en: Religion was highly plural, dominated by Vedic-local ritual traditions, early Brahmanical practice, and regional cults, alongside Greek worship of Zeus, Athena, Heracles, and other deities; contacts with śramaṇa traditions and ascetic communities were also likely.

philosophy.zh: 思想层面呈跨文化接触：希腊世界带来修辞、政治与自然哲思传统；印度本地则存在祭祀学、伦理思考、王权观念和苦行求道传统，双方交流仍处早期阶段。
philosophy.en: The intellectual sphere showed early cross-cultural contact: the Greek world brought traditions of rhetoric, politics, and natural speculation, while indigenous India possessed ritual learning, ethical thought, ideas of kingship, and ascetic quests; interaction between them remained in an early phase.

writingSystem.zh: 书写使用并不统一，行政上可能采用阿拉米字母与希腊文记录，地方则以口述、会计符号和早期印度次大陆书写传统并行；正式国家化碑铭体系尚未成熟。
writingSystem.en: Writing was not standardized: administration may have used Aramaic script and Greek for some records, while local practice relied on oral transmission, accounting marks, and early South Asian writing habits; a fully developed state inscriptional system had not yet emerged.

culturalAchievements.zh: 该地区的重要文化成就在于充当欧亚交流前沿：塔克西拉作为学术、商业与交通中心，汇集印度工艺、波斯行政遗产与希腊军事城市元素，为后来的希腊—佛教与希印艺术融合奠定基础。
culturalAchievements.en: Its major cultural achievement lay in serving as a Eurasian contact frontier: Taxila as a center of learning, trade, and movement combined Indian craftsmanship, Persian administrative legacies, and Greek military-urban elements, laying groundwork for later Greco-Buddhist and Indo-Hellenic syntheses.

languageFamily.zh: 语言环境复杂，以印度-雅利安语支方言为主，同时存在西北部伊朗语成分、阿拉米行政语影响及少量希腊语军政用语。
languageFamily.en: The linguistic environment was complex, dominated by Indo-Aryan dialects, with northwestern Iranian elements, Aramaic administrative influence, and limited Greek military-administrative usage.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以灌溉与河谷农业为基础，辅以畜牧、手工业、区域贸易和军需运输。由于战事与驻军负担，整体产出可观但高度不稳定，财富集中于城市节点、地方王公与军政精英手中。
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on irrigated and riverine agriculture, supplemented by pastoralism, handicrafts, regional trade, and military transport. Owing to warfare and the burden of garrisons, total output was substantial but highly unstable, with wealth concentrated in urban nodes, local princes, and military-administrative elites.

mainIndustries.zh: 农业、畜牧、纺织、金属加工、木工、陶器、象牙工艺、驮运与过境贸易
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture, pastoralism, textiles, metalworking, carpentry, pottery, ivory craft, caravan transport, and transit trade

tradeGoods.zh: 谷物、棉织物、羊毛制品、木材、战马、牛只、象牙、半宝石、香料与药材、金属器、盐
tradeGoods.en: Grain, cotton textiles, woolens, timber, horses, cattle, ivory, semi-precious stones, spices and medicinal goods, metalwares, and salt

name.zh: 德拉克马与地方银衡货币

householdWealth.zh: 普通农户财富多体现为耕地使用权、牛只、谷物储藏与家内工具；城市商人和税务承包者拥有更多白银、珠饰和贸易库存。边疆战争使家庭财富易遭征发与掠夺。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth was mainly held in cultivation rights, cattle, stored grain, and domestic tools; urban merchants and tax contractors possessed more silver, ornaments, and trading stock. Frontier warfare made household assets vulnerable to requisition and plunder.

economicSystem.zh: 经济制度为农业贡赋型与商业关税型并存的混合边疆经济，国家直接控制有限，更多依靠地方统治者、行会型工匠群体、商旅网络与军事征发。
economicSystem.en: The economic system was a mixed frontier economy combining agrarian tribute and commercial customs; direct state control was limited, with greater reliance on local rulers, craft groups resembling guild networks, merchant caravans, and military requisition.

category.en: Subsidies to local rulers and client management

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库规模中等，部分为可动用银币与银锭，部分为谷仓储备、缴获物资与地方贡品。因亚历山大去世后的权力震荡，资金调拨面临高风险。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was moderate in size, consisting partly of usable coin and bullion and partly of granary stores, captured supplies, and tribute goods. After Alexander's death, political upheaval made access to these resources precarious.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向战时维持：优先保障驻军与交通线，必要时提高贡赋与临时征发；对忠诚地方王公则以减税、礼物和自治交换支持。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was geared toward wartime maintenance: priority went to garrisons and communications, with tribute and emergency requisitions raised when necessary; loyal local rulers were rewarded with lighter burdens, gifts, and autonomy in exchange for support.

description.zh: 由马其顿与希腊士兵、殖民驻军及部分雇佣步兵组成，负责城防、要塞和主要道路节点。
description.en: Composed of Macedonian and Greek soldiers, settler-garrisons, and some mercenary infantry, responsible for cities, fortresses, and key road junctions.

description.zh: 包括马其顿骑兵、地方贵族骑手与侦察部队，适合快速镇压叛乱与边境巡逻。
description.en: Included Macedonian cavalry, local noble horsemen, and reconnaissance units, suited for rapid suppression of revolt and frontier patrol.

description.zh: 由地方王公、部族首领和城邑提供的临时兵员，战斗力参差不齐，但对维持广大乡村控制至关重要。
description.en: Temporary troops supplied by local princes, tribal chiefs, and towns; uneven in quality but crucial for maintaining control over the wider countryside.

description.zh: 包含象队、工兵、驮运、弓箭手和后勤人员；战象数量有限，但在威慑与会战中具有象征和实用价值。
description.en: Included elephant corps, engineers, transport handlers, archers, and logistical personnel; war elephants were limited in number but valuable both symbolically and tactically.

commanderInChief.zh: 总督兼最高军事统帅
commanderInChief.en: The Satrap as Commander-in-Chief

technology.zh: 军事技术融合马其顿方阵传统、长矛步兵、复合攻城器械、骑兵突击和印度本地弓兵、战象、河流渡运经验。攻城和桥梁架设能力较强，但补给线漫长。
technology.en: Military technology combined the Macedonian phalanx tradition, pike infantry, developed siege equipment, cavalry shock tactics, and Indian archery, elephants, and river-crossing expertise. Siege and bridging capacity were relatively strong, but supply lines were long.

threats.zh: 主要威胁包括亚历山大死后继业者之间的权力斗争、本地旁遮普统治者反叛、边远部族袭扰、驻军哗变以及来自印度恒河流域强权扩张的潜在压力。
threats.en: Main threats included the Successor power struggle after Alexander's death, rebellion by Punjabi rulers, raids from outlying tribes, mutiny among garrisons, and the long-term pressure of expanding powers from the Gangetic plain.

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要分布于旁遮普河谷、塔克西拉周边和灌溉较发达地区，乡村人口占绝对多数。民族与文化构成复杂，本地印度人居多，另有少量马其顿、希腊、伊朗与阿拉米语行政人群。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated mainly in the Punjab river valleys, around Taxila, and in better-irrigated zones, with an overwhelming rural majority. Its ethnic and cultural composition was complex: indigenous Indians formed the majority, with smaller Macedonian, Greek, Iranian, and Aramaic-speaking administrative elements.

socialClasses.zh: 上层为总督、驻军将领、地方王公与祭司贵族；中层包括商人、文书、工匠、税吏与城镇地主；下层为农民、牧民、佃作者、劳役者与奴仆。部族社群和地方武装在阶层之外仍具独立影响力。
socialClasses.en: Upper strata included the satrap, garrison commanders, local princes, and priestly nobles; the middle strata included merchants, scribes, artisans, tax collectors, and urban landholders; lower strata were made up of peasants, pastoralists, tenants, laborers, and slaves or dependents. Tribal communities and local armed groups retained influence outside rigid class categories.

allies.zh: 名义上效忠马其顿帝国中央权威，并依赖若干归附的地方王公、塔克西拉亲马其顿派和愿意提供兵粮的城邑精英。
allies.en: Nominally allied to the central Macedonian imperial authority, and dependent on several submissive local rulers, pro-Macedonian elites in Taxila, and urban notables willing to furnish troops and supplies.

enemies.zh: 敌对者包括反马其顿的旁遮普王公、拒绝纳贡的山地与河间部族，以及在继业者内战中可能试图接管该区的其他马其顿势力。
enemies.en: Enemies included anti-Macedonian Punjabi rulers, hill and interfluvial tribes refusing tribute, and rival Macedonian forces that might attempt to seize the region during the Successor conflicts.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策以守成为主：维持对印度河流域交通线和税源的控制，通过分封、赦免、联姻、交换人质与军事威慑稳定边疆，同时谨慎观察巴比伦与帝国核心区的权力变化。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy was primarily defensive: it aimed to retain control of the Indus communications network and revenue base, stabilizing the frontier through grants, pardons, marriage alliances, hostage exchange, and military deterrence, while carefully watching political shifts in Babylon and the imperial core.

era.zh: 希腊化时期早期
era.en: Early Hellenistic Period

keyInnovations.zh: 关键能力在于跨文化军政技术结合：马其顿方阵与攻城术、波斯式行省行政经验、印度河流域灌溉和战象运用、长距离道路与驿传组织。
keyInnovations.en: Key strengths lay in the combination of cross-cultural military and administrative techniques: Macedonian phalanx and siegecraft, Persian-style satrapal governance, Indus irrigation and elephant use, and long-distance road and relay organization.

strengths.zh: 优势在于控制塔克西拉这一枢纽城市、拥有较强驻军和攻城能力、能利用既有阿契美尼德行政遗产并吸纳地方王公协助统治，且处于连接中亚、伊朗与印度的贸易节点。
strengths.en: Its strengths lay in controlling Taxila as a strategic hub, maintaining substantial garrisons and siege capacity, drawing on former Achaemenid administrative practices while incorporating local princes, and occupying a trade junction linking Central Asia, Iran, and India.

weaknesses.zh: 弱点在于统治合法性薄弱、地域广而分散、对本地附庸与补给线依赖过重、马其顿驻军人数有限且可能哗变，亚历山大死后中央支持也极不确定。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses were fragile legitimacy, wide and fragmented territory, heavy dependence on local clients and supply routes, limited Macedonian manpower vulnerable to mutiny, and highly uncertain central support after Alexander's death.

outlook.zh: 323年前后的前景偏向不稳。短期内若总督与地方王公合作，仍可维持若干河谷与城市据点；中期则极可能因继业者战争、本地反抗和印度新兴强权压力而分裂、易手或被重新整合。
outlook.en: The outlook around 323 BCE was unstable. In the short term, cooperation between satraps and local rulers could preserve several river valleys and urban strongholds; in the medium term, however, the region was highly likely to fragment, change hands, or be reabsorbed under pressure from the Successor wars, local resistance, and rising Indian powers.

description.zh: 亚历山大在印度河流域的征服地由马其顿驻军与本地统治者共同维持。
description.en: Alexander's conquests in the Indus basin were maintained by a combination of Macedonian garrisons and local rulers.

name.zh: 达罗毗荼—泰米尔文化圈

ruler.zh: 早期朱罗、潘地亚、彻拉及其他地方统治者
ruler.en: Early Chola, Pandya, Chera, and other regional rulers

governmentForm.zh: 多王国分立的世袭君主制，以区域宫廷、部族首领与城市港口网络共同构成政治秩序
governmentForm.en: A hereditary monarchical order divided among multiple kingdoms, with regional courts, clan chieftains, and urban-port networks jointly shaping political authority

socialStructure.zh: 社会以王族、贵族战士、祭司与学者、农民、牧民、工匠、商人、海员及依附劳动者构成；地区间差异较大，宗族与职业共同体具有重要地位
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of royal lineages, warrior nobles, priests and learned specialists, cultivators, pastoralists, artisans, merchants, sailors, and dependent laborers; regional variation was substantial, and kinship groups and occupational communities were highly important

rulingClass.zh: 各王国王室、地方酋长、武士贵族与控制土地、战士和贸易节点的显族
rulingClass.en: Royal houses of the constituent kingdoms, local chieftains, warrior aristocracies, and prominent lineages controlling land, armed retainers, and trade nodes

succession.zh: 以王族世袭为主，但常受兄弟支系、地方贵族支持及武力竞争影响，继承规则并不完全固定
succession.en: Succession was primarily dynastic, but was often influenced by collateral branches, support from local nobles, and military competition; inheritance rules were not fully fixed

structure.zh: 不存在单一统一国家，而是由潘地亚、朱罗、彻拉及众多小王、纳都与酋邦组成。各王国宫廷依靠亲族、侍臣、税吏、军首领、港口监督者和村社领袖治理领地。
structure.en: There was no single unified state; rather, the region was composed of the Pandya, Chola, Chera, and many lesser kingships, nadus, and chieftaincies. Each royal court governed through kin networks, retainers, tax collectors, military leaders, port supervisors, and village elders.

function.zh: 管理宫廷、王室财产、仪式接待、馈赠分配与王命传达
function.en: Managed the court, royal estates, ceremonial hospitality, gift distribution, and transmission of royal orders

function.zh: 征收谷物、畜产、林产、手工业贡赋与附庸进贡，监督灌溉区和农业产出
function.en: Collected grain, livestock, forest products, craft dues, and tributary payments, and supervised irrigated tracts and agricultural output

function.zh: 管理沿海锚地、市场税、货物流通、度量衡与对外商人往来
function.en: Oversaw coastal anchorages, market taxes, movement of goods, weights and measures, and dealings with foreign merchants

function.zh: 组织王家卫队、召集附庸兵、守卫交通线并处理王国间冲突
function.en: Organized royal guards, mustered feudatory troops, protected routes, and managed conflicts among neighboring polities

localAdmin.zh: 基层治理依靠村社、氏族长老、地方首领与王室任命的收赋人；王权在核心农业区和重要港口较强，在内陆丘陵和边地则更依赖盟属关系
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied on village communities, clan elders, local chiefs, and royal-appointed collectors; royal authority was stronger in core agrarian zones and major ports, while inland uplands and frontier tracts were governed more through alliance and suzerainty

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法、王命、宗族调解和祭司见证为基础。财产、婚姻、血仇、赔偿、贡赋和贸易纠纷多由地方议决，重大案件由王庭裁断。
legalSystem.en: Law rested on customary norms, royal commands, kin arbitration, and priestly witnessing. Property, marriage, feud, compensation, tribute, and trade disputes were often settled locally, while major cases were judged at court.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收以农产分成、灌溉地赋、牲畜与林产贡纳、港口关税、市场税和附庸贡品为主，多以实物征收，货币税比例有限
taxationSystem.en: Taxation centered on shares of agricultural produce, irrigated land dues, livestock and forest tribute, port customs, market tolls, and payments from subordinate chiefs, with most revenue collected in kind and only a limited portion in money

religion.zh: 以早期南印度多神信仰为主，崇拜地方神灵、山林与河川神、母神、战争与英雄神，并与早期婆罗门仪式传统并存
religion.en: Religion was dominated by early South Indian polytheistic worship, including local deities, hill and river powers, mother goddesses, and warrior-hero cults, coexisting with early Brahmanical ritual traditions

philosophy.zh: 思想以英雄伦理、慷慨王德、家族荣誉、祭祀秩序与口传诗歌传统为核心，同时逐渐吸收来自北方的婆罗门宇宙观与达摩观念
philosophy.en: Thought centered on heroic ethics, royal generosity, family honor, ritual order, and oral poetic traditions, while gradually absorbing Brahmanical cosmology and ideas of dharma from the north

writingSystem.zh: 323年前后当地文学主要仍以口传为主，铭刻使用有限；随着北方接触增加，婆罗米文字将在后续世纪被泰米尔地区逐步采用
writingSystem.en: Around 323 BCE, local literary culture remained primarily oral, with limited inscriptional use; with increasing northern contact, Brahmi script would be gradually adopted in Tamil regions in later centuries

culturalAchievements.zh: 形成了成熟的海洋贸易文化、珍珠采集传统、灌溉农业社会、英雄歌谣与宫廷馈赠体系，为后世桑伽姆文学世界奠定社会文化基础
culturalAchievements.en: The region developed a mature maritime trading culture, pearl-fishing traditions, irrigated agrarian society, heroic bardic performance, and a courtly gift economy, laying the social foundations for the later Sangam literary world

languageFamily.zh: 达罗毗荼语系，以古泰米尔及相关南印度语言为主
languageFamily.en: Dravidian language family, especially early Tamil and related South Indian languages

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以稻作农业、畜牧、林产、渔业、珍珠业和沿海贸易为基础。由于缺乏统一币制且大量交换以实物、馈赠和地区性金属货衡完成，估值仅为跨文明比较用的近似折银口径。
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on rice agriculture, pastoralism, forest products, fishing, pearling, and coastal trade. Because there was no unified currency and much exchange occurred through barter, gift circuits, and regional metal values, the estimate is only an approximate silver-equivalent for cross-civilizational comparison.

mainIndustries.zh: 稻米种植、粟豆杂粮、畜牧、珍珠采集、渔盐业、纺织、金属加工、木材与香料性林产采集、海运贸易
mainIndustries.en: Rice cultivation, millet and pulse farming, pastoralism, pearl fishing, fisheries and salt production, textiles, metalworking, timber and aromatic forest extraction, and maritime trade

tradeGoods.zh: 珍珠、海产、象牙、宝石、香木、胡椒等林产与香料、棉织物、盐、铁器、稻米，以及输入的马匹、奢侈品和部分金属
tradeGoods.en: Pearls, marine products, ivory, gemstones, aromatic woods, pepper and related forest-spice goods, cotton textiles, salt, iron tools, rice, and imported horses, luxury items, and some metals

name.zh: 金属权衡与地方交换物

unitName.zh: 权衡银与各类实物单位
unitName.en: Weighted silver and various commodity units

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要体现为土地使用权、牲畜、谷物储备、首饰、工具、渔具与亲族支持网络；沿海商人与珍珠业经营者财富显著高于农户
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was mainly expressed in access to land, livestock, grain stores, ornaments, tools, fishing equipment, and kin support networks; coastal merchants and pearl operators were markedly wealthier than ordinary cultivators

economicSystem.zh: 以农业村社和宫廷再分配为基础，辅以部族贡纳、港口商税和远近海贸易的混合型经济
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy based on agrarian village production and courtly redistribution, supplemented by tribal tribute, port taxation, and coastal and overseas trade

source.en: Tribute from Subordinates and War Gifts

treasuryDescription.zh: 财政储备分散存在各王国宫廷、神庙性储藏、谷仓与贵金属饰物中，而非单一中央金库；实物储备的重要性常高于铸币或标准银锭
treasuryDescription.en: Treasury resources were dispersed across royal courts, temple-like storages, granaries, and precious ornaments rather than concentrated in a single central vault; reserves in kind were often more important than coin or standardized bullion

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向稳健保守，以维持王室威望、军队、灌溉与馈赠网络为优先，在丰年积谷积财，在战事与歉收时依赖附庸和商税补充
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was relatively conservative, prioritizing royal prestige, armed forces, irrigation, and gift networks; grain and wealth were accumulated in good years, while tributaries and trade taxes helped cover wartime or poor-harvest pressures

description.zh: 由持矛、盾、弓和短兵器的步兵构成，是各王国军力主干，平时部分为常备侍从，战时大量由地方征发
description.en: Composed of spearmen, shield-bearers, archers, and short-weapon infantry, this formed the core of regional armies; some served as permanent retainers in peacetime, while many were levied locally in war

description.zh: 战象具有威慑和冲击作用，常配以驾驭者和护卫，数量不大但象征王权与精锐力量
description.en: War elephants provided intimidation and shock value, typically with mahouts and escorts; their numbers were limited but they symbolized royal power and elite force

description.zh: 骑兵规模较小，因南印度优质军马依赖外来贸易，主要用于侦察、追击和贵族作战
description.en: Cavalry remained relatively small because quality warhorses in South India depended on external trade; they were used mainly for scouting, pursuit, and aristocratic combat

name.zh: 地方附庸兵与山地部队

description.zh: 由地方酋长、边地部族和附属首领提供，熟悉丘陵、森林和边境环境，机动性较强但统合度有限
description.en: Supplied by local chiefs, frontier tribes, and subordinate rulers, these troops were familiar with hills, forests, and borderlands, with strong mobility but limited uniform cohesion

description.zh: 并非大型远洋战舰体系，而是由港口守备、武装商船和沿海运输船构成，用于护航、巡逻和控制海岸贸易点
description.en: This was not a system of large blue-water war fleets, but rather port guards, armed merchant vessels, and coastal transport craft used for escort, patrol, and control of littoral trade points

commanderInChief.zh: 诸王亲自统军，辅以王族将领与地方酋长
commanderInChief.en: The kings themselves served as supreme commanders, assisted by royal kinsmen and local chiefs

technology.zh: 军事技术属铁器时代南亚水平，以铁制兵器、弓矛盾牌、战象、土木防御和适应森林丘陵地形的作战方式为特征
technology.en: Military technology reflected Iron Age South Asian standards, characterized by iron weapons, bows, spears, shields, war elephants, earthwork defenses, and fighting methods adapted to forests and hilly terrain

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近王国竞争、附庸叛离、山地与边地控制不稳，以及争夺港口、珍珠渔场和贸易通道的冲突；来自遥远地中海世界的威胁主要是间接贸易影响而非直接军事入侵
threats.en: The chief threats came from rivalry among neighboring kingdoms, defections by subordinates, unstable control of upland and frontier zones, and conflicts over ports, pearl banks, and trade routes; threats from the distant Mediterranean world were primarily indirect commercial influences rather than direct invasion

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要分布于泰米尔平原、河谷、沿海聚落和部分内陆高产区，也包括德干南部与西海岸的相关达罗毗荼人群。整体人口密度在灌溉农业区较高，在森林、丘陵和干旱地带较低。
populationDescription.en: Population was concentrated in the Tamil plains, river valleys, coastal settlements, and some productive inland tracts, while also including related Dravidian populations of the southern Deccan and west coast. Density was highest in irrigated agrarian zones and lower in forests, hills, and dry regions.

socialClasses.zh: 王族与贵族、地方酋长、武士与侍从、祭司和婆罗门群体、农民、牧民、渔民、工匠、商人、歌者诗人以及依附劳动者共同构成复杂社会层级，但尚未呈现后世更定型化的等级结构
socialClasses.en: Royal houses and nobles, local chiefs, warriors and retainers, priests and Brahmanical groups, cultivators, pastoralists, fishers, artisans, merchants, bards, and dependent laborers formed a complex hierarchy, though not yet the more rigidly formalized order of later periods

allies.zh: 对外关系以近邻港口、岛屿航线、斯里兰卡方向社群以及德干南部诸王与酋长的临时联盟为主，并无稳定统一的国际同盟体系
allies.en: External relations were based mainly on shifting arrangements with nearby ports, island routes, communities toward Sri Lanka, and southern Deccan kings and chiefs, rather than any stable unified international alliance system

enemies.zh: 敌对对象主要是彼此竞争的南印度王国、争夺贡赋与贸易线的地方首领，以及偶发对沿海资源和边地控制权的冲突者
enemies.en: Principal enemies were rival South Indian kingdoms, local chiefs contesting tribute and trade routes, and occasional opponents in struggles over coastal resources and frontier control

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策重在维持区域均势、保障海上贸易、控制珍珠与港口税源，并通过婚姻、馈赠、附庸关系和军事示威扩大影响
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on preserving regional balance, securing maritime trade, controlling pearl resources and port revenues, and extending influence through marriage, gift exchange, suzerainty, and military display

era.zh: 铁器时代晚期至早期历史时代南印度阶段，属希腊化时代同时期
era.en: Late Iron Age to Early Historic South Indian phase, contemporary with the Hellenistic Period

keyInnovations.zh: 铁器农具与武器、湿稻灌溉技术、沿海航行经验、珍珠采集与海产加工、纺织与染色工艺，以及逐渐扩展的远距离贸易组织
keyInnovations.en: Iron agricultural tools and weapons, wet-rice irrigation techniques, coastal navigation experience, pearl extraction and marine processing, textile and dyeing crafts, and increasingly extensive long-distance trade organization

strengths.zh: 优势在于人口充足、农业与海洋资源互补、多个港口连接印度洋贸易、地方王权具有韧性、珍珠和高价值特产能支持宫廷财政与外交馈赠
strengths.en: Strengths included a substantial population, complementary agrarian and maritime resources, multiple ports linked to Indian Ocean trade, resilient regional kingship structures, and pearls and high-value specialties that supported court finance and diplomatic gift exchange

weaknesses.zh: 弱点在于政治分裂、统一行政能力有限、军队多依赖附庸与临时征发、骑兵资源不足、财政标准化程度低，且内陆与边地整合不均衡
weaknesses.en: Weaknesses included political fragmentation, limited unified administrative capacity, armies heavily dependent on feudatories and temporary levies, insufficient cavalry resources, low fiscal standardization, and uneven integration of inland and frontier regions

outlook.zh: 323年前后整体局势稳定，短期内不易被北方帝国直接吞并。未来数世纪，随着海贸扩张、北方文字与宗教传统南传，以及区域王国竞争加剧，泰米尔世界有望进一步形成更清晰的王朝格局与文学文化传统。
outlook.en: Around 323 BCE the overall situation was stable, and the region was unlikely to be directly absorbed by northern empires in the short term. Over the coming centuries, expanding maritime trade, the southward spread of northern scripts and religious traditions, and intensified competition among regional kingdoms would likely produce clearer dynastic formations and a more defined literary-cultural tradition in the Tamil world.

description.zh: 德干与泰米尔地区分布着多个王国与港口网络，未受北印帝国直接统合。
description.en: The Deccan and Tamil regions contained multiple kingdoms and port networks beyond direct northern imperial integration.

governmentForm.zh: 经法家改革强化的中央集权君主制诸侯国，王权通过郡县、军功爵制与成文法直接控制地方与民众。
governmentForm.en: A centralized monarchical Warring States kingdom strengthened by Legalist reforms, in which royal authority governed localities and population directly through commanderies and counties, a merit-rank system, and written law.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以编户齐民的农民家庭为基础，上有国君、宗室与军功贵族，中有官吏、士人与富农，下有普通农民、工匠、商贾、刑徒与奴婢；身份虽有等级，但军功与仕进提供一定上升通道。
socialStructure.en: Society was based on registered peasant households, with the king, royal clan, and military merit aristocracy at the top; officials, retainers, and prosperous farmers in the middle; and ordinary peasants, artisans, merchants, convicts, and slaves or bonded servants below. Rank remained hierarchical, but military merit and office offered some upward mobility.

rulingClass.zh: 秦王、嬴姓宗室、受封君侯、高级文武官僚与凭军功受爵者共同构成统治阶层，其中以王廷官僚集团影响最大。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted of the Qin king, the Ying royal house, enfeoffed nobles, senior civil and military officials, and those ennobled through military merit, with the royal bureaucratic establishment being the most influential.

succession.zh: 王位原则上由王族嫡系继承，但实际继承常受宫廷政治、宗室平衡与重臣支持影响。
succession.en: The throne in principle passed through the direct royal line, though actual succession was often shaped by court politics, clan balance, and the support of leading ministers.

structure.zh: 秦国政府以国君为核心，中央设相、将军、御史等高官统辖政务、军事与监察；地方实行郡县制，由国君任命郡守、县令，削弱世卿世禄贵族的独立权力。
structure.en: The Qin government was centered on the king, with top central officials such as the chancellor, generals, and censors overseeing civil administration, military affairs, and supervision. Locally, Qin employed the commandery-county system, with governors and magistrates appointed by the king, reducing the autonomy of hereditary nobles.

function.zh: 总理全国政务、法令执行、官员协调与资源调配。
function.en: Managed overall state administration, execution of laws, coordination of officials, and allocation of resources.

function.zh: 监察百官、核查奏报、纠察违法与监督地方行政。
function.en: Supervised officials, audited reports, investigated misconduct, and monitored local administration.

function.zh: 管理征兵、军籍、粮秣、武库、战时动员与将领任命。
function.en: Handled conscription, military registration, grain supply, arsenals, wartime mobilization, and appointment of commanders.

name.en: Inner Secretariat and Royal Domain Offices

function.zh: 治理咸阳及其周边王畿，管理宫廷供给、工程与直属田地。
function.en: Administered Xianyang and the surrounding royal domain, managing court supply, public works, and crown lands.

function.zh: 征收田租、户赋与徭役，储存粮食并调配赈济与军粮。
function.en: Collected land taxes, household levies, and labor obligations, while storing grain and distributing relief and military provisions.

localAdmin.zh: 地方以郡、县为主，郡守统辖军事与行政，县令或县长直接管理编户、土地、税赋、徭役与治安；边地常附有军镇性质。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was organized chiefly into commanderies and counties. Commandery governors oversaw military and civil matters, while county magistrates directly managed registered households, land, taxation, labor service, and public order. Frontier regions often had a semi-military character.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系深受商鞅以来法家思想影响，以成文法、连坐、重农抑商、严刑峻罚和统一量责为特征；法律既是惩罚工具，也是国家组织社会与动员战争的核心手段。
legalSystem.en: The legal system was deeply shaped by Legalist thought since Shang Yang, characterized by written statutes, collective responsibility, promotion of agriculture over commerce, severe punishments, and standardized accountability. Law served both as a punitive instrument and as a central mechanism for organizing society and mobilizing for war.

taxationSystem.zh: 税制以田租、户赋、徭役和兵役为核心，并辅以山泽、盐铁、官营作坊收入及战利品；征收依户籍、土地登记与地方官审核进行。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation centered on land tax, household levies, labor corvée, and military service, supplemented by income from state-controlled resources, salt and iron activities, workshops, and spoils of war. Assessment relied on household registration, land records, and review by local officials.

religion.zh: 秦人的宗教生活以祖先崇拜、社稷祭祀、天地山川神祇祭祀和占卜礼仪为主，王权通过国家祭祀强化合法性；同时保留部分西方边地与古周传统信仰。
religion.en: Qin religious life centered on ancestor worship, state sacrifices to the soil and grain, rites to Heaven, Earth, mountains, and rivers, and divinatory practices. Royal legitimacy was reinforced through state ritual, while some western frontier and older Zhou traditions remained.

philosophy.zh: 主导政治思想为法家，强调法、术、势与国家富强；同时儒家礼制观念、阴阳五行思维、兵家与实用治理经验也在上层政治文化中并存。
philosophy.en: The dominant political philosophy was Legalism, emphasizing law, administrative technique, authority, and state strength. At the same time, Confucian ritual ideas, yin-yang and five phases thought, military theory, and practical statecraft coexisted within elite political culture.

writingSystem.zh: 使用战国时期汉字书写体系，秦文字在字形上较趋整齐统一，广泛用于官文书、律令、契券与铭文，为后来的小篆统一奠定基础。
writingSystem.en: The state used the Warring States Chinese writing system. Qin script already showed comparatively regularized forms and was widely used in administrative documents, legal texts, contracts, and inscriptions, laying groundwork for the later standardization of Small Seal script.

culturalAchievements.zh: 秦在制度文化上的成就尤为突出，包括郡县制深化、军功爵体系、严格户籍管理、道路与水利建设、标准化行政文书传统，以及将战争动员与国家治理高度结合的政治文化。
culturalAchievements.en: Qin's major cultural achievements were institutional: the deepening of the commandery-county system, the military merit rank order, strict household registration, road and hydraulic construction, increasingly standardized administrative documentation, and a political culture that tightly integrated warfare with governance.

languageFamily.zh: 汉藏语系汉语族，上层与民间主要使用古汉语秦地方变体。
languageFamily.en: Sino-Tibetan, Sinitic branch, with elites and commoners primarily speaking Old Chinese in Qin regional forms.

gdpDescription.zh: 秦国经济在战国后期属华夏诸国中最强之一，核心优势在于关中平原的高效农业、国家对人口与土地的直接控制、成熟的战时动员能力，以及不断通过征服与移民扩张生产面。
gdpDescription.en: By the late Warring States period, Qin had one of the strongest economies among the Huaxia states. Its core advantages lay in the efficient agriculture of the Guanzhong plain, direct state control over people and land, a mature capacity for wartime mobilization, and continual expansion of the productive base through conquest and resettlement.

mainIndustries.zh: 农业、畜牧、冶铁、青铜铸造、兵器制造、盐业、木工、陶器、车马制造、土木工程与官营手工业。
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture, animal husbandry, ironworking, bronze casting, weapons manufacture, salt production, carpentry, pottery, chariot and horse equipment production, civil engineering, and state-run handicrafts.

tradeGoods.zh: 粟、麦、豆、盐、铁器、青铜器、兵器、皮革、木材、漆器、马匹、布帛与各类官营作坊制品。
tradeGoods.en: Millet, wheat, beans, salt, iron tools, bronze goods, weapons, leather, timber, lacquerware, horses, textiles, and products from state-supervised workshops.

name.zh: 半两钱与秦制金属货币

householdWealth.zh: 普通农户财富主要体现为耕地、农具、牲畜、粮储和家庭劳动力；富裕之家可积累更多土地、车马、铜铁器与爵位相关特权。国家通过户籍与法令严密监控家庭资产与劳役能力。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was expressed mainly in arable land, farm tools, animals, grain stores, and family labor. Wealthier families could accumulate more land, carts, horses, bronze and iron tools, and privileges associated with rank. The state closely monitored household assets and labor capacity through registration and law.

economicSystem.zh: 以国家主导农业生产、严密户籍管理和法家规制为基础的战争型混合经济。民间市场存在且活跃，但国家优先保障粮食、兵员、运输与战略物资供应。
economicSystem.en: A war-oriented mixed economy grounded in state-directed agriculture, strict household registration, and Legalist regulation. Private markets existed and were active, but the state prioritized grain, manpower, transport, and strategic materials.

source.en: State Salt, Iron, and Workshop Income
Market, Transit, Resource, and Miscellaneous Taxes

treasuryDescription.zh: 秦国财政储备以粮仓、金属货币、兵器库存、牲畜与可调用劳力的综合形态存在，不仅是金属财宝，更是国家战争机器的后备资源。
treasuryDescription.en: Qin treasury reserves existed as an integrated stock of granary stores, metal currency, weapon inventories, livestock, and callable labor, representing not merely treasure but the reserve resources of a war state.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策强调增产、强征、积储与服务军事扩张。国家倾向压低非必要消费，将资源导向农业、军备、基础设施和边地开发，以维持长期争霸能力。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy emphasized increased production, strong extraction, reserve accumulation, and support for military expansion. The state tended to suppress nonessential consumption and direct resources toward agriculture, armaments, infrastructure, and frontier development in order to sustain long-term hegemonic competition.

description.zh: 军队主体，由征发农民与职业化边军构成，装备长兵、戟、剑、盾与弩，擅长大规模阵战与攻守城作战。
description.en: The core of the army, composed of conscripted peasants and more professional frontier troops, equipped with spears, halberds, swords, shields, and crossbows, effective in massed field battles and siege warfare.

description.zh: 秦军重要技术兵种，以强弩著称，用于压制敌阵、守城与配合步兵推进。
description.en: A key technical arm of the Qin military, famed for powerful crossbows, used to suppress enemy formations, defend fortifications, and support infantry advances.

description.zh: 主要部署于北部与西部边地，承担侦察、追击、侧击与对抗草原骑射势力的任务。
description.en: Primarily deployed on the northern and western frontiers, cavalry conducted reconnaissance, pursuit, flanking attacks, and operations against steppe mounted archers.

description.zh: 战车在战国晚期已非绝对核心，但仍具礼制、指挥与特定地形作战价值，并附带车乘、驭手与护卫。
description.en: By the late Warring States period chariots were no longer the absolute center of warfare, but they retained ceremonial, command, and limited tactical value in suitable terrain, along with their crews and escorts.

description.zh: 负责筑城、架桥、道路修整、攻城器械、军粮运输与营垒建设，是秦军持续作战能力的关键。
description.en: Responsible for fortification building, bridges, road maintenance, siege equipment, transport of provisions, and camp construction, they were essential to Qin's sustained operational capacity.

commanderInChief.zh: 秦惠文王（名义上）及其所任将军
commanderInChief.en: King Huiwen of Qin (nominally) and his appointed generals

technology.zh: 秦军技术优势体现在标准化兵器生产、强弩、铁制农具与军器并用、成熟的筑城和攻城技术、严格军法、完善道路运输与大规模动员体系。
technology.en: Qin military technology and organization were marked by standardized weapon production, powerful crossbows, broad use of iron tools and arms, mature fortification and siege techniques, strict military law, efficient road transport, and a large-scale mobilization system.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自东方六国尤其韩、魏、赵、楚的联盟与牵制，以及北方边地游牧与半游牧部族的袭扰；同时长期攻伐也对国内粮秣与人力造成压力。
threats.en: The main threats came from the eastern Warring States—especially Han, Wei, Zhao, and Chu—through alliances and balancing efforts, as well as raids by northern frontier nomadic and semi-nomadic groups. Prolonged campaigning also strained domestic grain and manpower.

populationDescription.zh: 秦国人口在战国诸强中居前列，集中分布于关中、渭河流域及新扩张郡县。国家通过编户齐民、移民实边与战后迁徙重组人口，使其既是农业生产基础，也是兵源与赋役来源。
populationDescription.en: Qin's population ranked among the largest of the major Warring States, concentrated in Guanzhong, the Wei River basin, and newly expanded commanderies. Through universal registration, frontier resettlement, and postwar population transfers, the state reorganized population as the foundation of agriculture, military recruitment, and taxation.

socialClasses.zh: 主要阶层包括王族宗室、军功贵族、官僚士人、自耕农与佃农、工匠、商人、士兵、边民、刑徒与奴婢。与旧式世卿政治相比，秦国更强调以国家法令和军功重组社会等级。
socialClasses.en: Major social strata included the royal clan, military merit nobility, bureaucratic officials and retainers, freeholding and tenant farmers, artisans, merchants, soldiers, frontier settlers, convicts, and slaves or bonded servants. Compared with older hereditary aristocratic politics, Qin more strongly reordered rank through state law and military merit.

allies.zh: 323年前后秦国在华夏列国中少有稳定长期盟友，更多采取连横策略，临时联合个别诸侯以分化东方合纵阵营；在周边戎狄与边地部族中也有阶段性附属或协作关系。
allies.en: Around 323 BCE, Qin had few stable long-term allies among the Huaxia states. It more often pursued a horizontal alliance strategy, temporarily aligning with individual states to break eastern coalitions. On the frontiers, some non-Zhou groups maintained temporary subordinate or cooperative ties.

enemies.zh: 主要对手为东方六国，尤以魏、韩、赵、楚最为关键；这些国家虽彼此竞争，但常因恐惧秦国西强而结成对秦联盟。
enemies.en: Qin's principal rivals were the eastern states, especially Wei, Han, Zhao, and Chu. Though they competed with one another, they often formed anti-Qin coalitions out of fear of Qin's western power.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策以富国强兵和东进扩张为核心，善用连横、威慑、间使、赂遗与军事压力相结合；其终极目标并非维持均势，而是削弱诸侯并逐步取得华夏世界主导权。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on enriching the state, strengthening the military, and expanding eastward. Qin skillfully combined alliance politics, intimidation, espionage, gifts, and military pressure. Its ultimate objective was not balance but the weakening of rival states and the gradual acquisition of dominance over the Huaxia world.

era.zh: 战国晚期，属广义希腊化时代的同时期东亚阶段
era.en: Late Warring States period, the East Asian contemporary phase of the broader Hellenistic age

keyInnovations.zh: 商鞅改革后的制度技术、郡县治理、户籍统计、标准化文书、铁农具推广、强弩制造、道路网络建设、水利开发与大规模军需组织，是秦国最关键的技术—制度创新。
keyInnovations.en: Qin's most important techno-institutional innovations included the post-Shang Yang reform order, commandery-county administration, household registration and statistics, increasingly standardized documentation, expanded use of iron agricultural tools, powerful crossbow manufacture, road networks, hydraulic development, and large-scale military supply organization.

strengths.zh: 秦国的最大优势在于中央集权、法令贯彻、农业基础雄厚、财政动员力强、军功体系有效、将吏执行力高，并拥有关中这一易守难攻且资源集中的核心腹地。
strengths.en: Qin's greatest strengths lay in centralized rule, effective enforcement of law, a strong agrarian base, powerful fiscal mobilization, an effective military merit system, highly disciplined officials and generals, and the secure, resource-rich heartland of Guanzhong.

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点在于政治高压、刑法严酷、社会负担沉重，对持续战争与行政控制高度依赖；若出现继承危机、重大战败或内部反弹，体制压力可能迅速积累。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses were political harshness, severe penal law, heavy social burdens, and deep dependence on continual warfare and administrative control. If succession crises, major defeats, or internal backlash emerged, systemic pressures could accumulate quickly.

outlook.zh: 在323年前后，秦国前景极为强劲。只要继续维持改革成果、压制贵族离心、稳固关中与边地并逐步瓦解东方联盟，秦最有可能在数十年内完成对华夏诸国的统一。
outlook.en: Around 323 BCE, Qin's outlook was exceptionally strong. If it maintained the achievements of reform, restrained aristocratic centrifugal forces, secured Guanzhong and the frontiers, and gradually dismantled eastern alliances, Qin was the state most likely to unify the Huaxia realms within a few decades.

description.zh: 战国西方强国，经商鞅变法后军政强盛，正迈向统一战争。
description.en: The major western Warring State, militarily and administratively strengthened after Shang Yang's reforms and advancing toward unification.

governmentForm.zh: 战国时期世袭君主制国家，王权居于核心，辅以贵族、封君、郡县与县级官僚共同治理。
governmentForm.en: A hereditary monarchy of the Warring States period, centered on royal authority and supported by nobles, enfeoffed lords, commandery-level jurisdictions, and county officials.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王室、贵族与宗族精英、军功官僚、士人、农民、工匠、商人以及边地部族群体构成，兼具中原官僚秩序与南方地方传统。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of the royal house, aristocrats and lineage elites, merit-based military officials, shi retainers, peasants, artisans, merchants, and frontier tribal populations, blending Central Plains bureaucracy with southern regional traditions.

rulingClass.zh: 芈姓王族、屈景昭等大族、受封君长及受王任命的高级文武官员。
rulingClass.en: The Mi royal clan, major aristocratic lineages such as Qu, Jing, and Zhao, enfeoffed regional lords, and high civil and military officials appointed by the king.

succession.zh: 通常由王室嫡系世袭继承，但在实际政治中常受王族内部博弈、贵族支持与宫廷力量平衡影响。
succession.en: Succession normally followed hereditary transfer within the royal line, though in practice it was shaped by intradynastic struggles, aristocratic backing, and court power balances.

structure.zh: 楚国中央政务围绕楚王与宫廷展开，以令尹、司马、司徒、司败等高官分掌政务、军事、赋役与司法；地方则由封君辖地与县制并存，重要区域逐渐加强王室直接控制。
structure.en: Chu’s central administration revolved around the king and court, with chief ministers such as the Lingyin, Sima, Situ, and Sibai managing state affairs, military matters, labor and taxation, and justice; locally, enfeoffed territories coexisted with counties, while major regions increasingly came under direct royal control.

function.zh: 统筹中央政务、王令执行、官员协调与战略决策，是最重要的行政中枢。
function.en: Coordinated central government affairs, implementation of royal orders, official oversight, and strategic decision-making; it was the principal administrative center.

function.zh: 掌军政、征兵、训练、军粮转运与边防部署。
function.en: Managed military administration, conscription, training, military grain transport, and frontier deployment.

function.zh: 负责土地、户籍、徭役征发、农业组织与部分地方行政监督。
function.en: Handled land, household registration, corvée labor, agricultural organization, and parts of local administrative supervision.

function.zh: 掌司法审判、刑罚执行、治安与重大案件审理。
