succession.en: The throne broadly followed hereditary succession, usually through male members of the royal line, but required recognition by major nobles. If royal authority weakened, local lords could shape succession or provoke fragmentation.

structure.zh: 王室控制主要河谷、贡赋与对外贸易，地方贵族在各自领地拥有较大自治权。沿海城镇和内陆领地并存，形成带有分层统合特征的王国体制。
structure.en: The crown controlled key river valleys, tribute, and foreign trade, while local nobles retained significant autonomy within their own territories. Coastal towns and inland lordships coexisted, producing a layered and integrated kingdom structure.

function.zh: 征收贡赋、管理金银与储藏物资，并监督对外贸易收入。
function.en: Collected tribute, managed precious metals and stored goods, and supervised income from external trade.

function.zh: 组织王家卫队、召集地方贵族兵役并协调边防。
function.en: Organized the royal guard, summoned noble levies, and coordinated frontier defense.

function.zh: 管理沿海港口、市税、黑海贸易和与希腊商人的往来。
function.en: Managed coastal ports, market dues, Black Sea trade, and relations with Greek merchants.

name.zh: 王室使节与神庙事务署
name.en: Royal Envoys and Temple Affairs Office

function.zh: 处理外交、盟约、祭祀秩序与神庙地产事务。
function.en: Handled diplomacy, alliances, ritual order, and temple landed affairs.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政依托贵族领地、河谷聚落和港口城市展开。国王通过封臣、税吏和港务官维持控制，但山地地区的实际统治较间接。
localAdmin.en: Local administration functioned through noble estates, river settlements, and port towns. The king maintained control through vassals, tax collectors, and port officials, though rule in upland regions was more indirect.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系以王室裁断、贵族习惯法和宗教誓约并行。商业纠纷在沿海城镇可能部分采用希腊商法习惯，而乡村事务多依本地传统解决。
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined royal judgment, noble customary law, and religious oaths. Commercial disputes in coastal towns may partly have followed Greek mercantile practice, while rural affairs were mostly settled by local tradition.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政收入来自土地贡赋、港口关税、市场税、矿产或金砂收益，以及附属部族和领主的朝贡。税收既有实物也有贵金属折算。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue came from land tribute, port customs, market taxes, mineral or alluvial gold income, and tribute from dependent tribes and lords. Taxation was collected in both kind and precious-metal equivalents.

religion.zh: 高加索多神教
religion.en: Caucasian polytheism

culturalAchievements.zh: 科尔基斯以金属工艺、木作造船、黑海贸易网络和多元文化交汇著称。其沿海地区吸收希腊风格陶器、建筑和宗教图像，同时保留本地贵族与祭祀传统。
culturalAchievements.en: Colchis was notable for metalworking, timber and shipbuilding, Black Sea trade networks, and a multicultural frontier environment. Its coastal districts absorbed Greek-style pottery, architecture, and religious imagery while preserving native aristocratic and ritual traditions.

languageFamily.zh: 卡特维尔语系（本地语言）并伴随沿海希腊语使用
languageFamily.en: Kartvelian languages locally, alongside Greek in coastal settings

philosophy.zh: 以高加索本地多神信仰、祖先崇拜与王权神圣性观念为主，同时受到希腊宗教与黑海贸易文化影响。
philosophy.en: It was rooted in native Caucasian polytheism, ancestor reverence, and ideas of sacral kingship, while also being influenced by Greek religion and Black Sea trading culture.

writingSystem.zh: 本地行政多依口传与记忆传统，沿海贸易和对外交流中可能使用希腊文书写与希腊字母。
writingSystem.en: Local administration relied largely on oral and mnemonic practice, while Greek writing and the Greek alphabet were likely used in coastal trade and foreign dealings.

gdpDescription.zh: 科尔基斯经济兼具农业、林产、矿产和黑海贸易收益，在高加索边缘地区属中等偏上的区域经济体。沿海港口和河谷平原是产出与交换核心，内地则提供木材、金砂、奴隶和山地物产。
gdpDescription.en: The Colchian economy combined agriculture, forest products, mineral wealth, and Black Sea trade income, making it a moderately prosperous regional economy by Caucasian standards. Coastal ports and river plains were the main centers of output and exchange, while the interior supplied timber, alluvial gold, slaves, and upland products.

mainIndustries.zh: 农业、葡萄栽培、畜牧、木材采伐、金属加工、采金、港口贸易、造船
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture, viticulture, pastoralism, timber extraction, metalworking, gold washing, port trade, and shipbuilding

tradeGoods.zh: 木材、金砂、铁器、蜂蜜、蜡、葡萄酒、兽皮、奴隶、亚麻、盐鱼
tradeGoods.en: Timber, alluvial gold, iron goods, honey, wax, wine, hides, slaves, linen, and salted fish

name.en: Greek silver coinage alongside payments in kind

householdWealth.zh: 沿海商人和贵族家庭拥有较多银器、进口陶器、武器、牲畜和土地；乡村家庭财富则主要体现为土地使用权、谷物、葡萄园和家畜。
householdWealth.en: Coastal merchant and noble households possessed more silverware, imported pottery, weapons, livestock, and land, while rural households held wealth mainly in land-use rights, grain, vineyards, and animals.

economicSystem.zh: 王室贡赋经济与地方领地经济并存，沿海港口则嵌入希腊化黑海商业网络。
economicSystem.en: A royal-tributary economy coexisted with local estate production, while coastal ports were integrated into the Hellenistic Black Sea commercial network.

source.en: Land tribute and taxes in kind
Tribute from subordinate lords and tribes

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库存于王室府库和沿海贸易仓储中，形式包括银币、金砂、谷物、蜂蜜、蜡和武器储备。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was held in royal storehouses and coastal trade depots, in the form of silver coin, alluvial gold, grain, honey, wax, and weapon reserves.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策重在维持王权、笼络地方贵族并利用黑海贸易补充实物税不足。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy aimed to uphold royal authority, secure local noble loyalty, and use Black Sea trade to supplement the limits of in-kind taxation.

description.zh: 由王室卫队、城镇兵与部分职业战士组成，装备矛、盾、短剑和少量希腊式甲具。
description.en: Composed of the royal guard, town troops, and some professional warriors, equipped with spears, shields, short swords, and limited Greek-style armor.

description.zh: 由地方领主率领，适合山地与河谷作战，战斗力依领主资源而异。
description.en: Led by local lords, these forces were suited to mountain and river-valley fighting, with quality varying by noble resources.

description.zh: 数量不大，主要用于侦察、追击和贵族展示，战车已属边缘兵种。
description.en: Not numerous, used mainly for reconnaissance, pursuit, and aristocratic display; chariots had become a marginal arm.

commanderInChief.zh: 科尔基斯王
commanderInChief.en: King of Colchis

technology.zh: 军事技术处于地区性铁器时代水平，使用铁矛、剑、木盾、皮甲和少量金属甲，沿海地区受希腊武备影响较深。
technology.en: Military technology stood at a regional Iron Age level, using iron spears, swords, wooden shields, leather armor, and limited metal armor, with stronger Greek influence in the coastal areas.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自高加索山地部族、黑海沿岸势力竞争，以及亚历山大死后周边继业者体系可能扩张带来的外交压力。
threats.en: Major threats came from Caucasian upland tribes, rival powers along the Black Sea coast, and diplomatic pressure from the expanding successor-state systems after Alexander's death.

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中于里奥尼河流域、沿海港口和肥沃低地，山地居民则相对分散。沿海地区因贸易联系而更加多族群化，可能包括本地人、希腊商民与混合社群。
populationDescription.en: Population was concentrated chiefly in the Rioni valley, coastal ports, and fertile lowlands, while mountain inhabitants were more dispersed. Coastal districts were more ethnically mixed due to trade, likely including natives, Greek merchants, and blended communities.

socialClasses.zh: 王室与贵族、神庙人员、城镇商人与工匠、自由农民、依附农户、奴隶与战俘构成主要阶层。
socialClasses.en: The main social classes were the royal house and nobility, temple personnel, urban merchants and artisans, free peasants, dependent cultivators, and slaves or war captives.

allies.zh: 与部分黑海贸易城镇、邻近低地领主和可交换利益的地方部族保持合作关系。
allies.en: Maintained cooperative ties with certain Black Sea trading towns, neighboring lowland lords, and local tribes where mutual advantage existed.

enemies.zh: 与高加索内陆部族、竞争性沿海势力及潜在外来扩张者存在长期戒备关系。
enemies.en: Held enduring suspicions toward inland Caucasian tribes, rival coastal powers, and potential outside expansionists.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策强调控制黑海出口、平衡沿海希腊势力与内陆贵族，并避免卷入过强继业者国家的直接统治之下。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy emphasized control of Black Sea outlets, balancing Greek coastal interests with inland nobles, and avoiding subordination to overly powerful successor states.

era.zh: 高加索地区铁器时代晚期至早期希腊化阶段
era.en: Late Iron Age to early Hellenistic stage in the Caucasian region

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术包括铁器锻造、淘金与贵金属加工、木构船只制造、葡萄酒生产和港口贸易管理。
keyInnovations.en: Key innovations included iron forging, gold washing and precious-metal work, timber ship construction, wine production, and port-trade management.

strengths.zh: 地理位置优越，兼有河谷农业、山地资源和黑海贸易通道；王国能在本地传统与希腊化商业之间取得平衡。
strengths.en: It possessed a favorable geographic position, combining riverine agriculture, upland resources, and Black Sea trade routes; the kingdom could balance local tradition with Hellenistic commerce.

weaknesses.zh: 地方贵族割据倾向较强，山地控制有限，行政整合度不足，面对更大希腊化王国时难以长期抗衡。
weaknesses.en: Local nobles had strong centrifugal tendencies, upland control was limited, administrative integration was incomplete, and the kingdom would struggle to resist larger Hellenistic monarchies for long.

outlook.zh: 短期内科尔基斯仍可依赖贸易和地理屏障保持稳定，但随着黑海与高加索政治竞争加剧，其自主性将持续受压。
outlook.en: In the short term Colchis could remain stable through trade and geographic barriers, but intensifying competition around the Black Sea and Caucasus would place growing pressure on its autonomy.

description.zh: 科尔基斯是一个位于黑海东岸、融合本地高加索王权与希腊化贸易文化的区域性王国。
description.en: Colchis was a regional kingdom on the eastern Black Sea that blended native Caucasian kingship with Hellenistic trade culture.

governmentForm.zh: 由马其顿征服后保留地方贵族基础、以总督转化而成的世袭君主制王国。
governmentForm.en: A hereditary monarchy that emerged from a former satrapy, preserving local noble structures after the Macedonian conquest.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以伊朗语贵族、祭司阶层、自由农牧民与依附民为主。山区与盆地聚落并存，地方氏族首领在乡村社会中仍有很强影响力。宫廷、军队与祆教祭司共同构成上层秩序。
socialStructure.en: Society was centered on Iranian-speaking nobles, priestly elites, free farmers and pastoralists, and dependent rural populations. Mountain and basin settlements coexisted, and local clan chiefs retained strong influence in the countryside. The court, the army, and Zoroastrian priests together formed the upper social order.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层主要由阿特罗帕特斯家族、波斯化的地方贵族与高级祭司组成。他们控制土地、军队征发权与地方司法。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted primarily of the house of Atropates, Persianized local nobles, and senior priests. They controlled land, military levies, and local justice.

succession.zh: 王位通常由家族内部世袭继承，多以长子优先，但须获得核心贵族承认。若继承有争议，地方豪族与军队支持往往决定结果。
succession.en: The throne was normally hereditary within the ruling house, usually favoring the eldest son, but it required recognition by key nobles. In disputed successions, support from major aristocrats and the army often proved decisive.

structure.zh: 阿特罗帕特尼保留了阿契美尼德时期行省治理传统，由国王统领中央宫廷与军事。地方则依靠贵族家族、城邑长官和宗教权威共同维持统治。由于地处山地，中央权力对边缘地区多采取间接控制。
structure.en: Atropatene preserved administrative traditions from the Achaemenid satrapal system, with the king directing the central court and military. Local governance relied on noble houses, town officials, and religious authorities. Because of its mountainous terrain, the center often ruled peripheral areas indirectly.

function.zh: 管理王室地产、贡赋征收、金银储备与对外馈赠。
function.en: Managed royal estates, tribute collection, silver reserves, and diplomatic gifts.

function.zh: 负责边防、征召骑兵与步兵、军粮运输及要塞守备。
function.en: Oversaw frontier defense, levy of cavalry and infantry, military supply, and garrisoning of forts.

function.zh: 联络地方贵族与城镇，统计人口、土地和贡纳义务。
function.en: Liaised with local nobles and towns, and recorded population, land, and tribute obligations.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以贵族封地、山地部族首领和城镇长官为主。国王通过任命亲信与索取人质、贡赋来确保忠诚。
localAdmin.en: Local administration rested on noble estates, mountain tribal leaders, and town officials. The king secured loyalty through trusted appointments, tribute obligations, and hostages.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以伊朗习惯法、王室敕令与宗教规范共同构成。土地、婚姻与血仇纠纷常由地方贵族和祭司裁断。
legalSystem.en: Law combined Iranian customary practice, royal decrees, and religious norms. Disputes over land, marriage, and blood-feud matters were often judged by local nobles and priests.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收主要来自土地税、畜群税、贸易关卡费与附庸贡赋。山区地区更多以实物和牲畜缴纳，城镇则以银计征。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation came mainly from land tax, herd tax, trade tolls, and subordinate tribute. Mountain districts paid more often in kind and livestock, while towns more commonly paid in silver.

religion.zh: 琐罗亚斯德教
religion.en: Zoroastrianism

culturalAchievements.zh: 该地区延续了伊朗高地的祭祀传统、马政文化与山地要塞建筑。其后世被视为阿塞拜疆地区伊朗文化连续性的早期核心之一。
culturalAchievements.en: The region sustained the Iranian highland traditions of ritual practice, horse culture, and mountain fortification. It later came to be seen as one of the early cores of Iranian cultural continuity in Azerbaijan.

languageFamily.zh: 印欧语系－伊朗语族
languageFamily.en: Indo-European - Iranian branch

philosophy.zh: 以祆教宇宙观、王权正统观念与伊朗贵族伦理为核心。
philosophy.en: Centered on Zoroastrian cosmology, royal legitimacy, and Iranian aristocratic ethics.

writingSystem.zh: 主要使用阿拉米文行政书写，口语为西北伊朗语方言；上层亦受古波斯与宫廷传统影响。
writingSystem.en: Administrative writing was mainly in Aramaic, while spoken language consisted of Northwestern Iranian dialects; elites were also shaped by Old Persian and courtly traditions.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以山间农业、畜牧业和区域转运贸易为基础。由于人口较少而地势险固，人均资源占有相对不低，但整体市场规模有限。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on mountain agriculture, pastoralism, and regional transit trade. Because the population was modest and the terrain defensible, per-capita resources were not especially low, though the overall market remained limited.

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物种植、牧马与畜牧、葡萄与园艺、金属手工业、山区商道运输
mainIndustries.en: Grain cultivation, horse-breeding and pastoralism, viticulture and horticulture, metal crafts, and mountain caravan transport

tradeGoods.zh: 马匹、牲畜、皮革、葡萄酒、谷物、金属器、木材、山地产物
tradeGoods.en: Horses, livestock, leather, wine, grain, metal goods, timber, and mountain products

name.zh: 银锭与外来希腊化银币
name.en: Silver bullion and imported Hellenistic coinage

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富多以土地使用权、羊群牛群、马匹和储粮体现，贵族则以庄园、附庸与金银器为主。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth was measured mainly in land use rights, flocks, horses, and stored grain, while noble wealth rested on estates, dependents, and precious metal goods.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以王室地产、贵族庄园和村社生产并存的混合型前现代经济。
economicSystem.en: A mixed premodern economy combining royal estates, aristocratic domains, and village production.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

source.zh: 附庸贡赋与王室地产收入
source.en: Subordinate tribute and royal estate income

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库储备主要来自历年盈余、王室地产收入和紧急征敛，足以支撑短期动员与外交赠礼。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury drew mainly from accumulated surpluses, royal estate income, and emergency levies, and was sufficient for short-term mobilization and diplomatic gifts.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏保守，重视储备、军费优先与通过地方贵族分担治理成本。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was conservative, prioritizing reserves, military spending, and sharing administrative costs through local nobles.

description.zh: 以贵族骑士和山地骑兵为骨干，适合机动袭扰与边境巡防。
description.en: Formed around noble horsemen and mountain cavalry, suited to mobile raiding and frontier patrol.

description.zh: 由征召农民、山地战士与城镇守军组成，负责主力防御与攻守要地。
description.en: Composed of levied farmers, mountain fighters, and town garrisons, serving as the main defensive force.

description.zh: 驻守山口、城塞与仓储据点，维持交通线安全。
description.en: Guarded passes, fortresses, and storage points, maintaining the security of transport routes.

commanderInChief.zh: 阿特罗帕特斯国王
commanderInChief.en: King Atropates

technology.zh: 军备以铁制长矛、短剑、复合弓、鳞甲和山地要塞工事为主，兼具波斯与早期希腊化影响。
technology.en: Military technology centered on iron spears, short swords, composite bows, scale armor, and mountain fortifications, blending Persian and early Hellenistic influences.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自亚历山大死后继业者势力重组、邻近米底与亚美尼亚高地势力变化，以及地方贵族潜在离心。
threats.en: Main threats came from the post-Alexander realignment of the Successor powers, shifting forces in Media and the Armenian highlands, and the potential centrifugal tendencies of local nobles.

populationDescription.zh: 人口约二十万，分散于山间谷地、牧场和若干城镇据点。族群上以伊朗语居民为主，兼有少量阿拉米语书吏与外来商人。
populationDescription.en: The population was about 200,000, dispersed across valleys, pastures, and a few urban centers. Ethnically it was predominantly Iranian-speaking, with smaller numbers of Aramaic scribes and foreign merchants.

socialClasses.zh: 王族与大贵族、祭司、武士与小贵族、自由农牧民、工匠商人、依附民与奴婢。
socialClasses.en: Royal family and great nobles, priests, warriors and lesser nobles, free farmers and pastoralists, artisans and merchants, dependents, and slaves.

allies.zh: 以地区性结盟和婚姻外交为主，可能与部分米底及高地贵族保持合作关系。
allies.en: Its alliances were mainly regional and dynastic, likely including cooperative ties with certain Median and highland nobles.

enemies.zh: 无固定死敌，但对继业者诸将、可能重建控制的马其顿政权及周边竞争贵族保持警惕。
enemies.en: It had no permanent mortal enemy, but remained wary of the Successor commanders, any restored Macedonian authority, and competing neighboring nobles.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交上重在保持自治，通过地形优势、忠诚姿态与有限贡纳在大国之间求存。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy aimed at preserving autonomy by leveraging difficult terrain, displays of loyalty, and limited tribute between larger powers.

era.zh: 早期希腊化时代的伊朗高地地区国家
era.en: An Iranian highland state of the early Hellenistic age

keyInnovations.zh: 山地要塞体系、骑兵组织、灌溉农业与沿袭阿契美尼德的行政记簿传统。
keyInnovations.en: Mountain fortress networks, cavalry organization, irrigated agriculture, and record-keeping traditions inherited from the Achaemenids.

strengths.zh: 地势险要、贵族骑兵传统强、地方认同稳固，且能继承波斯旧行政经验。
strengths.en: It benefited from defensible terrain, strong noble cavalry traditions, stable local identity, and inherited Persian administrative experience.

weaknesses.zh: 人口与财政规模有限，中央集权不深，容易受继业者大战波及。
weaknesses.en: Its population and fiscal base were limited, centralization was shallow, and it was vulnerable to the wars of the Successors.

outlook.zh: 短期内有望维持地方独立与稳定，但长期需在更强大的希腊化王朝之间谨慎周旋。
outlook.en: In the short term it could likely preserve local independence and stability, but over the long term it would need to navigate carefully among stronger Hellenistic kingdoms.

description.zh: 阿特罗帕特尼是亚历山大死后由阿特罗帕特斯控制的伊朗化山地王国，凭借地形与旧波斯行政传统维持自治。
description.en: Atropatene was an Iranianized mountain kingdom under Atropates after Alexander's death, sustaining autonomy through rugged terrain and inherited Persian administrative traditions.

governmentForm.zh: 由多个村落首领和氏族长老共同维系的松散部落联合体。
governmentForm.en: A loose tribal society maintained by multiple village headmen and clan elders rather than a centralized state.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以高原村落、家族群体和牧作结合的生产单位为基础。长老、祭仪主持者和拥有较多牲畜的家户在社区中更有威望。普通成员多从事种植、牧养与陶器制作。
socialStructure.en: Society was based on highland villages, kin groups, and mixed farming-pastoral production units. Elders, ritual specialists, and households owning more herds enjoyed higher prestige. Most people engaged in cultivation, herding, and pottery production.

rulingClass.zh: 并无固定王族，权威主要掌握在村落首领、氏族长老和宗教仪式主持者手中。地位通常建立在血缘、资源分配能力与祭祀声望之上。
rulingClass.en: There was no fixed royal house; authority lay mainly with village headmen, clan elders, and ritual leaders. Status rested on kinship, control over resources, and ritual prestige.

succession.zh: 首领职位多在家族内部推举或协商继承，并不完全固定。若出现争议，长老会议和村落共识往往比单一血统更重要。
succession.en: Leadership usually passed through family-based selection or community agreement rather than rigid inheritance. In disputes, councils of elders and village consensus often mattered more than strict bloodline.

structure.zh: 万卡拉尼文化并非集中式国家，而是由若干高原聚落构成。公共事务主要通过长老会议、村社协商和祭祀活动来组织。跨聚落协调通常只在交换、婚姻或防御需要时出现。
structure.en: The Wankarani culture was not a centralized state but a collection of highland communities. Public affairs were organized chiefly through councils of elders, village consultation, and ritual gatherings. Coordination across settlements usually appeared only for exchange, marriage ties, or defense.

function.zh: 处理土地分配、婚姻调解、村际关系与季节性劳作安排。
function.en: Handled land allocation, marriage mediation, inter-village relations, and seasonal labor organization.

function.zh: 主持祭祀、保管公共储粮和协调灾荒时期再分配。
function.en: Conducted rituals, guarded communal grain stores, and coordinated redistribution during shortages.

localAdmin.zh: 行政即村社自治，各聚落由首领和长老主持日常事务。较大的聚落对周边小定居点具有一定号召力，但并无稳定官僚体系。
localAdmin.en: Administration amounted to village self-rule, with headmen and elders managing daily affairs. Larger settlements exercised some influence over nearby hamlets, but there was no stable bureaucracy.

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要是口述习惯法，以长老裁决、赔偿和祭仪修复关系为核心。盗窃、婚姻和牲畜争端通常通过协商解决。
legalSystem.en: Law was chiefly oral customary practice centered on elder judgment, compensation, and ritual reconciliation. Theft, marriage matters, and livestock disputes were usually settled through negotiation.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正规税制，公共资源主要来自劳役、共享储粮和按户贡献的农产或牲畜。战时或祭典时会临时加重集体供给。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal tax system; communal resources came mainly from labor obligations, shared grain stores, and household contributions in produce or animals. Wartime or major rituals could temporarily increase collective contributions.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 万卡拉尼以高原定居生活、早期畜牧农业结合和特色陶器传统著称。其聚落与墓葬形态反映出安第斯高地早期复杂社会发展的重要阶段。
culturalAchievements.en: The Wankarani are noted for highland sedentary life, an early mixed herding-farming economy, and distinctive ceramic traditions. Their settlement and burial patterns reflect an important stage in the rise of complex societies in the Andean highlands.

languageFamily.zh: 可能属安第斯高地前文字语言群，具体归属不明
languageFamily.en: Probably part of a pre-literate Andean highland language continuum; precise affiliation unknown

philosophy.zh: 以祖灵崇拜、自然灵信仰和维系群体互助的礼仪观念为核心。
philosophy.en: Centered on ancestor reverence, belief in nature spirits, and ritual ideas that reinforced communal reciprocity.

writingSystem.zh: 无已知文字系统，知识通过口传、仪式与工艺传统代际传承。
writingSystem.en: No known writing system; knowledge was transmitted orally through ritual and craft traditions across generations.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济规模较小，以自给性农业和畜牧业为主，市场交换有限。其价值更多体现在牲畜、粮食储备和手工制品，而非货币化交易。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was small, based mainly on subsistence farming and herding, with limited market exchange. Value was expressed more in animals, stored food, and craft goods than in monetized transactions.

mainIndustries.zh: 高原农业、骆马类牧养、陶器制作、石骨器加工、地方交换
mainIndustries.en: Highland agriculture, camelid herding, pottery production, stone and bone tool making, and local exchange

tradeGoods.zh: 陶器、羊驼毛与皮革、干粮、石器、盐、地方矿物颜料
tradeGoods.en: Pottery, camelid wool and hides, dried food, stone tools, salt, and local mineral pigments

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要表现为牲畜数量、储粮、居所质量和陶器储备。拥有更多牧群的家户在婚姻交换与公共事务中更具影响力。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was measured chiefly by herd size, food storage, dwelling quality, and pottery stocks. Families with larger herds held greater influence in marriage exchange and communal affairs.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以村社自给、劳役互助和少量跨聚落交换为特征的部落经济。
economicSystem.en: A tribal economy characterized by village self-sufficiency, reciprocal labor, and modest inter-settlement exchange.

grams of silver equivalent per year

treasuryDescription.zh: 所谓“公共储备”主要是粮仓、干肉、毛皮和少量可交换物资，而非金属货币国库。
treasuryDescription.en: Its 'treasury' was primarily communal stores of grain, dried meat, hides, and a small stock of exchangeable goods rather than a metallic cash reserve.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政方式重在维持基本储备与灾年互助，平时征集有限，避免压迫村社生产。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice focused on maintaining basic reserves and mutual aid in poor years, with light routine collection so as not to burden village production.

description.zh: 由成年男性临时组织，使用投石器、长矛和木盾，负责近距离防御。
description.en: Raised from adult males on a temporary basis, using slings, spears, and wooden shields for local defense.

description.zh: 负责高地通道警戒、牲畜保护和传递警报。
description.en: Responsible for guarding highland routes, protecting herds, and relaying alarms.

commanderInChief.zh: 各聚落首领共同统率
commanderInChief.en: Joint command of settlement headmen

technology.zh: 军事技术较为简朴，以石制或铜制工具、木矛、投石器和地形防御为主。
technology.en: Military technology was simple, relying on stone or copper tools, wooden spears, slings, and terrain-based defense.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自高原环境波动、邻近社群间的牲畜冲突和资源争夺，而非大规模国家战争。
threats.en: The main threats came from environmental instability on the plateau, livestock conflicts with neighboring groups, and competition over resources rather than large state warfare.

populationDescription.zh: 总人口约二万，多分布在玻利维亚高原的小型定居点与周边牧场。聚落规模不大，但彼此通过婚姻、交换和仪式维持联系。
populationDescription.en: The total population was about 20,000, spread across small settlements and surrounding pasture zones on the Bolivian high plateau. Settlements were modest in size but connected through marriage, exchange, and ritual.

socialClasses.zh: 首领与长老、祭仪主持者、普通农牧户、工匠、边缘依附者。
socialClasses.en: Headmen and elders, ritual specialists, ordinary farming-herding households, artisans, and marginal dependents.

allies.zh: 以邻近村社间互婚和互助为主，无固定成文盟约。
allies.en: Alliances were based mainly on intermarriage and mutual aid among nearby communities, without formal treaties.

enemies.zh: 无长期固定敌国，但与竞争水源、草场的邻近群体可能发生冲突。
enemies.en: There were no permanent enemy states, but clashes could arise with neighboring groups competing for water and pasture.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外关系以维持交换网络、避免大规模冲突和确保高原生计资源为核心。
foreignPolicy.en: External relations aimed chiefly to preserve exchange networks, avoid major conflict, and secure highland subsistence resources.

era.zh: 安第斯高地晚期前国家部落社会
era.en: A late pre-state tribal society of the Andean highlands

keyInnovations.zh: 高原定居适应、牧农结合、生计储存技术、陶器烧制与投石器运用。
keyInnovations.en: High-altitude sedentary adaptation, mixed herding-farming, subsistence storage techniques, ceramic firing, and skilled sling use.

strengths.zh: 适应高原生态、社区互助强、储粮与牧群体系具韧性。
strengths.en: Its strengths were strong adaptation to the highland environment, robust communal reciprocity, and resilient storage and herd systems.

weaknesses.zh: 缺乏集中权力、技术层次有限、抗长期气候冲击能力受限。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses included lack of centralized authority, limited technological complexity, and vulnerability to prolonged climatic stress.

outlook.zh: 短期内可维持稳定部落生活，但长期将面临环境变化与更复杂安第斯政治体系兴起的压力。
outlook.en: In the short term it could maintain stable tribal life, but over time it would face pressure from environmental change and the rise of more complex Andean political systems.

description.zh: 万卡拉尼文化是安第斯高原上以村社农业、牧养和陶器传统为基础的稳定部落社会。
description.en: The Wankarani culture was a stable highland tribal society based on village farming, herding, and ceramic traditions.

governmentForm.zh: 由区域性酋长主持、以聚落网络维系的分散部落酋邦形态。
governmentForm.en: A decentralized chiefdom-like tribal system led by regional chiefs and sustained through settlement networks.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由沿海与河谷聚落组成，酋长家族、祭仪人物、工匠和普通农渔民构成主要层次。不同聚落之间通过交换、婚姻和宗教仪式联系，但政治整合程度有限。到公元前323年前后，这一文化传统已进入衰退尾声并被后续区域文化取代。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of coastal and river-valley settlements, with chief families, ritual figures, artisans, and ordinary farmers-fishers forming the main layers. Settlements were connected by exchange, marriage, and ritual, but political integration remained limited. By 323 BCE, this cultural tradition was already in its terminal decline and was being replaced by successor regional cultures.

rulingClass.zh: 统治者主要是地方酋长及其亲族，他们通过控制祭祀、交换渠道和精致器物分配建立权威。部分工艺精英可能依附于酋长中心。
rulingClass.en: Rulers were mainly local chiefs and their kin, who built authority by controlling ritual, exchange channels, and the distribution of prestige goods. Some craft specialists were likely attached to chiefly centers.

succession.zh: 酋长职位多由亲族内部继承，但也受声望、婚姻联盟和社区支持影响。若中心衰弱，附属聚落可迅速脱离。
succession.en: Chiefly office generally passed within kin groups, though prestige, marriage alliances, and community backing also mattered. When a center weakened, dependent settlements could quickly break away.

structure.zh: 乔雷拉文化缺乏成熟国家机器，政治核心是酋长居住的礼仪性聚落。治理主要依靠亲族关系、再分配和宗教威望。因其已处于衰退期，跨区域协调能力明显减弱。
structure.en: The Chorrera culture lacked a mature state apparatus, and its political core was the ceremonial settlement where the chief resided. Governance depended mainly on kinship, redistribution, and religious prestige. Since it was already in decline, its ability to coordinate across regions had notably weakened.

function.zh: 处理聚落关系、组织劳作并分配食物与礼品。
function.en: Managed settlement relations, organized labor, and redistributed food and gifts.

function.zh: 主持宗教活动并监督精致陶器、乐器和礼器制作。
function.en: Oversaw religious ceremonies and supervised the production of fine ceramics, instruments, and ritual goods.

localAdmin.zh: 各聚落由本地头人管理，较大中心对周边村落有礼仪和交换上的影响。随着文化衰退，这种影响力趋于碎片化。
localAdmin.en: Each settlement was run by local headmen, while larger centers exercised ritual and exchange influence over nearby villages. As the culture declined, that influence became increasingly fragmented.

legalSystem.zh: 主要依赖习惯法、酋长裁决和仪式性调解。冲突多围绕土地、水路、婚姻和交换义务展开。
legalSystem.en: It relied mainly on customary law, chiefly judgment, and ritual mediation. Conflicts often centered on land, waterways, marriage, and exchange obligations.

taxationSystem.zh: 没有成文税法，酋长中心依靠贡礼、劳役和食物再分配维持。衰退时期贡纳能力与组织效率明显下降。
taxationSystem.en: There was no codified taxation; chiefly centers were supported by tribute gifts, labor service, and food redistribution. In the decline phase, both tribute capacity and organizational efficiency weakened.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 乔雷拉文化以精美抛光陶器、拟声哨形瓶和广泛的区域风格传播著称。其工艺和象征体系对厄瓜多尔后续沿海文化产生深远影响。
culturalAchievements.en: The Chorrera culture was renowned for finely polished ceramics, whistling vessels, and the broad spread of its regional style. Its crafts and symbolic system had a deep influence on later coastal cultures of Ecuador.

languageFamily.zh: 前文字安第斯西北部语言群，具体归属不明
languageFamily.en: A pre-literate northwestern Andean language grouping of uncertain affiliation

philosophy.zh: 以自然灵、祖先力量、祭仪表演和酋长神圣权威观念为核心。
philosophy.en: Centered on nature spirits, ancestral power, ritual performance, and the sacred authority of chiefs.

writingSystem.zh: 无文字系统，文化记忆通过口传、仪式、音乐与器物图像保存。
writingSystem.en: There was no writing system; cultural memory was preserved through oral tradition, ritual, music, and imagery on artifacts.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以玉米农业、渔业、河海运输和手工业为主，区域交换较活跃。然在衰退期，政治网络收缩使其再分配体系和高端工艺支持能力下降。
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on maize agriculture, fishing, riverine and coastal transport, and craft production, with fairly active regional exchange. Yet in its decline phase, shrinking political networks undermined redistribution and support for high-end craft specialization.

mainIndustries.zh: 玉米与园艺农业、渔业、陶器制作、贝壳与石器工艺、区域交换
mainIndustries.en: Maize and garden agriculture, fishing, pottery production, shell and stone craftwork, and regional exchange

tradeGoods.zh: 精致陶器、贝壳饰品、鱼干、玉米、棉织物、木器、颜料
tradeGoods.en: Fine ceramics, shell ornaments, dried fish, maize, cotton textiles, wooden goods, and pigments

name.zh: 无铸币，礼品与物物交换
name.en: No coinage, gift and barter exchange

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要体现在耕地、独木舟、渔具、储粮和高质量陶器。上层家庭则拥有更多礼器与交换网络资源。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was reflected mainly in cultivated plots, canoes, fishing gear, food stores, and quality ceramics. Elite households possessed more prestige goods and broader exchange connections.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以酋长再分配、家庭生产和沿海—内陆交换并存的部落酋邦经济。
economicSystem.en: A tribal-chiefdom economy combining chiefly redistribution, household production, and coastal-inland exchange.
