technology.en: Based on stone, wood, and bone weapons with bows; no metal smelting.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 严冬。
threats.en: Harsh winters.

populationDescription.zh: 巴塔哥尼亚草原。
populationDescription.en: Patagonian steppe.

socialClasses.zh: 首领家族、长老、自由成员、依附者。
socialClasses.en: Chiefly families, elders, free members, dependents.

allies.zh: 无固定盟国，依赖亲缘和交换伙伴形成临时联盟。
allies.en: No fixed allies; temporary alliances formed through kin ties and exchange partners.

enemies.zh: 无持久性敌国，但邻近群体间常有资源争夺。
enemies.en: No permanent enemies, though resource competition with neighboring groups was common.

foreignPolicy.zh: 以维护领地安全与交换关系为先。
foreignPolicy.en: Policy prioritized territorial security and exchange relationships.

era.zh: 新石器至早期部族阶段
era.en: Neolithic to early tribal stage

keyInnovations.zh: 流星锤。
keyInnovations.en: Boleadoras.

strengths.zh: 环境适应力。
strengths.en: Environmental adaptation.

weaknesses.zh: 人口极少。
weaknesses.en: Very small population.

outlook.zh: 将延续。
outlook.en: Would persist.

description.zh: 巴塔哥尼亚猎人以追猎原驼为生。
description.en: Patagonian Hunters lived by guanaco pursuit.

governmentForm.zh: 波斯裔世袭君主制，在希腊化影响下逐步引入希腊式行政机构，将伊朗王权传统与希腊城邦自治元素结合。
governmentForm.en: A Persian-descended hereditary monarchy gradually incorporating Hellenistic administrative institutions, blending Iranian royal traditions with elements of Greek polis autonomy.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由波斯裔王族和贵族、希腊城市公民、安纳托利亚本地农民、黑海沿岸商人和奴隶构成。伊朗-希腊双重文化精英共同构成统治阶层。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of the Persian-descended royal family and aristocracy, Greek urban citizens, Anatolian indigenous farmers, Black Sea coastal merchants, and slaves. An Irano-Greek dual-culture elite formed the ruling class.

rulingClass.zh: 密特拉达梯王族、波斯裔贵族和希腊化城市精英
rulingClass.en: The Mithridatic royal house, Persian-descended aristocrats, and Hellenized urban elites

succession.zh: 王位在密特拉达梯家族内部世袭传承，长子优先，但宫廷阴谋和贵族干预可能改变继承结果。
succession.en: The throne was inherited within the Mithridatic family with primogeniture preferred, though court intrigue and aristocratic intervention could alter succession outcomes.

structure.zh: 中央以国王为核心，设有波斯式宫廷机构和希腊式行政官员。黑海沿岸希腊城市享有一定内部自治，内陆领地由总督和贵族封臣治理。
structure.en: The center was built around the king, with both Persian-style court institutions and Greek-style administrative officials. Black Sea coastal Greek cities enjoyed a degree of internal autonomy, while interior territories were governed by satraps and noble vassals.

function.zh: 处理国政决策、外交往来和宫廷仪式。
function.en: Handled state decisions, diplomatic affairs, and court ceremonies.

function.zh: 管理王室军队、边防要塞和舰队。
function.en: Managed the royal army, frontier fortresses, and fleet.

function.zh: 征收税赋、管理王室地产收入和贸易关税。
function.en: Collected taxes, managed royal estate income, and trade customs.

function.zh: 管理内陆各省、维持治安和征兵。
function.en: Governed interior provinces, maintained order, and raised troops.

localAdmin.zh: 沿海希腊城市保留内部自治权但须缴纳贡赋和提供军事支持，内陆地区由波斯式总督和安纳托利亚地方贵族共同治理。
localAdmin.en: Coastal Greek cities retained internal autonomy but owed tribute and military support; interior regions were co-governed by Persian-style satraps and Anatolian local aristocrats.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系融合波斯王令、希腊城市法和安纳托利亚习惯法，不同社区适用不同法律传统。
legalSystem.en: The legal system blended Persian royal decrees, Greek civic law, and Anatolian customary law, with different legal traditions applied to different communities.

taxationSystem.zh: 收入来自土地税、贸易关税、矿产收益、城市贡赋和王室地产。税收体系继承阿契美尼德传统并加入希腊化元素。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue came from land taxes, trade customs, mining income, city tribute, and royal estates. The tax system inherited Achaemenid traditions with added Hellenistic elements.

religion.zh: 伊朗-希腊混合宗教，以密特拉/阿胡拉·马兹达崇拜与希腊宙斯/阿波罗崇拜并存，兼有安纳托利亚本地神灵祭祀
religion.en: Irano-Greek syncretic religion, with Mithra/Ahura Mazda worship coexisting with Greek Zeus/Apollo cult, alongside Anatolian indigenous deity worship

culturalAchievements.zh: 本都王国是伊朗与希腊文化融合的典范，在建筑、雕塑和宫廷仪式中兼采两种传统。黑海贸易网络促进了多元文化交流。
culturalAchievements.en: The Kingdom of Pontus exemplified the fusion of Iranian and Greek culture, drawing on both traditions in architecture, sculpture, and court ceremony. The Black Sea trade network promoted multicultural exchange.

languageFamily.zh: 印欧语系：官方使用希腊语，宫廷兼用古波斯语/中波斯语，民间通行安纳托利亚诸语言
languageFamily.en: Indo-European: Greek as official language, Old/Middle Persian also used at court, Anatolian languages spoken among the populace

philosophy.zh: 波斯二元论思想与希腊哲学传统并存，宫廷融合祆教宇宙观和希腊政治理论。
philosophy.en: Persian dualistic thought coexisted with Greek philosophical tradition; the court blended Zoroastrian cosmology with Greek political theory.

writingSystem.zh: 官方文书使用希腊字母和希腊语，宫廷文件可能兼用阿拉美文字。
writingSystem.en: Official documents used the Greek alphabet and language; court documents may also have employed Aramaic script.

gdpDescription.zh: 本都经济依托黑海沿岸贸易、内陆农业和矿产资源。作为黑海-地中海贸易的重要节点，港口城市繁荣兴旺。铁矿和其他金属矿产也是重要收入来源。
gdpDescription.en: The Pontic economy was supported by Black Sea coastal trade, interior agriculture, and mineral resources. As a key node in Black Sea-Mediterranean trade, port cities thrived. Iron and other metal mines were also significant income sources.

mainIndustries.zh: 黑海贸易、谷物种植、畜牧、铁矿与金属冶炼、造船、渔业、木材出口与橄榄油生产
mainIndustries.en: Black Sea trade, grain cultivation, animal husbandry, iron and metal smelting, shipbuilding, fishing, timber exports, and olive oil production

tradeGoods.zh: 谷物、铁器、木材、鱼干、橄榄油、葡萄酒、蜂蜜、蜡和奴隶
tradeGoods.en: Grain, ironware, timber, dried fish, olive oil, wine, honey, wax, and slaves

name.zh: 本都德拉克马与阿提卡标准银币
name.en: Pontic drachma and Attic-standard silver coinage

householdWealth.zh: 沿海希腊市民以贸易投资和城市房产为主要财富；内陆农民以土地和牲畜为主；王室贵族控制矿产和大地产。
householdWealth.en: Coastal Greek citizens held wealth mainly in trade investments and urban property; interior farmers in land and livestock; the royal aristocracy controlled mines and large estates.

economicSystem.zh: 以王室地产和贸易关税为核心的混合经济，兼有希腊城市商业自治和波斯式贡赋体系。
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy centered on royal estates and trade customs, combining Greek urban commercial autonomy with a Persian-style tribute system.

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库以阿马西亚王室金库中的金银储备为主，另有矿产储备和粮食仓储。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury consisted mainly of gold and silver reserves in the royal coffers at Amaseia, plus mineral stockpiles and grain stores.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政以扩军备战、巩固黑海贸易网络和建设希腊化城市为优先。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy prioritized military expansion, consolidation of the Black Sea trade network, and construction of Hellenistic cities.

name.zh: 波斯式骑兵与铁甲骑兵

description.zh: 继承阿契美尼德传统的重装和轻装骑兵，是本都军事力量的核心。
description.en: Heavy and light cavalry inheriting Achaemenid traditions, forming the core of Pontic military power.

description.zh: 按马其顿方阵编制的步兵，从希腊定居者和雇佣兵中招募。
description.en: Infantry organized in Macedonian phalanx formation, recruited from Greek settlers and mercenaries.

name.zh: 安纳托利亚辅助兵与黑海舰队
name.en: Anatolian Auxiliaries and Black Sea Fleet

description.zh: 安纳托利亚本地征召兵和黑海舰队水手，提供多样化军事支持。
description.en: Anatolian locally levied troops and Black Sea fleet sailors, providing diverse military support.

commanderInChief.zh: 国王本人或王室指定的大将军
commanderInChief.en: The king himself or a royally appointed supreme general

technology.zh: 融合波斯骑兵传统和希腊步兵战术，铁甲骑兵（cataphract）是其军事特色。黑海舰队配备希腊式战舰。
technology.en: Blended Persian cavalry traditions with Greek infantry tactics; the cataphract was its military hallmark. The Black Sea fleet was equipped with Greek-style warships.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自塞琉古帝国的领土要求、加拉太凯尔特人的侵扰和黑海北岸游牧民族的压力。
threats.en: Main threats came from Seleucid territorial claims, Galatian Celtic raids, and nomadic pressure from the northern Black Sea shore.

populationDescription.zh: 人口分布于黑海沿岸港口城市和内陆山地农业区，以安纳托利亚本地居民为主，沿岸有大量希腊定居者。
populationDescription.en: Population distributed across Black Sea coastal port cities and interior highland agricultural zones, predominantly Anatolian natives with substantial Greek settlers along the coast.

socialClasses.zh: 王族与波斯裔贵族、希腊城市公民、安纳托利亚自由农民、商人阶层、工匠与奴隶
socialClasses.en: Royal family and Persian-descended aristocrats, Greek urban citizens, Anatolian free farmers, merchant class, artisans, and slaves

allies.zh: 与黑海沿岸希腊城邦和部分安纳托利亚小王国保持友好关系。
allies.en: Maintained friendly relations with Black Sea coastal Greek poleis and some smaller Anatolian kingdoms.

enemies.zh: 塞琉古帝国是主要竞争对手，加拉太凯尔特人构成持续骚扰。
enemies.en: The Seleucid Empire was the main rival, with Galatian Celts constituting persistent harassment.

foreignPolicy.zh: 以在继业者战争的夹缝中扩大领土、巩固黑海霸权和维持独立为外交目标。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy aimed at expanding territory amid the Diadochi wars, consolidating Black Sea hegemony, and maintaining independence.

era.zh: 早期希腊化时期
era.en: Early Hellenistic period

keyInnovations.zh: 伊朗-希腊军事技术融合（铁甲骑兵）、黑海造船术、铁矿冶炼和希腊化城市建设。
keyInnovations.en: Irano-Greek military technology fusion (cataphract), Black Sea shipbuilding, iron ore smelting, and Hellenistic urban construction.

strengths.zh: 控制黑海南岸战略要地和丰富矿产，伊朗-希腊双重文化传统提供灵活的治理和军事能力，黑海贸易网络带来持续收入。
strengths.en: Control of strategic southern Black Sea coast and rich mineral resources, Irano-Greek dual cultural tradition providing flexible governance and military capability, and the Black Sea trade network bringing sustained income.

weaknesses.zh: 作为新建王国根基尚浅，人口和资源不及大型继业者国家，多民族构成可能产生内部张力。
weaknesses.en: As a newly established kingdom its foundations were still shallow, population and resources were inferior to larger successor states, and its multi-ethnic composition could generate internal tensions.

outlook.zh: 本都王国有潜力在继业者混战中持续壮大，尤其在控制黑海贸易和扩展安纳托利亚领地方面。若能避免与塞琉古正面对抗，前景看好。
outlook.en: The Kingdom of Pontus had potential to keep growing amid the Diadochi conflicts, especially in controlling Black Sea trade and expanding Anatolian territories. If it could avoid direct confrontation with the Seleucids, prospects were favorable.

description.zh: 本都王国是黑海南岸的伊朗裔希腊化王国，融合波斯与希腊传统，在继业者时代迅速崛起。
description.en: The Kingdom of Pontus was an Iranian-descended Hellenistic kingdom on the southern Black Sea coast, blending Persian and Greek traditions and rising rapidly in the Diadochi era.

capital.zh: 尼科美底亚（早期为齐普特亚）

governmentForm.zh: 色雷斯裔世袭君主制，正在向希腊化王国模式过渡，引入希腊式行政和城市建设。
governmentForm.en: A Thracian-descended hereditary monarchy in transition toward the Hellenistic kingdom model, introducing Greek-style administration and urban construction.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由色雷斯裔王族和武士贵族、希腊城市商人和定居者、比提尼亚本地农民和奴隶构成。希腊化转型正在改变传统等级。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of Thracian-descended royals and warrior aristocrats, Greek urban merchants and settlers, Bithynian native farmers, and slaves. Hellenistic transformation was reshaping traditional hierarchies.

rulingClass.zh: 齐普特斯王族及其色雷斯裔武士贵族与新兴的希腊化精英
rulingClass.en: The Zipoetid royal house, its Thracian-descended warrior aristocracy, and an emerging Hellenized elite

succession.zh: 王位在齐普特斯家族内部世袭，但继承争端和宫廷暗斗时有发生。
succession.en: The throne was inherited within the Zipoetid family, though succession disputes and court intrigues occurred periodically.

structure.zh: 以国王为核心的集权体制，正从色雷斯部族王权向希腊化官僚体系过渡。希腊城市享有有限自治，乡村地区由王室任命的地方官治理。
structure.en: A centralized system around the king, transitioning from Thracian tribal kingship toward a Hellenistic bureaucratic system. Greek cities enjoyed limited autonomy, while rural areas were governed by royally appointed local officials.

function.zh: 处理国务、外交和朝廷仪式。
function.en: Handled state affairs, diplomacy, and court ceremonies.

function.zh: 管理军队、要塞和边境安全。
function.en: Managed the army, fortresses, and border security.

function.zh: 征收税赋和管理王室地产收入。
function.en: Collected taxes and managed royal estate income.

localAdmin.zh: 希腊城市保有内部自治但须纳贡，乡村地区由王室任命官吏管理。
localAdmin.en: Greek cities retained internal autonomy but owed tribute; rural areas were administered by royally appointed officials.

legalSystem.zh: 融合色雷斯习惯法与希腊城市法，王室法令对全境有效。
legalSystem.en: A blend of Thracian customary law and Greek civic law, with royal decrees effective throughout the realm.

taxationSystem.zh: 收入来自土地税、贸易关税、城市贡赋和王室地产收益。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue came from land taxes, trade customs, city tribute, and royal estate income.

religion.zh: 色雷斯-希腊混合宗教，传统色雷斯骑士英雄崇拜与希腊奥林匹斯诸神崇拜并行
religion.en: Thraco-Greek syncretic religion, with traditional Thracian horseman hero cult alongside Greek Olympian worship

culturalAchievements.zh: 比提尼亚正从色雷斯传统向希腊化文明快速转型，城市建设和希腊式建筑正在兴起，将成为后世希腊化世界的重要文化中心。
culturalAchievements.en: Bithynia was rapidly transitioning from Thracian traditions to Hellenistic civilization, with urban construction and Greek-style architecture rising, eventually becoming an important cultural center of the Hellenistic world.

languageFamily.zh: 印欧语系：官方向希腊语过渡，民间仍有色雷斯语使用
languageFamily.en: Indo-European: transitioning to Greek as the official language, with Thracian still spoken among the populace

philosophy.zh: 色雷斯武士传统正与希腊哲学和政治思想融合，希腊化城市中逐步出现知识阶层。
philosophy.en: Thracian warrior traditions were merging with Greek philosophy and political thought, with an intellectual class gradually emerging in Hellenistic cities.

writingSystem.zh: 使用希腊字母和希腊语作为官方书写系统。
writingSystem.en: Used the Greek alphabet and language as the official writing system.

gdpDescription.zh: 比提尼亚经济受益于其位于博斯普鲁斯海峡附近的战略位置，控制着黑海-爱琴海贸易通道。农业富庶且造船业发达。
gdpDescription.en: Bithynia's economy benefited from its strategic location near the Bosphorus, controlling the Black Sea-Aegean trade passage. Agriculture was prosperous and shipbuilding well-developed.

mainIndustries.zh: 过境贸易、谷物种植、畜牧、造船、渔业、木材出口和橄榄油生产
mainIndustries.en: Transit trade, grain cultivation, animal husbandry, shipbuilding, fishing, timber exports, and olive oil production

tradeGoods.zh: 谷物、木材、船舶、橄榄油、葡萄酒、鱼干和手工品
tradeGoods.en: Grain, timber, ships, olive oil, wine, dried fish, and manufactures

name.zh: 比提尼亚银币与阿提卡标准
name.en: Bithynian silver coinage and Attic standard

householdWealth.zh: 城市商人以贸易和房产为主要财富，农民以土地和牲畜为主，贵族控制大地产和贸易特权。
householdWealth.en: Urban merchants held wealth mainly in trade and property, farmers in land and livestock, and aristocrats controlled large estates and trade privileges.

economicSystem.zh: 以王室控制和希腊化城市商业并存的过渡型经济，过境贸易比重高。
economicSystem.en: A transitional economy combining royal control with Hellenistic urban commerce, with a high proportion of transit trade.

category.zh: 城市建设与希腊化改造

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库以王室金库中的金银储备和粮食仓储为主。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury consisted mainly of gold and silver reserves in the royal coffers and grain stores.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政以巩固国防、推进希腊化城市建设和维持过境贸易收入为重心。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on strengthening defense, advancing Hellenistic urban construction, and maintaining transit trade income.

description.zh: 继承色雷斯武士传统的精锐骑兵，逐步采用希腊化装备。
description.en: Elite cavalry inheriting Thracian warrior traditions, gradually adopting Hellenistic equipment.

description.zh: 按马其顿方阵模式编制的步兵，从本地居民和希腊雇佣兵中招募。
description.en: Infantry organized in Macedonian phalanx style, recruited from local inhabitants and Greek mercenaries.

description.zh: 轻装步兵、弓手和小型沿海舰队。
description.en: Light infantry, archers, and a small coastal fleet.

commanderInChief.zh: 国王本人
commanderInChief.en: The king himself

technology.zh: 色雷斯骑兵传统与马其顿方阵步兵相结合，正在引入希腊化攻城技术。
technology.en: Combined Thracian cavalry tradition with Macedonian phalanx infantry, and was introducing Hellenistic siege technology.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自马其顿继业者（尤其是安提柯家族）的干涉、加拉太凯尔特人入侵和塞琉古势力的压力。
threats.en: Main threats came from Macedonian successor interference (especially the Antigonid house), Galatian Celtic invasions, and Seleucid pressure.

populationDescription.zh: 人口集中于马尔马拉海沿岸和比提尼亚内陆平原，以比提尼亚本地居民为主体，沿岸有希腊定居者。
populationDescription.en: Population concentrated along the Sea of Marmara coast and Bithynian interior plains, predominantly Bithynian natives with Greek settlers on the coast.

socialClasses.zh: 王族与色雷斯武士贵族、希腊城市公民与商人、本地自由农民、工匠与奴隶
socialClasses.en: Royal family and Thracian warrior aristocrats, Greek urban citizens and merchants, native free farmers, artisans, and slaves

allies.zh: 与赫拉克利亚·彭提卡等黑海城邦和部分安纳托利亚小王国保持友好。
allies.en: Maintained friendly relations with Heracleia Pontica and other Black Sea poleis, and some smaller Anatolian kingdoms.

enemies.zh: 与马其顿继业者（尤其安提柯一世）存在领土冲突，加拉太人构成持续威胁。
enemies.en: In territorial conflict with Macedonian successors (especially Antigonus I), with Galatians posing a persistent threat.

foreignPolicy.zh: 以维护独立、在继业者列强间寻求平衡和利用希腊化转型提升国际地位为外交目标。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy aimed at preserving independence, seeking balance among successor powers, and leveraging Hellenistic transformation to enhance international standing.

era.zh: 早期希腊化时期
era.en: Early Hellenistic period

keyInnovations.zh: 希腊化城市规划与建设、马其顿式军事组织引入、造船技术和农业灌溉改良。
keyInnovations.en: Hellenistic urban planning and construction, introduction of Macedonian-style military organization, shipbuilding technology, and agricultural irrigation improvements.

strengths.zh: 地处博斯普鲁斯战略要地，过境贸易收入可观，色雷斯骑兵传统提供军事优势，希腊化转型提升治理能力。
strengths.en: Strategically located near the Bosphorus, transit trade income substantial, Thracian cavalry tradition providing military advantage, and Hellenistic transformation enhancing governance.

weaknesses.zh: 国力有限，夹在多个强邻之间，希腊化转型尚未完成，文化认同处于过渡期。
weaknesses.en: Limited state power, squeezed between multiple powerful neighbors, Hellenistic transformation incomplete, and cultural identity in a transitional phase.

outlook.zh: 比提尼亚有望通过继续希腊化改革和利用战略位置在继业者世界中确立一席之地。下一代国王若能建设尼科美底亚为大城市，将大幅提升国力。
outlook.en: Bithynia could potentially establish its place in the successor world through continued Hellenistic reforms and leveraging its strategic position. If the next generation of kings could build Nicomedia into a great city, state power would be significantly enhanced.

description.zh: 比提尼亚是安纳托利亚西北部的色雷斯裔王国，正从部族王权向希腊化王国快速转型。
description.en: Bithynia was a Thracian-descended kingdom in northwestern Anatolia, rapidly transitioning from tribal kingship to a Hellenistic kingdom.

governmentForm.zh: 马其顿-希腊裔军事统治者继承埃及法老体制，将马其顿军事领导权、希腊城市行政和古埃及官僚传统三者融合为一。
governmentForm.en: A Macedonian-Greek military ruler inheriting the Egyptian pharaonic system, fusing Macedonian military leadership, Greek civic administration, and ancient Egyptian bureaucratic traditions into one.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由马其顿-希腊军事和行政精英、埃及本土神官和地主、自由农民、手工工匠、外邦商人和大量农业依附人口构成。种族-文化分层与法老时代的等级传统并存。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of Macedonian-Greek military and administrative elites, Egyptian native priests and landowners, free peasants, craft artisans, foreign merchants, and a large agricultural dependent population. Ethno-cultural stratification coexisted with pharaonic-era hierarchical traditions.

rulingClass.zh: 托勒密王族、马其顿将领集团、希腊官僚精英和与王权合作的埃及祭司阶层
rulingClass.en: The Ptolemaic royal house, the Macedonian officer corps, Greek bureaucratic elite, and Egyptian priestly class cooperating with royal power

succession.zh: 王位在托勒密家族内世袭，遵循马其顿传统但正融入埃及法老神圣王权观念。继承可能涉及宫廷内斗和军队支持。
succession.en: The throne was inherited within the Ptolemaic family following Macedonian tradition but incorporating Egyptian divine pharaonic concepts. Succession could involve court intrigue and army support.

structure.zh: 中央以国王为核心，设有希腊式宫廷和秘书机构，继承并扩展法老时代的诺姆行省体系。对埃及本土实施严密的行政和经济控制，希腊城市（如亚历山大里亚）享有特殊地位。
structure.en: The center was built around the king with a Greek-style court and secretariat, inheriting and expanding the pharaonic nome-province system. Tight administrative and economic control was exercised over native Egypt, while Greek cities (such as Alexandria) held special status.

function.zh: 处理最高国务决策、外交和王室事务。
function.en: Handled supreme state decisions, diplomacy, and royal affairs.

function.zh: 掌管全国税收、王室垄断收入、谷物储备和货币发行。
function.en: Oversaw national taxation, royal monopoly income, grain reserves, and currency issuance.

function.zh: 管理马其顿-希腊职业军队、要塞系统和海军舰队。
function.en: Managed the Macedonian-Greek professional army, fortress system, and naval fleet.

function.zh: 管理上下埃及各行省、税收征集和地方司法。
function.en: Governed the provinces of Upper and Lower Egypt, tax collection, and local justice.

name.zh: 亚历山大里亚城市管理

function.zh: 管理帝国首都的港口、市场、图书馆和公共设施。
function.en: Administered the imperial capital's harbor, markets, library, and public facilities.

function.zh: 管理与埃及神庙系统的关系、宗教赞助和法老仪式。
function.en: Managed relations with the Egyptian temple system, religious patronage, and pharaonic ritual.

localAdmin.zh: 地方以诺姆（行省）为单位，由希腊裔总督（strategos）和埃及本土地方官共同管理。税收和粮食征集体系极为精密，深入每个村庄。亚历山大里亚享有独立的城市治理体系。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was organized by nomes (provinces), co-managed by Greek-origin strategoi and Egyptian native local officials. The tax and grain collection system was extremely sophisticated, reaching every village. Alexandria enjoyed an independent municipal governance system.

legalSystem.zh: 双轨法律体系：希腊定居者适用希腊法，埃及本地人适用传统埃及法，王室法令对全境有效。亚历山大里亚有独立的司法系统。
legalSystem.en: A dual-track legal system: Greek settlers were subject to Greek law, Egyptian natives to traditional Egyptian law, and royal decrees were effective throughout the realm. Alexandria had its own judicial system.

taxationSystem.zh: 极为复杂和高效的税收体系，包括土地税、人头税、手工业税、贸易关税、王室垄断（油料、啤酒、纸莎草、矿产等）收入和神庙贡赋。税收以货币和实物并征。
taxationSystem.en: An extremely complex and efficient tax system including land taxes, poll taxes, craft taxes, trade customs, royal monopoly income (oils, beer, papyrus, minerals, etc.), and temple contributions. Taxes were collected in both currency and kind.

religion.zh: 希腊-埃及融合宗教，法老崇拜、埃及传统诸神（伊西斯、奥西里斯、荷鲁斯等）与希腊奥林匹斯诸神并存，塞拉皮斯新崇拜正在被塑造为跨文化纽带
religion.en: Greco-Egyptian syncretic religion, with pharaonic cult, traditional Egyptian deities (Isis, Osiris, Horus, etc.) coexisting with Greek Olympians, and the new Serapis cult being fashioned as a cross-cultural bond

culturalAchievements.zh: 托勒密一世正在将亚历山大里亚建设为世界文化首都：亚历山大图书馆和缪斯宫（博物馆）正在规划或初建中，法罗斯灯塔即将开工。希腊化科学、文学和哲学的中心正在形成。
culturalAchievements.en: Ptolemy I was building Alexandria as a world cultural capital: the Library of Alexandria and the Mouseion (Museum) were being planned or in early construction, and the Pharos Lighthouse was about to begin. A center of Hellenistic science, literature, and philosophy was taking shape.

languageFamily.zh: 印欧语系希腊语族（官方）与亚非语系埃及语族（民间），另有阿拉美语等商业语言
languageFamily.en: Indo-European Hellenic (official) and Afro-Asiatic Egyptian (popular), plus Aramaic and other commercial languages

philosophy.zh: 亚历山大里亚正在成为希腊化哲学和科学研究的新中心，亚里士多德传统、柏拉图学说和早期亚历山大学术风格正在融合。
philosophy.en: Alexandria was becoming a new center of Hellenistic philosophy and scientific research, with Aristotelian tradition, Platonic thought, and the early Alexandrian scholarly style converging.

writingSystem.zh: 官方使用希腊字母和希腊语，埃及行政和宗教使用世俗体（demotic）和圣书体（hieroglyphic），多语言环境。
writingSystem.en: Officially used the Greek alphabet and language; Egyptian administration and religion used Demotic and Hieroglyphic scripts, in a multilingual environment.

gdpDescription.zh: 托勒密埃及是希腊化世界最富裕的继业者国家，依托尼罗河谷极为丰产的农业、王室垄断体系、亚历山大里亚港的地中海-印度洋贸易枢纽地位和高效的税收体系。
gdpDescription.en: Ptolemaic Egypt was the wealthiest successor state in the Hellenistic world, built on the extremely productive Nile Valley agriculture, royal monopoly system, Alexandria harbor's position as a Mediterranean-Indian Ocean trade hub, and a highly efficient tax system.

mainIndustries.zh: 尼罗河谷农业（小麦、大麦、亚麻）、纸莎草制造、亚麻织物、玻璃器、香料中转贸易、造船、采矿、啤酒与油料生产
mainIndustries.en: Nile Valley agriculture (wheat, barley, flax), papyrus manufacturing, linen textiles, glassware, spice transit trade, shipbuilding, mining, and beer and oil production

tradeGoods.zh: 谷物、纸莎草、亚麻织物、玻璃器、香料（中转）、象牙、黄金、宝石和各类手工品
tradeGoods.en: Grain, papyrus, linen textiles, glassware, spices (transit), ivory, gold, gemstones, and various manufactures

householdWealth.zh: 希腊精英以地产、贸易投资和城市房产为主；埃及农民财富有限，受制于沉重的税赋和王室垄断；亚历山大里亚商人阶层积累可观商业资本。
householdWealth.en: Greek elites held wealth in estates, trade investments, and urban property; Egyptian peasant wealth was limited under heavy taxation and royal monopolies; Alexandria's merchant class accumulated considerable commercial capital.

economicSystem.zh: 高度国家化的计划-垄断经济与地中海自由贸易并存。王室通过垄断（油、纸莎草、啤酒、矿产等）、全面土地税和关税控制经济命脉，同时允许亚历山大里亚等城市维持商业活力。
economicSystem.en: A highly statized planned-monopoly economy coexisting with Mediterranean free trade. The crown controlled economic arteries through monopolies (oil, papyrus, beer, minerals, etc.), comprehensive land taxes, and customs, while allowing cities like Alexandria to maintain commercial vitality.

source.zh: 人头税、手工业税与其他
source.en: Poll tax, craft taxes, and miscellaneous

category.zh: 亚历山大里亚建设与文化机构

treasuryDescription.zh: 托勒密国库以亚历山大里亚王室金库为核心，储备巨量金银、谷物和各类战略物资，是希腊化世界最充裕的国家金库。
treasuryDescription.en: The Ptolemaic treasury was centered on the royal coffers in Alexandria, storing enormous quantities of gold, silver, grain, and strategic materials — the most abundant state treasury in the Hellenistic world.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政以最大化农业和垄断收入、建设亚历山大里亚世界级都城、维持强大海军和资助文化机构为目标。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy aimed at maximizing agricultural and monopoly income, building Alexandria as a world-class capital, maintaining a powerful navy, and funding cultural institutions.

description.zh: 以萨里沙长矛为主武器的马其顿式方阵步兵，从马其顿定居者和希腊雇佣兵中招募。
description.en: Macedonian-style phalanx infantry armed with sarissa pikes, recruited from Macedonian settlers and Greek mercenaries.

description.zh: 包括伙伴骑兵和轻装骑兵，核心由马其顿和色萨利精锐组成。
description.en: Including companion cavalry and light horse, with a core of Macedonian and Thessalian elites.

description.zh: 地中海最强舰队之一，配备大型多列桨战舰，控制东地中海海权。
description.en: One of the most powerful fleets in the Mediterranean, equipped with large polyremes, controlling eastern Mediterranean naval supremacy.

name.zh: 埃及本土辅助兵与雇佣兵

description.zh: 包括埃及本地征召兵、努比亚弓手、加拉太雇佣兵和各类辅助部队。
description.en: Including Egyptian native levies, Nubian archers, Galatian mercenaries, and various auxiliary units.

commanderInChief.zh: 国王/法老本人（托勒密一世·索忒尔，亚历山大大帝麾下宿将）
commanderInChief.en: The king/pharaoh himself (Ptolemy I Soter, a veteran general of Alexander the Great)

technology.zh: 继承亚历山大帝国最先进的军事技术，包括马其顿方阵、联合兵种战术、先进攻城技术和大型战舰建造。托勒密一世本人是杰出军事家。
technology.en: Inherited the most advanced military technology of Alexander's empire, including the Macedonian phalanx, combined-arms tactics, advanced siege techniques, and large warship construction. Ptolemy I himself was an outstanding military leader.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自其他继业者（尤其安提柯家族）争夺叙利亚和东地中海控制权、利比亚游牧部族的西部边境骚扰和努比亚方向的南部压力。
threats.en: Main threats came from rival successors (especially the Antigonids) contesting control of Syria and the eastern Mediterranean, Libyan nomadic tribal harassment on the western frontier, and Nubian pressure from the south.

populationDescription.zh: 人口高度集中于尼罗河谷和三角洲，亚历山大里亚正在快速膨胀为世界级大都市。埃及本地人口占绝大多数，马其顿-希腊定居者集中于亚历山大里亚、孟菲斯和法尤姆。
populationDescription.en: Population was highly concentrated in the Nile Valley and Delta, with Alexandria rapidly growing into a world-class metropolis. Native Egyptians formed the vast majority, while Macedonian-Greek settlers concentrated in Alexandria, Memphis, and the Fayyum.

socialClasses.zh: 托勒密王族、马其顿-希腊军事贵族、希腊官僚和商人、埃及祭司阶层、自由农民、工匠、外邦商人与奴隶
socialClasses.en: Ptolemaic royals, Macedonian-Greek military aristocracy, Greek bureaucrats and merchants, Egyptian priestly class, free peasants, artisans, foreign merchants, and slaves

allies.zh: 与罗得岛、部分爱琴海岛屿城邦和友好的继业者势力保持联盟。
allies.en: Maintained alliances with Rhodes, some Aegean island poleis, and friendly successor powers.

enemies.zh: 安提柯家族是争夺继承权的主要对手，塞琉古势力在叙利亚方向构成潜在冲突。
enemies.en: The Antigonid house was the main rival for succession, with Seleucid power in the Syrian direction posing potential conflict.

foreignPolicy.zh: 以确保埃及本土安全、控制东地中海海权、争夺科勒叙利亚和在继业者战争中扩大影响力为外交目标。亚历山大里亚的文化投资也是软实力工具。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy aimed at securing Egypt proper, controlling eastern Mediterranean naval supremacy, contesting Coele-Syria, and expanding influence in the Diadochi wars. Cultural investment in Alexandria also served as a soft-power tool.

era.zh: 早期希腊化时期
era.en: Early Hellenistic period

keyInnovations.zh: 亚历山大里亚正在成为世界科技中心：图书馆和缪斯宫汇聚各地学者，法罗斯灯塔设计中，尼罗河灌溉系统改良，大型战舰建造技术和托勒密铸币体系的建立。
keyInnovations.en: Alexandria was becoming a world scientific center: the Library and Mouseion gathering scholars from everywhere, the Pharos Lighthouse under design, Nile irrigation system improvements, large warship construction technology, and the establishment of the Ptolemaic coinage system.

strengths.zh: 坐拥尼罗河谷的巨大农业财富和人口基数，亚历山大里亚的战略港口位置无可匹敌，继承了亚历山大帝国最先进的军事和行政传统，托勒密一世本人是最精明的继业者之一。
strengths.en: Possessed the enormous agricultural wealth and population base of the Nile Valley, Alexandria's strategic harbor position was unmatched, inherited the most advanced military and administrative traditions of Alexander's empire, and Ptolemy I himself was among the shrewdest of the successors.

weaknesses.zh: 马其顿-希腊统治者与埃及本地人口之间的文化鸿沟是长期隐患，叙利亚方向的领土野心将导致与塞琉古长期对抗，对外国雇佣兵的依赖和王位继承制度的不确定性也是潜在弱点。
weaknesses.en: The cultural divide between Macedonian-Greek rulers and the native Egyptian population was a long-term vulnerability, territorial ambitions toward Syria would lead to prolonged conflict with the Seleucids, and dependence on foreign mercenaries and uncertainty in succession were also potential weaknesses.

outlook.zh: 托勒密埃及在短期内前景极为光明，有望成为希腊化世界最强大和最富裕的国家。亚历山大里亚将发展为无与伦比的文化和商业中心。长远看，保持军事优势和管理多民族帝国的挑战将决定其持久性。
outlook.en: Ptolemaic Egypt's short-term prospects were extremely bright, poised to become the most powerful and wealthiest state in the Hellenistic world. Alexandria would develop into an unparalleled cultural and commercial center. In the long run, maintaining military superiority and the challenge of managing a multi-ethnic empire would determine its durability.

description.zh: 托勒密一世正在将埃及打造为希腊化世界最富裕的继业者国家，亚历山大里亚正崛起为世界文化与贸易之都。
description.en: Ptolemy I was building Egypt into the wealthiest successor state of the Hellenistic world, with Alexandria rising as the world capital of culture and trade.

description.zh: 亚历山大大帝于公元前323年6月在巴比伦驾崩，年仅三十二岁，未留下明确继承人。他的帝国横跨从希腊到印度的广袤领土，其突然死亡引发了继业者之间数十年的战争。
description.en: Alexander the Great died in Babylon in June 323 BCE at the age of thirty-two, leaving no clear successor. His empire, stretching from Greece to India, was plunged into decades of wars among his generals, the Diadochi.

title.zh: 拉米亚战争——希腊反马其顿起义

description.zh: 雅典和希腊各邦趁亚历山大去世之机发动拉米亚战争，试图摆脱马其顿统治。马其顿将领安提帕特击败联军，迫使雅典接受寡头政治，标志着雅典独立民主的终结。
description.en: Athens and other Greek states launched the Lamian War after Alexander's death, attempting to throw off Macedonian rule. Macedonian general Antipater defeated the allies and forced Athens to accept oligarchic government, marking the end of Athenian independent democracy.

title.zh: 第一次继业者战争爆发
title.en: First War of the Diadochi Erupts

description.zh: 亚历山大的将军们为争夺帝国控制权爆发第一次继业者战争。佩尔迪卡斯以摄政身份试图维持帝国统一，但遭到托勒密、安提帕特和安提柯等人的联合反对。
description.en: Alexander's generals erupted into the First War of the Diadochi over control of the empire. Perdiccas, as regent, attempted to maintain imperial unity but faced a coalition of Ptolemy, Antipater, and Antigonus.

title.zh: 托勒密劫夺亚历山大灵柩至埃及
title.en: Ptolemy Diverts Alexander's Funeral to Egypt

description.zh: 托勒密将亚历山大遗体劫至埃及并安葬于亚历山大里亚，以此宣示其作为亚历山大正统继承者的地位。此举确立了托勒密王朝在埃及的统治合法性，也使亚历山大里亚成为新兴的文化中心。
description.en: Ptolemy diverted Alexander's body to Egypt and eventually entombed it in Alexandria, proclaiming himself as the legitimate successor. This act established the Ptolemaic dynasty's legitimacy in Egypt and positioned Alexandria as an emerging cultural center.

title.zh: 旃陀罗笈多建立孔雀王朝
title.en: Chandragupta Maurya Founds the Maurya Dynasty

description.zh: 旃陀罗笈多在谋士考底利耶辅佐下推翻难陀王朝，建立孔雀王朝，定都华氏城。孔雀帝国迅速统一了恒河流域，并向西北扩张，与亚历山大留下的希腊总督势力发生冲突。
description.en: Chandragupta Maurya, advised by Chanakya (Kautilya), overthrew the Nanda Dynasty and founded the Maurya Dynasty with its capital at Pataliputra. The Maurya Empire rapidly unified the Ganges basin and expanded northwestward, clashing with the Greek satrapal forces left by Alexander.

description.zh: 独眼安提柯宣布对亚历山大帝国的亚洲部分拥有主权，引发第二次继业者大战。他驱逐了巴比伦的塞琉古，迫使后者逃往托勒密的埃及寻求庇护。
description.en: Antigonus the One-Eyed declared sovereignty over the Asian portion of Alexander's empire, triggering the Second Diadochi War. He expelled Seleucus from Babylon, forcing him to flee to Ptolemy's Egypt for refuge.

title.zh: 加沙战役与塞琉古回归
title.en: Battle of Gaza and Seleucus' Return

description.zh: 托勒密在加沙战役中击败安提柯之子德米特里，塞琉古趁势返回巴比伦，重新建立统治。公元前312年被定为塞琉古纪元的起点，标志着塞琉古王朝的正式肇始。
description.en: Ptolemy defeated Antigonus' son Demetrius at the Battle of Gaza, allowing Seleucus to return to Babylon and reestablish his rule. The year 312 BCE was adopted as the start of the Seleucid Era, marking the formal beginning of the Seleucid dynasty.

title.zh: 秦国商鞅变法遗产延续
title.en: Legacy of Shang Yang's Reforms Continues in Qin

description.zh: 秦国在商鞅变法的制度遗产下持续壮大，推行郡县制、军功爵制和法家治国方略。秦昭襄王时期频繁东征，逐步蚕食韩、魏等邻国领土，为最终统一中国积蓄力量。
description.en: Qin continued to grow powerful under the institutional legacy of Shang Yang's reforms, implementing the commandery-county system, military merit rankings, and Legalist governance. Under King Zhaoxiang, Qin launched frequent eastern campaigns, gradually consuming territory from Han and Wei.

description.zh: 安提柯、托勒密、塞琉古、利西马库斯和卡山德相继正式称王，放弃了对亚历山大统一帝国的名义效忠。这标志着希腊化世界多极格局的正式形成。
description.en: Antigonus, Ptolemy, Seleucus, Lysimachus, and Cassander each formally proclaimed themselves kings, abandoning nominal allegiance to a unified Alexandrian empire. This marked the formal emergence of the multipolar Hellenistic world order.

title.zh: 塞琉古与旃陀罗笈多和约

description.zh: 塞琉古一世与孔雀王朝的旃陀罗笈多签订和约，将印度河以西部分领土（包括健陀罗）割让给孔雀帝国，换取五百头战象。此约确立了希腊化世界与南亚帝国的边界。
description.en: Seleucus I concluded a treaty with Chandragupta Maurya, ceding territories west of the Indus (including Gandhara) to the Maurya Empire in exchange for 500 war elephants. The treaty established the boundary between the Hellenistic world and the South Asian empire.

title.zh: 伊普苏斯战役——安提柯败亡
title.en: Battle of Ipsus — Fall of Antigonus

description.zh: 在伊普苏斯战役中，塞琉古、利西马库斯和卡山德联军击败安提柯，独眼安提柯在战场上阵亡。这场决战结束了统一帝国的最后可能，希腊化世界正式分裂为多个王国。
description.en: At the Battle of Ipsus, the combined forces of Seleucus, Lysimachus, and Cassander defeated Antigonus, who fell on the battlefield. This decisive battle ended the last possibility of a reunified empire and formally split the Hellenistic world into multiple kingdoms.

title.zh: 亚历山大里亚图书馆建立
title.en: Founding of the Library of Alexandria

description.zh: 托勒密一世在亚历山大里亚创建大图书馆和博物馆，广泛收集希腊文及各种语言的典籍，致力于建设人类知识的最大宝库。亚历山大里亚图书馆成为古代世界学术研究的中心。
description.en: Ptolemy I established the Great Library and Mouseion in Alexandria, collecting texts in Greek and many other languages to create the greatest repository of human knowledge. The Library of Alexandria became the premier center of scholarly research in the ancient world.

description.zh: 罗马共和国与萨姆尼特联盟进行第三次战争，争夺意大利中南部的控制权。罗马的军事组织和工程能力——特别是修筑军事大道——使其逐步征服亚平宁半岛各民族。
description.en: The Roman Republic fought the Third Samnite War against the Samnite Confederation for control of central and southern Italy. Rome's military organization and engineering capability—particularly its construction of military roads—enabled the gradual conquest of the Italic peoples.

title.zh: 长平之战前奏——秦赵对峙

description.zh: 秦国与赵国在上党地区展开激烈争夺，双方频繁爆发边境冲突。秦国的军功制度和高效行政体系使其在消耗战中占据优势，为后来的长平之战埋下伏笔。
description.en: Qin and Zhao engaged in fierce competition over the Shangdang region, with frequent border conflicts. Qin's military merit system and efficient administrative apparatus gave it the edge in wars of attrition, foreshadowing the decisive Battle of Changping.

description.zh: 迦太基在西西里、撒丁岛和北非建立了强大的贸易帝国，与希腊殖民城邦在西西里持续争夺势力范围。迦太基的海军力量和商业网络使其成为西地中海无可争议的霸主。
description.en: Carthage built a powerful trading empire across Sicily, Sardinia, and North Africa, contesting spheres of influence with Greek colonial cities in Sicily. Carthage's naval power and commercial networks made it the undisputed hegemon of the western Mediterranean.

description.zh: 伟大的数学家和发明家阿基米德约于公元前287年出生于西西里的叙拉古。他将在浮力原理、杠杆原理和军事工程等领域做出划时代贡献，成为古代世界最杰出的科学家之一。
description.en: The great mathematician and inventor Archimedes was born around 287 BCE in Syracuse, Sicily. He would make epoch-making contributions to the principles of buoyancy, levers, and military engineering, becoming one of the most outstanding scientists of the ancient world.

description.zh: 凯尔特（高卢）部落大规模入侵色雷斯和马其顿，对希腊化王国造成严重冲击。部分凯尔特人最终越过海峡进入安纳托利亚，在那里建立了加拉太地区。
description.en: Celtic (Gallic) tribes launched a massive invasion of Thrace and Macedon, severely impacting the Hellenistic kingdoms. Some Celts eventually crossed into Anatolia, where they established the region known as Galatia.

description.zh: 托勒密二世继承埃及王位，开创了托勒密王朝的鼎盛时期。他扩建亚历山大里亚图书馆，组织七十士将希伯来圣经译为希腊文，并开凿连接尼罗河与红海的运河。
description.en: Ptolemy II Philadelphus inherited the Egyptian throne, inaugurating the golden age of the Ptolemaic dynasty. He expanded the Library of Alexandria, commissioned the Septuagint translation of Hebrew scripture into Greek, and built a canal connecting the Nile to the Red Sea.

description.zh: 伊庇鲁斯国王皮洛士应南意大利希腊城邦邀请率军进入意大利，与罗马共和国作战。皮洛士赢得了多场战术胜利但损失惨重，"皮洛士式胜利"一词由此而来。
description.en: King Pyrrhus of Epirus invaded Italy at the invitation of the Greek cities of southern Italy, fighting against the Roman Republic. Pyrrhus won several tactical victories but at devastating cost, giving rise to the term "Pyrrhic victory."

title.en: Kingdom of Kush Shifts Capital to Meroë

description.zh: 库什王国将政治重心从纳帕塔南移至梅罗埃，远离了埃及的影响范围。梅罗埃发展出独特的本土文字和铁器工业，成为非洲东北部独立文明的典型代表。
description.en: The Kingdom of Kush shifted its political center southward from Napata to Meroë, distancing itself from Egyptian influence. Meroë developed a distinctive indigenous script and iron industry, becoming a prime example of independent civilization in northeastern Africa.

title.zh: 玛雅前古典期城市发展

description.zh: 玛雅低地的前古典期城市如纳克贝和米拉多尔开始建造大型金字塔和公共建筑群，发展出早期的象形文字和历法系统。这些城市中心的规模和复杂性表明了高度的社会组织。
description.en: Maya lowland Preclassic cities like Nakbe and El Mirador began constructing massive pyramids and public building complexes, developing early hieroglyphic writing and calendar systems. The scale and complexity of these urban centers indicated highly organized societies.

description.zh: 黑海北部草原的斯基泰游牧联盟面临萨尔马提亚人从东方的压力，势力逐渐收缩。斯基泰人的黄金工艺和骑射文化深刻影响了整个欧亚草原带的游牧民族。
description.en: The Scythian nomadic confederacy of the northern Black Sea steppe faced pressure from Sarmatians advancing from the east, gradually contracting its territory. Scythian goldwork and mounted archery culture profoundly influenced nomadic peoples across the entire Eurasian steppe belt.
